api/FluentGpu.Animation.AnimChannel.html.title: Enum AnimChannel | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Animation.AnimChannel.html.summary: Enum AnimChannel Namespace FluentGpu.Animation Assembly FluentGpu.Animation.dll Animatable channels. Transform channels compose into LocalTransform (TransformDirty); Opacity + the presented SizeW/SizeH (the "Reveal" presented extent the recorder draws the fill + child-clip at) → PaintDirty. LayoutW/LayoutH are the one deliberate exception to "animation never relays out": a SizeMode.Reflow track writes the interpolated size into LayoutInput each tick and the host re-solves the nearest layout boundary, so neighbours reflow smoothly. public enum AnimChannel : byte Fields ClipB = 13 ClipL = 10 ClipR = 12 ClipT = 11 LayoutH = 15 LayoutW = 14 Opacity = 5 Rotation = 4 ScaleX = 2 ScaleY = 3 SizeH = 7 SizeW = 6 StrokeTrimEnd = 9 StrokeTrimStart = 8 TranslateX = 0 TranslateY = 1

api/FluentGpu.Animation.AnimEngine.html.title: Class AnimEngine | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Animation.AnimEngine.html.summary: Class AnimEngine Namespace FluentGpu.Animation Assembly FluentGpu.Animation.dll Generic, composition-style animation runtime (phase 7) — the Web-Animations model on a fixed channel set. Tracks are eased (multi-keyframe + per-segment easing, time- OR driven-timeline) or springs (ODE with velocity handoff on retarget). Per (node,channel) tracks combine by Replace/Add/Accumulate. Each tick composes the surviving channel values into NodePaint.LocalTransform (T∘R∘S) + Opacity and marks Transform/PaintDirty — animation NEVER relays out. public sealed class AnimEngine Inheritance object AnimEngine Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors AnimEngine(SceneStore) public AnimEngine(SceneStore scene) Parameters scene SceneStore Properties Clocks public DrivenClockTable Clocks { get; } Property Value DrivenClockTable HasActive public bool HasActive { get; } Property Value bool IncrementalRoots Nodes whose presented SIZE changed this tick under SizeMode.Relayout — the host re-solves just these subtrees (scoped layout) so their text re-wraps live. Cleared by the host after it consumes them. public List<NodeHandle> IncrementalRoots { get; } Property Value List<NodeHandle> LoopTrackCount Looping tracks (Loop==true) — O(1) maintained counter, not a scan. public int LoopTrackCount { get; } Property Value int ReflowRoots Nodes whose REFLOW size advanced this tick — the host re-solves their boundary scope, then refreshes the Trailing child-shift from the fresh bounds. Cleared by the host after it consumes them. public List<NodeHandle> ReflowRoots { get; } Property Value List<NodeHandle> TrackCount Active animation tracks (springs + eased/driven), all channels — O(1) census. public int TrackCount { get; } Property Value int TransitionCount Live per-node layout-transition specs (the _transitions side-table) — O(1) census. public int TransitionCount { get; } Property Value int Methods Animate(NodeHandle, AnimChannel, float, float, float, Easing, CompositeOp, float) Eased two-point tween (retargets any live track on the same node+channel). public void Animate(NodeHandle node, AnimChannel channel, float from, float to, float durationMs, Easing easing = Easing.EaseInOut, CompositeOp composite = CompositeOp.Replace, float delayMs = 0) Parameters node NodeHandle channel AnimChannel from float to float durationMs float easing Easing composite CompositeOp delayMs float Animate(NodeHandle, AnimChannel, float, float, float, EasingSpec, CompositeOp, float) public void Animate(NodeHandle node, AnimChannel channel, float from, float to, float durationMs, EasingSpec easing, CompositeOp composite = CompositeOp.Replace, float delayMs = 0) Parameters node NodeHandle channel AnimChannel from float to float durationMs float easing EasingSpec composite CompositeOp delayMs float AnimateBounds(NodeHandle, in RectF, in RectF, in LayoutTransition) FLIP the node from its captured presented rect to its new laid-out rect, seeding/retargeting the channels the spec requests. Position is velocity-continuous: a running spring keeps its velocity and shifts its offset by the layout delta (no jump on interruption); a fresh spring starts offset by the full delta and settles to 0. The host calls this once per commit for every BoundsAnimated node that moved; Tick then advances it every frame. public void AnimateBounds(NodeHandle node, in RectF fromAbs, in RectF toAbs, in LayoutTransition spec) Parameters node NodeHandle fromAbs RectF toAbs RectF spec LayoutTransition Cancel(NodeHandle, AnimChannel) public void Cancel(NodeHandle node, AnimChannel channel) Parameters node NodeHandle channel AnimChannel CancelAll(NodeHandle) public void CancelAll(NodeHandle node) Parameters node NodeHandle CancelToRest(NodeHandle, AnimChannel) Cancel + reset the channel's paint to its settle-time resting sentinel — symmetric with the settle path (StrokeTrim/PresentedW/H → NaN, Clip → Infinite). A bare Cancel(NodeHandle, AnimChannel) only drops the track, freezing the last interpolated value in paint with no spec fallback (a deactivated ProgressRing froze a partial arc). Channels without a rest sentinel (transform/opacity/layout) behave exactly like Cancel — their resting value is the reconciler's static re-assert. public void CancelToRest(NodeHandle node, AnimChannel channel) Parameters node NodeHandle channel AnimChannel ClearTransition(NodeHandle) Drop a node's layout-transition spec (the element stopped declaring Animate, or the slot was freed). public void ClearTransition(NodeHandle node) Parameters node NodeHandle ConsumeReflowWrites() True if any reflow track wrote LayoutInput this tick (interp advance or settle restore) — the host runs a boundary-scoped re-solve before record. Self-clearing. public bool ConsumeReflowWrites() Returns bool Drive(NodeHandle, AnimChannel, Keyframe[], int, float, float, CompositeOp) Scroll/value-driven track: progress comes from a DrivenClock source mapped through [domainMin,domainMax]. public void Drive(NodeHandle node, AnimChannel channel, Keyframe[] keys, int drivenRef, float domainMin, float domainMax, CompositeOp composite = CompositeOp.Replace) Parameters node NodeHandle channel AnimChannel keys Keyframe[] drivenRef int domainMin float domainMax float composite CompositeOp Ease(Easing, float) Evaluate an easing curve (kept for source compatibility; the implementation lives in Foundation). public static float Ease(Easing e, float t) Parameters e Easing t float Returns float HasTracks(NodeHandle) True while any track targets this node (used by the host to detect a settled exit orphan). public bool HasTracks(NodeHandle node) Parameters node NodeHandle Returns bool Keyframes(NodeHandle, AnimChannel, Keyframe[], float, bool, CompositeOp, float) Multi-keyframe eased track (@keyframes). Offsets must be ascending in 0..1. public void Keyframes(NodeHandle node, AnimChannel channel, Keyframe[] keys, float durationMs, bool loop = false, CompositeOp composite = CompositeOp.Replace, float delayMs = 0) Parameters node NodeHandle channel AnimChannel keys Keyframe[] durationMs float loop bool composite CompositeOp delayMs float SeedEnter(NodeHandle, in EnterExit, in LayoutTransition) An inserted node animates FROM the enter terminal (offset/scale/opacity) TO identity. public void SeedEnter(NodeHandle node, in EnterExit e, in LayoutTransition spec) Parameters node NodeHandle e EnterExit spec LayoutTransition SeedExit(NodeHandle, in EnterExit, in LayoutTransition) A removed node (now an Exiting orphan) animates FROM its current state TO the exit terminal; when all its tracks settle (HasTracks(NodeHandle) == false) the host reclaims it. public void SeedExit(NodeHandle node, in EnterExit e, in LayoutTransition spec) Parameters node NodeHandle e EnterExit spec LayoutTransition SetTransition(NodeHandle, in LayoutTransition) Attach (or replace) a node's layout-transition spec. Called by the reconciler from BoxEl.Animate. public void SetTransition(NodeHandle node, in LayoutTransition t) Parameters node NodeHandle t LayoutTransition Spring(NodeHandle, AnimChannel, float, in SpringParams, float?, CompositeOp, float) Spring toward to. If a spring already runs on this node+channel, it RETARGETS — keeping position + velocity (no snap), the iOS/Compose velocity-handoff. public void Spring(NodeHandle node, AnimChannel channel, float to, in SpringParams spring, float? initial = null, CompositeOp composite = CompositeOp.Replace, float delayMs = 0) Parameters node NodeHandle channel AnimChannel to float spring SpringParams initial float? composite CompositeOp delayMs float Tick(float) public void Tick(float dtMs) Parameters dtMs float TryGetTrackValue(NodeHandle, AnimChannel, out float) The LIVE value of an in-flight track on node+channel — so an interrupting eased tween can start FROM where the animation currently is instead of a recomputed endpoint (WinUI AnimatedIcon queues/blends transitions, AnimatedIcon.cpp:235-267 — a mid-flight chevron reversal must not snap). False = no live track (caller uses its resting value). public bool TryGetTrackValue(NodeHandle node, AnimChannel channel, out float value) Parameters node NodeHandle channel AnimChannel value float Returns bool TryGetTransition(NodeHandle, out LayoutTransition) Read a node's layout-transition spec (set by the reconciler this commit). public bool TryGetTransition(NodeHandle node, out LayoutTransition t) Parameters node NodeHandle t LayoutTransition Returns bool

api/FluentGpu.Animation.CaretBlinker.htm.title: Class CaretBlinker | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Animation.CaretBlinker.htm.summary: Class CaretBlinker Namespace FluentGpu.Animation Assembly FluentGpu.Animation.dll Drives the focused editor's caret blink (the RepeatTicker phase-7 idiom): while an editable text node is focused, the CaretVisible bit toggles every half-period (Win32 GetCaretBlinkTime semantics — the host passes the OS value; headless uses the fixed 500ms default) and the node is marked PaintDirty so the recorder re-emits. One focused editor at a time (focus is singular). The host includes HasActive in its work gate so the loop keeps ticking at blink granularity while an editor is focused — and goes fully idle the moment it blurs. Zero alloc per tick (writes a POD row + a flag bit). public sealed class CaretBlinker Inheritance object CaretBlinker Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors CaretBlinker(SceneStore) public CaretBlinker(SceneStore scene) Parameters scene SceneStore Fields DefaultBlinkMs Default half-period (caret-on / caret-off time) — the Win32 GetCaretBlinkTime default. public const float DefaultBlinkMs = 500 Field Value float Properties HasActive An editor is focused → the frame loop must keep ticking (at blink granularity). public bool HasActive { get; } Property Value bool Methods Blur(NodeHandle) Stop blinking for this editor (focus lost): caret hidden, Focused cleared. public void Blur(NodeHandle textNode) Parameters textNode NodeHandle BlurAll() Blur whichever editor is blinking (window deactivated / overlay swallowed focus). public void BlurAll() Focus(NodeHandle, float) Begin blinking for the (newly focused) editor's text node: caret shown, blink phase reset. blinkMs is the half-period (GetCaretBlinkTime); ≤ 0 falls back to the default. public void Focus(NodeHandle textNode, float blinkMs = 500) Parameters textNode NodeHandle blinkMs float ResetBlink(NodeHandle) An edit happened: the caret snaps visible and the blink phase restarts (WinUI/Win32 behavior — the caret never blinks away mid-typing). public void ResetBlink(NodeHandle textNode) Parameters textNode NodeHandle Tick(float) Phase-7 tick: accumulate; on each elapsed half-period toggle the caret bit. A slow frame spanning several half-periods nets the parity (an even count is a visual no-op — no spurious repaint). public void Tick(float dtMs) Parameters dtMs float

api/FluentGpu.Animation.MotionSprings.ht.title: Class MotionSprings | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Animation.MotionSprings.ht.summary: Class MotionSprings Namespace FluentGpu.Animation Assembly FluentGpu.Animation.dll The WinUI/Fluent-2 motion vocabulary as named presets, so controls share ONE set of spring/duration constants instead of inlining response/damping pairs. Durations: ControlFaster = 83ms (brush transitions, hover/press), ControlFast = 167ms (knob travel, settle animations), ControlNormal = 250ms (reposition, pane motion). The composition-spring presets match the WinUI indicator/selection-pill feel (slight overshoot, fast settle). public static class MotionSprings Inheritance object MotionSprings Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties NavPill The NavigationView selection-pill vertical slide (WinUI composition spring: snappy, faint overshoot). public static SpringParams NavPill { get; } Property Value SpringParams PartSettle A snappy critically-damped settle for small template parts (knobs, chevrons). public static SpringParams PartSettle { get; } Property Value SpringParams SelectorPill The SelectorBar underline/pill slide between items. public static SpringParams SelectorPill { get; } Property Value SpringParams

api/FluentGpu.Animation.RepeatTicker.htm.title: Class RepeatTicker | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Animation.RepeatTicker.htm.summary: Class RepeatTicker Namespace FluentGpu.Animation Assembly FluentGpu.Animation.dll Drives WinUI RepeatButton auto-repeat: while a repeat node is held, its click handler is invoked once on press, then after an initial delay, then at a fixed interval. A host system armed by the dispatcher on press / released on up (the InteractionAnimator/ScrollAnimator idiom), ticked once per frame in the host loop and reported through HasActive so frames keep flowing while held — and stop the instant it's released (no busy loop). Zero alloc on a steady frame (invokes the already-stored click Action). Delay/Interval come from the node (InteractionInfo.RepeatDelayMs/RepeatIntervalMs; NaN/non-positive = the WinUI DP defaults, validated positive like RepeatButton_Partial.cpp:149-182). While the held pointer leaves the node the ticker PAUSES; re-entry resumes with a fresh initial delay and never an immediate re-fire (RepeatButton_Partial.cpp:530-548, :565-574). public sealed class RepeatTicker Inheritance object RepeatTicker Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors RepeatTicker(SceneStore) public RepeatTicker(SceneStore scene) Parameters scene SceneStore Properties HasActive True while armed AND ticking — a paused ticker needs no frames (the resuming pointer move wakes it). public bool HasActive { get; } Property Value bool Methods Arm(NodeHandle) Begin auto-repeat for node: fire the click once now, then schedule the repeat. public void Arm(NodeHandle node) Parameters node NodeHandle Disarm(NodeHandle) Stop auto-repeat (on pointer-up / key-up / cancel) if node is the active one. public void Disarm(NodeHandle node) Parameters node NodeHandle Pause(NodeHandle) The held pointer left the armed node: stop ticking (idempotent; stays armed for a Resume). public void Pause(NodeHandle node) Parameters node NodeHandle Resume(NodeHandle) The held pointer re-entered the armed node: restart with a FRESH initial delay — never an immediate re-fire (idempotent while already running). public void Resume(NodeHandle node) Parameters node NodeHandle Tick(float) public void Tick(float dtMs) Parameters dtMs float

api/FluentGpu.Animation.ScrollAnimator.h.title: Class ScrollAnimator | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Animation.ScrollAnimator.h.summary: Class ScrollAnimator Namespace FluentGpu.Animation Assembly FluentGpu.Animation.dll Smooth scrolling (phase 7): eases each armed viewport's live offset toward its target (the WinUI inertial wheel feel), applies the content's -offset transform, re-realizes the virtual window at guard-band edges, and drives the auto-hiding scrollbar's WinUI "conscious" state machine. Only viewports actively scrolling/transitioning are ticked; zero work / zero alloc once everything settles (HasActive == false). Conscious-scrollbar timing — verified against microsoft-ui-xaml controls\dev\CommonStyles\ScrollBar_themeresources.xaml: Expand: pointer dwells over the scrollbar lane for ScrollBarExpandBeginTime = 400ms (:188), then the thumb grows / track+buttons fade in over ScrollBarExpandDuration = 167ms (:173) with KeySpline 0,0,0,1 (:587) — FluentPopOpen. Contract (lane left, pointer still inside the viewport): begins after ScrollBarContractBeginTime = 500ms (:189), plays over ScrollBarContractDuration = 167ms (:176), same spline (:543). Indicator fades over ScrollBarOpacityChangeDuration = 83ms (:174), linear (the storyboards' plain DoubleAnimation). Idle auto-hide after a SCROLL: ScrollBarContractDelay = 2s (:178; ScrollViewer's SeparatorContractDelay is the same 2s — ScrollViewer_themeresources.xaml) then the 83ms fade. ENGINE-DELIBERATE (documented deviation): when the pointer leaves the whole viewport and the bar was revealed by hover alone (no scroll since it appeared), it contracts immediately and retires after ContractBeginTime + ContractDuration (≈667ms) + the 83ms fade — WinUI would hold the expanded bar 500ms and the thin indicator the full 2s; an untouched hover-flash retiring early reads cleaner and keeps the frame loop idle. Scroll-revealed bars keep the WinUI 2s ContractDelay. While the pointer rests inside the viewport the thin indicator stays visible (the WinUI MouseIndicator hold); it never idle-hides under the pointer. public sealed class ScrollAnimator Inheritance object ScrollAnimator Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors ScrollAnimator(SceneStore) public ScrollAnimator(SceneStore scene) Parameters scene SceneStore Fields ContractBeginMs public const float ContractBeginMs = 500 Field Value float ExpandBeginMs public const float ExpandBeginMs = 400 Field Value float ExpandContractMs public const float ExpandContractMs = 167 Field Value float FadeMs public const float FadeMs = 83 Field Value float IdleHideMs public const float IdleHideMs = 2000 Field Value float LeaveHideMs Engine-deliberate hover-flash retire delay (see class remarks): contract-begin + contract. public const float LeaveHideMs = 667 Field Value float Properties ActiveCount Viewports currently scrolling/transitioning (the armed set) — O(1) census. public int ActiveCount { get; } Property Value int ConsciousStateCount Viewports retaining a conscious-scrollbar timer row (survives drops until fully hidden) — O(1) census. public int ConsciousStateCount { get; } Property Value int HasActive public bool HasActive { get; } Property Value bool RequestRerender public Action RequestRerender { get; set; } Property Value Action Methods Arm(NodeHandle) public void Arm(NodeHandle n) Parameters n NodeHandle Hover(NodeHandle, bool) Reveal a viewport's scrollbar (pointer is over the scrollable area) and reset its idle timer so it stays up while hovered; lane hover arms the WinUI 400ms-delayed expand. public void Hover(NodeHandle n, bool overScrollbar) Parameters n NodeHandle overScrollbar bool Leave(NodeHandle) public void Leave(NodeHandle n) Parameters n NodeHandle Tick(float) public void Tick(float dtMs) Parameters dtMs float

LayoutW/LayoutH are the one deliberate exception to "animation never relays out": a SizeMode.Reflow track writes the interpolated size into LayoutInput each tick and the host re-solves the nearest layout boundary, so neighbours reflow smoothly. api/FluentGpu.Controls.AnnotatedScrollBa.summary: Class AnnotatedScrollBar Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll WinUI AnnotatedScrollBar (controls\dev\AnnotatedScrollBar) — the jump-list rail: a column of right-aligned labels beside a thin accent thumb, with CLICK-TO-JUMP on the rail, thumb drag, and repeat-hold increment/decrement buttons. Template facts (AnnotatedScrollBar.xaml + _themeresources.xaml; the _perf2026 variant differs only in storyboard→setter form, identical values): • Control MinWidth = LabelsGridMinWidth 44 (xaml:4, :37) — the control hugs ~44 unless its host stretches it. • Root grid rows: increment RepeatButton on TOP (glyph EDDB, HorizontalAlignment=Right — xaml:31-39), the vertical grid (star), decrement RepeatButton on the BOTTOM (glyph EDDC — xaml:94-102). • Thumb = 30×3 @ r1.5 (ThumbWidth :36 / ThumbHeight :35 / ThumbCornerRadius :41), VerticalThumbBrush = AccentFillColorDefault (:11/:21), HorizontalAlignment=Right + VerticalAlignment=Top at the scroll position (xaml:84-92). • Ghost thumb = same geometry in AccentFillColorDisabled (PART_VerticalThumbGhost xaml:74-83) — the hover preview of where a rail click would land. • PART_LabelsGrid: HorizontalAlignment=Center, MinWidth 44, transparent background (xaml:41-45); the LabelTemplate is a right-aligned BodyTextBlockStyle TextBlock (14px Normal — TextBlock_themeresources.xaml:4/:23) with Margin 0,-5,0,-2 (xaml:8-19). • PART_ToolTipRail: a 1px right-aligned tooltip anchor rail (xaml:46) whose ToolTip is Placement=Top, MaxWidth 360 / MinHeight 40 (:38/:39), BaseTextBlockStyle content (14px SemiBold) — modeled by detailLabel: the resolved text floats in a chip above the pointer (the DetailLabelRequested seam). • ScrollButtonStyle: MinWidth/MinHeight 16 (:43-44), FontSize 8 (:40/:51), CornerRadius = ControlCornerRadius 4 (:49), background SubtleFillColorTransparent in EVERY state (:5; the :56-87 visual states recolor ONLY the foreground: TextFillColorPrimary → Secondary hover → Tertiary pressed → Disabled, :6-9), IsTabStop=False (xaml:37/:100) — each steps by SmallChange (default ViewportSize / ratio — AnnotatedScrollBar.cpp:484-489 EnsureSmallChangeValue). • Rail click / drag / buttons raise Scrolling with the offset + kind, CANCELABLE (AnnotatedScrollBarScrollingEventArgs.Cancel; cpp:960+ RaiseScrolling) — modeled by the onScrolling filter returning false to cancel. ENGINE SEMANTIC (the bug this file once had): inside a ZStack, children land at the LEFT edge and AlignSelf is the VERTICAL overlay placement — RIGHT alignment therefore uses a full-width row wrapper (Direction=0, Justify=FlexJustify.End) per overlay part, never AlignSelf. Thumb position rides a TransformBind on the position signal (compositor-instant, no re-render). public static class AnnotatedScrollBar Inheritance object AnnotatedScrollBar Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields PartGhost The hover-preview ghost-thumb row (WinUI PART_VerticalThumbGhost; mounted only while hovering). api/FluentGpu.Controls.AppBarButton.html.summary:
Class AppBarButton Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI AppBarButton (AppBarButton_themeresources.xaml). Three layout families:
  • FullSize (default style): a 68-wide (:133 Width=68), 64-min-high (AppBarThemeMinHeight, CommandBar_themeresources.xaml:71) vertical stack — 16px icon (AppBarButtonContentHeight) over a 12px Caption label.
  • Compact (isCompact: true — the closed CommandBar): the label collapses; icon-only at the 48px compact height (AppBarThemeCompactHeight :72), ContentViewboxCompactMargin 0,12,0,12 (:111).
  • Overflow ( — the CommandBar overflow menu): a HORIZONTAL row — optional toggle-check column, optional 16×16 icon at the left (OverflowWithMenuIcons :211-223), the label as a 14px Body run (OverflowTextLabel, padding 0,5,0,8 = AppBarButtonOverflowTextLabelPadding :114; lead margin 12 / 38 with toggles / 76 with toggles+icons :192-238), and right-aligned 12px accelerator text (KeyboardAcceleratorTextLabel Margin 24,0,12,0 :371).
State fills are the subtle ramp (AppBarButtonBackground/​PointerOver/​Pressed = SubtleFill Transparent/Secondary/ Tertiary); press dims the foreground to TextSecondary. FocusVisualMargin = −3 (:135). api/FluentGpu.Controls.AppBarToggleButto.summary: Class AppBarToggleButton Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI AppBarToggleButton: like AppBarButton (a vertically-stacked 16px icon glyph over a 12px label) but a stateful toggle. When checked it becomes a SOLID accent pill (AccentDefault fill, white TextOnAccentPrimary icon/label, with AccentSecondary/AccentTertiary hover/press) and gains the accent elevation-border (AppBarToggleButtonBorderBrushChecked); unchecked it is transparent with the standard subtle hover/press states. Press dims the foreground (TextSecondary unchecked, TextOnAccentSecondary checked) to match WinUI via the per-state foreground ramps. Used inside a CommandBar; the whole control is one click target that flips the checked state. api/FluentGpu.Controls.AutoSuggestBox.ht.title: Class AutoSuggestBox | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.AutoSuggestBox.ht.summary: Class AutoSuggestBox Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI AutoSuggestBox: an EditableText field whose filtered suggestions are hosted in a light-dismiss popup (an anchored overlay), not inline — so the list floats over content instead of reflowing the page and auto-flips above the box when there is no room below (same plumbing as ComboBox). The list is opened iff the query is non-empty and there is at least one match (WinUI UpdateSuggestionListVisibility: open iff maxHeight > 0 ∧ count > 0), driven from a UseEffect keyed on the query so the open/close runs after the render commits (no side effects in render). The popup body is wrapped in its own Comp<T>(Func<T>) FluentGpu.Controls.SuggestionsList Component that subscribes to the shared query/highlight Signal<T>s, so it RE-FILTERS on every keystroke — an overlay content thunk is otherwise a one-shot snapshot (only re-evaluated when the OverlayHost version bumps on open/close). TextChanged + reason (AutoSuggestBox_Partial.cpp OnTextBoxTextChanged :451–503). EVERY text change — user edit, programmatic restore, suggestion preview — Stop()+Start()s the 150ms timer (s_textChangedEventTimerDuration = 1500000 ticks) carrying the change's TextChangeReason (cpp:464), so the public TextChanged fires once typing pauses, with the LAST reason. Only UserInput changes cache m_userTypedText, reset the keyboard cursor and update the list visibility (cpp:477–496). Set DebounceMs=0 to fire synchronously. SuggestionChosen + arrow preview (cpp OnSuggestionSelectionChanged :2298–2411). Moving the suggestion-list selection (arrow keys, row click) raises SuggestionChosen for the newly selected item; when UpdateTextOnSelect (default true, cpp:2366–2381) the item text is also previewed into the field with reason SuggestionChosen (cpp:2380). Stepping PAST either end restores the user-typed text with reason ProgrammaticChange and clears the selection (cpp:1101/1109). Enter submits the query (QueryText = the field text, ChosenSuggestion = the highlighted item) WITHOUT re-raising SuggestionChosen (cpp:1149–1160 + SubmitQuery :857–878). A row click runs SelectionChanged → SuggestionChosen → QuerySubmitted sequentially (cpp OnListViewItemClick :2413–2437). Escape restores the typed text and closes (cpp:634–643). Corner-joining (WinUI AutoSuggestBoxHelper::UpdateCornerRadius / KeepInteriorCornersSquare=true). While the list is open the field squares the corners that abut the popup so the field+list read as one piece. WinUI squares the field BOTTOM (open-down) or TOP (open-up) and the matching popup edge; we square the field bottom corners (open-down — the dominant case) here. The popup-surface corner squaring is host-owned: FluentGpu.Controls.FlyoutSurface already receives the CornerJoin from FlyoutPositioner on its FluentGpu.Controls.OverlayEntry but currently renders Radii.OverlayAll unconditionally — see the deferred note in the task report (cannot be fixed from this file). public sealed class AutoSuggestBox : Component Inheritance object Component AutoSuggestBox Inherited Members Component.Context Component.RunsOnce Component.RenderWithHooks() Component.Unmount() object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods MotionHooks.UseEntrance(Component, float, object?) MotionHooks.UseHoverScale(Component, bool, float) Fields DebounceMs public float DebounceMs Field Value float IconFontSize public const float IconFontSize = 12 Field Value float ItemMinHeight public const float ItemMinHeight = 40 Field Value float MaxHeight public float MaxHeight Field Value float MaxPopupHeight public const float MaxPopupHeight = 374 Field Value float OnQuerySubmitted public Action<string>? OnQuerySubmitted Field Value Action<string> OnSuggestionChosen public Action<string>? OnSuggestionChosen Field Value Action<string> OnTextChanged public Action<string>? Owned: OnClick (submit), Role. public const string PartQueryButton = "QueryButton" Field Value string PartQueryIcon The query glyph (a TextEl — use Parts.Set<TextEl>; the glyph STRING is the QueryIcon prop). Owned: none. public const string PartQueryIcon = "QueryIcon" Field Value string PartRoot The field surface root (the chrome + popup anchor). Owned: OnKeyDown (the Up/Down suggestion nav), Role, Children, OnRealized (the anchor capture, chained). The open-state corner squaring (KeepInteriorCornersSquare) is recomputed per render BEFORE the modifier — override Corners to opt out. public const string PartRoot = "Root" Field Value string PartSuggestionItem EVERY suggestion row plate (a repeated part — the popup list is built per render, NOT virtualized/recycled). Owned: OnClick (choose + submit), Role. The keyboard-cursor/selected Fill is recomputed per render BEFORE the modifier. public const string PartSuggestionItem = "SuggestionItem" Field Value string PartSuggestionsList The popup scroll host (WinUI SuggestionsList; a ScrollEl — use Parts.Set<ScrollEl>). Owned: Content (the row column), ContentSized (the size-to-rows mechanic). api/FluentGpu.Controls.BackgroundSizing..summary: Enum BackgroundSizing Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll WinUI BackgroundSizing: whether the control fill stops at the inner border edge or extends under the border to its outer edge. WinUI DefaultButtonStyle = InnerBorderEdge, AccentButtonStyle = OuterBorderEdge (microsoft-ui-xaml controls\dev\CommonStyles\Button_themeresources.xaml:156,238); ToggleButton flips InnerBorderEdge → OuterBorderEdge while checked (ToggleButton_themeresources.xaml:182,6). Carried on the button-family styles as the API/parity contract. HONEST NOTE: the renderer today draws every fill to the OUTER rounded-rect edge with the border stroked on top — i.e. OuterBorderEdge-equivalent for all values; the 1px fill inset of InnerBorderEdge is a named E9 render-primitive gap, not silently dropped. With the WinUI hairline (1px) translucent elevation borders the visual delta is sub-pixel-class. public enum BackgroundSizing : byte Fields InnerBorderEdge = 0 OuterBorderEdge = 1

api/FluentGpu.Controls.BreadcrumbBar.htm.title: Class BreadcrumbBar | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.BreadcrumbBar.htm.summary: Class BreadcrumbBar Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI BreadcrumbBar (controls\dev\Breadcrumb): a horizontal trail of items separated by right chevrons (BreadcrumbBarChevronLeftToRight = E974, BreadcrumbBar_themeresources.xaml:5). The last item is the current page — TextFillColorPrimary at FontWeight Normal like every other crumb (BreadcrumbBarItemFontWeight, BreadcrumbBar_themeresources.xaml:8) and non-interactive (the LastItem state collapses PART_ItemButton, BreadcrumbBar.xaml:89-96); earlier items are clickable, dim to TextFillColorSecondary on hover and TextFillColorTertiary on press (themeresources:10-11), and raise onSelect(index). Keyboard: every crumb is a tab stop (DefaultBreadcrumbBarItemStyle IsTabStop=True, BreadcrumbBar.xaml:13); Left/Right move focus crumb-to-crumb (OnChildPreviewKeyDown, BreadcrumbBar.cpp:514-560). NOT implemented: the overflow ellipsis + hidden-items flyout (BreadcrumbLayout.cpp:62-69 collapses leading crumbs when the accumulated width exceeds the available size) — that needs measured-width feedback the engine does not surface yet (layout effects re-run only on re-render, never on a window-resize relayout). Per-part restyling goes through the optional parts (see TemplateParts for the contract). public static class BreadcrumbBar Inheritance object BreadcrumbBar Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields PartItem Each crumb's box — the SAME modifier runs for every crumb, the last/current one included. Owned: OnClick (navigate — re-asserted on the clickable, non-last crumbs only), Role, TabStop, OnKeyDown (arrow focus moves), OnRealized (focus-handle capture, chained). public const string PartItem = "Item" Field Value string PartSeparator The right-chevron separator between crumbs (WinUI PART_ChevronTextBlock) — a TextEl, so style it via the generic map: parts.Set<TextEl>(BreadcrumbBar.PartSeparator, t => t with { … }). Owned: nothing (pure styling). public const string PartSeparator = "Separator" Field Value string Methods Create(IReadOnlyList<string>, Action<int>?, TemplateParts?) public static Element Create(IReadOnlyList<string> items, Action<int>? onSelect = null, TemplateParts? parts = null) Parameters items IReadOnlyList<string> onSelect Action<int> parts TemplateParts Returns Element

api/FluentGpu.Controls.Button.html.title: Class Button | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.Button.html.summary: Class Button Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll The Button control: barebone behavior (clickable, hover/pressed, focusable) + a default Fluent style. Overrideable globally (AccentStyleOverride/StandardStyleOverride), per-instance (pass a Button.Style), or ad-hoc (chain modifiers). Defaults are sourced from WinUI 3 DefaultButtonStyle / AccentButtonStyle (microsoft-ui-xaml controls\dev\CommonStyles\Button_themeresources.xaml): ButtonPadding 11,5,11,6 (line 152); ControlCornerRadius 4 (line 168); ControlContentThemeFontSize 14; ButtonBorderThemeThickness 1 (lines 29/90/127); FocusVisualMargin −3 (line 167); BackgroundTransition 83ms (lines 173-175). WinUI Button has NO scale animation — its state storyboards swap Background/BorderBrush/Foreground only (Button_themeresources.xaml:176-229), so no Hover/PressScale here (removed at the Wave-1 parity pass). Keyboard: the engine arms a pressed visual on Space/Enter key-DOWN and clicks on key-UP — held keys never repeat, any other key cancels without firing (WinUI ButtonBaseKeyProcess.h; E2) — and draws the keyboard focus ring itself (E1) — nothing control-side. The pointer cursor stays the WinUI arrow (only HyperlinkButton shows a hand — dxaml\xcp\dxaml\lib\HyperLinkButton_Partial.cpp:32). partial so apps/framework can add variants. public static class Button Inheritance object Button Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields AccentStyleOverride Set to globally replace the accent-button default style. public static Button.Style? AccentStyleOverride Field Value Button.Style PartLabel The label run — a TextEl, so customize via parts.Set<TextEl>(Button.PartLabel, …). Owned: none (the P2 foreground ramp is style-driven and a modifier may override it). public const string PartLabel = "Label" Field Value string PartRoot The button chrome (the WinUI ContentPresenter root). Owned: OnClick, Role, Children (the label slot). public const string PartRoot = "Root" Field Value string StandardStyleOverride Set to globally replace the standard-button default style. public static Button.Style? StandardStyleOverride Field Value Button.Style Properties AccentStyle public static Button.Style AccentStyle { get; } Property Value Button.Style StandardStyle public static Button.Style StandardStyle { get; } Property Value Button.Style Methods Accent(string, Action, Style?, bool, TemplateParts?) An accent (primary) button. api/FluentGpu.Controls.CalendarDatePicke.summary: Class CalendarDatePicker Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI CalendarDatePicker: a single button showing the chosen date (or a "Pick a date" placeholder — WinUI's localized default, calendardatepicker_partial.cpp:45-48) with a trailing calendar glyph (E787). Clicking opens an interactive CalendarView in a light-dismissable flyout anchored below the button; the calendar's SelectedDatesChanged is wired back — picking a date sets Date, CLOSES the flyout and raises OnDateChanged (calendardatepicker_partial.cpp:201-204, :259-276; a deselect nulls the date but keeps the flyout open). api/FluentGpu.Controls.CaptionButton.htm.title: Class CaptionButton | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.CaptionButton.htm.summary: Class CaptionButton Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll An engine-drawn window caption button (minimize / maximize-restore / close) for the custom-frame TitleBar — the Win11 shell look: 46×bar-height, square-edged (caption buttons abut the window edge, no corner radius), a 10px Segoe Fluent chrome glyph, subtle hover/press fills (close = the shell red with a white glyph). The OS owns those pixels' hit-testing (WM_NCHITTEST returns HTMINBUTTON/HTMAXBUTTON/HTCLOSE so the Win11 snap-layouts flyout works); the Win32 PAL synthesizes pointer input back into the engine from the WM_NC* mouse messages, so these ramps run through the normal InteractionAnimator path even though the pixels are non-client. Visual states = BoxEl fill ramp + TextEl foreground ramp at 83ms (WinUI ControlFasterAnimationDuration) with the BoxEl default (0,0,0,1) spline — never a per-control state machine. public static class CaptionButton Inheritance object CaptionButton Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields GlyphSize Chrome glyph point size (FluentAvalonia WindowDecorations.axaml:117). public const float GlyphSize = 10 Field Value float Width Win11 caption button width @100% (FluentAvalonia WindowDecorations.axaml:9; ×scale at hit-test). public const float Width = 46 Field Value float Properties Close Close ramp: the Win11 shell red on hover/press with a white glyph (pressed glyph at ~70%). public static CaptionButton.Style Close { get; } Property Value CaptionButton.Style MinMax Minimize/maximize ramp: transparent → SubtleSecondary → SubtleTertiary fills; glyph primary → secondary. public static CaptionButton.Style MinMax { get; } Property Value CaptionButton.Style Methods Create(string, Action, Style, bool, float, Action<NodeHandle>?) active = window activation (false dims the glyph to InactiveForeground — fills stay wired; Win11 shows hover states on inactive captions too). height = the bar height (caption buttons stretch the full bar, flush to the top edge). onRealized captures the node handle for the TitleBar's WM_NCHITTEST region report. public static BoxEl Create(string glyph, Action onClick, CaptionButton.Style style, bool active = true, float height = 48, Action<NodeHandle>? onRealized = null) Parameters glyph string onClick Action style CaptionButton.Style active bool height float onRealized Action<NodeHandle> Returns BoxEl

api/FluentGpu.Controls.CheckBox.html.title: Class CheckBox | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.CheckBox.html.summary: Class CheckBox Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI CheckBox: a 20px box + label. Two-state via the bool overload; three-state (adds the mixed "indeterminate" glyph) via the CheckState overload, which cycles Unchecked → Checked → Indeterminate → Unchecked on click. Controlled — the caller owns the state. Visuals follow WinUI 1:1 but WITHOUT WinUI's 12-state matrix (Unchecked/Checked/Indeterminate × Normal/PointerOver/ Pressed/Disabled, each restating 7 setters). Instead each property is a StateBrush ramp over the interaction axis, picked by the logical state; the engine eases Rest→Hover→Pressed using the element's interaction timing specs and the disabled leg is a flat swap. The glyph uses WinUI's AnimatedAccept draw-on timeline plus PressScale for the pressed "squish". So ramps + one authored stroke-trim timeline express all reachable states — see ControlMotion. public static class CheckBox Inheritance object CheckBox Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields PartBox The 20px check square (WinUI NormalRectangle). Owned: Children (the conditional draw-on mark mount — checkmark when Checked, dash when Indeterminate, none when Unchecked). public const string PartBox = "Box" Field Value string PartLabel The label (a TextEl — use Parts.Set<TextEl>(CheckBox.PartLabel, …)). Owned: none (the fg ramp sits in the stock build; a modifier may restyle it). public const string PartLabel = "Label" Field Value string PartMark The drawn checkmark / indeterminate dash (WinUI CheckGlyph) — a PolylineStrokeEl, so use Parts.Set<PolylineStrokeEl>(CheckBox.PartMark, …). Owned: TrimStart/TrimEnd (the StrokeTrimEnd draw-on keyframes land there). public const string PartMark = "Mark" Field Value string PartRoot The returned clickable row (WinUI RootGrid). Owned: OnClick (the toggle), Role, Children. public const string PartRoot = "Root" Field Value string StyleOverride public static CheckBox.Style? StyleOverride Field Value CheckBox.Style Properties DefaultStyle public static CheckBox.Style DefaultStyle { get; } Property Value CheckBox.Style Methods Create(string, CheckState, Action<CheckState>, Style?, bool, TemplateParts?) public static BoxEl Create(string label, CheckState state, Action<CheckState> onChange, CheckBox.Style? style = null, bool isEnabled = true, TemplateParts? parts = null) Parameters label string state CheckState onChange Action<CheckState> style CheckBox.Style isEnabled bool parts TemplateParts Returns BoxEl Create(string, bool, Action, Style?, bool, TemplateParts?) public static BoxEl Create(string label, bool isChecked, Action onToggle, CheckBox.Style? style = null, bool isEnabled = true, TemplateParts? parts = null) Parameters label string isChecked bool onToggle Action style CheckBox.Style isEnabled bool parts TemplateParts Returns BoxEl

api/FluentGpu.Controls.ColorPicker.html.summary: Class ColorPicker Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI ColorPicker: a 2-D saturation/value spectrum (two stacked linear gradients — white→hue over transparent→black, since the engine has no 2-D gradient), a segmented rainbow hue rail (≤4-stop gradients ×6 to span 360°), an optional alpha rail, a preview swatch, and editable Hex / R / G / B channel fields. HSV is the internal editing space (so hue is stable at the grays); the selected color is written to a caller Signal<T> so a page can read it. CUSTOMIZATION goes through Parts (the one generic door — no per-feature knobs): every named template part accepts arbitrary element props; the scrub mechanics and value-driven geometry are re-asserted after any modifier, so customization can restyle everything but break nothing. public sealed class ColorPicker : Component Inheritance object Component ColorPicker Inherited Members Component.Context Component.RunsOnce Component.RenderWithHooks() Component.Unmount() object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods MotionHooks.UseEntrance(Component, float, object?) api/FluentGpu.Controls.ComboBox.html.title: Class ComboBox | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.ComboBox.html.summary: Class ComboBox Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI ComboBox: a closed field showing the selected item + a chevron that opens a dropdown list (an anchored, light-dismiss overlay whose width matches the field). In Editable mode ONE chrome-owning box (the template's single Background border spanning BOTH grid columns, ComboBox_themeresources.xaml:571 Grid.ColumnSpan="2") hosts a chromeless EditableText spanning the full width (:580 ColumnSpan="2", BorderBrush="Transparent", padding ComboBoxEditableTextPadding 11,5,38,6 :342) with the 30-wide DropDownOverlay button (:581) and the 12×12 chevron glyph (:582–587) overlaid at the right — never a second bordered box. Selection is a caller Signal<T> so a page can read it. 1:1 with ComboBox_themeresources(_perf2026).xaml + ComboBoxHelper.cpp. Exact tokens/sizes: field MinHeight=32 (ComboBoxMinHeight), padding 12,5,0,7 (ComboBoxPadding), CornerRadius=4 (ControlCornerRadius), BorderThickness=1, MinWidth=64 (ComboBoxThemeMinWidth); the border is the ControlElevationBorder gradient (ComboBoxBorderBrush), pressed flattens to a flat stroke (ComboBoxBorderBrushPressed=ControlStrokeColorDefault). Field fills rest/hover/pressed/disabled = ControlFillColor Default/Secondary/Tertiary/Disabled; foreground ramps Primary→(pressed)Secondary→(disabled)Disabled; placeholder Secondary→(pressed)Tertiary. Chevron column width 38, glyph  12px, foreground TextFillColorSecondary, right-inset 14 (Margin 0,0,14,0). Items (ComboBoxItem): padding 11,5,11,7 (ComboBoxItemThemePadding), margin 5,2,5,2, CornerRadius=3 (ComboBoxItemCornerRadius); the 3×16 left accent pill (ComboBoxItemPill, corner 1.5, fill AccentFillColorDefault, flush left — no margin in the ITEM template) shows only on the selected row. Item state matrix mirrors the XAML: rest=SubtleTransparent, PointerOver=SubtleSecondary, Pressed=SubtleTertiary; Selected=SubtleSecondary, SelectedPointerOver=SubtleTertiary, SelectedPressed=SubtleSecondary; foreground Primary, pressed→Secondary. Popup: AcrylicInApp background + SurfaceStrokeColorFlyout 1px + OverlayCornerRadius(8) + presenter margin 0,4 (ComboBoxDropdownContentMargin) + MaxDropDownHeight=504 with internal scroll — all supplied by the overlay host's FlyoutSurface; here we return only the rows. Open/close = the overlay host's WinUI MenuPopupThemeTransition (250ms cubic-bezier(0,0,0,1) clip-reveal + opacity; 83ms close fade) — identical to OverlayOpeningAnimation/OverlayClosingAnimation (ControlNormal/Fast AnimationDuration). Focus is captured on open and restored on close by the host; the popup is opened with PopupOptions(FocusTrap:true) so Tab/arrow roving stays inside the list. Keyboard: when CLOSED, Up/Down/Home/End step the selection in place and Space/Enter/F4/Alt+Down open; when OPEN, Up/Down/Home/End move a highlighted row, Enter/Space commits it, Escape closes (restoring selection). Type-ahead jumps to the next item whose label starts with the typed prefix (WinUI ComboBox text-search), CLOSED or OPEN. Corner-join (ComboBoxHelper.UpdateCornerRadius): while open-DOWN the field squares its BOTTOM corners so the dropdown reads as one piece with the field (the popup squares the abutting TOP corners host-side via the placement result's CornerJoin). The non-editable field overlaps the popup by design, so the join is applied while open. public sealed class ComboBox : Component Inheritance object Component ComboBox Inherited Members Component.Context Component.RunsOnce Component.RenderWithHooks() Component.Unmount() object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods MotionHooks.UseEntrance(Component, float, object?) api/FluentGpu.Controls.CommandBar.html.title: Class CommandBar | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.CommandBar.html.summary: Class CommandBar Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI CommandBar (CommandBar_themeresources.xaml): a horizontal toolbar of primary commands with a trailing "…" MoreButton that opens the SECONDARY commands as an overflow menu and expands the bar. Closed — chromeless: Background = CommandBarBackground = ControlFillColorTransparent (:9), no border; height = AppBarThemeCompactHeight 48 (:72) in Compact mode (icon-only buttons), 24 in Minimal, 0 in Hidden. Open — Background swaps to CommandBarBackgroundOpen = AcrylicInAppFillColorDefault (:10) with the ControlCornerRadius 4 + OpenBorder; primary buttons grow to the labeled 64px FullSize layout (ContentTransform.Y → CompactVerticalDelta) and the overflow popup opens under the … button. The WinUI DisplayModeStates storyboards run the open leg over ControlNormalAnimationDuration (250ms) and the close leg over ControlFastAnimationDuration (167ms), both on the ControlFastOutSlowInKeySpline 0,0,0,1 (:124-247 CompactClosed↔CompactOpenUp/Down VisualTransitions) — the bar's height/label reveal rides a matching LayoutTransition tween; the overflow popup itself is the overlay's menu reveal. Overflow — CommandBarOverflowPresenter: MinWidth 160 / MaxWidth 480 (:5/:7), acrylic + transient-border chrome from the overlay's FlyoutSurface; secondary commands render as Overflow-state AppBarButtons (icons + toggle checks + right-aligned accelerator text) with AppBarSeparator Overflow rows. MoreButton — Width 48 (AppBarExpandButtonThemeWidth, generic.xaml:23), E712 ellipsis, subtle fills, inner-border margin 2,6,6,6 (AppBarEllipsisButtonInnerBorderMargin :73), FocusVisualMargin −3. IsSticky — a non-sticky open bar light-dismisses (click-outside/Escape closes); sticky stays open until the … button is clicked again (WinUI AppBar sticky semantics). public sealed class CommandBar : Component Inheritance object Component CommandBar Inherited Members Component.Context Component.RunsOnce Component.RenderWithHooks() Component.Unmount() object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods MotionHooks.UseEntrance(Component, float, object?) api/FluentGpu.Controls.CommandBarFlyout..title: Class CommandBarFlyout | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.CommandBarFlyout..summary:
Class CommandBarFlyout Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI CommandBarFlyout: a trigger button that opens a contextual command toolbar anchored below it. The popup has a horizontal PRIMARY row of icon AppBarButtons plus a trailing "…" More ellipsis toggle, and a vertical SECONDARY overflow menu of labeled rows that the … toggle expands. 1:1 with the V2 sources controls/dev/CommandBarFlyout/CommandBarFlyout_themeresources.xaml + CommandBarFlyoutCommandBar.cpp:
  • Primary buttons: MinWidth 40 ContentRoot (:285), InnerBorderMargin 2 (:107), 16px icon, subtle fill ramp with the 83ms BrushTransition (:282) on every state swap. CHECKED primary toggles paint the accent pill (Checked/CheckedPointerOver/CheckedPressed, :386-406; brushes :24-:30) with on-accent foreground; CheckedDisabled drops the pill (:407-416). Primary clicks do NOT auto-close (closeFlyoutFunc is hooked only on SecondaryCommands, CommandBarFlyout.cpp:67-74).
  • Overflow rows: plate inset 2 (InnerBorderMargin :107) inside the presenter's 3px ItemsPresenter margin (:556); check glyph E73E @12 (:505); 16×16 menu icon; OverflowTextLabel Body 14 Padding 0,6,0,7 at the 12/39/67 lead ladder (:301/:152/:180); KeyboardAcceleratorTextLabel Caption 12 Margin 24,0,12,0 (:302); SubItemChevron E76C @12 (:303). CHECKED overflow toggles show ONLY the check glyph — no accent pill (OverflowChecked sets just OverflowCheckGlyph.Opacity=1, :434-438; hover/press stay the Subtle ramp, :439-458). Every secondary command — toggles included — closes the flyout on invoke (Click/Checked/Unchecked → Hide(), CommandBarFlyout.cpp:97-105).
  • Expand/collapse: the V2 ExpansionStates transitions (themeresources:697-857) — the overflow reveals through a HALF-HEIGHT CLIP slide with static content (ExpandDownAnimationStartPosition = −h/2, CommandBarFlyoutCommandBar.cpp:891; ExpandedUp from +h/2, :888), synced with the WIDTH expansion (content clip right edge from the midpoint, WidthExpansionAnimationStartPosition = −delta/2, :922; MoreButton glide delta/2→0, :973) — 250ms open / 167ms close on ControlFastOutSlowInKeySpline 0,0,0,1. ExpandedUp places the overflow ABOVE the primary row with flipped seam/corners (:966-973) when the popup is bottom-anchored (shouldExpandUp, :609-657).
  • Flyout open/close: an 83ms opacity fade (Opening/ClosingOpacityStoryboard, themeresources:655-662) — the menu clip-unfold is explicitly disabled (AreOpenCloseAnimationsEnabled(false), CommandBarFlyout.cpp:44).
The flyout body returns INNER content only — 's FlyoutSurface supplies the acrylic backdrop, 1px stroke, shadow and rounded corners. The popup is WINDOWED (ShouldConstrainToRootBounds=False, CommandBarFlyout.cpp:43). public sealed class CommandBarFlyout : Component Inheritance object Component CommandBarFlyout Inherited Members Component.Context Component.RunsOnce Component.RenderWithHooks() Component.Unmount() object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods MotionHooks.UseEntrance(Component, float, object?) Owned: OnRealized (the expand clip storyboard's node capture, chained), ClipToBounds, Children. public const string PartOverflow = "Overflow" Field Value string PartPrimaryRow The horizontal primary icon row (WinUI PrimaryItemsRoot). Owned: Children. public const string PartPrimaryRow = "PrimaryRow" Field Value string PartRoot The popup body root. Owned: ClipToBounds (the expand clips must never paint past the popup's rounded corners), Children, OnRealized (the width-expansion clip + open/close fade node capture, chained). public const string PartRoot = "Root" Field Value string PartTrigger The trigger button that opens the flyout (engine-local — WinUI flyouts have no built-in trigger). api/FluentGpu.Controls.ContentDialog.htm.summary: Class ContentDialog Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll WinUI-style ContentDialog: a modal, page-level smoke scrim with a centered solid card. The dialog is hosted through OverlayHost instead of being drawn inside the sample card, so it overlays the whole page. 1:1 with ContentDialog_themeresources.xaml: Chrome — smoke #4D000000 (SmokeFillColorDefault), background SolidBackgroundFillColorBase, 1px SurfaceStrokeColorDefault border, OverlayCornerRadius 8, dialog padding 24 (ContentDialogPadding), title margin 0,0,0,12 (ContentDialogTitleMargin), button spacing 8 (ContentDialogButtonSpacing), separator CardStrokeColorDefault 0,0,0,1, Min/Max 320×184 / 548×756 (:12-15). Motion — open: scale 1.05→1.0 over ControlNormal (250ms) + opacity 0→1 over ControlFaster (83ms, linear); close: scale 1.0→1.05 over ControlFast (167ms) + opacity 1→0 over 83ms; both scale legs on the ControlFastOutSlowInKeySpline (0,0,0,1) — ContentDialog_themeresources.xaml:77-117 DialogShowing/Hidden transitions, driven by the OverlayHost's Modal chrome. Keyboard — Enter invokes the DefaultButton (the accent-styled one); Escape closes with None (the overlay's Escape preview); Tab/Shift-Tab CYCLE inside the dialog (DialogShowing sets BackgroundElement.TabFocusNavigation=Cycle, :123 — a REAL dispatcher focus scope via the host's PopupOptions.FocusTrap). Initial focus lands on the default button (TabIndex ranks it first). public sealed class ContentDialog : Component Inheritance object Component ContentDialog Inherited Members Component.Context Component.RunsOnce Component.RenderWithHooks() Component.Unmount() object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods MotionHooks.UseEntrance(Component, float, object?) api/FluentGpu.Controls.DatePicker.html.summary: Class DatePicker Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI DatePicker: a single Button FACE that shows Day / Month / Year text columns (ordered per locale from CurrentCulture's ShortDatePattern, month names FULL — WinUI formats the face/flyout with "day month.full year", DatePicker_Partial.cpp:1811) separated by 1px vertical spacers, and on tap opens a DatePickerFlyout popup via the Overlay service. The popup is placed so the centered 40px accent highlight band overlays the face (DateTimePickerFlyoutHelper.cpp:39-71) and is width-matched to the face's realized width (DatePickerFlyout_Partial.cpp:202-206). It hosts three side-by-side LOOPING selector columns (item height 40) following the WinUI LoopingSelector RepeatButton model — caret step buttons (EDDB/EDDC) revealed on column hover plus click-to-select — under the highlight band, with a 41px Accept(E8FB) / Dismiss(E711) bar. SelectedDate is nullable (a null value shows the day/month/year placeholders). Day/Month/Year columns can each be hidden; the year range defaults to today ±100 years. public sealed class DatePicker : Component Inheritance object Component DatePicker Inherited Members Component.Context Component.RunsOnce Component.RenderWithHooks() Component.Unmount() object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods MotionHooks.UseEntrance(Component, float, object?) api/FluentGpu.Controls.DropDownButton.ht.title: Class DropDownButton | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.DropDownButton.ht.summary: Class DropDownButton Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI DropDownButton: a standard Button with a trailing chevron that opens a MenuFlyout of choices below it on click. WinUI's DropDownButton derives from Button, so it IS the Button template (RootGrid background/border/corner + an 83ms BrushTransition) plus a 2-column grid — content (*) and a 12×12 chevron AnimatedIcon (Auto, Margin 8,0,0,0). The chevron foreground is DropDownButtonForegroundSecondary (TextFillColorSecondaryBrush; Tertiary on hover/press, the disabled foreground when disabled); its FontIcon fallback is glyph  at FontSize 8. Open/close goes through the overlay popup service (Service): re-click toggles, Escape / click-outside light-dismiss, and focus is captured on open + restored on close (host-wired). Keyboard: Enter/Space (the engine's focused- clickable activation) and Down open the flyout. The overlay raises the WinUI ExpandCollapse semantics (Collapsed↔Expanded); IsEnabled gates all interaction via the engine and swaps the resting fill/border/foreground to the disabled tokens. The chevron itself does NOT rotate on open — matching WinUI's AnimatedChevronDownSmall, whose only states are Normal/PointerOver/Pressed (no Expanded state). api/FluentGpu.Controls.EditableText.html.title: Class EditableText | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.EditableText.html.summary: Class EditableText Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll The WinUI-parity text editor every text control composes (TextBox/PasswordBox/NumberBox/AutoSuggestBox/editable ComboBox/ColorPicker), built on TextEditCore (document + caret/selection/undo) and the IFontSystem editor queries (hit-test / caret metrics / range rects — the SAME layout pipeline the renderer draws with). Document ↔ signal. The caller-visible Signal<T> stays the single source of truth: a user edit mutates the core document and then makes ONE text.Value write (TextBind updates just the text node — no component re-render per keystroke); an external/programmatic signal write is folded back into the document by the TextBind evaluation (core.ResetText, reason = ProgrammaticChange). An IME composition is the one sanctioned divergence: the PROVISIONAL text lives in the document (so it renders, with clause underlines) but the signal updates only on commit. Rendering. Caret/selection/IME decorations are retained scene state (TextEditState + the pooled rect slab on the editor's TEXT node), replayed by the recorder — never composed elements. All geometry this control writes (caret X, selection/underline rects) is in RAW text-node-local (document-space) coordinates; the caret-follow -ScrollX is applied ONCE, as the text node's wrapper transform (a TransformBind reading a FloatSignal — the compositor-bypass idiom), so the recorder's world transform shifts glyphs and decorations together. ScrollX mirrors the applied offset for tests/IME math. Focus. Focus state is engine-driven (OnFocusChanged — WinUI GotFocus/LostFocus): gained → arm the caret blinker + IME (sink + editable) + capture the Escape-revert snapshot; keyboard (Tab) focus additionally selects all (pointer focus keeps the press-placed caret). Lost → disarm blinker/IME; the selection STATE is kept but its visuals hide (SelectionActive off). Enter fires OnCommit and KEEPS focus (WinUI); Escape reverts to the focus-time snapshot, fires OnCancel, and blurs (the established engine contract that NumberBox/ComboBox/ColorPicker rely on). Deviations (documented, not hidden): BeforeTextChanging gates INSERTIONS (typing, paste, IME commit, newline); deletions and undo/redo are not cancelable (WinUI fires it for every change). A Sanitize that rewrites the value replaces the whole document as one undo step with the caret at the end. Drag after a double-click extends by character, not by word. IME clause underlines sit at the line bottom (face-metric underline placement arrives with the DirectWrite decoration work). Focused border. WinUI's Focused state swaps BorderBrush to TextControlElevationBorderFocusedBrush (the accent-bottom-stop gradient) with BorderThickness 1,1,1,2 (TextBox_themeresources.xaml, the Focused VisualState ~lines 295–304 + the brush definition ~48–66). Visually that is a neutral 1px ring whose BOTTOM edge reads as a 2px accent bar. We keep the neutral Tok.ControlElevationBorder ring in every state and draw the separate 2px accent bottom bar while focused — the same pixels without double-drawing an accent gradient under an accent bar. public sealed class EditableText : Component Inheritance object Component EditableText Inherited Members Component.Context Component.RunsOnce Component.RenderWithHooks() Component.Unmount() object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods MotionHooks.UseEntrance(Component, float, object?) Caret/selection/IME/padding and focus behavior stay intact, and the part keeps ONLY the WinUI TextControl interaction fills it paints inside the shared border (ComboBoxTextBoxStyle PointerOver/Focused, ComboBox_themeresources.xaml:786–803): hover = TextControlBackgroundPointerOver = ControlFillColorSecondary (TextBox_themeresources.xaml:24/:131), focused = TextControlBackgroundFocused = ControlFillColorInputActive (:25/:132). api/FluentGpu.Controls.Expander.html.title: Class Expander | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.Expander.html.summary: Class Expander Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI-flavoured Expander: a clickable header row with a trailing chevron over a collapsible content panel. The header toggles local Component state (or a controlled IsExpanded signal); the single chevron glyph is ROTATED by the computed ExpanderTemplateSettings (down collapsed → up expanded). MOTION — a DELIBERATE divergence from WinUI: the card height EASES (WinUI's ExpandDown/CollapseUp storyboards SNAP the layout space and only translate the content, Expander.xaml:39-90), because FluentGpu's design goal is smooth transitions of content, not discrete layout repositions. The storyboard TIMINGS and EASINGS stay WinUI-exact: expand 333ms KeySpline(0,0,0,1), collapse 167ms KeySpline(1,1,0,1). The whole open/close is one declarative engine transition — Reflow on the content clip wrapper (the interpolated height runs through real layout each tick, so neighbouring content reflows smoothly and RIGIDLY), with Trailing riding the content's bottom edge on the reveal edge (the WinUI slide-out-from-under-the-header look, rounded bottom corners visible mid-motion). No control-local ticker, no per-frame re-render: the engine owns the motion. CUSTOMIZATION goes through Parts (the one generic door — no per-feature knobs): every named template part accepts arbitrary element props, e.g. a sticky pinned header ([PartHeader] = b => b with { StickyTop = 8f, OnPinned = …, Fill = stuck.Value ? … : b.Fill }) or an edge-to-edge content panel ([PartContent] = c => c with { Padding = Edges4.All(0) }). Mechanics-critical props are re-asserted after the modifier, so customization can restyle everything but break nothing. public sealed class Expander : Component Inheritance object Component Expander Inherited Members Component.Context Component.RunsOnce Component.RenderWithHooks() Component.Unmount() object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods MotionHooks.UseEntrance(Component, float, object?) api/FluentGpu.Controls.FlipView.html.title: Class FlipView | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.FlipView.html.summary: Class FlipView Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI FlipView: a clipped frame showing exactly one item at a time, with thin acrylic navigation bars hugging the frame edges (no dot/pip indicator — WinUI FlipView has none). Selection CLAMPS at the ends (MoveNext / MovePrevious, FlipView_Partial.cpp:131-139/:165-171 — no wrap-around) and the end bar is not rendered (nothingPrevious/nothingNext, :1242-1278). The bars are hidden until pointer movement / keyboard focus shows them, then fade out after FLIP_VIEW_BUTTONS_SHOW_DURATION_MS = 3000ms (FlipView_Partial.h:13). Adjacent selection changes slide (UseTouchAnimationsForAllNavigation, default TRUE — FlipView_Partial.cpp:1639-1645); non-adjacent changes jump (SetOffsetToSelectedIndex, :1719). Wheel flips one item per distinct event (200ms throttle, FlipView_Partial.h:10), arrow keys navigate (Selector::HandleNavigationKey, Selector_Partial.cpp:3694-3773), and the content pans with a drag, snapping MandatorySingle on release (ScrollingHost snap points, FlipView_themeresources.xaml:297). All items are realized (no virtualization — WinUI's VirtualizingStackPanel is out of scope for this control surface). public static class FlipView Inheritance object FlipView Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods Create(IReadOnlyList<Element>, float, float, int, Action<int>?, bool, bool) Arbitrary per-item content (the WinUI FlipViewItem surface — FlipViewItem_themeresources.xaml:23-25). Each item fills its page cell (FlipViewItem Horizontal/VerticalContentAlignment = Stretch, :17-18). selectedIndex is live-controlled: a changed value navigates the view (the Selector SelectedIndex DP, FlipView_Partial.cpp:1607-1613); internal navigation reports through onSelectionChanged (SelectionChanged). public static Element Create(IReadOnlyList<Element> items, float width = 400, float height = 240, int selectedIndex = 0, Action<int>? onSelectionChanged = null, bool useTouchAnimationsForAllNavigation = true, bool vertical = false) Parameters items IReadOnlyList<Element> width float height float selectedIndex int onSelectionChanged Action<int> useTouchAnimationsForAllNavigation bool vertical bool Returns Element Create(IReadOnlyList<string>, float, float, int, Action<int>?, bool, bool) String-item convenience: each entry renders centered at 20px primary text. public static Element Create(IReadOnlyList<string> items, float width = 400, float height = 240, int selectedIndex = 0, Action<int>? onSelectionChanged = null, bool useTouchAnimationsForAllNavigation = true, bool vertical = false) Parameters items IReadOnlyList<string> width float height float selectedIndex int onSelectionChanged Action<int> useTouchAnimationsForAllNavigation bool vertical bool Returns Element

api/FluentGpu.Controls.FlyoutButton.html.summary: Class FlyoutButton Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI Flyout: a button that opens an anchored, light-dismissable flyout holding arbitrary content (not a menu). Opens through the shared overlay service (OverlayHost) exactly like DropDownButton; the host wraps the body in the acrylic FlyoutSurface (the FlyoutPresenterBackground acrylic + 1px FlyoutBorderThemeBrush stroke + elevation shadow + OverlayCornerRadius corners + the MenuPopupThemeTransition reveal), and captures/restores focus on open/close. The body returned by the content thunk is the WinUI FlyoutPresenter card: FlyoutContentPadding 16,15,16,17 with the Flyout*Theme min/max constraints. Toggling re-click closes it (WinUI Flyout toggle semantics). Light dismiss + Escape are host-provided. public sealed class FlyoutButton : Component Inheritance object Component FlyoutButton Inherited Members Component.Context Component.RunsOnce Component.RenderWithHooks() Component.Unmount() object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods MotionHooks.UseEntrance(Component, float, object?) MotionHooks.UseHoverScale(Component, bool, float) Fields Content public Func<Element> Content Field Value Func<Element> Label public string Label Field Value string OpenOnMount public bool OpenOnMount Field Value bool Placement Placement of the flyout relative to its anchor. Defaults to BottomLeft (the positioner flips above the anchor when it does not fit below). public FlyoutPlacement Placement Field Value FlyoutPlacement Methods Create(string, Func<Element>) public static Element Create(string label, Func<Element> content) Parameters label string content Func<Element> Returns Element Create(string, Func<Element>, FlyoutPlacement) public static Element Create(string label, Func<Element> content, FlyoutPlacement placement) Parameters label string content Func<Element> placement FlyoutPlacement Returns Element Render() public override Element Render() Returns Element

api/FluentGpu.Controls.FlyoutPlacement.h.title: Enum FlyoutPlacement | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.FlyoutPlacement.h.summary: Enum FlyoutPlacement Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll Where a flyout opens relative to its anchor — the full WinUI FlyoutPlacementMode matrix (FlyoutBase_Partial.cpp:84-110 maps each mode to a major side + an edge justification) plus the engine's pre-existing aliases kept for source compatibility: BottomLeft/TopLeft ≡ WinUI BottomEdgeAlignedLeft/TopEdgeAlignedLeft. BottomCenter/TopCenter ≡ WinUI Bottom/Top (center-justified). BottomStretch = the attached ComboBox/AutoSuggest dropdown (flush, width-matched, corner-joined) — not a FlyoutBase mode; WinUI ComboBox places its own popup over/under the field. public enum FlyoutPlacement : byte Fields Bottom = 6 BottomCenter = 2 BottomEdgeAlignedLeft = 11 BottomEdgeAlignedRight = 12 BottomLeft = 0 BottomStretch = 1 Full = 17 Left = 7 LeftEdgeAlignedBottom = 14 LeftEdgeAlignedTop = 13 OverlapStretch = 18 The non-editable ComboBox dropdown: width-matched and laid OVER the field — the popup's top-left sits at the (caller-shifted) anchor origin so the SELECTED item lines up over the closed field (the WinUI ComboBox positions its own popup via TemplateSettings.DropDownOffset, not FlyoutBase). Clamped into the container; no corner join (the popup keeps OverlayCornerRadius on all corners and overlaps the field). Right = 8 RightEdgeAlignedBottom = 16 RightEdgeAlignedTop = 15 Top = 5 TopCenter = 4 TopEdgeAlignedLeft = 9 TopEdgeAlignedRight = 10 TopLeft = 3

api/FluentGpu.Controls.FlyoutPositioner..title: Class FlyoutPositioner | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.FlyoutPositioner..summary: Class FlyoutPositioner Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll Pure placement math for an anchored flyout — a faithful port of WinUI's FlyoutBase::CalculatePlacementPrivate (FlyoutBase_Partial.cpp:2503-2659): try the requested major side, walk the per-side fallback order (Top:[T,B,L,R] / Bottom:[B,T,L,R] / Left:[L,R,T,B] / Right:[R,L,T,B], cpp:2559-2593), justify on the secondary axis and clamp both axes into the container (TryPlacement, cpp:415-483); when NOTHING fits, keep the first choice and resize along the major axis to the side with more space (ResizeToFit/SelectSideWithMoreSpace, cpp:2605-2614, 581-635); finally shift by FlyoutMargin away from the anchor (cpp:2622-2646) and, for non-windowed popups only, clamp the min edge to the container origin (cpp:2648-2653 — windowed popups may go negative, i.e. above/left of the window). public static class FlyoutPositioner Inheritance object FlyoutPositioner Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields FlyoutMargin Margin between the flyout and its placement target / the window edge — WinUI FlyoutBase::FlyoutMargin = 4 (FlyoutBase_Partial.cpp:65). Attached/stretch dropdowns stay flush (0). public const float FlyoutMargin = 4 Field Value float Methods Place(in RectF, in Size2, in RectF, FlyoutPlacement, bool) Container-rect placement. container is the collision box in the SAME coordinate space as anchor — the viewport rect for constrained popups, the monitor WORK AREA (translated into window space) for windowed out-of-bounds popups (FlyoutBase_Partial.cpp:3382-3392 useMonitorBounds). isWindowed skips the final min-edge clamp (cpp:2648-2653) so a windowed popup may extend above/left of the window. public static PopupPlacementResult Place(in RectF anchor, in Size2 popup, in RectF container, FlyoutPlacement placement, bool isWindowed) Parameters anchor RectF popup Size2 container RectF placement FlyoutPlacement isWindowed bool Returns PopupPlacementResult Place(in RectF, in Size2, in Size2, FlyoutPlacement) Viewport-constrained placement (the container is the window's DIP rect at origin). public static PopupPlacementResult Place(in RectF anchor, in Size2 popup, in Size2 viewport, FlyoutPlacement placement) Parameters anchor RectF popup Size2 viewport Size2 placement FlyoutPlacement Returns PopupPlacementResult

api/FluentGpu.Controls.HyperlinkButton.h.title: Class HyperlinkButton | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.HyperlinkButton.h.summary: Class HyperlinkButton Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI HyperlinkButton: accent-colored link text with a subtle hover/press surface and a HAND cursor (WinUI sets MouseCursorHand at initialize — dxaml\xcp\dxaml\lib\HyperLinkButton_Partial.cpp:28-34). Raises a click (in-app navigation); pass navigateUri to additionally launch the OS default handler after Click, exactly WinUI's OnClick order (Click event first, then Launcher::TryInvokeLauncher — HyperLinkButton_Partial.cpp:149-177) via the IPlatformApp.OpenUri PAL seam (host-wired through OpenUri; headless records). Underline: WinUI 3 sets the HyperlinkUnderlineVisible resource to FALSE (controls\dev\CommonStyles\Hyperlink_themeresources.xaml:20), and HyperlinkButton only underlines when that directive is true OR a HighContrast theme is active (HyperLinkButton_Partial.cpp:207-212) — so the WinUI 3 default is NO underline; UnderlineVisible is the opt-in (the E8 HighContrast pass forces it on). Style source: controls\dev\CommonStyles\HyperlinkButton_themeresources.xaml (Default = dark :4-18, Light :19-33). public static class HyperlinkButton Inheritance object HyperlinkButton Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields PartLabel The link text — a TextEl, so customize via parts.Set<TextEl>(HyperlinkButton.PartLabel, …). Owned: none (the accent foreground ramp and the Underline opt-in are style-driven; a modifier may override them). public const string PartLabel = "Label" Field Value string PartRoot The link surface. Owned: OnClick (Click → OpenUri order), Role, Children (the label slot). public const string PartRoot = "Root" Field Value string StyleOverride public static HyperlinkButton.Style? StyleOverride Field Value HyperlinkButton.Style Properties DefaultStyle public static HyperlinkButton.Style DefaultStyle { get; } Property Value HyperlinkButton.Style Methods Create(string, Action, Style?, bool, TemplateParts?) A link that raises onClick (in-app navigation). public static BoxEl Create(string text, Action onClick, HyperlinkButton.Style? style = null, bool isEnabled = true, TemplateParts? parts = null) Parameters text string onClick Action style HyperlinkButton.Style isEnabled bool parts TemplateParts Returns BoxEl Create(string, string, Style?, bool, Action?, TemplateParts?) A link with a WinUI NavigateUri: raises Click first, then launches navigateUri in the OS default handler (browser/mail) — WinUI's exact OnClick order (Click → Launcher::TryInvokeLauncher, HyperLinkButton_Partial.cpp:166-173) through the IPlatformApp.OpenUri PAL seam. Headless hosts record the URI instead of launching (HeadlessPlatformApp.OpenedUris). public static BoxEl Create(string text, string navigateUri, HyperlinkButton.Style? style = null, bool isEnabled = true, Action? onClick = null, TemplateParts? parts = null) Parameters text string navigateUri string style HyperlinkButton.Style isEnabled bool onClick Action parts TemplateParts Returns BoxEl

api/FluentGpu.Controls.IconButton.html.summary: Class IconButton Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A compact icon/glyph button (transport play/next/prev, command-bar actions). WinUI shape: AppBarButton/MediaTransportControls — a rounded-square (ControlCornerRadius 4), subtle fills, a 16px glyph. State colors verified against controls\dev\CommonStyles\AppBarButton_themeresources.xaml (Default = dark :5-12, Light :75-82). The BOX never scales (WinUI AppBarButton states swap colors only — lines 244-259); the inner glyph wrapper carries the AnimatedIcon-analogue hover/press scale (WinUI sets AnimatedIcon.State on the presenter — Button_themeresources.xaml:192/208 — and the animated glyph responds; see AnimatedIcon.cs). Stateless/controlled: pass a click handler. Size/glyph/colours live on IconButton.Style; tweak via with. public static class IconButton Inheritance object IconButton Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields PartGlyph The glyph run — a TextEl, so customize via parts.Set<TextEl>(IconButton.PartGlyph, …).
