api/FluentGpu.Controls.InfoBadge.html.title: Class InfoBadge | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.InfoBadge.html.summary: Class InfoBadge Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI InfoBadge: a small accent (or severity) pill that surfaces a notification count, an icon, or a bare dot. Three display kinds, resolved exactly as WinUI's InfoBadge::OnDisplayKindPropertiesChanged does: Value (Value >= 0): an 11px count, clamped to a 16-tall pill (single digits stay circular because WinUI's MeasureOverride squares a badge narrower than it is tall). Icon (Value < 0, glyph supplied): a Segoe Fluent glyph in the same 16-tall pill, scaled by the WinUI IconPresenter Viewbox to the content-box height (8 for the padded Attention/Informational FontIcon styles and the SymbolIcon severities, 14 for an unpadded FontIcon — see InfoBadgeTemplateSettings). Dot (neither): a bare 4x4 disc. Background = AccentFillColorDefault (Default), or a SystemFillColor* per InfoBadgeSeverity; foreground = TextOnAccentFillColorPrimary. The corner radius is the computed pill (height/2) from InfoBadgeTemplateSettings. public static class InfoBadge Inheritance object InfoBadge Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields GlyphAttention public const string GlyphAttention = "" Field Value string GlyphCaution public const string GlyphCaution = "" Field Value string GlyphCritical public const string GlyphCritical = "" Field Value string GlyphInformational public const string GlyphInformational = "" Field Value string GlyphSuccess public const string GlyphSuccess = "" Field Value string PartRoot The badge pill itself (WinUI RootGrid — the control's only named element). Owned: Children (the value text / icon glyph — the display-kind content; the Dot has none). The per-kind geometry and severity fill are stock per-render styling a modifier may override. public const string PartRoot = "Root" Field Value string Methods Count(int, InfoBadgeSeverity, TemplateParts?) public static BoxEl Count(int value, InfoBadgeSeverity severity, TemplateParts? parts = null) Parameters value int severity InfoBadgeSeverity parts TemplateParts Returns BoxEl Count(int, ColorF?, TemplateParts?) The Value badge: a notification count. WinUI clamps to a 16-tall pill; single digits stay circular via the MinWidth = Height square. Pass severity to recolor. public static BoxEl Count(int value, ColorF? color = null, TemplateParts? parts = null) Parameters value int color ColorF? parts TemplateParts Returns BoxEl DefaultGlyph(InfoBadgeSeverity) public static string DefaultGlyph(InfoBadgeSeverity severity) Parameters severity InfoBadgeSeverity Returns string Dot(InfoBadgeSeverity, TemplateParts?) Severity dot — same geometry as Dot(ColorF?, TemplateParts?) with the severity background. public static BoxEl Dot(InfoBadgeSeverity severity, TemplateParts? parts = null) Parameters severity InfoBadgeSeverity parts TemplateParts Returns BoxEl Dot(ColorF?, TemplateParts?) The bare 4x4 dot. WinUI Dot state (no Value, no Icon): 4x4, corner = 2 (pill at that height). public static BoxEl Dot(ColorF? color = null, TemplateParts? parts = null) Parameters color ColorF? parts TemplateParts Returns BoxEl Icon(InfoBadgeSeverity, TemplateParts?) Severity icon using WinUI's default per-severity IconSource (Attention 0xEA38, Informational 0xF13F — FontIconSource; Success Accept, Caution Important, Critical Cancel — SymbolIconSource), scaled by the per-state IconPresenter Viewbox target (8 in every severity style; see InfoBadgeTemplateSettings). public static BoxEl Icon(InfoBadgeSeverity severity, TemplateParts? parts = null) Parameters severity InfoBadgeSeverity parts TemplateParts Returns BoxEl Icon(string, InfoBadgeSeverity, TemplateParts?) public static BoxEl Icon(string glyph, InfoBadgeSeverity severity, TemplateParts? parts = null) Parameters glyph string severity InfoBadgeSeverity parts TemplateParts Returns BoxEl Icon(string, ColorF?, TemplateParts?) The Icon badge: a Segoe Fluent glyph in the 16-tall pill. A custom glyph is WinUI's FontIconSource → the FontIcon state (margin 4,0,4,2, InfoBadge_themeresources.xaml:14/:71) — the IconPresenter Viewbox scales the glyph to the content-box height (14 = 16 − 2 for the ad-hoc, no-severity overloads; the Attention/Informational severity styles pad the root 0,4,0,2 → 8). public static BoxEl Icon(string glyph, ColorF? color = null, TemplateParts? parts = null) Parameters glyph string color ColorF? parts TemplateParts Returns BoxEl

api/FluentGpu.Controls.InfoBadgeTemplate.title: Struct InfoBadgeTemplateSettings | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.InfoBadgeTemplate.summary: Struct InfoBadgeTemplateSettings Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll Computed template settings for the InfoBadge — the typed-record convention modelled on ExpanderTemplateSettings. Mirrors WinUI's generated InfoBadgeTemplateSettings: the display kind (Dot vs Value vs Icon, derived from Value/Icon exactly like OnDisplayKindPropertiesChanged), the resolved badge height/width and the pill corner radius (= ActualHeight/2, per OnSizeChanged), and the per-kind content margin. public readonly record struct InfoBadgeTemplateSettings : IEquatable<InfoBadgeTemplateSettings> Implements IEquatable<InfoBadgeTemplateSettings> Inherited Members ValueType.Equals(object) ValueType.GetHashCode() ValueType.ToString() object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Constructors InfoBadgeTemplateSettings(byte, float, float, float, Edges4) Computed template settings for the InfoBadge — the typed-record convention modelled on ExpanderTemplateSettings. Mirrors WinUI's generated InfoBadgeTemplateSettings: the display kind (Dot vs Value vs Icon, derived from Value/Icon exactly like OnDisplayKindPropertiesChanged), the resolved badge height/width and the pill corner radius (= ActualHeight/2, per OnSizeChanged), and the per-kind content margin. public InfoBadgeTemplateSettings(byte Kind, float Height, float MinWidth, float CornerRadius, Edges4 ContentMargin) Parameters Kind byte 0 = Dot, 1 = Value (text), 2 = Icon (font/glyph). Height float Resolved badge height: 4 for a Dot, otherwise clamped to MaxHeight = 16. MinWidth float Resolved minimum width: 4 for a Dot, otherwise 16 (MeasureOverride squares a too-narrow badge). CornerRadius float InfoBadgeCornerRadius = Height / 2 — a full pill (2 for a Dot, 8 for a 16-tall badge). ContentMargin Edges4 Per-kind inner margin: Value/FontIcon = 4,0,4,2; a non-font IconElement = 4,4,4,4 (IconInfoBadgeIconMargin, InfoBadge_themeresources.xaml:16). Fields ContentMarginSymbolIcon public static readonly Edges4 ContentMarginSymbolIcon Field Value Edges4 ContentMarginValueOrFontIcon public static readonly Edges4 ContentMarginValueOrFontIcon Field Value Edges4 IconGlyphSizeFull public const float IconGlyphSizeFull = 14 Field Value float IconGlyphSizePadded WinUI renders the icon inside a Viewbox (IconPresenter, VerticalAlignment=Stretch) that fills the RootGrid content box, so the glyph is scaled to the box HEIGHT, not a fixed font size. api/FluentGpu.Controls.InfoBar.html.title: Class InfoBar | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.InfoBar.html.summary: Class InfoBar Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI InfoBar: an inline, app-wide status surface. A severity icon (a filled circle glyph with an inverse-colored status glyph on top), a Title (SemiBold) + Message content panel (laid out horizontally when it fits on one line, vertically otherwise, like WinUI's InfoBarPanel), an optional trailing action button, and an optional close button. The background is a subtle severity tint over a card stroke. Controlled by isOpen. public static class InfoBar Inheritance object InfoBar Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields PartCloseButton The trailing 38x38 X button (WinUI CloseButton). Owned: OnClick (the Closing→onClose→Closed lifecycle chain — kept whenever the control has close handlers; a modifier OnClick survives only when it has none), Role. public const string PartCloseButton = "CloseButton" Field Value string PartIcon The 16x16 standard severity icon Z-stack (WinUI StandardIcon area). Owned: none — the per-severity glyph colors are recomputed per render in the stock build (override-able). public const string PartIcon = "Icon" Field Value string PartMessage The message body (WinUI Message). A TextEl part — restyle via parts.Set<TextEl>(InfoBar.PartMessage, t => t with { … }). Owned: none. public const string PartMessage = "Message" Field Value string PartRoot The severity-tinted bar root. Owned: Children (the icon / panel / close columns — conditional mounts), Role. The severity tint/stroke are stock per-render styling a modifier may override. public const string PartRoot = "Root" Field Value string PartTitle The SemiBold title (WinUI Title). A TextEl part — restyle via parts.Set<TextEl>(InfoBar.PartTitle, t => t with { … }). Owned: none. public const string PartTitle = "Title" Field Value string Methods Create(InfoBarSeverity, string, string, Action?, bool, bool, bool, Element?, Action<InfoBarClosingEventArgs>?, Action<InfoBarClosedEventArgs>?, Element?, string?, TemplateParts?) public static Element Create(InfoBarSeverity severity, string title, string message, Action? onClose = null, bool isOpen = true, bool isClosable = true, bool isIconVisible = true, Element? actionButton = null, Action<InfoBarClosingEventArgs>? onClosing = null, Action<InfoBarClosedEventArgs>? onClosed = null, Element? content = null, string? iconGlyph = null, TemplateParts? parts = null) Parameters severity InfoBarSeverity Selects the status glyph + severity colors via InfoBar.InfoBarTemplateSettings. title string SemiBold title line (WinUI InfoBarTitleFontWeight). Empty => omitted. message string Regular message body. Empty => omitted. onClose Action Invoked when the X is clicked (WinUI sets reason=CloseButton then IsOpen=false). Pair with onClosing/onClosed for the full lifecycle. isOpen bool Whether the bar is shown. WinUI's IsOpen defaults to false; this stateless factory defaults to true so a constructed bar renders (callers gate visibility with their own signal/state). isClosable bool When true (the WinUI default) the trailing 38x38 close button is shown. isIconVisible bool When true (the WinUI default) the standard severity icon is shown. actionButton Element Optional trailing action slot (WinUI ActionButton) - laid out inline after the message when it fits, else below it. Pass e.g. Button.Create(...) or HyperlinkButton.Create(...). onClosing Action<InfoBarClosingEventArgs> Closing hook (WinUI Closing). Set e.Cancel = true to veto: the close is aborted and onClose/onClosed do not run (the caller keeps the bar open). onClosed Action<InfoBarClosedEventArgs> Closed hook (WinUI Closed), fired after a non-canceled close. content Element Optional expanded content (WinUI Content/ContentTemplate, InfoBar.idl:94-95): rendered in the second row of the content column, below the title/message/action panel (ContentArea, InfoBar.xaml:126). When title, message AND action are all absent it becomes the sole content of the column (the NoBannerContent state, InfoBar.xaml:86-90 / InfoBar.cpp:282-286). iconGlyph string Optional custom icon glyph (WinUI IconSource, InfoBar.idl:77). When set (and isIconVisible) it replaces the standard severity circle+status stack — the UserIconVisible state (InfoBar.cpp:261-264) — capped at 16x16 with the standard icon margin (UserIconBox, InfoBar.xaml:111). parts TemplateParts Lightweight per-part styling (CSS ::part): modifiers keyed by the PartXxx consts — see TemplateParts for the contract. Title/Message are TextEl parts (parts.Set<TextEl>(InfoBar.PartTitle, …)). Returns Element

api/FluentGpu.Controls.ItemContainer.htm.title: Class ItemContainer | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.ItemContainer.htm.summary: Class ItemContainer Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll WinUI ItemContainer (controls\dev\ItemContainer\ItemContainer.xaml) — the single-item wrapper every modern items control (ItemsView) realizes around its templates: pointer-state plate, dual-stroke selection visual, multi-select checkbox, disabled dimming, focusability. Built as a STATIC factory returning a plain keyed BoxEl tree (no Component, no reactive binds) so a realized window of containers stays on the virtualization recycle fast path (TreeReconciler.IsRecyclable). Template mapping (ItemContainer.xaml:11-145, values verified in ItemContainer_themeresources.xaml): • PART_ContainerRoot — the root box: Background = ItemContainerBackground = SubtleFillColorTransparent (:5/:37, transparent both themes); Disabled → Opacity 0.3 (ItemContainerDisabledOpacity, :54). • PART_SelectionVisual (xaml:116-126) — 3px border, BorderBrush = ItemContainerSelectionVisualBackground = AccentFillColorDefault (:14/:46 → Tok.AccentDefault, #60CDFF dark / #005FB8 light), deferred (x:DeferLoadStrategy="Lazy") → mounted only while selected; SelectedPointerOver/Pressed fade it in over ControlFastAnimationDuration with KeySpline 0,0,0,1 (xaml:54-56) → enter-fade 167ms decelerate. Disabled collapses it (xaml:108-110). • PART_CommonVisual (xaml:127-135) — ONE full-stretch rectangle declared AFTER the child placeholder (the child is inserted at index 0, ItemContainer.cpp:66), so it overlays the item content. It carries BOTH faces: – the pointer-state fill (xaml:17-33): PointerOver = ItemContainerPointerOverBackground = SubtleFillColorSecondary (#0FFFFFFF dark :6 / #09000000 light :38); Pressed = ItemContainerPressedBackground = SubtleFillColorTertiary (#0AFFFFFF dark :7 / #06000000 light :39); the Selected* ramp resolves to the SAME brushes (:12-13/:44-45). Drawn ABOVE the content so the tint reads over opaque photo-wall tiles. – the 1px selected inner stroke: Stroke = ItemContainerSelectedInnerBorderBrush = ControlSolidFillColorDefault (:17/:49 → Tok.FillControlSolid, #FF454545 dark / #FFFFFFFF light; Common_themeresources_any.xaml:24/:228), StrokeThickness = ItemContainerSelectedInnerThickness = 1 (:58), selected Margin = ItemContainerSelectedInnerMargin = 2 (:57). At rest the stroke is transparent (ItemContainerBorderBrush, :8). • PART_SelectionCheckbox (xaml:136-144, style :63-139) — top-right (:59-60), Margin 4,−2 (:56), 20px box, unchecked plate = ItemContainerCheckboxBackgroundUnchecked = ControlOnImageFillColorDefault (:18 → Tok.FillControlOnImage, #B31C1C1C dark / #C9FFFFFF light), stroke = CheckBoxCheckBackgroundStrokeUnchecked (→ Tok.StrokeControlStrongDefault); Multiple mode fades it in over ControlFastAnimationDuration (xaml:93-99). Interaction mapping (engine-deliberate): selection fires at the PRESS edge via OnPointerPressed — the one pointer callback carrying the modifier chord/click count (WinUI processes selection at PointerReleased; the visual outcome is identical and Win32 lists select on button-down). The container takes NO OnClick so Enter and Space reach OnKeyDown distinctly (the dispatcher's Enter/Space activation would otherwise collapse them), giving WinUI's EnterKey-vs-SpaceKey invoke split (ItemsView.cpp:423-426). public static class ItemContainer Inheritance object ItemContainer Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields CheckboxGlyphSize public const float CheckboxGlyphSize = 12 Field Value float CheckboxSize public const float CheckboxSize = 20 Field Value float DisabledOpacity public const float DisabledOpacity = 0.3 Field Value float FadeMs public const float FadeMs = 167 Field Value float SelectedInnerMargin public const float SelectedInnerMargin = 2 Field Value float SelectedInnerThickness public const float SelectedInnerThickness = 1 Field Value float SelectionVisualThickness public const float SelectionVisualThickness = 3 Field Value float Methods Build(Element, bool, Action<ItemContainerTrigger, KeyModifiers>?, bool, bool, bool, CornerRadius4?, Action<bool>?, float, float, bool, PartDelta) The full container. onInteraction receives the WinUI interaction trigger + the live modifier chord — a composing control (ItemsView) routes it through its selector. The returned tree is plain (recyclable); realize-time closures are the only managed cost. isTabStop is the roving-tab-stop seam: ItemsView keeps it true on the keyboard-current container ONLY (TabNavigation="Once", ItemsView.xaml:7). public static BoxEl Build(Element child, bool isSelected, Action<ItemContainerTrigger, KeyModifiers>? onInteraction = null, bool isEnabled = true, bool showSelectionCheckbox = false, bool isChecked = false, CornerRadius4? corners = null, Action<bool>? onFocusChanged = null, float width = NaN, float height = NaN, bool isTabStop = true, PartDelta partDelta = default) Parameters child Element isSelected bool onInteraction Action<ItemContainerTrigger, KeyModifiers> isEnabled bool showSelectionCheckbox bool isChecked bool corners CornerRadius4? onFocusChanged Action<bool> width float height float isTabStop bool partDelta PartDelta Returns BoxEl Build(Element, bool, Action<bool>, bool, CornerRadius4?) Standalone two-state convenience: toggles isSelected on tap/Space (the WinUI out-of-ItemsView ItemContainer behavior, IsSelected get/set per ItemContainer.idl:69). public static BoxEl Build(Element child, bool isSelected, Action<bool> onSelectionChanged, bool isEnabled = true, CornerRadius4? corners = null) Parameters child Element isSelected bool onSelectionChanged Action<bool> isEnabled bool corners CornerRadius4? Returns BoxEl

api/FluentGpu.Controls.ItemsView.html.title: Class ItemsView | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.ItemsView.html.summary: Class ItemsView Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll THE premiere collection control (a deliberate, documented SUPERSET of WinUI ItemsView, controls\dev\ItemsView) — E11-L3: the L2 repeater substrate + SelectionModel + the SelectorVisual chrome presets + keyboard navigation/typeahead + StartBringItemIntoView + BUILT-IN drag-reorder, composed. List(IReadOnlyList<string>, Signal<int>?, Action<int>?) and Grid(IReadOnlyList<string>, int, float) are the built-in presets (the former ListView/GridView controls, folded onto ItemsView); the goal is no WinUI-style capability cliffs — every layout × every selection mode × every selector × reorder works in any combination. Three pluggable axes, each available with every other (the superset over WinUI's fixed ListView/GridView pairings): • LAYOUT preset — any RepeatLayout: Stack, Grid, HorizontalStrip, LinedFlow (the WinUI photo-wall), Measured, SpanGrid or a custom seam layout, over ONE virtualized viewport (VirtualListEl). • SELECTION mode — None/Single/Multiple/Extended (SelectionModel, range-based: decoupled from realization). • SELECTOR VISUAL — Selector: AccentPill (the WinUI ListView accent bar), Check (GridView corner check), FullRow, Border (the default ItemContainer), None, or a custom ContainerFactory hook. Every item template is wrapped in the chosen selector chrome (selection visuals, pointer states, multi-select checkbox). Reorder (the WinUI live "siblings part to make room") rides the ONE substrate via ItemDisplacement + DisplacementVersion — a capability WinUI's own ItemsView lacks. Behavior contract (verified against the WinUI sources): • SelectionMode None/Single/Multiple/Extended (ItemsView.idl:6-12; default Single, ItemsView.h s_defaultSelectionMode) with the selector semantics in SelectionModel.OnInteractedAction/OnFocusedAction (Single/Multiple/ExtendedSelector.cpp). • ItemInvoked gating (ItemsView.cpp:404-432 CanRaiseItemInvoked): requires IsItemInvokedEnabled; with SelectionMode None, DoubleTap never invokes; with a selection mode active, Tap and Space select WITHOUT invoking (Enter and DoubleTap invoke). • Ctrl+A selects all in Multiple/Extended only (ItemsViewInteractions.cpp:35-50). • Arrows move the current item per the layout's index orientation (ItemsViewInteractions.cpp:923-1102): a vertical stack maps Up/Down to ±1 (Left/Right no-op), a grid maps Left/Right ±1 and Up/Down ±columns, custom layouts get geometric nearest-in-direction. Every walk skips disabled items (the SharedHelpers::IsFocusableElement gate, cpp:203/:321). Home/End bring item 0 / count−1 into view edge-aligned, then focus the first/last FOCUSABLE element (cpp:990-1044); PageUp/Down run the railed three-phase page navigation (cpp:1103-1242). Keyboard moves run the selector's OnFocusedAction and focus the realized container (engine focus ring). • TabNavigation="Once" (ItemsView.xaml:7): ONE roving tab stop — the keyboard-current container; tab-in with no current lands on the selected item (Single mode) else the first focusable item (the GettingFocus redirect, ItemsViewInteractions.cpp:645-721). • Typeahead: printable chars accumulate (1s reset) and jump to the next prefix-matching item from current+1, wrapping (the ListView typeahead shape; the plan's L3 requirement). • Selection is DECOUPLED from realization: SelectAll over 50k items stores one range; only the realized window re-skins (this component subscribes to SelectionModel.Version). public sealed class ItemsView : Component Inheritance object Component ItemsView Inherited Members Component.Context Component.RunsOnce Component.RenderWithHooks() Component.Unmount() object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods MotionHooks.UseEntrance(Component, float, object?) MotionHooks.UseHoverScale(Component, bool, float) Fields Columns public int Columns Field Value int ContainerFactory L4 skin seam: replaces the default ItemContainer chrome (the List/Grid presets + TreeView). public ItemContainerFactory? ContainerFactory Field Value ItemContainerFactory Controller public ItemsViewController? Controller Field Value ItemsViewController DisplacementVersion Bumped by the owner on every drag-delta / dwell-commit; ItemsView subscribes (its .Value) so the frozen-ComponentEl boundary (Reconciler.cs:220-221 — a parent bump alone never re-renders this autonomous component) is crossed and the displacement edge-trigger re-seeds. This is the WinUI on-timer reorder cadence, NOT per frame. public IReadSignal<int>? DisplacementVersion Field Value IReadSignal<int> DraggedSlot OPTIONAL redundant hint: the resting index currently pointer-dragged. The displacement seed already skips the dragged node UNCONDITIONALLY via its DragGhost scene flag (its translate is owned by the DragController and must never be animated), so this is needed only by callers whose drag does not flow through that flag. api/FluentGpu.Controls.MenuBar.html.title: Class MenuBar | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.MenuBar.html.summary:
Class MenuBar Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI MenuBar: a horizontal bar of top-level menu titles (File, Edit, View, …). Clicking a title opens its MenuFlyout below the title; re-clicking the open title closes it. 1:1 behavior with controls/dev/MenuBar:
  • Alt access keys — each title carries an Alt+letter mnemonic (, default the first title letter) that opens its menu (MenuBarItem.xaml:12 ExitDisplayModeOnAccessKeyInvoked=False; MenuBarItem.cpp:249-253).
  • Hover-switch — while ANY menu in the bar is open, pointer-enter on another title opens that one (MenuBarItem.cpp:130-138 OnMenuBarItemPointerEntered → IsFlyoutOpen → ShowMenuFlyout).
  • Keyboard — a focused title opens on Down/Enter/Space (cpp:158-165); Left/Right move focus between titles (cpp:166-191); while a menu is OPEN, Left/Right open the ADJACENT menu (cpp:205-228 OnPresenterKeyDown → OpenFlyoutFrom), cycling. Escape closes (the overlay's light-dismiss preview).
  • Style — bar MinHeight 40 (MenuBarHeight, MenuBar_themeresources.xaml:44), item button padding 10,4,10,4 (MenuBarItemButtonPadding :45), item margin 4 (MenuBarItemMargin :46), ControlCornerRadius 4, fills rest/hover/ pressed/selected = SubtleFill Transparent/Secondary/Tertiary/Tertiary (:7-10), FocusVisualMargin −3 (MenuBarItem.xaml:14).

api/FluentGpu.Controls.MenuFlyout.html.summary: Class MenuFlyout Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll Builds the popup body for a dropdown menu — a vertical list of command rows (each Role = MenuItem). Selecting a row runs its command and closes the overlay. Used by DropDownButton / SplitButton / ToggleSplitButton / MenuBar / TimePicker. The host's FlyoutSurface already supplies the acrylic backdrop, 1px flyout stroke, OverlayCornerRadius, elevation shadow and the (0,2,0,2) presenter padding — this returns INNER content only. public static class MenuFlyout Inheritance object MenuFlyout Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields PartItem EVERY command/toggle/radio/sub-menu row — ONE modifier restyles all rows (popup-built each open, not recycled). Owned: Role, TabIndex, OnClick, OnKeyDown (roving), OnHoverMove (cascade bookkeeping), OnRealized (row-node capture, chained), Children (the check/icon/label/accelerator/chevron columns). public const string PartItem = "Item" Field Value string PartPresenter The items column inside the viewport (WinUI ItemsPresenter). Owned: OnCharInput (first-letter jump), Children (the rows + cascade timers). public const string PartPresenter = "Presenter" Field Value string PartScrollViewer The scrollable viewport (WinUI MenuFlyoutPresenterScrollViewer) — a ScrollEl, so style it via the generic map: Parts.Set<ScrollEl>(MenuFlyout.PartScrollViewer, s => s with { MaxHeight = … }). Owned: Content (the items column), ContentSized. public const string PartScrollViewer = "ScrollViewer" Field Value string PartSeparator A separator row (pure chrome — no owned props). public const string PartSeparator = "Separator" Field Value string Properties SubMenuShowDelayMs Sub-menu hover-open AND delay-close interval — WinUI CascadingMenuHelper reads the user's HKCU MenuShowDelay, defaulting to DefaultMenuShowDelay = 400ms (MenuFlyout_Partial.h:13; CascadingMenuHelper.cpp:93-95 fallback, :439/:508 open/close timer intervals). The platform pump writes the live registry value into SystemParams at startup; headless stays 400. public static float SubMenuShowDelayMs { get; } Property Value float Methods Build(IReadOnlyList<MenuFlyoutItem>, Action, float, TemplateParts?) public static Element Build(IReadOnlyList<MenuFlyoutItem> items, Action close, float minWidth = 96, TemplateParts? parts = null) Parameters items IReadOnlyList<MenuFlyoutItem> close Action minWidth float parts TemplateParts Returns Element Build(IReadOnlyList<MenuFlyoutItem>, Action, float, Action<int>?, TemplateParts?) onNavigate: Left(-1)/Right(+1) pressed while the menu is open and the focused row is not a sub-menu boundary — MenuBar uses it to move between adjacent open menus (MenuBarItem.cpp:205-228 OnPresenterKeyDown → OpenFlyoutFrom). public static Element Build(IReadOnlyList<MenuFlyoutItem> items, Action close, float minWidth, Action<int>? onNavigate, TemplateParts? parts = null) Parameters items IReadOnlyList<MenuFlyoutItem> close Action minWidth float onNavigate Action<int> parts TemplateParts Returns Element

api/FluentGpu.Controls.NavigationView.ht.summary: Class NavigationView Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll Adaptive NavigationView modeled on WinUI's SplitView-backed template: expanded / compact / minimal LEFT modes plus the TOP display mode (horizontal items + overflow flyout), a WinUI-style pane toggle row, section headers, separators, pinned footer items (+ the auto settings item), an AutoSuggestBox slot, per-item InfoBadge slots, and the sliding selection indicator. Behavior verified against microsoft-ui-xaml controls\dev\NavigationView\NavigationView.cpp: • Adaptive thresholds (cpp:1448-1458): width ≥ ExpandedModeThresholdWidth → Expanded; 0 < width < CompactModeThresholdWidth → Minimal; else Compact. Defaults 1008 / 641 (NavigationView.idl:176-181 MUX_DEFAULT_VALUE). An adaptive transition INTO Compact/Minimal closes the pane (cpp:1477-1483). • Pane close on invoke (cpp:4838-4845 ClosePaneIfNeccessaryAfterItemIsClicked): only when the pane is open AND the display mode is not Expanded AND the clicked item has no children. • Home/End focus the first/last item (cpp:2755-2761 → KeyboardFocusFirstItemFromItem/Last); Up/Down move through the visible rows; Right expands / first child, Left collapses / parent (the hierarchy semantics). • Alt+Left closes a light-dismissible (overlay) pane (cpp:3127-3136; IsLightDismissible cpp:1841-1849 = the SplitView is not Inline/CompactInline — here: the adaptive mode is not the inline Expanded pane). • PaneClosing is CANCELABLE (cpp:1708-1714); DisplayModeChanged fires on mode transitions (SetDisplayMode). • SelectionFollowsFocus (cpp:3408-3411): keyboard focus moves select the focused item. • Settings item: an auto-generated footer item with the Setting symbol (E713) named "Settings" (cpp:1318-1349 CreateAndHookEventsToSettings; appended to the footer source, cpp:868-874). Opt-in here via IsSettingsVisible (engine default FALSE — WinUI defaults true, but existing apps author their own settings footer item; enabling both would double it). • Item ramp (NavigationView_themeresources.xaml:9-34): rest=SubtleFillColorTransparent, hover=Secondary, pressed=Tertiary, selected=Secondary, selectedHover=Tertiary, selectedPressed=Secondary; foreground = TextFillColorPrimary in every state EXCEPT Pressed/SelectedPressed → TextFillColorSecondary (:21-34). • Overlay pane background = NavigationViewDefaultPaneBackground = AcrylicInAppFillColorDefaultBrush (:5); the always-visible Expanded pane = SolidBackgroundFillColorTransparent (:6) so the window Mica shows through. • Selection indicator 3×16 @ r2 (:220-222), AccentFillColorDefault (:48), slid by the NavPill spring. Top mode uses the horizontal variant (16×3 under the item). • Pane toggle: 40×36 (PaneToggleButtonWidth/Height :205-206) with the static E700 hamburger — the shipped template's FALLBACK glyph for the AnimatedIcon source (PaneToggleButtonStyle FontIcon); the engine renders the fallback path (press-scale affordance via AnimatedIcon.Glyph), it does not rotate (neither does WinUI's). public sealed class NavigationView : Component Inheritance object Component NavigationView Inherited Members Component.Context Component.RunsOnce Component.RenderWithHooks() Component.Unmount() object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods MotionHooks.UseEntrance(Component, float, object?) api/FluentGpu.Controls.NumberBox.html.summary: Class NumberBox Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll WinUI NumberBox: an editable numeric EditableText field with an optional spin affix. The numeric model is a caller-owned Value signal (double.NaN = cleared); the visible text is derived from it through the Formatter and re-parsed on commit. Spin buttons step by SmallChange and are Hidden by default. Inline puts the two side-by-side repeat buttons (▲E70E / ▼E70D, NumberBox.xaml:174–175) at the trailing edge of the field; Compact shows the EC8F popup-indicator chevron (NumberBox.xaml:365) and opens the up/down buttons in an Overlay popup while focused (the popup auto-opens on focus and closes on blur — NumberBox.cpp:414–438; the indicator is a non-interactive TextBlock, not a toggle). Keyboard stepping follows OnNumberBoxKeyDown (NumberBox.cpp:533–558): Up/Down = ±SmallChange, PageUp/PageDown = ±LargeChange, handled on key DOWN so OS auto-repeat repeats the step. Validation (clamp / revert) runs on Enter and on blur per ValidationMode; with AcceptsExpression a typed arithmetic expression is evaluated on commit (shunting-yard over + - * / ^ and parentheses — NumberBoxParser port). Mouse-wheel over the field while it is focused also steps ±SmallChange and consumes the event before any enclosing viewport scrolls (PointerWheelChanged → OnNumberBoxScroll, NumberBox.cpp:40 + :578–597). public sealed class NumberBox : Component Inheritance object Component NumberBox Inherited Members Component.Context Component.RunsOnce Component.RenderWithHooks() Component.Unmount() object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods MotionHooks.UseEntrance(Component, float, object?) api/FluentGpu.Controls.PasswordBox.html.title: Class PasswordBox | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.PasswordBox.html.summary: Class PasswordBox Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll WinUI PasswordBox: a single-line field built on EditableText with Mask = true — the model text is kept verbatim, rendered as PasswordChar dots; copy/cut are blocked (paste allowed), even while revealed. Inherits the EditableText field chrome unchanged (fill states, gradient ControlElevationBorder, the 1px ring + 2px accent bottom-bar focus affordance = WinUI's TextControlBorderThemeThicknessFocused 1,1,1,2 — PasswordBox_themeresources.xaml:156–157 + Common_themeresources.xaml:11 — no second divergent border here). The reveal button (Peek): glyph U+F78D @ PasswordBoxIconFontSize 12 (PasswordBox_themeresources.xaml:100 + :9), button Width 30, VerticalAlignment Stretch (PasswordBox_themeresources.xaml:193), the TextControlButton state ramp (shared with the TextBox DeleteButton via InnerButton(string, float, Action, Action<Point2>, Action<NodeHandle>)). Visibility is the dxaml ButtonStates rule (the template pair PasswordBox_themeresources.xaml:165–178 is driven by CPasswordBox::UpdateVisualState, PasswordBox.cpp:532–565): ButtonVisible iff CanInvokeRevealButton() = canShow ∧ hasSpace ∧ focused (PasswordBox.cpp:618–626), where canShow (m_fCanShowRevealButton) arms ONLY when content changes from EMPTY — "only allow password reveal button if transitioning from empty to non-empty state" (OnContentChanged, PasswordBox.cpp:366–377) — clears whenever the box becomes empty again (PasswordBox.cpp:430–434) AND on every focus gain (OnGotFocus, PasswordBox.cpp:572–581: "Password reveal button should not appear if focus got moved to the PasswordBox control"). Net effect: the eye shows while the user is TYPING a fresh password, never from focusing/refocusing a populated box. hasSpace (m_fHasSpaceForRevealButton) requires width > 5 × FontSize (ArrangeOverride, PasswordBox.cpp:237–248). Press-and-HOLD reveals — release anywhere re-masks (the watcher polls the button's engine Pressed flag, so drag-off and release-outside re-mask too). Composition note: the mounted EditableText component persists across renders (constructor props freeze at mount), so the reveal flips and the affix visibility mutate the LIVE instance (SetRevealed(bool) / SetRightAffix(Element)) from the focus/text callbacks — SetRightAffix bumps EditableText's render-subscribed affix epoch, so the eye mounts/unmounts even when no focus flip re-renders the field (the mid-typing arming case). public sealed class PasswordBox : Component Inheritance object Component PasswordBox Inherited Members Component.Context Component.RunsOnce Component.RenderWithHooks() Component.Unmount() object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods MotionHooks.UseEntrance(Component, float, object?) api/FluentGpu.Controls.PersonPicture.htm.title: Class PersonPicture | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.PersonPicture.htm.summary: Class PersonPicture Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI PersonPicture: a circular avatar that shows — in WinUI's order of precedence — a circular-cropped photo (Create(string,float,string?,bool,int,string?,ColorF?) via imageSource), otherwise the contact's initials, otherwise a generic Contact/People fallback glyph, all on the quaternary alt control fill (ControlAltFillColorQuarternary) ringed by a 1px card stroke. An optional accent badge (number / glyph) floats at the top-right. Default diameter 96; the initials font is 42% of the diameter (the WinUI design rule), the badge plate is 50% of the diameter, and the badge font is 60% of the badge plate. public static class PersonPicture Inheritance object PersonPicture Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods Create(string, float, string?, bool, int, string?, string?, ColorF?) public static BoxEl Create(string initials, float size = 96, string? displayName = null, bool isGroup = false, int badgeNumber = 0, string? badgeGlyph = null, string? imageSourcePath = null, ColorF? fill = null) Parameters initials string Explicit initials (highest precedence). When empty, displayName drives the initials via the WinUI InitialsGenerator rules; when both are empty, the Contact/People fallback glyph shows. size float Diameter (Width == Height); the control stays circular. Default 96. displayName string Contact display name; initials are generated from it (strip-trailing-brackets, first letter of first + last word, uppercased) only when initials is empty. isGroup bool When true, the avatar shows the People (group) glyph regardless of initials. badgeNumber int When > 0, draws an accent number badge (top-right): ≤99 shows the number, >99 shows "99+". A NEGATIVE value shows no badge at all (WinUI: a non-zero BadgeNumber owns the badge slot and ≤0 maps to NoBadge — the glyph never substitutes, PersonPicture.cpp:191-198). badgeGlyph string When set (and badgeNumber is 0), draws an accent glyph badge (top-right) in the symbol font. imageSourcePath string When set, shows a circular-cropped photo (UniformToFill) instead of initials/glyph — WinUI ProfilePicture. Takes precedence over initials, but not over isGroup. fill ColorF? Override the ellipse fill (default ControlAltFillColorQuarternary); also the photo placeholder tint. Returns BoxEl InitialsFromDisplayName(string) Port of InitialsGenerator::InitialsFromDisplayName: strip a trailing bracket group, split on spaces, take the first full character of the first (and, if more than one word, the last) word, uppercased. Returns "" for symbolic/glyph scripts (CJK, Arabic, Greek, etc.) so the caller falls back to the Contact glyph. public static string InitialsFromDisplayName(string displayName) Parameters displayName string Returns string

api/FluentGpu.Controls.PopupChrome.html.title: Enum PopupChrome | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.PopupChrome.html.summary:
Enum PopupChrome Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll Which presenter chrome + open/close MOTION the overlay host supplies around popup content. Each kind maps 1:1 to the WinUI transition family that owns that surface:
  • — menus (MenuFlyout/DropDownButton/SplitButton/MenuBar/context): MenuPopupThemeTransition (clip-reveal + content translate 250ms cubic-bezier(0,0,0,1); close = 83ms linear fade) — the default.
  • — the WinUI Flyout/FlyoutPresenter (FlyoutBase attaches PopupThemeTransition, FlyoutBase_Partial.cpp:1968–1975): OS-PVL TAS_SHOWPOPUP/TAS_HIDEPOPUP (slide ±50 + delayed fade; close 83ms fade).
  • — the ComboBox dropdown: SplitOpen/SplitCloseThemeAnimation (generic.xaml:9047/9056) — clip reveal 250ms with NO content translate and NO open fade; close = 167ms clip collapse + late 83ms fade. With set, the band is centred on the SEAM (selected row) and grows/shrinks both ways (WinUI OffsetFromCenter); otherwise it reveals from the anchor edge.
  • — ToolTip/Slider value tip: FadeIn/FadeOutThemeAnimation (TAS_FADEIN/TAS_FADEOUT, 167ms linear).
  • — ContentDialog scale/fade; — muxc expand/contract scale.
  • — a bare WinUI Popup with no transitions (the AutoSuggestBox SuggestionsPopup, generic.xaml AutoSuggestBox template — no TransitionCollection and AutoSuggestBox_Partial.cpp adds none): instant.
public enum PopupChrome : byte Fields Dropdown = 5 Flyout = 0 Modal = 2 Popup = 4 Raw = 1 Static = 6 TeachingTip = 3

Set FALSE where WinUI windows its popups (menus, ComboBox tall dropdowns, CommandBarFlyout): the host then asks the platform for a top-level popup window, places against the MONITOR work area, and renders the subtree into the popup window's own swapchain. (The full FlyoutShowMode surface — Transient / TransientWithDismissOnPointerMoveAway 80px — remains open; tracked as engine item 31 in docs/plans/winui-parity-sweep.md.) public Func<NodeHandle>? api/FluentGpu.Controls.ProgressBar.html.summary: Class ProgressBar Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI ProgressBar (1:1). A 3px-min band (ProgressBarMinHeight) holding a 1px track (ProgressBarTrackHeight, ControlStrongStrokeColorDefault) under an accent indicator (AccentFillColorDefaultBrush) with a 1.5px corner. Determinate(float, float, ProgressBarState, TemplateParts?) fills the indicator to a 0..1 value; Indeterminate(float, ProgressBarState, TemplateParts?) sweeps the two clipped accent indicators across the track on the looping translate keyframes WinUI binds from ProgressBarTemplateSettings (Container/Container2 positions). Paused/Error stop the sweep and settle a full-width bar at ContainerAnimationMidPosition, recolored to SystemFillColorCaution / SystemFillColorCritical (instant recolor — no fill-color anim channel). public static class ProgressBar Inheritance object ProgressBar Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields PartFill The foreground indicator (WinUI DeterminateProgressBarIndicator, and BOTH IndeterminateProgressBarIndicator/2 — the modifier runs on each sweeping indicator). The value-driven Width and the Paused/Error recolor are stock per-render styling (override-able). Owned: OnRealized (the indeterminate sweep refs, chained with any modifier-supplied handler). public const string PartFill = "Fill" Field Value string PartTrack The 1px under-track (WinUI ProgressBarTrack). Owned: none — its Opacity-0 in the indeterminate states is stock per-render styling a modifier may override. public const string PartTrack = "Track" Field Value string Methods Determinate(float, float, ProgressBarState, TemplateParts?) Determinate progress; value is clamped to 0..1, indicator width = value * width. state selects the indicator color (Normal accent / Paused caution / Error critical). parts = per-part styling keyed by PartTrack/PartFill. public static BoxEl Determinate(float value, float width = 240, ProgressBarState state = ProgressBarState.Normal, TemplateParts? parts = null) Parameters value float width float state ProgressBarState parts TemplateParts Returns BoxEl Indeterminate(float, ProgressBarState, TemplateParts?) Indeterminate progress: the two clipped accent indicators sweeping across the track on the WinUI ProgressBarTemplateSettings translate keyframes. In Paused/Error, the track hides and only indicator2 shows, recolored to caution/critical (matching WinUI's IndeterminatePaused / IndeterminateError visual states). parts = per-part styling keyed by PartTrack/PartFill (the PartFill modifier runs on BOTH sweeping indicators). public static Element Indeterminate(float width = 240, ProgressBarState state = ProgressBarState.Normal, TemplateParts? parts = null) Parameters width float state ProgressBarState parts TemplateParts Returns Element

api/FluentGpu.Controls.ProgressRing.html.summary: Class ProgressRing Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI ProgressRing brought to 1:1 with controls/dev/ProgressRing. WinUI renders the ring via a Lottie AnimatedVisualPlayer (a continuous accent ring, NOT dots): a determinate variant whose accent arc's TrimEnd sweeps 0→1 over a (default-transparent) track ring, and an indeterminate variant whose accent arc rotates while its trim breathes. We reproduce both on the engine's SDF arc primitive (ArcSpec) — the determinate via a ProgressRing.ProgressRingTemplateSettings-computed sweep, the indeterminate via a rotation Keyframes loop at the exact WinUI cadence (900° per 2.0s, two cubic-bezier 0.167,0.167→0.833,0.833 half-segments). Dimensional canon (ProgressRing.xaml / AnimatedVisuals/ProgressRingIndeterminate.cpp): Width=Height=32 default, MinWidth=MinHeight=16. Stroke is the Lottie weight, NOT the unused ProgressRingStrokeThickness=4 themeresource: the indeterminate ellipse uses StrokeThickness(1.5F) inside an 80px viewbox at Scale:5,5 (effective 1.5·5 = 7.5 in the viewbox), which maps to the 32px control as 7.5·32/80 = 3.0px (= size·0.09375). We scale it proportionally so larger rings keep the same visual weight. Foreground=AccentFillColorDefaultBrush (Tok.AccentDefault), Background=ControlFillColorTransparentBrush (transparent — the default track is invisible, exactly like WinUI). IsActive=false → the LayoutRoot fades to Opacity 0 (the Inactive visual state). public static class ProgressRing Inheritance object ProgressRing Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields DefaultSize public const float DefaultSize = 32 Field Value float MinSize public const float MinSize = 16 Field Value float PartRing The accent ring arc (the Lottie ellipse — determinate sweep / indeterminate spinner). The ArcSpec (color, stroke weight, sweep) is stock per-render computed styling a modifier sees and may override (b => b with { Arc = … }). Owned: OnRealized (the indeterminate trim-loop ref, chained with any modifier-supplied handler; the determinate arc owns nothing). public const string PartRing = "Ring" Field Value string Methods Determinate(float, float, bool, ColorF?, ColorF?, TemplateParts?) A determinate ring: the accent arc's sweep is value·360° from 12 o'clock clockwise, over a track ring. WinUI's default Background is ControlFillColorTransparentBrush, so the track is invisible unless a track color is supplied. foreground defaults to AccentFillColorDefault. parts = per-part styling keyed by PartRing (the accent arc). public static BoxEl Determinate(float value, float size = 32, bool isActive = true, ColorF? foreground = null, ColorF? track = null, TemplateParts? parts = null) Parameters value float size float isActive bool foreground ColorF? track ColorF? parts TemplateParts Returns BoxEl Indeterminate(float, bool, ColorF?, TemplateParts?) An indeterminate spinner: a round-capped accent arc rotating continuously at the WinUI cadence (900° per 2.0s with the Lottie cubic-bezier spline). isActive=false fades it out (Inactive). parts = per-part styling keyed by PartRing (the spinning arc). public static Element Indeterminate(float size = 32, bool isActive = true, ColorF? foreground = null, TemplateParts? parts = null) Parameters size float isActive bool foreground ColorF? parts TemplateParts Returns Element

api/FluentGpu.Controls.RadioButtons.html.summary: Class RadioButtons Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll The WinUI RadioButtons container (microsoft-ui-xaml controls\dev\RadioButtons): a header + a column-major grid of mutually-exclusive RadioButton items with the container keyboard contract. Layout = ColumnMajorUniformToLargestGridLayout (RadioButtons.xaml:22-26): items flow top-to-bottom column-major into at most maxColumns columns — the first count % columns columns take one extra item (ColumnMajorUniformToLargestGridLayout.cpp ArrangeOverride :48-120; CalculateColumns clamps to min(MaxColumns, count), :132-163 — the available-width clamp needs measure and is not applied here). ColumnSpacing = 7, RowSpacing = 8, header margin 0,0,0,8 (RadioButtons_themeresources.xaml:18-20); header foreground TextFillColorPrimary / Disabled (:4-10). Keyboard (RadioButtons.cpp): ONE tab stop — the container is IsTabStop=False + TabNavigation=Once (RadioButtons.xaml:5-6) and focus entering the group lands on the selected item (OnGettingFocus redirect, :80-97); here only the selected (or first) item is Focusable, the roving-tab-stop equivalent. Up/Down move focus ±1 in data order (OnChildPreviewKeyDown :135-165 → MoveFocus, :414-447); Left/Right move across columns (:167-183, the XYFocus directional move constrained to the repeater); SELECTION FOLLOWS FOCUS unless Ctrl is held (:100-107). At the edges the key is swallowed when the source is the first/last item (HandleEdgeCaseFocus, :216-242 — WinUI then tries to move focus OUT of the group, which a control factory cannot do; deviation noted). Controlled. public static class RadioButtons Inheritance object RadioButtons Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields ColumnSpacing public const float ColumnSpacing = 7 Field Value float HeaderGap public const float HeaderGap = 8 Field Value float RowSpacing public const float RowSpacing = 8 Field Value float Methods Create(IReadOnlyList<string>, int, Action<int>, string?, int, bool, Style?, TemplateParts?) api/FluentGpu.Controls.RatingControl.htm.title: Class RatingControl | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.RatingControl.htm.summary: Class RatingControl Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI RatingControl: a row of stars set by click or press-and-sweep, with an optional caption. The control is a two-layer star row -- an always-present unset/background layer (outline glyph E734) and a clipped Set layer (filled glyph E735) revealed to the current rating. Faithful port of RatingControl.cpp: State model -- Value (caller FloatSignal; -1 == unset), an unset PlaceholderValue shown until a real value is set, and an optional Caption. Pointer preview -- press-and-sweep fills the stars to the pointer (with a partial tail) and lifts the foreground to the pointer-over visual-state color; release commits. IsClearEnabled clears the rating when you click the star equal to the current value. Implicit capture keeps the sweep alive OFF the strip while pressed, so dragging past the LEFT edge and releasing clears to NoValueSet (RatingControl.cpp:799-805, 856-863, 888-906). A TOUCH press lifts the hover-scale scalar to c_touchOverScale = 1.0 (vs mouse 0.8) so the focal star grows to full size under the finger. Keyboard -- Left/Down -1, Right/Up +1, Home clears, End sets MaxRating (RatingControl::OnKeyDown). Read-only / disabled -- ReadOnly renders a fixed rating with no interaction; IsEnabled=false dims the foreground to the disabled brush and gates all input. Custom font/image item-info (RatingItemFontInfo/RatingItemImageInfo per-state glyphs/images) is deferred. public sealed class RatingControl : Component Inheritance object Component RatingControl Inherited Members Component.Context Component.RunsOnce Component.RenderWithHooks() Component.Unmount() object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods MotionHooks.UseEntrance(Component, float, object?) MotionHooks.UseHoverScale(Component, bool, float) Fields Caption public string Caption Field Value string InitialSetValue public int InitialSetValue Field Value int IsClearEnabled public bool IsClearEnabled Field Value bool IsEnabled public bool IsEnabled Field Value bool ItemSpacing public float ItemSpacing Field Value float MaxRating public int MaxRating Field Value int NoValueSet The WinUI c_noValueSetSentinel: Value <= this means "unset". public const float NoValueSet = -1 Field Value float OnChange public Action<float>? OnChange Field Value Action<float> PartCaption The trailing caption (WinUI Caption TextBlock) — a TextEl part (Parts.Set<TextEl>(…)). No owned props. public const string PartCaption = "Caption" Field Value string PartRoot The returned control root (star strip + caption). Owned: OnKeyDown (the arrow/Home/End rating keys), Role, Children. public const string PartRoot = "Root" Field Value string PartStarCell Each star cell (applied to EVERY star — full, empty and partial alike). Owned: TransformBind + TransformOriginX/Y (the focal hover-scale expression about the cell centre). public const string PartStarCell = "StarCell" Field Value string PartStarRow The interactive star strip (WinUI RatingBackgroundStackPanel). Owned: OnHoverMove, OnPointerDown, OnPointerPressed, OnDrag, OnClick, OnPointerExit (the hover/sweep/commit lifecycle), Children. NOTE: the sweep math maps pointer X over the STOCK width (StarSize/ItemSpacing) — resizing this part skews the star-under-pointer mapping. public const string PartStarRow = "StarRow" Field Value string Parts Lightweight per-part styling (CSS ::part): modifiers keyed by the PartXxx consts; see TemplateParts for the contract. public TemplateParts? Parts Field Value TemplateParts PlaceholderValue public float PlaceholderValue Field Value float ReadOnly public bool ReadOnly Field Value bool StarSize public float StarSize Field Value float Value public FloatSignal Value Field Value FloatSignal Methods Coerce(float, int) Programmatic value coercion (RatingControl::CoerceValueBetweenMinAndMax): a negative value snaps to the NoValueSet (-1) "unset" sentinel; a value in (0,1] snaps up to 1 (a single star); a value above max clamps down to max. Fractional values in (1,max] keep their tail (the partial-star fill). Applied to both Value and PlaceholderValue at render time. public static float Coerce(float value, int max) Parameters value float max int Returns float Create(FloatSignal, int, float, string, bool, bool, bool, Action<float>?) public static Element Create(FloatSignal value, int max = 5, float placeholder = -1, string caption = "", bool readOnly = false, bool isEnabled = true, bool isClearEnabled = true, Action<float>? onChange = null) Parameters value FloatSignal max int placeholder float caption string readOnly bool isEnabled bool isClearEnabled bool onChange Action<float> Returns Element Render() public override Element Render() Returns Element

api/FluentGpu.Controls.RatingControlTemp.title: Struct RatingControlTemplateSettings | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.RatingControlTemp.summary: Struct RatingControlTemplateSettings Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll Computed template settings for the RatingControl (the typed-record convention -- see PersonPicture.PersonPictureTemplateSettings / Expander.ExpanderTemplateSettings): the resolved display value (the rating shown right now -- pointer-over preview, else committed value, else placeholder), the foreground/background brushes for the current visual state, and the partial-star clip width baked for the foreground "Set" layer. Mirrors the geometry WinUI's RatingControl::UpdateRatingItemsAppearance derives each pass and binds into its two clipped stack panels. Pure factory: no signals are read here -- the caller passes the already-resolved inputs, so this struct is cheap to build per render and never allocates in a hot bind. The star foreground recolor is INSTANT, never eased: WinUI drives every star visual state with VisualStateManager::GoToState(useTransitions:false) (see UpdateRatingItemsAppearance), so the brush steps the instant the pointer-over preview resolves. We honor that by computing the resting Foreground directly from the sweep and leaving the glyph's HoverColor at default (A==0 ⇒ the recorder leaves the color untouched and runs no hover ramp) -- a granular re-render swaps the resting color in one step, no cross-fade. public readonly record struct RatingControlTemplateSettings : IEquatable<RatingControlTemplateSettings> Implements IEquatable<RatingControlTemplateSettings> Inherited Members ValueType.Equals(object) ValueType.GetHashCode() ValueType.ToString() object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Constructors RatingControlTemplateSettings(float, ColorF, ColorF) Computed template settings for the RatingControl (the typed-record convention -- see PersonPicture.PersonPictureTemplateSettings / Expander.ExpanderTemplateSettings): the resolved display value (the rating shown right now -- pointer-over preview, else committed value, else placeholder), the foreground/background brushes for the current visual state, and the partial-star clip width baked for the foreground "Set" layer. Mirrors the geometry WinUI's RatingControl::UpdateRatingItemsAppearance derives each pass and binds into its two clipped stack panels. Pure factory: no signals are read here -- the caller passes the already-resolved inputs, so this struct is cheap to build per render and never allocates in a hot bind. The star foreground recolor is INSTANT, never eased: WinUI drives every star visual state with VisualStateManager::GoToState(useTransitions:false) (see UpdateRatingItemsAppearance), so the brush steps the instant the pointer-over preview resolves. We honor that by computing the resting Foreground directly from the sweep and leaving the glyph's HoverColor at default (A==0 ⇒ the recorder leaves the color untouched and runs no hover ramp) -- a granular re-render swaps the resting color in one step, no cross-fade. public RatingControlTemplateSettings(float DisplayValue, ColorF Foreground, ColorF Background) Parameters DisplayValue float Foreground ColorF Background ColorF Fields MouseOverScale public const float MouseOverScale = 0.8 Field Value float NoPointerOverFocal public const float NoPointerOverFocal = -100 Field Value float TouchOverScale public const float TouchOverScale = 1 Field Value float Properties Background public ColorF Background { get; init; } Property Value ColorF DisplayValue public float DisplayValue { get; init; } Property Value float Foreground public ColorF Foreground { get; init; } Property Value ColorF Methods For(float, float, float, int, bool, bool) public static RatingControlTemplateSettings For(float value, float placeholder, float preview, int max, bool isEnabled, bool readOnly) Parameters value float Committed rating, or NoValueSet (-1) when unset. placeholder float Placeholder rating, or -1 for none. preview float Pointer-over preview value, or -1 when not hovering/interacting. max int isEnabled bool readOnly bool Returns RatingControlTemplateSettings StarCenter(int, float, float) Star center in panel-local on-screen px (RatingControl::CalculateStarCenter, RatingControl.cpp:926-932): ActualRatingFontSize·(index+0.5) + index·ItemSpacing, with ActualRatingFontSize = 32/2 = 16 (cpp:51-54). public static float StarCenter(int index, float starSize, float itemSpacing) Parameters index int starSize float itemSpacing float Returns float StarScale(float, float) Mouse-scalar convenience overload (the pre-touch-damping signature, kept for source compat). public static float StarScale(float starCenterX, float focalX) Parameters starCenterX float focalX float Returns float StarScale(float, float, float) The re-based per-star scale at starCenterX given the live focalX (both panel-local on-screen px) and the device pointerScalar (MouseOverScale / TouchOverScale). 1.0 = resting (our 16px glyph); api/FluentGpu.Controls.RelativePanel.htm.summary: Class RelativePanel Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll WinUI-style RelativePanel: positions children relative to the panel and to each other. WinUI resolves a constraint graph (AlignLeftWith, RightOf, AlignTopWithPanel, …) into absolute offsets; for this self-contained demo we accept the already-resolved absolute (X, Y) offsets (like Canvas) and render them in a ZStack where each child is wrapped in a BoxEl whose OffsetX/OffsetY translate it into place — enough to show a relative layout result. Children are clipped to the panel bounds. public static class RelativePanel Inheritance object RelativePanel Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods Create(float, float, IReadOnlyList<RelativeChild>) public static BoxEl Create(float width, float height, IReadOnlyList<RelativeChild> children) Parameters width float height float children IReadOnlyList<RelativeChild> Returns BoxEl

api/FluentGpu.Controls.ReorderList.html.title: Class ReorderList | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.ReorderList.html.summary: Class ReorderList Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll Reorder geometry for a one-axis item strip (the WinUI live-reorder model — ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp): given the items' resting main-axis extents and the dragged item's accumulated drag delta (DragEventArgs.TotalDy for a vertical list / TotalDx for a horizontal strip), it computes the PENDING insertion slot under the pointer (midpoint rule: the dragged item's centre crossing a sibling's midpoint claims its slot — the engine analogue of IItemLookupPanel.GetClosestElementInfo + LiveReorderHelper::MovedItems::GetDragOverIndex, ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:984-1063), the dwell-committed TARGET slot — WinUI moves displaced items only after the drag-over index has been stable for the live-reorder timer (200ms list / 300ms grid — LISTVIEW_LIVEREORDER_TIMER / GRIDVIEW_LIVEREORDER_TIMER, ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:50-51), and per-sibling displacement hints (OffsetFor(int)): items between the dragged slot and the target shift by one full dragged-extent (+spacing), the live-reorder "items part to make room" motion (LiveReorderTimerTickHandler → MoveItemsForLiveReorder, ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:2125-2158). Consumers (ListView/GridView/TabView/TreeView item hosts) wire it to the drag lifecycle: Begin in OnDragStarted, Update(e.TotalDy) in OnDragDelta, Advance(dtMs) from the frame clock; when Advance commits a new target, re-render with the children ORDER projected to it (ProjectOrder(Span<int>) — stable keys keep node identity) and a LayoutTransition (Animate) on every item: displaced siblings genuinely change slots, so the engine's FLIP pipeline animates the part-to-make-room motion, and Input.DragController re-anchors the pointer-held visual to its moved slot (no jump). OffsetFor(int) remains for offset-hint consumers — but an authored OffsetX/Y hint must NOT be combined with Animate on the same node: FLIP position tracks own the whole translate channel (CompositeOp.Replace — AnimEngine.ReframePosition) and would stomp the hint at seed and settle. Complete() in OnDragCompleted fires OnCommit with the collection move; Cancel() in OnDragCanceled drops every hint. All state is grow-only — steady-state reorder (drag at pointer rate) allocates nothing. TWO-DIMENSIONAL MODE (Begin2D(int, int, int) / Update2D(float, float, float, float) / OffsetFor2D(int, float, float, out float, out float)) absorbs the GridView live-reorder geometry: the pending slot comes from the dragged tile's accumulated (dx,dy) against the grid (row from the vertical stride, column from the realized column width), with the 300ms grid dwell (GRIDVIEW_LIVEREORDER_TIMER = 300ms — ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:51) re-armed on every drag-over change. A one-slot grid shift can WRAP A ROW (the trailing tile of a row dropping to the head of the next), so the 2-D displacement hint carries BOTH axes. The dwell ticker (Advance(float)), the moved-items projection (ProjectOrder(Span<int>) — row-major, identical to 1-D), Complete() and Cancel() are shared with the 1-D path unchanged. Columns = 0 selects the 1-D path; Begin2D(int, int, int) sets it. public sealed class ReorderList Inheritance object ReorderList Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields GridDwellMs GridView's dwell — GRIDVIEW_LIVEREORDER_TIMER = 300ms (ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:51). public const float GridDwellMs = 300 Field Value float ListDwellMs Hint dwell before displaced items shift — LISTVIEW_LIVEREORDER_TIMER = 200ms (ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:50). public const float ListDwellMs = 200 Field Value float OnCommit The collection move, fired by Complete() when the item lands on a new slot: (fromIndex, toIndex) in the ORIGINAL order — remove at from, insert at to. public Action<int, int>? OnCommit Field Value Action<int, int> Properties Columns Grid column count for the 2-D mode (0 ⇒ 1-D mode). Set by Begin2D(int, int, int); the 1-D Begin(int, ReadOnlySpan<float>, float) overloads leave it 0. public int Columns { get; } Property Value int Count public int Count { get; } Property Value int DraggedIndex public int DraggedIndex { get; } Property Value int DraggedTargetStart The resting main-axis start the dragged item will occupy at the current TargetIndex — the settle target for a consumer-drawn drop preview. public float DraggedTargetStart { get; } Property Value float DwellMs Dwell before a new pending slot becomes the shown target (WinUI restarts the timer on every drag-over index change — ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:1068-1074). 0 ⇒ hints follow the pointer immediately. public float DwellMs { get; set; } Property Value float IsActive public bool IsActive { get; } Property Value bool PendingIndex The latest computed insertion slot under the pointer (becomes TargetIndex after the dwell). public int PendingIndex { get; } Property Value int TargetIndex The dwell-committed slot the displacement hints currently show (= DraggedIndex until the first dwell elapses). public int TargetIndex { get; } Property Value int Methods Advance(float) Advance the live-reorder dwell (drive from the frame clock while a drag is active). Returns true when the shown target changed — re-render the siblings with the new OffsetFor(int) hints (their LayoutTransition FLIP animates the shift). public bool Advance(float dtMs) Parameters dtMs float Returns bool Begin(int, int, float, float) Uniform-extent overload (fixed-row ListView / tab strip): all count items share itemExtent. public void Begin(int draggedIndex, int count, float itemExtent, float spacing = 0) Parameters draggedIndex int count int itemExtent float spacing float Begin(int, ReadOnlySpan<float>, float) Start a reorder for draggedIndex over items with the given resting main-axis itemExtents, separated by spacing (the container's Gap). Copies the extents into grow-only storage (no per-drag steady alloc once grown). public void Begin(int draggedIndex, ReadOnlySpan<float> itemExtents, float spacing = 0) Parameters draggedIndex int itemExtents ReadOnlySpan<float> spacing float Begin2D(int, int, int) Begin a 2-D (grid) reorder for draggedIndex over count tiles laid out row-major in columns columns (absorbs the GridView live-reorder geometry). No resting extents are stored — the 2-D slot math is grid-geometric (Update2D(float, float, float, float) takes the realized column width and row stride per move). Sets Columns > 0 to select the 2-D path. public void Begin2D(int draggedIndex, int count, int columns) Parameters draggedIndex int count int columns int Cancel() Abort the reorder (drag canceled): drop every hint without committing. public void Cancel() Complete() Finish the reorder at the LATEST pending slot (the release point under the pointer — the dwell shown state never delays the actual drop). Resets all hints BEFORE firing OnCommit, so the commit's re-render reads zero offsets. Returns the destination index (−1 when idle). public int Complete() Returns int MoveTarget(int) Keyboard lift-mode move (rbd a11y — E5-L3): shift the shown target directly by delta slots, clamped to the list, with NO dwell (a deliberate keystroke needs no stabilization timer; pending and target stay in lockstep so Complete() commits exactly what the user sees). Returns true when the shown target changed — re-render with ProjectOrder(Span<int>) and the FLIP pipeline animates the move. public bool MoveTarget(int delta) Parameters delta int Returns bool Move<T>(IList<T>, int, int) Apply a committed reorder to a collection in place: remove at from, insert at to — exactly the OnCommit payload, and exactly WinUI's drop commit (ListViewBase::ReorderItemsTo → RemoveAt(realItemIndex) + InsertAt(insertIndex), ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:1536-1537). Out-of-range / no-op indices are ignored. Cold path (one drop). public static void Move<T>(IList<T> list, int from, int to) Parameters list IList<T> from int to int Type Parameters T OffsetFor(int) The main-axis displacement hint for sibling index at the current shown target: items between the dragged slot and the target shift one dragged-extent (+spacing) to make room; everything else (and the dragged item itself) is 0. public float OffsetFor(int index) Parameters index int Returns float OffsetFor2D(int, float, float, out float, out float) The 2-D per-tile displacement hint at the current shown target: a tile in the block between the dragged slot and the target shifts by ONE slot toward the vacated source (row-major), which can WRAP A ROW — so the hint carries both axes (dx, dy). The dragged tile and everything outside the block get (0,0). A verbatim port of the GridView 2-D OffsetFor (ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:2125-2158). public void OffsetFor2D(int index, float colWidth, float rowStride, out float dx, out float dy) Parameters index int colWidth float rowStride float dx float dy float ProjectOrder(Span<int>) Fill order (length ≥ Count) with ORIGINAL item indices in the current dwell-committed projected order: the dragged item occupies TargetIndex, everything else keeps its relative order (the live-reorder "moved items" view — LiveReorderHelper::MovedItems, ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:2125-2157). Re-render the children from this projection (stable keys!) with a LayoutTransition and the FLIP pipeline animates the displaced siblings. Span-filling — 0 alloc. public void ProjectOrder(Span<int> order) Parameters order Span<int> Update(float) Recompute the pending slot from the dragged item's accumulated main-axis translation (DragEventArgs.TotalDy / TotalDx). Midpoints are evaluated in RESTING coordinates, so the hint motion never feeds back into the slot math. Returns true when the pending slot changed (the dwell re-arms — ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:1068-1074). public bool Update(float dragDelta) Parameters dragDelta float Returns bool Update2D(float, float, float, float) Recompute the 2-D pending slot from the dragged tile's accumulated translation (totalDx, totalDy): column from colWidth, row from rowStride, clamped to the grid. Returns true when the pending slot changed (the dwell re-arms to DwellMs — already 300 for the grid preset; ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:1068-1074). A verbatim port of the GridView 2-D update. public bool Update2D(float totalDx, float totalDy, float colWidth, float rowStride) Parameters totalDx float totalDy float colWidth float rowStride float Returns bool
