api/FluentGpu.Controls.Reorderable.html.title: Class Reorderable | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.Reorderable.html.summary: Class Reorderable Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll E5-L3 — drag-reorder for ANY one-axis item list (incl. Virtual.List), composed from the engine layers beneath it (plan Part B E5; user ruling: the Flutter Draggable/DragTarget + react-beautiful-dnd + SwiftUI model, deliberately NOT WinUI's OLE loop): each item is an L2 DragSource of Kind, the list body is ONE accepting DropTargetSpec, the slot math is ReorderList (WinUI's midpoint rule + live-reorder dwell — ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:984-1063, :50-51), sibling displacement is the engine FLIP pipeline (each wrapped item carries a LayoutTransition; re-rendering the PROJECTED order moves them through their springs), the ghost/lift visuals and the drop-glide are L1 DragController, and edge auto-scroll is engine-level in DragDropContext (WinUI's 100px/150–1500px/s gradient) — this class adds none of those mechanics, only the list semantics. Usage (inside a component render): var ro = UseMemo(() => new Reorderable("track") { OnReorder = (f, t) => Move(tracks, f, t) }); ro.Scene = Context.Scene; ro.RequestRender = Context.RequestRerender; ro.ItemCount = tracks.Count; ro.ItemExtent = 48f; return ro.List(Virtual.List(tracks.Count, 48f, slot => ro.Item(ro.ItemAt(slot), RowFor(tracks[ro.ItemAt(slot)]), key: tracks[ro.ItemAt(slot)].Id))); Render slots through ItemAt(int) with STABLE per-item keys: mid-drag the projection moves the dragged item to the dwell-committed slot, the keyed diff keeps node identity, and the FLIP pass animates the displaced siblings (the part-to-make-room motion) while DragController.Move re-anchors the pointer-held visual. CROSS-LIST: two Reorderables sharing a Kind. The target list accepts the foreign session, shows the insertion line while hovered, and on drop routes the ONE OnCrossCommit (target's, else the source's) — the source's gesture completion then cancels its local reorder instead of committing (no double mutation). KEYBOARD lift mode (the react-beautiful-dnd a11y pattern): Space on a focused item lifts it (a pointer-free reorder; the item dims to the L1 drag opacity with the lifted shadow), Up/Down (Left/Right when Horizontal) move the insertion slot with NO dwell, Space drops (commits), Escape cancels, and focus leaving the lifted item cancels. All slot moves re-render the projection → FLIP animates them. public sealed class Reorderable Inheritance object Reorderable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors Reorderable(string) public Reorderable(string kind) Parameters kind string Fields AutoDwell Advance the dwell from the wall clock on every drag delta (default). Set false for deterministic tests and drive Advance(float) yourself (or set DwellMs = 0). public bool AutoDwell Field Value bool ExtentOf Variable extents: resting main-axis extent per ORIGINAL index (sampled once at lift into pooled storage). Cross-list insertion math still assumes the uniform ItemExtent pitch. public Func<int, float>? ExtentOf Field Value Func<int, float> Horizontal Horizontal strip (TotalDx drives the slot math; Left/Right are the keyboard-lift arrows). public bool Horizontal Field Value bool ItemCount Item count this render (drives Begin(int, ReadOnlySpan<float>, float) at lift). public int ItemCount Field Value int ItemExtent Uniform resting main-axis extent per item (ignored when ExtentOf is set). public float ItemExtent Field Value float ItemOf The app item carried as Item (what a foreign target receives). public Func<int, object?>? ItemOf Field Value Func<int, object> LiftOpacity Keyboard-lift visual opacity — the SAME lift visual the pointer path gets from L1 (WinUI ListViewItemDragThemeOpacity = 0.80 — microsoft-ui-xaml controls\dev\CommonStyles\ListViewItem_themeresources.xaml:7, identical in all ThemeDictionaries). public const float LiftOpacity = 0.8 Field Value float LiveProject Live mid-drag projection (default): ItemAt(int) returns the PROJECTED order while lifted, so displaced siblings FLIP to make room (the rbd motion). REQUIRES key-preserving children (plain keyed children / ForEl / Repeater): the keyed diff moves nodes with their items. Set FALSE over Virtual.List/VirtualListEl — its window diff recycles nodes POSITIONALLY (no keys), so a mid-drag projection would swap content under the lifted node; instead the resting order holds during the drag and the insertion LINE shows the pending slot (commit still lands exactly where shown). public bool LiveProject Field Value bool OnCrossCommit CROSS-LIST commit (see ReorderCrossCommit). The drop TARGET's delegate runs; when it has none, the SOURCE's is used — set the one shared callback on both lists. public ReorderCrossCommit? OnCrossCommit Field Value ReorderCrossCommit OnReorder SAME-LIST commit: (fromIndex, toIndex) in the ORIGINAL order — remove at from, insert at to (Move<T>(IList<T>, int, int) applies it to an IList<T>). public Action<int, int>? OnReorder Field Value Action<int, int> RequestRender Re-render request (wire Context.RequestRerender or a signal bump): fired whenever the projected order / lift / insertion-line state changed and the consumer must re-render the list. public Action? RequestRender Field Value Action Scene The scene (wire Context.Scene) — needed only for CROSS-LIST insertion math (pointer → slot) and the insertion-line offset; same-list reorder works without it. public SceneStore? Scene Field Value SceneStore ShowInsertionLine Show the built-in 2px accent insertion line while a FOREIGN session hovers this list, and for same-list feedback when LiveProject is off. Default true. public bool ShowInsertionLine Field Value bool Spacing The container's main-axis Gap between items. public float Spacing Field Value float Properties Core The slot-math core (midpoint rule + dwell + displacement hints + projection). Exposed for advanced composition; normal consumers only read the surface properties here. public ReorderList Core { get; } Property Value ReorderList DwellMs Live-reorder dwell before displaced items shift (WinUI LISTVIEW_LIVEREORDER_TIMER 200ms / GRIDVIEW 300ms — ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:50-51). 0 ⇒ displacement follows the pointer immediately. public float DwellMs { get; set; } Property Value float InsertionIndex The slot the line marks: the foreign session's insertion slot (0..ItemCount), else the same-list pending slot (−1 when nothing shows). public int InsertionIndex { get; } Property Value int InsertionLineOffset Main-axis offset (list-wrapper space, scroll-corrected) of the insertion boundary — where the built-in InsertionLine() sits. Foreign sessions mark the slot boundary; a same-list (LiveProject-off) lift marks the resting start the dragged item will land at (DraggedTargetStart... computed at the PENDING slot for immediacy). public float InsertionLineOffset { get; } Property Value float InsertionVisible The insertion line is showing: a foreign session hovers the list, OR a same-list lift with LiveProject off has a pending slot away from home (the virtual-list feedback mode). public bool InsertionVisible { get; } Property Value bool IsKeyboardLifted True while the lift is the pointer-free keyboard mode. public bool IsKeyboardLifted { get; } Property Value bool IsLifted A lift is in flight on THIS list (pointer drag or keyboard lift). public bool IsLifted { get; } Property Value bool Kind The drag kind this list emits AND accepts (the cast-free L2 discriminator). public string Kind { get; } Property Value string LiftedIndex The lifted item's ORIGINAL index (−1 when idle). public int LiftedIndex { get; } Property Value int TargetIndex The dwell-committed slot the projection currently shows (−1 when idle). public int TargetIndex { get; } Property Value int Methods Advance(float) Deterministic dwell driver (tests / a FrameClock subscriber when AutoDwell is off). Returns true when the shown target changed (a re-render was requested). public bool Advance(float dtMs) Parameters dtMs float Returns bool InsertionLine() The built-in cross-list insertion indicator: a 2px accent bar at the insertion boundary, stretched across the list (rbd's insertion cue; WinUI's native cue is displacement, which same-list reorder gets via FLIP). Non-hit-testable. Consumers composing their own List(Element) wrapper can place it manually from InsertionVisible/InsertionLineOffset. public Element InsertionLine() Returns Element Item(int, Element, string?, LayoutTransition?) Wrap one item's content: an L2 DragSource of Kind (payload = ReorderPayload, resolved once at promotion), the L1 lifecycle wired into the slot math, a FLIP LayoutTransition (default Slide) for the displacement motion, focusable with the keyboard lift-mode keys, and the keyboard-lift visuals when lifted that way (the pointer path gets identical visuals from L1). index is the ORIGINAL item index (pass ItemAt(slot)). public Element Item(int index, Element content, string? key = null, LayoutTransition? transition = null) Parameters index int content Element key string transition LayoutTransition? Returns Element ItemAt(int) The ORIGINAL item index shown at slot under the current projection (identity when idle): render slot s with the item at ItemAt(s) and the item's STABLE key — mid-drag the dragged item occupies the dwell-committed slot and the keyed diff + FLIP animate the displaced siblings. With LiveProject off this stays identity while lifted (virtual-list mode — the insertion line is the feedback; the commit reorders the model itself). public int ItemAt(int slot) Parameters slot int Returns int List(Element) Wrap the list body as the ONE accepting drop target of Kind (a ZStack so the insertion-line overlay can ride above the body): same-list releases commit through the gesture completion, foreign sessions get the insertion line + OnCrossCommit. Captures the wrapper node for the pointer→slot math (set Scene). public Element List(Element body) Parameters body Element Returns Element OffsetFor(int) Displacement hint passthrough (OffsetFor(int)) for offset-hint consumers that render the RESTING order with translation hints instead of the projected order. Do not combine an authored offset hint with a FLIP Animate on the same node (see the ReorderList class remarks). public float OffsetFor(int originalIndex) Parameters originalIndex int Returns float

api/FluentGpu.Controls.RepeatButton.html.summary: Class RepeatButton Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A button that raises its click repeatedly while held (WinUI RepeatButton): the host's RepeatTicker fires the click once on press, then after an initial delay, then at a steady interval, until release (or until the pointer drags off — WinUI stops the timer when IsPointerOver drops, RepeatButton_Partial.cpp:530-548). Opting in is just Repeats = true on a clickable node; the scheduling lives in the host (see RepeatTicker). WinUI defaults: Delay = 500ms, Interval = 33ms — the DP metadata defaults (dxaml\xcp\components\DependencyObject\DependencyProperty.cpp:714-720) — overridable per instance via DelayMs/IntervalMs (RepeatButton_Partial.cpp:149-182 validates positive). Keyboard mirrors WinUI exactly: a held Space arms the SAME engine timer (RepeatButton_Partial.cpp:212-217 — m_keyboardCausingRepeat; OS key auto-repeat is ignored), a held Enter yields exactly ONE click on its down edge (the ClickMode.Press initialize, RepeatButton_Partial.cpp:29). Style source: controls\dev\CommonStyles\RepeatButton_themeresources.xaml (Default = dark :4-27, Light :52-75) — state storyboards are color-only (lines 100-143), so no press scale (Wave-1 parity pass removed it). public static class RepeatButton Inheritance object RepeatButton Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields PartLabel The label run — a TextEl, so customize via parts.Set<TextEl>(RepeatButton.PartLabel, …). Stack/Grid/Custom virtualize (only the window is realized); Wrap/Inline are non-virtual (build the whole child list — use for SMALL collections like a nav pane or a toolbar). api/FluentGpu.Controls.Repeater.html.title: Class Repeater | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.Repeater.html.summary: Class Repeater Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll public static class Repeater Inheritance object Repeater Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods ItemsRepeater(int, Func<int, Element>, in RepeatLayout, Func<int, string>?, int, Action<int>?, Action<int>?, Action<int, int>?, Action<int, int>?, ItemCollectionTransition?) A data-driven items panel (WinUI's ItemsRepeater): count items × a template over a pluggable layout. Virtualizing layouts (Stack/Grid/Custom/Measured/LinedFlow/SpanGrid) realize only the window and recycle over the slab free-list — 0-alloc steady scroll; non-virtual layouts (Wrap/Inline) build the full child list (use for small collections). No built-in selection/focus — compose that above it (ItemsView is exactly that composition). E11-L2 surface: elementPrepared/elementClearing/elementIndexChanged are WinUI's ItemsRepeater lifecycle events (ItemsRepeater.idl:186-188 ElementPrepared/ElementClearing/ ElementIndexChanged), fired with item indices at realize time; visibleRange is the prefetch hook (first,last); transition maps ItemCollectionTransition onto the FLIP/Animate pipeline. public static Element ItemsRepeater(int count, Func<int, Element> template, in RepeatLayout layout, Func<int, string>? keyOf = null, int overscan = 4, Action<int>? elementPrepared = null, Action<int>? elementClearing = null, Action<int, int>? elementIndexChanged = null, Action<int, int>? visibleRange = null, ItemCollectionTransition? transition = null) Parameters count int template Func<int, Element> layout RepeatLayout keyOf Func<int, string> overscan int elementPrepared Action<int> elementClearing Action<int> elementIndexChanged Action<int, int> visibleRange Action<int, int> transition ItemCollectionTransition? api/FluentGpu.Controls.RichTextBlock.htm.summary: Class RichTextBlock Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll WinUI RichTextBlock equivalent: read-only, multi-paragraph rich text laid out as a vertical column of paragraphs. Each paragraph is a SpanTextEl — an array of typed inline runs (the WinUI Run/Bold/Hyperlink inline model, RichTextBlock.g.h:63 get_Blocks) that shapes as ONE wrapped flow (rtb-01). Selection (rtb-02): ON by default — IsTextSelectionEnabled=TRUE by default on CRichTextBlock (RichTextBlock.cpp:1730); mouse drag selects (word on double-click, all on triple), Ctrl+C copies through the clipboard seam (TextSelectionManager.cpp:30-41), the highlight reuses the editor's selection brushes (TextControlSelectionHighlightColor ≡ the system accent, TextSelectionManager.cpp:52-56) and the I-beam shows while selectable. SelectionHighlightColor overrides the brush per control (api-04 — TextBlock.cpp:266/330 SelectionHighlightColor). Honest scope: selection is PER PARAGRAPH (the drag clamps to the paragraph it anchored in); WinUI's cross-paragraph selection arrives with the block-collection pass. Hyperlinks: Hyperlink(string, Action) spans get the WinUI inline-Hyperlink treatment — accent foreground (HyperlinkForeground = AccentTextFillColorPrimary, generic.xaml:1120-1122), underline, the Hand api/FluentGpu.Controls.ScrollBar.html.title: Class ScrollBar | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.ScrollBar.html.summary: Class ScrollBar Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll WinUI ScrollBar. Two surfaces: Create(float, float, Action<float>, float, Style?, bool, TemplateParts?) — the legacy thin PANNING-indicator variant (thumb-only, absolute drag), kept source/behavior-compatible (VerticalSlice check 48 and the gallery drive it); it mirrors the template's VerticalPanningThumb (ScrollBar_themeresources.xaml:714 — Width 2, MinHeight 32, Margin 2,0,2,0, NO hover/press states — the touch indicator). Anatomy(float, float, Action<float>, float, bool, TemplateParts?, bool, float, float) — the full WinUI mouse scrollbar: 12px rail (ScrollBarSize :180), acrylic track (ScrollBarTrackFill = AcrylicInAppFillColorDefaultBrush, :31/:143 both themes), two arrow RepeatButtons (vertical glyphs EDDB up / EDDC down, horizontal EDD9 left / EDDA right, FontSize 8 — :387/:344/:301/:258/:186; pressed arrow scale 0.875 :187; Interval=50 like every template RepeatButton :681-711), track-click PAGE zones (LargeDecrease/LargeIncrease RepeatButtons :704/:710 — first page on press, then 500ms delay + 50ms repeat re-evaluated against the live thumb), and the conscious expand/collapse: collapsed 2px visible thumb ↔ expanded 6px (the 8px/12px thumb minus the 6px transparent stroke trick — ScrollBarVerticalThumbMinWidth 8 :182, ScrollBarThumbStrokeThickness 6 :185, ScrollBarSize 12 :180; both states leave the FILL's far edge inset 3px from the lane edge: collapsed rect [4,12] / expanded [0,12], fill inset 3 per side → [7,9] / [3,9]). Expand begins 400ms after lane hover (ScrollBarExpandBeginTime :188) / collapse begins 500ms after leave (ScrollBarContractBeginTime :189), both 167ms with KeySpline 0,0,0,1 (:587/:543 → FluentPopOpen); track + arrows fade 83ms LINEAR (ScrollBarOpacityChangeDuration :174, plain DoubleAnimation :575-584) after the same begin times. While the thumb is DRAGGED a lane exit does NOT start the contract dwell (WinUI OnPointerExited skips UpdateVisualState while IsDragging, ScrollBar_Partial.cpp:548-561) — the leave replays at the drag's release edge. The arrow CELLS are ALWAYS in the grid (rows 0/4, fixed extent = ScrollBarSize :703/:711) — only their OPACITY animates, so the track length and thumb geometry never jump on hover. Thumb fill = ControlStrongFillColorDefault with NO hover/press recolor (:26-28); arrow button background stays SubtleFillColorTransparent in EVERY state (:14 — the :226-248 storyboards recolor only the Arrow foreground); disabled = ControlStrongFillColorDisabled (:29) + root opacity 0.5 (:436) + the mouse thumb itself hidden (ThumbVisual Opacity→0, the VerticalThumbTemplate Disabled storyboard :399-406). IsTabStop = false (:206). Vertical by default; horizontal: true mirrors the HorizontalRoot anatomy on the X axis (:672-693). Thumb POSITION is compositor-bound (a TransformBind on the position signal): scrolling moves the thumb the same frame with no re-render/relayout and never enters the FLIP pipeline — WinUI's instant Thumb layout. ONLY the conscious cross-axis 2px↔6px expand (and the 83ms chrome fades) animate, after the debounced begin times; that choreography is stepped by a mounted FrameClock ticker at the engine's 16ms-per-frame convention (the UseAnimatedValue precedent), so it is deterministic on the headless FixedFrameTimeSource host. The auto-hiding OVERLAY scrollbar on scroll viewports is engine-drawn (SceneRecorder.EmitScrollbar) with its timing in Animation.ScrollAnimator — this control is the standalone (always-visible) ScrollBar element. public static class ScrollBar Inheritance object ScrollBar Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields ArrowFontSize public const float ArrowFontSize = 8 Field Value float ArrowScalePressed public const float ArrowScalePressed = 0.875 Field Value float CollapsedThumb public const float CollapsedThumb = 2 Field Value float ContractBeginMs public const float ContractBeginMs = 500 Field Value float ExpandBeginMs public const float ExpandBeginMs = 400 Field Value float ExpandMs public const float ExpandMs = 167 Field Value float ExpandedThumb public const float ExpandedThumb = 6 Field Value float FadeMs public const float FadeMs = 83 Field Value float LineDeltaPx public const float LineDeltaPx = 16 Field Value float MinThumbLength public const float MinThumbLength = 30 Field Value float PanningThumbMargin public const float PanningThumbMargin = 2 Field Value float PartDecreaseButton The LEADING arrow cell (WinUI VerticalSmallDecrease — glyph EDDB, scrolls up; horizontal HorizontalSmallDecrease — glyph EDD9, scrolls left). Owned: same set as PartIncreaseButton. public const string PartDecreaseButton = "sb-up" Field Value string PartIncreaseButton The TRAILING arrow cell (WinUI VerticalSmallIncrease — glyph EDDC, scrolls down; horizontal HorizontalSmallIncrease — glyph EDDA, scrolls right). Owned: Key, the bound Opacity (the chrome fade), Repeats + RepeatIntervalMs + OnClick (the small-change repeat step), OnHoverMove/OnPointerExit (the conscious hover lane). public const string PartIncreaseButton = "sb-down" Field Value string PartThumb The draggable thumb (WinUI VerticalThumb/HorizontalThumb). Owned (Anatomy): Key, the main-axis length + the cross-axis Width/HeightBind (the conscious-expand eased cross-axis size — bind-driven geometry, not style), TransformBind (the compositor-bound scroll position). On the legacy panning variant everything is style (drag lives on the rail, no binds). public const string PartThumb = "sb-thumb" Field Value string PartTrack The always-mounted acrylic track (WinUI Vertical/HorizontalTrackRect). Owned: Key, the bound Opacity (the 83ms chrome fade). public const string PartTrack = "sb-track" Field Value string RailSize public const float RailSize = 12 Field Value float RepeatDelayMs public const float RepeatDelayMs = 500 Field Value float RepeatIntervalMs public const float RepeatIntervalMs = 50 Field Value float StyleOverride public static ScrollBar.Style? StyleOverride Field Value ScrollBar.Style ThumbInset public const float ThumbInset = 3 Field Value float Properties DefaultStyle public static ScrollBar.Style DefaultStyle { get; } Property Value ScrollBar.Style Methods Anatomy(float, Signal<float>, Action<float>, float, bool, TemplateParts?, bool, float, float) The full WinUI mouse-scrollbar anatomy (see the class doc for cites). fraction = viewport/content (thumb proportion); position = offset/(content−viewport) in 0..1, read through a signal so writes move the thumb compositor-instantly (no re-render); onScroll receives the new position for every interaction: thumb drag (absolute), track-click paging (one VIEWPORT per page — LargeDecrement→IScrollInfo::PageUp = SetVerticalOffset(offset − viewport), ScrollViewer_Partial.cpp:3146-3150 / ScrollContentPresenter_Partial.cpp:543-547 — auto-repeating at the template's Interval=50 after the 500ms RepeatButton delay, stopping when the thumb reaches the pointer), and arrow small-change (one 16px LINE per step — ScrollViewerLineDelta, ScrollViewer_Partial.h:27). horizontal mirrors the anatomy on the X axis (HorizontalRoot, :672-693). largeChange01/smallChange01 override the page/line amounts in 0..1 position units (NaN = the WinUI-derived defaults above; pass 16/scrollableExtentPx for the exact line when the real content extent is known). parts = per-part styling keyed by the PartXxx consts (see TemplateParts for the contract). public static Element Anatomy(float fraction, Signal<float> position, Action<float> onScroll, float length = 200, bool disabled = false, TemplateParts? parts = null, bool horizontal = false, float largeChange01 = NaN, float smallChange01 = NaN) Parameters fraction float position Signal<float> onScroll Action<float> length float disabled bool parts TemplateParts horizontal bool largeChange01 float smallChange01 float Returns Element Anatomy(float, float, Action<float>, float, bool, TemplateParts?, bool, float, float) The full WinUI mouse-scrollbar anatomy with a FROZEN position (component props freeze at mount — use the Anatomy(float, Signal<float>, Action<float>, float, bool, TemplateParts?, bool, float, float) signal overload for a live, app-controlled position). public static Element Anatomy(float fraction, float position, Action<float> onScroll, float length = 200, bool disabled = false, TemplateParts? api/FluentGpu.Controls.SelectionModel.ht.title: Class SelectionModel | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.SelectionModel.ht.summary: Class SelectionModel Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll Index/RANGE-based selection state (E11-L3) — WinUI's SelectionModel flattened to one level (our items controls are flat projections; TreeView flattens before selecting). DECOUPLED FROM REALIZATION: selection is a sorted list of disjoint inclusive index ranges, so SelectAll() over 50k rows stores ONE range and realizes nothing — realized items read their state on prepare via IsSelected(int) (O(log ranges)). Signals-first: every mutation bumps Version; a composing control reads Version.Value in its render so a selection change re-renders exactly that control (which re-skins only its realized window). SelectionChanged fires once per mutating call that actually changed state (WinUI SelectionChanged). Pointer/keyboard semantics are the verified WinUI selector trio (controls\dev\ItemsView): OnInteractedAction(int, bool, bool) / OnFocusedAction(int, bool, bool) implement SingleSelector.cpp:25-57, MultipleSelector.cpp:18-92 and ExtendedSelector.cpp:18-83 — anchor+shift ranges, ctrl toggles, focus-follow. public sealed class SelectionModel Inheritance object SelectionModel Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields SelectionChanged Fired once per mutating call that changed the selection (WinUI SelectionModel.SelectionChanged). public Action? SelectionChanged Field Value Action Properties AnchorIndex The shift-range anchor (WinUI SelectionModel.AnchorIndex). −1 = none. Single item ops move it. public int AnchorIndex { get; set; } Property Value int FirstSelectedIndex public int FirstSelectedIndex { get; } Property Value int ItemCount The collection size selection indices are clamped to. Shrinking trims out-of-range selection. public int ItemCount { get; set; } Property Value int LastSelectedIndex public int LastSelectedIndex { get; } Property Value int Mode Selection mode. Coercing to Single keeps only the first selected item; None leaves existing (programmatic) selection readable but routes interactions to a null selector. public ItemsSelectionMode Mode { get; set; } Property Value ItemsSelectionMode RangeCount public int RangeCount { get; } Property Value int SelectedCount Total selected items (sum over ranges, O(ranges) — never walks indices). public int SelectedCount { get; } Property Value int SingleSelectionFollowsFocus In Single mode, moving keyboard focus without Ctrl selects the focused item (WinUI SingleSelector.h m_followFocus, default true). public bool SingleSelectionFollowsFocus { get; set; } Property Value bool Version Bumped on every actual change — read it in a render to subscribe that component (signals-first). public Signal<int> Version { get; } Property Value Signal<int> Methods ClearSelection() Alias for DeselectAll() (WinUI ClearSelection). public void ClearSelection() Deselect(int) public void Deselect(int index) Parameters index int DeselectAll() public void DeselectAll() DeselectRange(int, int) public void DeselectRange(int start, int end) Parameters start int end int DeselectRangeFromAnchorTo(int) WinUI DeselectRangeFromAnchorTo: deselect [anchor, index]; anchor stays. public void DeselectRangeFromAnchorTo(int index) Parameters index int GetRange(int) The i-th selected range (inclusive) — for tests/serialization without realizing indices. public (int Start, int End) GetRange(int i) Parameters i int Returns (int Start, int End) GetSelectedIndices(List<int>) Append every selected index into into (cleared first). The WinUI SelectedItems projection — call only when a materialized list is genuinely needed. public void GetSelectedIndices(List<int> into) Parameters into List<int> InvertSelection() Complement over [0, ItemCount): the gaps become the ranges — O(ranges), realizes nothing. public void InvertSelection() IsSelected(int) public bool IsSelected(int index) Parameters index int Returns bool OnFocusedAction(int, bool, bool) Keyboard focus moved to item index (arrows/Home/End/typeahead). public void OnFocusedAction(int index, bool ctrl, bool shift) Parameters index int ctrl bool shift bool OnInteractedAction(int, bool, bool) Pointer tap / Space / Enter on item index with the live modifier chord. public void OnInteractedAction(int index, bool ctrl, bool shift) Parameters index int ctrl bool shift bool RemapMove(int, int) Remap selected indices after a collection RemoveAt(from) + Insert(to) reorder, preserving selected ITEM identity rather than the old slot. Cold drop path. public void RemapMove(int from, int to) Parameters from int to int Select(int) Select one item. In Single mode this REPLACES the selection (WinUI SingleSelect). public void Select(int index) Parameters index int SelectAll() ONE stored range regardless of count — select-all over 50k realizes nothing. public void SelectAll() SelectRange(int, int) Select an inclusive index range (clamped). api/FluentGpu.Controls.SelectorBar.html.summary: Class SelectorBar Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI SelectorBar: a segmented horizontal row of text (optionally icon+text) items. The selected item is marked by a SHORT CENTERED accent pill flush with the item bottom (not a full-width underline, not bold); text stays TextPrimary at rest for every item and DIMS on hover/press — backgrounds are transparent in every state (SelectorBar_themeresources.xaml:21-25). Stateless — the caller owns selected and reacts to onSelect. Keyboard: ONE tab stop (the selected item — TabNavigation=Once, SelectorBar.xaml:13); Left/Right/ Home/End move focus item-to-item and SELECTION FOLLOWS FOCUS (SelectorBarTests.cs:63-66); focus entering a bar without a selection auto-selects (SelectorBar.cpp:98-126). Per-part restyling goes through the optional parts (see TemplateParts for the contract). public static class SelectorBar Inheritance object SelectorBar Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields PartItem Each item's clickable box (WinUI SelectorBarItem). api/FluentGpu.Controls.SelectorVisual.ht.title: Enum SelectorVisual | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.SelectorVisual.ht.summary: Enum SelectorVisual Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll The selector-VISUAL presets — the user-pickable item-chrome skins the premiere ItemsView exposes (the WinUI "ListView's accent bar" the user likes, GridView's corner check, ItemContainer's accent ring, …) as a single DATA enum so any layout × any selection mode can wear any selector (no WinUI-style capability cliffs): AccentPill — the WinUI ListViewItem SelectionIndicator (the 3×16 accent bar at the row's left edge over a subtle backplate); the ListView preset's default. Check — the WinUI GridViewItem dual-border + top-right corner check; the GridView preset's default. FullRow — a documented SUPERSET (no exact WinUI analogue): the AccentPill row plate WITHOUT the left pill, so the whole row fills with the accent-subtle selected highlight (full-bleed selected backplate). Border — the WinUI ItemContainer chrome (3px accent ring + 1px inner stroke + checkbox). None — no selection chrome at all (a bare recyclable container) for app-drawn selection. public enum SelectorVisual : byte Fields AccentPill = 0 Border = 3 Check = 1 FullRow = 2 None = 4

api/FluentGpu.Controls.SelectorVisuals.h.title: Class SelectorVisuals | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.SelectorVisuals.h.summary: Class SelectorVisuals Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll The SelectorVisual presets as ItemContainerFactory-shaped builders: each returns the WinUI item chrome for one preset around the engine's ONE selection + keyboard substrate, signature-identical to the ItemContainerFactory delegate (ItemsView.cs:52-54) so a preset is just ContainerFactory = SelectorVisuals.<Name>. Each builder is PLAIN/recyclable — a keyed BoxEl tree with NO Component / no reactive bind (TransformBind/OpacityBind/FillBind/WidthBind/HeightBind/OnRealized would break TreeReconciler.IsRecyclable, Reconciler.cs:748-749); Animate (a LayoutTransition) is allowed because it is declarative. Per-item chrome SKIN goes through the ContainerFactory/SelectorVisual seam; per-item VARIATION goes through the PartDelta value seam (fill/fg/opacity/corner/padding/glyph as values, applied during construction — shape-stable, 0-alloc, CI-enforced; docs/guide/control-fidelity.md §6). WinUI chrome values are cited inline; they are re-verified against the live ListViewBaseItemChrome.cpp metrics (s_selectionIndicatorSize {3,16}, s_selectionIndicatorHeightShrinkage 6, s_selectionIndicatorMargin.left 4, s_backplateMargin {4,2}, s_multiSelectRoundedSquareInlineMargin 14, s_multiSelectRoundedContentOffset 28, s_multiSelectSquareOverlayMargin {0,2,2,0}) and the per-state brush ramps in {ListView,GridView,ItemContainer}_themeresources.xaml. public static class SelectorVisuals Inheritance object SelectorVisuals Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString()

api/FluentGpu.Controls.Slider.html.title: Class Slider | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.Slider.html.summary: Class Slider Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI 3 Slider. Geometry (Slider_themeresources.xaml): a 4px rail (SliderTrackFill = ControlStrongFillColorDefault, radius 2) with an accent value-fill, and the Fluent thumb — a STATIC 22px ring (the 18px Thumb part + its Border's −2 margin, lines 169-172 + 198) of ControlSolidFillColorDefault with a 1px ControlElevationBorder, holding the only animated part: the 12px accent inner dot whose storyboards scale it 0.86 at rest / 1.167 on hover / 0.71 on press / 1.167 disabled (lines 204-254). Value-fill and inner dot recolor with the CONTROL-wide PointerOver/Pressed states (AccentFillColorDefault → Secondary → Tertiary; lines 14-17, 24-27). Create(float, Action<float>, float, float, Style?, bool, string?, TemplateParts?)/Bind(FloatSignal, Action<float>?, float, float, Style?, bool, string?, TemplateParts?) — the lightweight 0..1 scrub primitives (media seek/volume). Keyboard, focus visuals and the optional header are included; the value tooltip is not (see Ranged(float, Action<float>, Options, float, float, Style?, bool, TemplateParts?)). Ranged(float, Action<float>, Options, float, float, Style?, bool, TemplateParts?) — the full WinUI-parity control over Slider.Options: arbitrary range, step snapping, ticks (incl. inline), Header, SmallChange/LargeChange keyboard, and the IsThumbToolTipEnabled "disambiguation UI" value tooltip with a ThumbToolTipValueConverter-equivalent formatter. Keyboard (KeyPress::Slider::KeyDown, SliderKeyProcess.h:28-71): Left/Down decrease and Right/Up increase by SmallChange, Home/End jump to Minimum/Maximum; PageUp/PageDown move by LargeChange (not in XAML's KeyPress::Slider — kept per the parity contract, riding the same Slider::Step math, Slider_Partial.cpp:1713-1783). RTL note: WinUI inverts Left/Right by (FlowDirection==RightToLeft) ^ IsDirectionReversed (SliderKeyProcess.h:26); the engine has no FlowDirection yet — the LTR mapping ships now, the inversion lands with the Wave-6 RTL sweep. public static class Slider Inheritance object Slider Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields PartContainer The interactive full-size track surface (WinUI SliderContainer): the hit target carrying the scrub + keyboard (and, on Ranged(float, Action<float>, Options, float, float, Style?, bool, TemplateParts?), tooltip) mechanics. Owned: OnPointerDown, OnDrag, OnKeyDown, Role, Children — and on Ranged(float, Action<float>, Options, float, float, Style?, bool, TemplateParts?) also OnRealized (track ref, chained) plus the tooltip wiring (OnClick/OnHoverMove/OnPointerExit/OnFocusChanged) while the tooltip is enabled. public const string PartContainer = "Container" Field Value string PartInnerDot The 12px animated accent dot inside the thumb (WinUI SliderInnerThumb — the only animated thumb part). Owned: the storyboard scale ramp — ScaleX/ScaleY (the rest/disabled base), HoverScale, PressScale; colors, size and durations stay restylable. public const string PartInnerDot = "InnerDot" Field Value string PartRail The 4px resting rail (WinUI HorizontalTrackRect / VerticalTrackRect). Owned: nothing — pure styling (the hover/press fill pins are stock state styling a modifier may override). public const string PartRail = "Rail" Field Value string PartThumb The static 22px thumb ring (WinUI HorizontalThumb / VerticalThumb). Owned: Children (the inner dot), OnRealized (the Ranged tooltip anchor, chained), TransformBind on the Bind(FloatSignal, Action<float>?, float, float, Style?, bool, string?, TemplateParts?) path, and the value-position OffsetX/OffsetY on the vertical Ranged(float, Action<float>, Options, float, float, Style?, bool, TemplateParts?) path. public const string PartThumb = "Thumb" Field Value string PartTickBar Every tick bar on Ranged(float, Action<float>, Options, float, float, Style?, bool, TemplateParts?) (WinUI TopTickBar/BottomTickBar/HorizontalInlineTickBar — one part name, applied to every mounted instance). Owned: Children (the marks sit at value positions). public const string PartTickBar = "TickBar" Field Value string PartValueFill The accent value fill (WinUI HorizontalDecreaseRect / VerticalDecreaseRect). Owned: the value-position geometry — Width (horizontal) / Height+OffsetY (vertical) on the layout paths; Width+TransformBind on the signal-bound Bind(FloatSignal, Action<float>?, float, float, Style?, bool, string?, TemplateParts?) path (the grow-from-left math assumes the full track Width). public const string PartValueFill = "ValueFill" Field Value string StyleOverride public static Slider.Style? StyleOverride Field Value Slider.Style Properties DefaultStyle public static Slider.Style DefaultStyle { get; } Property Value Slider.Style Methods Bind(FloatSignal, Action<float>?, float, float, Style?, bool, string?, TemplateParts?) Signals-native slider (the "even better than React" path): the value is a FloatSignal bound straight to the value-fill's composited ScaleX and the thumb's composited OffsetX. A drag writes the signal, which updates exactly those two node transforms on the compositor fast path — zero render, zero reconcile, zero relayout per pointer-move (the slider-tank fix). onChange is invoked for side effects (e.g. a value readout that takes its own scoped re-render); pass null for a purely-visual scrub. Keyboard steps write the signal the same compositor-only way. No value tooltip: opening/observing one requires a re-render path, which would break this overload's zero-re-render contract — use Ranged(float, Action<float>, Options, float, float, Style?, bool, TemplateParts?) for the WinUI-parity surface. public static BoxEl Bind(FloatSignal value, Action<float>? onChange = null, float width = 200, float height = 24, Slider.Style? style = null, bool isEnabled = true, string? header = null, TemplateParts? parts = null) Parameters value FloatSignal onChange Action<float> width float height float style Slider.Style isEnabled bool header string parts TemplateParts Returns BoxEl Create(float, Action<float>, float, float, Style?, bool, string?, TemplateParts?) The minimal 0..1 controlled slider (media seek/volume). Press-to-seek + drag-to-scrub with the raw x/width mapping, full keyboard (SmallChange 0.01 / LargeChange 0.1 — WinUI's defaults 1/10 re-based from its 0–100 default range, Slider_Partial.h:13-15), focus ring at FocusVisualMargin −7,0,−7,0 (Slider_themeresources.xaml:184), optional header. No value tooltip (that lives on Ranged(float, Action<float>, Options, float, float, Style?, bool, TemplateParts?), the WinUI-parity surface). public static BoxEl Create(float value, Action<float> onChange, float width = 200, float height = 24, Slider.Style? style = null, bool isEnabled = true, string? header = null, TemplateParts? parts = null) Parameters value float onChange Action<float> width float height float style Slider.Style isEnabled bool header string parts TemplateParts Returns BoxEl FormatThumbValue(float, float) The default thumb-tooltip text — DefaultDisambiguationUIConverter (Slider_Partial.cpp:1859-1936): the number of decimal places follows StepFrequency's mantissa, capped at 4; the value is rounded to that digit. A continuous slider (step ≤ 0) formats like WinUI's default StepFrequency = 1 → no decimals. public static string FormatThumbValue(float value, float step) Parameters value float step float Returns string Ranged(float, Action<float>, Options, float, float, Style?, bool, TemplateParts?) A slider over an arbitrary Min..Max range, with step snapping, tick marks (incl. inline), vertical orientation, Header, the full WinUI keyboard map and the IsThumbToolTipEnabled value tooltip. value is in range units; length is the track length (px) and thickness the cross size (WinUI SliderHorizontalHeight/SliderVerticalWidth = 32, Slider_themeresources.xaml:167-168). Controlled: the caller owns the value. Internally a stateful component (the tooltip's overlay lifetime spans re-renders); props are pushed through a context provider — the engine's sanctioned prop channel for embedded components. public static BoxEl Ranged(float value, Action<float> onChange, Slider.Options o, float length = 220, float thickness = 32, Slider.Style? style = null, bool isEnabled = true, TemplateParts? parts = null) Parameters value float onChange Action<float> o Slider.Options length float thickness float style Slider.Style isEnabled bool parts TemplateParts Returns BoxEl

api/FluentGpu.Controls.SplitView.html.summary:
Class SplitView Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI SplitView: a side pane and a flexible content area, composed per SplitViewDisplayMode × PanePlacement × IsPaneOpen (the WinUI visual-state table, SplitView_Partial.cpp:34-59):
  • Overlay (the WinUI default) — the open pane floats over the content without reflow (PaneRoot Canvas.ZIndex=1 over a ColumnSpan=2 ContentRoot, SplitView_themeresources.xaml:700, :718) and light-dismisses on content click / Escape / a host resize, with the WinUI 0.35s/0.12s slide.
  • Inline — the open pane reserves paneWidth of layout; the content pre-slides in sync (ContentTransform, SplitView_themeresources.xaml:345-348) over the 0.2s/0.1s inline curves.
  • CompactOverlay / CompactInline — closed leaves a compactPaneLength (48) rail: the SAME pane, clipped to the rail (a clip-reveal, never a resize — core SplitView.cpp:343-377).
State is caller-owned (isPaneOpen is a prop, the WinUI IsPaneOpen DP): wire setIsPaneOpen so light dismiss can write the close back. PaneOpening/PaneOpened/ PaneClosing(cancelable on light dismiss)/PaneClosed mirror the WinUI events (XCPTypesAutoGen SplitView.cs:82-96). public static class SplitView Inheritance object SplitView Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields PartContent The flexible content area (WinUI ContentRoot). Owned: Animate (the inline content pre-slide), Children (the content slot). public const string PartContent = "Content" Field Value string PartPane The pane surface (WinUI PaneRoot's inner Border) — mounted while the pane is open OR while a compact rail shows. Owned: Width (always the full OpenPaneLength — the compact rail CLIPS it), OnRealized (the focus-contract handle capture, chained), Children (the pane content slot). public const string PartPane = "Pane" Field Value string Methods Create(Element, Element, float, bool, SplitViewDisplayMode, SplitViewPanePlacement, float, Action?, Action?, Action?, Action?, Action?, TemplateParts?) public static Element Create(Element pane, Element content, float paneWidth = 320, bool isPaneOpen = false, SplitViewDisplayMode displayMode = SplitViewDisplayMode.Overlay, SplitViewPanePlacement panePlacement = SplitViewPanePlacement.Left, float compactPaneLength = 48, Action? setIsPaneOpen = null, Action? onPaneOpening = null, Action? onPaneOpened = null, Action? onPaneClosing = null, Action? onPaneClosed = null, TemplateParts? parts = null) Parameters pane Element The pane content (WinUI SplitView.Pane). content Element The main content (WinUI SplitView.Content). paneWidth float WinUI OpenPaneLength — default 320 (SplitViewOpenPaneThemeLength, SplitView_themeresources.xaml:5). isPaneOpen bool WinUI IsPaneOpen — CLR default false (XCPTypesAutoGen SplitView.cs:58-60; PaneRoot starts Collapsed, SplitView_themeresources.xaml:700). displayMode SplitViewDisplayMode Default Overlay (the WinUI DP default — SplitView_Partial.cpp:136, :446). panePlacement SplitViewPanePlacement compactPaneLength float WinUI CompactPaneLength — default 48 (SplitViewCompactPaneThemeLength, SplitView_themeresources.xaml:6). setIsPaneOpen Action Write-back for the caller-owned open state — light dismiss (content click, Escape, host resize) closes through this. Null ⇒ light dismiss is inert (state cannot be flipped). onPaneOpening Action onPaneOpened Action onPaneClosing Action Cancelable on light dismiss only; an app-initiated close (the caller flipping isPaneOpen) raises it but ignores Cancel (core SplitView.cpp:432-456). onPaneClosed Action parts TemplateParts Returns Element

api/FluentGpu.Controls.StateBrush.html.title: Struct StateBrush | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.StateBrush.html.summary: Struct StateBrush Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll Reusable, WinUI-accurate discrete-state motion presets for control sub-elements — the checkmarks, dots and glyphs that appear/disappear on a logical state flip (checked, selected, expanded…). These ride the engine's existing enter/exit lifecycle: attach one as Animate on a node the reconciler INSERTS on enter and REMOVES on exit, and it scales+fades in (on insert) / out (orphaned, then reclaimed when the spring settles, on removal). The motion is property-level and signals-native — the state IS the element's presence and the animation falls out of the reconcile diff — so there is no per-control animation code and no VisualStateManager: the opposite of WinUI's stringly-typed GoToState storyboards, and a strict superset in composability. Springs (velocity-carrying, interruptible) are the default; durations map to the Fluent motion tokens. public readonly record struct StateBrush : IEquatable<StateBrush> Implements IEquatable<StateBrush> Inherited Members ValueType.Equals(object) ValueType.GetHashCode() ValueType.ToString() object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Constructors StateBrush(ColorF, ColorF, ColorF, ColorF) Reusable, WinUI-accurate discrete-state motion presets for control sub-elements — the checkmarks, dots and glyphs that appear/disappear on a logical state flip (checked, selected, expanded…). The motion is property-level and signals-native — the state IS the element's presence and the animation falls out of the reconcile diff — so there is no per-control animation code and no VisualStateManager: the opposite of WinUI's stringly-typed GoToState storyboards, and a strict superset in composability. Springs (velocity-carrying, interruptible) are the default; durations map to the Fluent motion tokens. public StateBrush(ColorF Rest, ColorF Hover, ColorF Pressed, ColorF Disabled) Parameters Rest ColorF Hover ColorF Pressed ColorF Disabled ColorF Properties Disabled public ColorF Disabled { get; init; } Property Value ColorF Hover public ColorF Hover { get; init; } Property Value ColorF Pressed public ColorF Pressed { get; init; } Property Value ColorF Rest public ColorF Rest { get; init; } Property Value ColorF Methods Flat(ColorF) A flat ramp (all four the same) — for a property that doesn't react to interaction. public static StateBrush Flat(ColorF c) Parameters c ColorF Returns StateBrush Resting(bool) The resting value for the current enablement (the hover/pressed legs are eased by the engine). public ColorF Resting(bool enabled) Parameters enabled bool Returns ColorF

api/FluentGpu.Controls.SwipeControl.html.title: Class SwipeControl | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.SwipeControl.html.summary: Class SwipeControl Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI SwipeControl (controls\dev\SwipeControl): drag the content sideways to reveal action items underneath. The content pans with the pointer; releasing past min(items width, 100px) rests it open (c_ThresholdValue — SwipeControl.cpp:21, ConfigurePositionInertiaRestingValues :937-1005), otherwise it springs closed; an already-open side closes on ANY inward movement (UpdateThresholdReached, :1626-1645). Items are invokable (Invoked + BehaviorOnInvoked); Execute mode stretches one item across the control and auto-invokes it on open. Esc and a tap on the content dismiss an open swipe (AttachDismissingHandlers, SwipeControl.h:74-76). Horizontal (Left/Right) items only — WinUI's vertical Top/Bottom axis is not offered on this surface, so its "both axes populated" rejection (ThrowIfHasVerticalAndHorizontalContent, SwipeControl.cpp:1648-1676) cannot arise. Deliberate superset: WinUI only swipes on touch/pen (VisualInteractionSource); ours pans with the mouse too. public static class SwipeControl Inheritance object SwipeControl Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods Create(Element, IReadOnlyList<SwipeAction>?, IReadOnlyList<SwipeAction>?, SwipeMode, SwipeMode) Arbitrary content with leading (left) and/or trailing (right) action lists (WinUI LeftItems/RightItems — SwipeControl.idl:48-51); the drag direction picks the side. public static Element Create(Element content, IReadOnlyList<SwipeAction>? leftActions = null, IReadOnlyList<SwipeAction>? rightActions = null, SwipeMode leftMode = SwipeMode.Reveal, SwipeMode rightMode = SwipeMode.Reveal) Parameters content Element leftActions IReadOnlyList<SwipeAction> rightActions IReadOnlyList<SwipeAction> leftMode SwipeMode rightMode SwipeMode Returns Element Create(string, IReadOnlyList<SwipeAction>, SwipeMode) String-content convenience (the gallery surface): a card-filled text cell with TRAILING (right) actions. api/FluentGpu.Controls.TabView.html.title: Class TabView | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.TabView.html.summary: Class TabView Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI TabView (controls\dev\TabView): a horizontal strip of tab headers atop the selected tab's content. The control OWNS its live tab collection (the WinUI TabItems vector, TabView.idl:135), seeded once from Items/Tabs at mount: the close button / middle-click / Ctrl+F4 remove the tab (after raising OnTabCloseRequested) with WinUI's reselect-at-the-removed-index rule (TabView.cpp:786-812); the add button appends what OnAddTabButtonClick returns; pointer drag reorders tabs (CanReorderTabs, default true). Equal width mode clamps tabs to 100–240px of the measured strip; overflowing tabs scroll behind 32×24 repeat scroll buttons (±50px, disabled at the extremes) and the selected tab is auto-scrolled into view. The selected header uses the SolidBackgroundFillColorTertiary surface and fuses flush with the content area (the strip's 1px CardStroke baseline is per-tab and omitted under the selected header). Per-part restyling goes through Parts (see TemplateParts for the contract). public sealed class TabView : Component Inheritance object Component TabView Inherited Members Component.Context Component.RunsOnce Component.RenderWithHooks() Component.Unmount() object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods MotionHooks.UseEntrance(Component, float, object?) Null handler = the button clicks to nothing, like an unhandled WinUI event. public Func<TabViewItem?>? api/FluentGpu.Controls.TeachingTip.html.title: Class TeachingTip | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.TeachingTip.html.summary: Class TeachingTip Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll WinUI TeachingTip (controls/dev/TeachingTip/TeachingTip.xaml + _themeresources + .cpp/.idl): an anchored (targeted) or window-relative (untargeted) non-modal callout that teaches/highlights a feature. The body is a SOLID surface — SolidBackgroundFillColorTertiary background, SurfaceStrokeColorDefault 1px border, OverlayCornerRadius (8), an elevation shadow, and the 1px TeachingTipTopHighlightBrush top highlight (#0DFFFFFF dark / #99FFFFFF light, TeachingTip_themeresources.xaml:6/:28) — with (top→bottom) an optional hero image, an icon + title + subtitle row, the main content, and a footer button panel (Action + Close). A targeted tip grows a 16×8 tail/beak that points at the target; an untargeted tip has no tail. NOT light-dismiss by default (IsLightDismissEnabled=false): only the close button, Escape, or a programmatic close dismisses it. The FULL 13-placement matrix (TeachingTip.xaml PlacementStates :115-264) is honored — Top/Bottom/Left/Right, the 8 corner variants, and Center — each mapping to the matching FlyoutPositioner placement with the tail pinned to the joined edge per the state's alignment setters (e.g. TopRight → tail at the LEFT of the bottom edge, :160-170). PlacementMargin nudges the tip away from the target (TeachingTip.cpp:480/:588 offset); ShouldConstrainToRootBounds (default true, the WinUI idl default) opts the popup out of the window bounds via the E4 windowed-popup seam when false. 1:1 tokens/sizes (TeachingTip_themeresources.xaml, unitless = epx): • MinWidth 320, MaxWidth 336, MinHeight 40, MaxHeight 520 (TeachingTipMin/MaxWidth/Height). • Content margin 12 on all sides (TeachingTipContentMargin); button panel top margin 0,12,0,0 (TeachingTipButtonPanelMargin); both-buttons split — Action 0,12,4,0 (Left), Close 4,12,0,0 (Right). • MainContent present-margin 0,12,0,0 (gap below the title block when content exists); icon→title gap 12 (TeachingTipIconPresenterMarginWithIcon); title-stack right margin 28 when the header (alternate) close button shows. • Title FontSize 16 SemiBold; Subtitle FontSize 14; body content FontSize 14 (Foreground = TextPrimary). • Alternate (header) close button: 40×40 (TeachingTipAlternateCloseButtonSize), E711 glyph at 16px, SubtleFill ramp, ControlCornerRadius. The footer ActionButton's DEFAULT style is the STANDARD button (TeachingTip.xaml:10 ActionButtonStyle = DefaultButtonStyle) — set ActionButtonIsAccent for the AccentButtonStyle the gallery samples use. Open/close motion (host-driven, PopupChrome.TeachingTip): expand scale → 1 over 300ms cubic-bezier(0.1,0.9,0.2,1); contract scale → 20/Width over 200ms cubic-bezier(0.7,0,1,0.5) (TeachingTip.cpp:1660-1712; .h:234-235 + :304-307 control points) — both at full opacity (the storyboards carry no opacity keyframes). public sealed class TeachingTip : Component Inheritance object Component TeachingTip Inherited Members Component.Context Component.RunsOnce Component.RenderWithHooks() Component.Unmount() object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods MotionHooks.UseEntrance(Component, float, object?) When empty, the MainContentPresenter collapses (NoContent VSM). public string Body Field Value string CloseButtonClick public Action? CloseButtonClick Field Value Action CloseButtonContent WinUI CloseButtonContent — the standard footer close button. Empty = the close moves to the 40×40 header (alternate) close button (WinUI HeaderCloseButton VSM); set it to show a labelled footer close instead. public string CloseButtonContent Field Value string Closed public Action<TeachingTip.CloseReason>? When false the tip is untargeted (no beak unless TailVisibility forces one) and is placed against the WINDOW edge — WinUI PositionUntargetedPopup: default Bottom of the window, UntargetedEdgeMargin = 24px from the edges, PreferredPlacement picking the corner/edge (TeachingTip.cpp:571-668, :2074-2090). public bool HasTarget Field Value bool HeroContent Arbitrary hero content slot (overrides HeroImage when set) — WinUI HeroContent is a UIElement, not just an image. public Element? HeroContent Field Value Element HeroContentPlacement public TeachingTip.HeroPlacement HeroContentPlacement Field Value TeachingTip.HeroPlacement HeroImage Hero image source (WinUI HeroContent): a full-bleed banner pinned to the top or bottom edge. public string HeroImage Field Value string IconGlyph Glyph icon (Segoe Fluent Icons codepoint) shown left of the title (WinUI IconSource). Empty = none. public string IconGlyph Field Value string IsLightDismissEnabled WinUI IsLightDismissEnabled (default false). When true, clicking outside dismisses the tip (and the surface swaps to the transient acrylic background — the LightDismiss VSM, TeachingTip.xaml:19-26). public bool IsLightDismissEnabled Field Value bool IsOpen WinUI Boolean IsOpen (TeachingTip.idl, MUX_DEFAULT_VALUE false) as a CONTROLLED two-way signal: when set, writes from anywhere open/close the tip with the full expand/contract motion, and EVERY close path (close button, light dismiss, Escape, programmatic) writes false back — WinUI's OnCloseButtonClicked and its light-dismiss handler both end in IsOpen(false) (TeachingTip.cpp:1271-1276, :1382-1388). A cancelled Closing restores true (the WinUI deferral revert). Null = open via the trigger/OpenOnMount only. public Signal<bool>? Opened Field Value Action PartBubble The solid callout card (WinUI ContentRootGrid): fill, border, corners, shadow, min/max sizes… The tail/beak and the 1px top highlight are composed AGAINST the card OUTSIDE this part (tail mechanics stay control-owned — restyling the card fill does not recolor the beak). api/FluentGpu.Controls.TitleBar.html.summary: Class TitleBar Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll The WinUI 3 TitleBar control (WinAppSDK 1.7, microsoft-ui-xaml controls\dev\TitleBar) over a custom frame (CustomFrame): back + pane-toggle buttons (40w, 16px glyphs), a 16×16 app-identity icon, 12px Caption title/subtitle, a centered content column (the gallery's AutoSuggestBox), and — unlike WinUI, which reserves space for SHELL-drawn caption buttons — three ENGINE-drawn min/max/close CaptionButtons (46w, full bar height, close = shell red) so the whole bar is one GPU-rendered surface. api/FluentGpu.Controls.ToggleButton.html.summary: Class ToggleButton Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A two/three-state toggle (shuffle/repeat/follow). Accent-filled when on — WinUI ToggleButton's checked state (AccentFillColorDefault bg + TextOnAccent fg + AccentControlElevationBorder); the unchecked AND indeterminate states are a standard control fill. Controlled: pass the state and a toggle/cycle callback. Style source: controls\dev\CommonStyles\ToggleButton_themeresources.xaml (Default = dark :4-61, Light :120-177; DefaultToggleButtonStyle :180-363). The full WinUI matrix is wired: {Unchecked, Checked, Indeterminate} × {Normal, PointerOver, Pressed, Disabled} for fill, foreground and border, verified in both themes. State flips (checked↔unchecked) cross-fade over the WinUI BrushTransition 83ms (ContentPresenter.BackgroundTransition, ToggleButton_themeresources.xaml:199-201) — the E3 primitive. api/FluentGpu.Controls.ToggleSplitButton.title: Class ToggleSplitButton | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.ToggleSplitButton.summary: Class ToggleSplitButton Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI ToggleSplitButton (SplitButton + checked state). Like SplitButton it is one joined chrome with two independently-clickable halves — a primary half on the left and a dropdown half on the right separated by a 1px divider — but the primary half toggles on/off instead of running a one-shot action: clicking it (or Enter/Space activation) flips IsChecked and raises OnToggle; the dropdown half opens a MenuFlyout. WinUI derives ToggleSplitButton from SplitButton and they share one ControlTemplate; the checked visual-states are driven by InternalIsChecked() (SplitButton.cpp UpdateVisualStates) — so this control is the SplitButton chrome with the accent Checked* token ramps folded in. When checked, both halves fill accent (SplitButtonBackgroundChecked = AccentFillColorDefault), the foreground is TextOnAccent, the outer border is AccentControlElevationBorder, and the inner divider switches to the on-accent divider stroke. Open/close goes through the overlay popup service (Service): re-click toggles, Escape / click-outside light-dismiss, focus captured on open + restored on close (host-wired), FocusTrap roves the menu, and ExpandCollapse Collapsed↔Expanded is raised by the overlay lifecycle. Keyboard: Enter/Space toggle the primary (the engine's focused-clickable activation), Down opens the flyout. IsEnabled gates all interaction via the engine and folds the chrome to the disabled tokens. The checked state is a caller Signal<T> so a page can read/display it; pass OnToggle to observe flips. public sealed class ToggleSplitButton : Component Inheritance object Component ToggleSplitButton Inherited Members Component.Context Component.RunsOnce Component.RenderWithHooks() Component.Unmount() object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods MotionHooks.UseEntrance(Component, float, object?) api/FluentGpu.Controls.ToggleSwitch.html.summary: Class ToggleSwitch Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI ToggleSwitch — geometry/colors/motion from the shipped lifted style (microsoft-ui-xaml controls\dev\CommonStyles\ToggleSwitch_themeresources.xaml, "the template"): a 40×20 pill track (OuterBorder/SwitchKnobBounds, template:507-508) + a knob inside a 20×20 positioning host (SwitchKnob grid, template:509) that translates 0→20 on toggle (KnobTranslateTransform To=20, template:445). The knob is 12×12 at rest (template:510/515 + Normal storyboard :231-242), grows to 14×14 on hover (:268-279), and STRETCHES to 17×14 pinned 3px from the near edge while pressed (size :311-322; the Pressed VisualState.Setters pin SwitchKnobOn right / SwitchKnobOff left with a 3px margin, :284-287). All knob size/anchor changes tween 83ms ControlFasterAnimationDuration with the ControlFastOutSlowInKeySpline (0,0,0,1) (Common_themeresources_any.xaml:602/606 — FluentPopOpen); the disabled return is 250ms ControlNormalAnimationDuration (:357-368); knob travel is WinUI's RepositionThemeAnimation (:418-439), mapped per the repo motion canon to ControlFast = 167ms (Dsl/Motion.cs — the PVL reposition timing is system data, not in the XAML). Behavior follows dxaml\xcp\dxaml\lib\ToggleSwitch_Partial.cpp: tap toggles (TapHandler :874-905); DRAG-TO-TOGGLE — the knob follows the pointer's horizontal delta clamped to the travel (DragDeltaHandler/MoveDelta :823-841/:579-589, SetTranslations clamp :455-457) and on release toggles iff the knob crossed half the travel range (MoveCompleted :591-619); pointer loss mid-drag cleans up without toggling (OnPointerCaptureLost :728-746). Space toggles via the engine's Space-on-keyup activation → OnClick (HandlesKey = Space/GamepadA only, :1002-1007); arrow/Home/End keys toggle directionally (ToggleSwitchKeyProcess.h:52-71 — WinUI acts on KeyUp; the engine routes OnKeyDown, a known timing-only deviation). Controlled — the caller owns isOn. public static class ToggleSwitch Inheritance object ToggleSwitch Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields DragThresholdPx Horizontal slop before a press becomes a knob drag — the Win32 drag box (SM_CXDRAG = 4px), the same base threshold Input.DragController uses (ListViewBaseItem_Partial.cpp:1871-1877). public const float DragThresholdPx = 4 Field Value float PartContentLabel The On/OffContent label (WinUI Off/OnContentPresenter) — a TextEl part (Parts.Set<TextEl>(…)). api/FluentGpu.Controls.ToolTip.html.title: Class ToolTip | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.ToolTip.html.summary: Class ToolTip Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI ToolTip (framework ToolTip + ToolTipService): wraps a target element and surfaces a small text bubble anchored above it (or at the pointer in Mouse). 1:1 with the framework's automatic-tooltip behavior: Hover trigger — pointer-over the target opens the bubble after the initial show delay (SPI_GETMOUSEHOVERTIME 400ms × 2 = 800ms; a re-show within BetweenShowDelayMs = 200ms uses the reshow delay — see MouseReshowDelayMs for why that is 400ms, not the spec'd 600ms). Pointer-leave cancels a PENDING open immediately (ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:1435-1442); an OPEN bubble is kept and closed by the safe-zone monitor instead — see the safe-zone item below. Safe zone — an open bubble does NOT close the instant the pointer leaves the target: WinUI keeps it while the pointer is inside owner ∪ tooltip (∪ their convex hull) and a 1s check timer closes it once outside (ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:1433-1453 owner-exit only records the owner; cpp:349-381 OnSafeZoneCheck; cpp:1060-1098 IsToolTipInSafeZone; .h:22 s_safeZoneCheckTimerDuration = 1s). The engine surfaces no global pointer position, so the hull is approximated by the same 1s cadence: leaving owner ∪ bubble arms a SafeZoneCheckMs grace; re-entering either cancels it; the grace elapsing closes the bubble. Keyboard trigger — KEYBOARD focus landing inside the target opens the bubble after 800ms (×2, normal AND reshow: ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:1777-1779), exactly WinUI's OnOwnerGotFocus gate (ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:1648-1664: only FocusState::Keyboard shows the tooltip — pointer focus does not). Focus leaving closes it (cpp:1696-1706 OnOwnerLostFocus → OnOwnerLeaveInternal). Press dismiss — a pointer press over the target closes an open (or pending) bubble, and it stays closed until the pointer LEAVES and re-enters (classic tooltip behavior; WinUI 3's ToolTipService itself registers no PointerPressed handler — it closes via safe-zone exit monitoring, ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:1437-1453 — but cannot re-open a dismissed owner until a real leave + re-enter either, cpp:725-737 CancelAutomaticToolTip). Auto-dismiss — an open bubble closes itself after ShowDurationMs (SPI_GETMESSAGEDURATION default 5s), and the dismissal LATCHES like press dismiss: WinUI's only show trigger is PointerEntered (ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:1395-1418), so in-place hover moves can never re-open a timed-out tooltip — it takes a real leave + re-enter. No click trigger — the wrapper adds NO OnClick (ToolTipService registers none, ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:176-220), so a tooltipped element is never a tab stop by itself. Chrome matches ToolTip_themeresources.xaml: AcrylicInAppFillColorDefault fill, 1px SurfaceStrokeColorFlyout stroke, ControlCornerRadius (4px) corners, the light tooltip elevation (Tooltip), 9,6,9,8 padding, 12px TextFillColorPrimary text, MaxWidth 320, TextWrapping=Wrap. Open/close are the WinUI FadeIn/FadeOutThemeAnimation fades (167ms — Raw in the overlay host). The bubble does NOT trap focus and does NOT light-dismiss on outside click (None) — dismissal is owned by hover-leave/focus-leave/press + the auto-dismiss timer, exactly like ToolTipService. public sealed class ToolTip : Component Inheritance object Component ToolTip Inherited Members Component.Context Component.RunsOnce Component.RenderWithHooks() Component.Unmount() object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods MotionHooks.UseEntrance(Component, float, object?) MotionHooks.UseHoverScale(Component, bool, float) Fields BetweenShowDelayMs BETWEEN_SHOW_DELAY_MS = 200ms (ToolTipService_Partial.h:17) — a re-open inside this window of the last close uses the reshow delay (cpp:659 GetTickCount() - s_lastToolTipOpenedTime < BETWEEN_SHOW_DELAY_MS). public const float BetweenShowDelayMs = 200 Field Value float KeyboardPlacementOffset DEFAULT_KEYBOARD_OFFSET = 12 (ToolTip_Partial.h:10) — the dock-rect inflation when the tooltip was opened by keyboard focus. public const float KeyboardPlacementOffset = 12 Field Value float KeyboardShowDelayMs Keyboard show delay: hover time × 2 = 800ms for BOTH normal and reshow (ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:1777-1779 — Keyboard ignores isReshow). public const float KeyboardShowDelayMs = 800 Field Value float MouseHoverTimeMs SPI_GETMOUSEHOVERTIME default (DEFAULT_SPI_GETMOUSEHOVERTIME, ToolTipService_Partial.h:18) = 400ms. public const float MouseHoverTimeMs = 400 Field Value float MousePlacementOffset DEFAULT_MOUSE_OFFSET = 20 (ToolTip_Partial.h:11): target-mode placement inflates the dock rect by this on BOTH axes for a mouse-opened automatic tooltip before positioning (ToolTip_Partial.cpp:1224-1258 InflateRect(&rcDockTo, horizontalOffset, verticalOffset) + :1275). public const float MousePlacementOffset = 20 Field Value float MousePlacementVerticalOffset Pointer-mode vertical offset below the cursor — ToolTip_Partial.h:56 m_mousePlacementVerticalOffset = 11 (the brief's "14px" did not survive source verification). public const float MousePlacementVerticalOffset = 11 Field Value float MouseReshowDelayMs Mouse RE-show delay = 400ms (× 1), NOT the spec-comment's × 1.5 = 600ms: the shipping code is ticks *= static_cast<INT64>(isReshow ? 1.5 : 2) (ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:1775) and the C++ static_cast<INT64>(1.5) TRUNCATES to 1 — behavior parity follows the compiled code, not the comment table at cpp:1737-1742. public const float MouseReshowDelayMs = 400 Field Value float MouseShowDelayMs Mouse initial show delay: hover time × 2 = 800ms (GetInitialShowDelay, Mouse, first show — ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:1774-1775). public const float MouseShowDelayMs = 800 Field Value float OpenOnMount public bool OpenOnMount Field Value bool PartBubble The text bubble surface (the control-built chrome inside the raw overlay host — acrylic fill, flyout stroke, 4px corners, tooltip elevation, 9,6,9,8 padding). api/FluentGpu.Controls.Tween.html.title: Class Tween | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.Tween.html.summary: Class Tween Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll The typed computed-"TemplateSettings" convention — the antidote to WinUI's stringly TemplateSettings bound into storyboards. A control declares a local readonly record struct <Control>TemplateSettings(...) of typed values (rotations, heights, clip rects, open/close animation positions), computes it ONCE per state change (a pure factory called in render, or a UseMemo/OnRealized effect that reads the control's own signals), and feeds the fields into the engine's existing channels: static props, TransformBind/OpacityBind/WidthBind/ HeightBind, AnimEngine keyframe seeds, or the clip-rect channel (AnimChannel.ClipL/T/R/B). Never rebuild the record inside a hot bind/effect body — compute it in the memo/factory. ExpanderTemplateSettings is the reference; the same shape serves CommandBarFlyout, NavigationView, Progress*, TeachingTip, TabView, TreeView, … These small helpers are the shared interpolation math those records use. public static class Tween Inheritance object Tween Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods Clamp01(float) public static float Clamp01(float v) Parameters v float Returns float Lerp(float, float, float) public static float Lerp(float a, float b, float t) Parameters a float b float t float Returns float SmoothStep(float) public static float SmoothStep(float t) Parameters t float Returns float

api/FluentGpu.Controls.VariableSizedWrap.title: Class VariableSizedWrapGrid | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.VariableSizedWrap.summary: Class VariableSizedWrapGrid Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI VariableSizedWrapGrid: a grid that wraps tiles of varying sizes. This is a self-contained, non-virtualized demo — fixed-base cell tiles where some span multiple cells. Tiles are greedily packed left-to-right into rows that hold columns cells (by ColSpan sum); a tile that would overflow the current row starts a new one. Cells are contiguous (no inter-cell gap) — an N-span tile is exactly N*cell — matching the WinUI base panel, which is an unstyled layout host (no per-tile chrome). A faint fill is kept only for visibility. public static class VariableSizedWrapGrid Inheritance object VariableSizedWrapGrid Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods Create(IReadOnlyList<WrapTile>, float, int) public static BoxEl Create(IReadOnlyList<WrapTile> tiles, float cell = 60, int columns = 4) Parameters tiles IReadOnlyList<WrapTile> cell float columns int Returns BoxEl

api/FluentGpu.Controls.Viewbox.html.title: Class Viewbox | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Controls.Viewbox.html.summary: Class Viewbox Namespace FluentGpu.Controls Assembly FluentGpu.Controls.dll A WinUI Viewbox: scales its single child by a composited transform (no relayout). WinUI computes the scale from a layout measure pass; this engine has no measure-override seam on BoxEl, so the scale is supplied either as an explicit factor (the scale overload) or computed from a known content size and target box via ViewboxStretch/ViewboxStretchDirection logic (the sized overload). The child is wrapped in a centered BoxEl carrying a uniform ScaleX/ScaleY. public static class Viewbox Inheritance object Viewbox Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods Create(Element, float) Explicit-factor Viewbox (no layout measure): scales the child uniformly by scale×. public static BoxEl Create(Element child, float scale = 1.5) Parameters child Element scale float Returns BoxEl Create(Element, float, float, float, float, ViewboxStretch, ViewboxStretchDirection) WinUI-faithful Viewbox: computes the scale from the child's natural size and a target box, applying stretch and stretchDirection. (Sizes are caller-supplied because the engine exposes no measure-override seam — see the design note in the type summary.) public static BoxEl Create(Element child, float contentWidth, float contentHeight, float availableWidth, float availableHeight, ViewboxStretch stretch = ViewboxStretch.Uniform, ViewboxStretchDirection stretchDirection = ViewboxStretchDirection.Both) Parameters child Element contentWidth float contentHeight float availableWidth float availableHeight float stretch ViewboxStretch stretchDirection ViewboxStretchDirection Returns BoxEl
