Route A serializable navigation destination — a route name + an optional argument (e.g. a playlist id). ScrollBar WinUI ScrollBar. Two surfaces: Create(float, float, Action, float, Style?, bool, TemplateParts?) — the legacy thin PANNING-indicator variant (thumb-only, absolute drag), kept source/behavior-compatible (VerticalSlice check 48 and the gallery drive it); it mirrors the template's VerticalPanningThumb (ScrollBar_themeresources.xaml:714 — Width 2, MinHeight 32, Margin 2,0,2,0, NO hover/press states — the touch indicator). Anatomy(float, float, Action, float, bool, TemplateParts?, bool, float, float) — the full WinUI mouse scrollbar: 12px rail (ScrollBarSize :180), acrylic track (ScrollBarTrackFill = AcrylicInAppFillColorDefaultBrush, :31/:143 both themes), two arrow RepeatButtons (vertical glyphs EDDB up / EDDC down, horizontal EDD9 left / EDDA right, FontSize 8 — :387/:344/:301/:258/:186; pressed arrow scale 0.875 :187; Interval=50 like every template RepeatButton :681-711), track-click PAGE zones (LargeDecrease/LargeIncrease RepeatButtons :704/:710 — first page on press, then 500ms delay + 50ms repeat re-evaluated against the live thumb), and the conscious expand/collapse: collapsed 2px visible thumb ↔ expanded 6px (the 8px/12px thumb minus the 6px transparent stroke trick — ScrollBarVerticalThumbMinWidth 8 :182, ScrollBarThumbStrokeThickness 6 :185, ScrollBarSize 12 :180; both states leave the FILL's far edge inset 3px from the lane edge: collapsed rect [4,12] / expanded [0,12], fill inset 3 per side → [7,9] / [3,9]). Expand begins 400ms after lane hover (ScrollBarExpandBeginTime :188) / collapse begins 500ms after leave (ScrollBarContractBeginTime :189), both 167ms with KeySpline 0,0,0,1 (:587/:543 → FluentPopOpen); track + arrows fade 83ms LINEAR (ScrollBarOpacityChangeDuration :174, plain DoubleAnimation :575-584) after the same begin times. While the thumb is DRAGGED a lane exit does NOT start the contract dwell (WinUI OnPointerExited skips UpdateVisualState while IsDragging, ScrollBar_Partial.cpp:548-561) — the leave replays at the drag's release edge. The arrow CELLS are ALWAYS in the grid (rows 0/4, fixed extent = ScrollBarSize :703/:711) — only their OPACITY animates, so the track length and thumb geometry never jump on hover. Thumb fill = ControlStrongFillColorDefault with NO hover/press recolor (:26-28); arrow button background stays SubtleFillColorTransparent in EVERY state (:14 — the :226-248 storyboards recolor only the Arrow foreground); disabled = ControlStrongFillColorDisabled (:29) + root opacity 0.5 (:436) + the mouse thumb itself hidden (ThumbVisual Opacity→0, the VerticalThumbTemplate Disabled storyboard :399-406). IsTabStop = false (:206). Vertical by default; horizontal: true mirrors the HorizontalRoot anatomy on the X axis (:672-693). Thumb POSITION is compositor-bound (a TransformBind on the position signal): scrolling moves the thumb the same frame with no re-render/relayout and never enters the FLIP pipeline — WinUI's instant Thumb layout. ONLY the conscious cross-axis 2px↔6px expand (and the 83ms chrome fades) animate, after the debounced begin times; that choreography is stepped by a mounted FrameClock ticker at the engine's 16ms-per-frame convention (the UseAnimatedValue precedent), so it is deterministic on the headless FixedFrameTimeSource host. The auto-hiding OVERLAY scrollbar on scroll viewports is engine-drawn (SceneRecorder.EmitScrollbar) with its timing in Animation.ScrollAnimator — this control is the standalone (always-visible) ScrollBar element. ScrollBar.Style SelectionModel Index/RANGE-based selection state (E11-L3) — WinUI's SelectionModel flattened to one level (our items controls are flat projections; TreeView flattens before selecting). DECOUPLED FROM REALIZATION: selection is a sorted list of disjoint inclusive index ranges, so SelectAll() over 50k rows stores ONE range and realizes nothing — realized items read their state on prepare via IsSelected(int) (O(log ranges)). Signals-first: every mutation bumps Version; a composing control reads Version.Value in its render so a selection change re-renders exactly that control (which re-skins only its realized window). SelectionChanged fires once per mutating call that actually changed state (WinUI SelectionChanged). Pointer/keyboard semantics are the verified WinUI selector trio (controls\dev\ItemsView): OnInteractedAction(int, bool, bool) / OnFocusedAction(int, bool, bool) implement SingleSelector.cpp:25-57, MultipleSelector.cpp:18-92 and ExtendedSelector.cpp:18-83 — anchor+shift ranges, ctrl toggles, focus-follow. SelectorBar A WinUI SelectorBar: a segmented horizontal row of text (optionally icon+text) items. The selected item is marked by a SHORT CENTERED accent pill flush with the item bottom (not a full-width underline, not bold); text stays TextPrimary at rest for every item and DIMS on hover/press — backgrounds are transparent in every state (SelectorBar_themeresources.xaml:21-25). Stateless — the caller owns selected and reacts to onSelect. Keyboard: ONE tab stop (the selected item — TabNavigation=Once, SelectorBar.xaml:13); Left/Right/ Home/End move focus item-to-item and SELECTION FOLLOWS FOCUS (SelectorBarTests.cs:63-66); focus entering a bar without a selection auto-selects (SelectorBar.cpp:98-126). Per-part restyling goes through the optional parts (see TemplateParts for the contract). SelectorVisuals The SelectorVisual presets as ItemContainerFactory-shaped builders: each returns the WinUI item chrome for one preset around the engine's ONE selection + keyboard substrate, signature-identical to the ItemContainerFactory delegate (ItemsView.cs:52-54) so a preset is just ContainerFactory = SelectorVisuals.. Each builder is PLAIN/recyclable — a keyed BoxEl tree with NO Component / no reactive bind (TransformBind/OpacityBind/FillBind/WidthBind/HeightBind/OnRealized would break TreeReconciler.IsRecyclable, Reconciler.cs:748-749); Animate (a LayoutTransition) is allowed because it is declarative. Per-item chrome SKIN goes through the ContainerFactory/SelectorVisual seam; per-item VARIATION goes through the PartDelta value seam (fill/fg/opacity/corner/padding/glyph as values, applied during construction — shape-stable, 0-alloc, CI-enforced; docs/guide/control-fidelity.md §6). WinUI chrome values are cited inline; they are re-verified against the live ListViewBaseItemChrome.cpp metrics (s_selectionIndicatorSize {3,16}, s_selectionIndicatorHeightShrinkage 6, s_selectionIndicatorMargin.left 4, s_backplateMargin {4,2}, s_multiSelectRoundedSquareInlineMargin 14, s_multiSelectRoundedContentOffset 28, s_multiSelectSquareOverlayMargin {0,2,2,0}) and the per-state brush ramps in {ListView,GridView,ItemContainer}_themeresources.xaml. Slider A WinUI 3 Slider. Geometry (Slider_themeresources.xaml): a 4px rail (SliderTrackFill = ControlStrongFillColorDefault, radius 2) with an accent value-fill, and the Fluent thumb — a STATIC 22px ring (the 18px Thumb part + its Border's −2 margin, lines 169-172 + 198) of ControlSolidFillColorDefault with a 1px ControlElevationBorder, holding the only animated part: the 12px accent inner dot whose storyboards scale it 0.86 at rest / 1.167 on hover / 0.71 on press / 1.167 disabled (lines 204-254). Value-fill and inner dot recolor with the CONTROL-wide PointerOver/Pressed states (AccentFillColorDefault → Secondary → Tertiary; lines 14-17, 24-27). Create(float, Action, float, float, Style?, bool, string?, TemplateParts?)/Bind(FloatSignal, Action?, float, float, Style?, bool, string?, TemplateParts?) — the lightweight 0..1 scrub primitives (media seek/volume). Keyboard, focus visuals and the optional header are included; the value tooltip is not (see Ranged(float, Action, Options, float, float, Style?, bool, TemplateParts?)). Ranged(float, Action, Options, float, float, Style?, bool, TemplateParts?) — the full WinUI-parity control over Slider.Options: arbitrary range, step snapping, ticks (incl. inline), Header, SmallChange/LargeChange keyboard, and the IsThumbToolTipEnabled "disambiguation UI" value tooltip with a ThumbToolTipValueConverter-equivalent formatter. Keyboard (KeyPress::Slider::KeyDown, SliderKeyProcess.h:28-71): Left/Down decrease and Right/Up increase by SmallChange, Home/End jump to Minimum/Maximum; PageUp/PageDown move by LargeChange (not in XAML's KeyPress::Slider — kept per the parity contract, riding the same Slider::Step math, Slider_Partial.cpp:1713-1783). RTL note: WinUI inverts Left/Right by (FlowDirection==RightToLeft) ^ IsDirectionReversed (SliderKeyProcess.h:26); the engine has no FlowDirection yet — the LTR mapping ships now, the inversion lands with the Wave-6 RTL sweep. Slider.Options Extended slider configuration: range, step snapping, ticks, orientation, header, keyboard step sizes and the thumb value tooltip ("disambiguation UI"). Slider.Style SplitButton A WinUI 3 SplitButton: a primary action button joined to a secondary dropdown half, sharing one rounded chrome with a 1px divider between them. The two halves are independently interactive — the primary half runs OnInvoke (the Click event / OnClickPrimary), the secondary half opens a MenuFlyout anchored below the whole control (OnClickSecondary → OpenFlyout). Each half carries its own hover/press fill + foreground ramp; the inner corner where each half meets the divider is squared so the pair reads as one control (WinUI PrimaryButtonBorder 4,0,0,4 / SecondaryButtonBorder 0,4,4,0). Source of truth: microsoft-ui-xaml/controls/dev/SplitButton/SplitButton.xaml (+ _themeresources.xaml, SplitButton.cpp). Tokens, sizes, padding, corners and the state matrix are 1:1 with that template. SplitView A WinUI SplitView: a side pane and a flexible content area, composed per SplitViewDisplayMode × PanePlacement × IsPaneOpen (the WinUI visual-state table, SplitView_Partial.cpp:34-59):
  • Overlay (the WinUI default) — the open pane floats over the content without reflow (PaneRoot Canvas.ZIndex=1 over a ColumnSpan=2 ContentRoot, SplitView_themeresources.xaml:700, :718) and light-dismisses on content click / Escape / a host resize, with the WinUI 0.35s/0.12s slide.
  • Inline — the open pane reserves paneWidth of layout; the content pre-slides in sync (ContentTransform, SplitView_themeresources.xaml:345-348) over the 0.2s/0.1s inline curves.
  • CompactOverlay / CompactInline — closed leaves a compactPaneLength (48) rail: the SAME pane, clipped to the rail (a clip-reveal, never a resize — core SplitView.cpp:343-377).
State is caller-owned (isPaneOpen is a prop, the WinUI IsPaneOpen DP): wire setIsPaneOpen so light dismiss can write the close back. PaneOpening/PaneOpened/ PaneClosing(cancelable on light dismiss)/PaneClosed mirror the WinUI events (XCPTypesAutoGen SplitView.cs:82-96). SplitViewPaneClosingArgs WinUI SplitViewPaneClosingEventArgs (XCPTypesAutoGen SplitView.cs:31-39): set Cancel to keep the pane open. Honored on LIGHT-DISMISS closes only (core SplitView.cpp:212-244 TryCloseLightDismissiblePane → CSplitViewPaneClosingExecutor:27-38); an app-initiated close raises the event but ignores Cancel (core SplitView.cpp:432-456). SwipeAction One swipe action (WinUI SwipeItem — SwipeControl.idl:69-91): an icon glyph, a label, an optional custom background Color (SwipeItem.Background — flips the content to on-accent text), the OnInvoked callback (the Invoked event / Command) and BehaviorOnInvoked. SwipeControl A WinUI SwipeControl (controls\dev\SwipeControl): drag the content sideways to reveal action items underneath. The content pans with the pointer; releasing past min(items width, 100px) rests it open (c_ThresholdValue — SwipeControl.cpp:21, ConfigurePositionInertiaRestingValues :937-1005), otherwise it springs closed; an already-open side closes on ANY inward movement (UpdateThresholdReached, :1626-1645). Items are invokable (Invoked + BehaviorOnInvoked); Execute mode stretches one item across the control and auto-invokes it on open. Esc and a tap on the content dismiss an open swipe (AttachDismissingHandlers, SwipeControl.h:74-76). Horizontal (Left/Right) items only — WinUI's vertical Top/Bottom axis is not offered on this surface, so its "both axes populated" rejection (ThrowIfHasVerticalAndHorizontalContent, SwipeControl.cpp:1648-1676) cannot arise. Deliberate superset: WinUI only swipes on touch/pen (VisualInteractionSource); ours pans with the mouse too. TabView A WinUI TabView (controls\dev\TabView): a horizontal strip of tab headers atop the selected tab's content. The control OWNS its live tab collection (the WinUI TabItems vector, TabView.idl:135), seeded once from Items/Tabs at mount: the close button / middle-click / Ctrl+F4 remove the tab (after raising OnTabCloseRequested) with WinUI's reselect-at-the-removed-index rule (TabView.cpp:786-812); the add button appends what OnAddTabButtonClick returns; pointer drag reorders tabs (CanReorderTabs, default true). Equal width mode clamps tabs to 100–240px of the measured strip; overflowing tabs scroll behind 32×24 repeat scroll buttons (±50px, disabled at the extremes) and the selected tab is auto-scrolled into view. The selected header uses the SolidBackgroundFillColorTertiary surface and fuses flush with the content area (the strip's 1px CardStroke baseline is per-tab and omitted under the selected header). Per-part restyling goes through Parts (see TemplateParts for the contract). TabViewItem One tab (WinUI TabViewItem, TabView.idl:205-230): a Header string, an optional leading icon glyph (the IconSource slot, idl:214-215), per-tab IsClosable (default true, idl:217-219), and a Content factory rendered in the content area while this tab is selected. TeachingTip WinUI TeachingTip (controls/dev/TeachingTip/TeachingTip.xaml + _themeresources + .cpp/.idl): an anchored (targeted) or window-relative (untargeted) non-modal callout that teaches/highlights a feature. The body is a SOLID surface — SolidBackgroundFillColorTertiary background, SurfaceStrokeColorDefault 1px border, OverlayCornerRadius (8), an elevation shadow, and the 1px TeachingTipTopHighlightBrush top highlight (#0DFFFFFF dark / #99FFFFFF light, TeachingTip_themeresources.xaml:6/:28) — with (top→bottom) an optional hero image, an icon + title + subtitle row, the main content, and a footer button panel (Action + Close). A targeted tip grows a 16×8 tail/beak that points at the target; an untargeted tip has no tail. NOT light-dismiss by default (IsLightDismissEnabled=false): only the close button, Escape, or a programmatic close dismisses it. The FULL 13-placement matrix (TeachingTip.xaml PlacementStates :115-264) is honored — Top/Bottom/Left/Right, the 8 corner variants, and Center — each mapping to the matching FlyoutPositioner placement with the tail pinned to the joined edge per the state's alignment setters (e.g. TopRight → tail at the LEFT of the bottom edge, :160-170). PlacementMargin nudges the tip away from the target (TeachingTip.cpp:480/:588 offset); ShouldConstrainToRootBounds (default true, the WinUI idl default) opts the popup out of the window bounds via the E4 windowed-popup seam when false. 1:1 tokens/sizes (TeachingTip_themeresources.xaml, unitless = epx): • MinWidth 320, MaxWidth 336, MinHeight 40, MaxHeight 520 (TeachingTipMin/MaxWidth/Height). • Content margin 12 on all sides (TeachingTipContentMargin); button panel top margin 0,12,0,0 (TeachingTipButtonPanelMargin); both-buttons split — Action 0,12,4,0 (Left), Close 4,12,0,0 (Right). • MainContent present-margin 0,12,0,0 (gap below the title block when content exists); icon→title gap 12 (TeachingTipIconPresenterMarginWithIcon); title-stack right margin 28 when the header (alternate) close button shows. • Title FontSize 16 SemiBold; Subtitle FontSize 14; body content FontSize 14 (Foreground = TextPrimary). • Alternate (header) close button: 40×40 (TeachingTipAlternateCloseButtonSize), E711 glyph at 16px, SubtleFill ramp, ControlCornerRadius. The footer ActionButton's DEFAULT style is the STANDARD button (TeachingTip.xaml:10 ActionButtonStyle = DefaultButtonStyle) — set ActionButtonIsAccent for the AccentButtonStyle the gallery samples use. Open/close motion (host-driven, PopupChrome.TeachingTip): expand scale → 1 over 300ms cubic-bezier(0.1,0.9,0.2,1); contract scale → 20/Width over 200ms cubic-bezier(0.7,0,1,0.5) (TeachingTip.cpp:1660-1712; .h:234-235 + :304-307 control points) — both at full opacity (the storyboards carry no opacity keyframes). TeachingTip.ClosingEventArgs WinUI TeachingTipClosingEventArgs (synchronous Cancel slice; async Deferral is deep-deferred). TextBlocks WinUI TextBlock equivalents: read-only styled text helpers mirroring the WinUI type ramp (Title / Subtitle / Body / BodyStrong / Caption). Display only — no input. TextBox WinUI TextBox: a text field built on EditableText, optionally with a header label stacked above and a description row below (the WinUI template's HeaderContentPresenter / DescriptionPresenter, TextBox_themeresources.xaml:335/340). Header-less it is just the bare field. EditableText already supplies the bordered surface (fill, gradient ControlElevationBorder, focus affordance, 4px corners) — TextBox wraps no second border. Single-line shows the DeleteButton (✕ E894 while focused ∧ non-empty); multi-line (acceptsReturn) hides it (WinUI: the DeleteButton is suppressed when TextWrapping/AcceptsReturn make the box multi-line). TimePicker A WinUI TimePicker: a single Button FACE split into Hour | Minute | AM/PM text columns (equal star widths, TimePicker_themeresources.xaml:234-239) separated by 1px vertical spacers, and on tap opens ONE TimePickerFlyout presenter via the Overlay service (the whole face is a single FlyoutButton, TimePicker_themeresources.xaml:232). The popup is placed so the centered 40px accent highlight band overlays the face (DateTimePickerFlyoutHelper.cpp:39-71) and is width-matched to the face's realized width (TimePickerFlyout_Partial.cpp:196-200). It hosts side-by-side LOOPING selector columns (item height 40) — the shared FluentGpu.Controls.DateTimeLoopColumn — under the highlight band, with a 41px Accept(E8FB) / Dismiss(E711) bar. SelectedTime is nullable (null shows the hour/minute/AM placeholders, the WinUI HasNoTime state); ClockIdentifier selects the 12/24-hour clock and MinuteIncrement coarsens the minute column. TitleBar The WinUI 3 TitleBar control (WinAppSDK 1.7, microsoft-ui-xaml controls\dev\TitleBar) over a custom frame (CustomFrame): back + pane-toggle buttons (40w, 16px glyphs), a 16×16 app-identity icon, 12px Caption title/subtitle, a centered content column (the gallery's AutoSuggestBox), and — unlike WinUI, which reserves space for SHELL-drawn caption buttons — three ENGINE-drawn min/max/close CaptionButtons (46w, full bar height, close = shell red) so the whole bar is one GPU-rendered surface. Non-client plumbing: the control captures its part handles (OnRealized) and, in a layout effect (after layout, before paint — push-on-relayout only, never per frame), reports TitleBarRegions through SetTitleBarRegions: interactive islands (back/pane/content) FIRST as Client, then the three button rects (→ HTMIN/HTMAX/HTCLOSE — the Win11 snap-layouts flyout requires HTMAXBUTTON), then the whole bar as the catch-all Caption drag band (first match wins in WM_NCHITTEST). Window activation/placement changes arrive via the host-bumped WindowChromeEpoch signal: deactivation dims title→tertiary, icon/content→50% opacity, caption glyphs→disabled (the WinUI Deactivated visual state); maximize re-glyphs max↔restore. ToggleButton A two/three-state toggle (shuffle/repeat/follow). Accent-filled when on — WinUI ToggleButton's checked state (AccentFillColorDefault bg + TextOnAccent fg + AccentControlElevationBorder); the unchecked AND indeterminate states are a standard control fill. Controlled: pass the state and a toggle/cycle callback. Style source: controls\dev\CommonStyles\ToggleButton_themeresources.xaml (Default = dark :4-61, Light :120-177; DefaultToggleButtonStyle :180-363). The full WinUI matrix is wired: {Unchecked, Checked, Indeterminate} × {Normal, PointerOver, Pressed, Disabled} for fill, foreground and border, verified in both themes. State flips (checked↔unchecked) cross-fade over the WinUI BrushTransition 83ms (ContentPresenter.BackgroundTransition, ToggleButton_themeresources.xaml:199-201) — the E3 primitive. No scale animation: WinUI state storyboards are color-only (lines 202-357). ToggleButton.Style ToggleSplitButton A WinUI ToggleSplitButton (SplitButton + checked state). Like SplitButton it is one joined chrome with two independently-clickable halves — a primary half on the left and a dropdown half on the right separated by a 1px divider — but the primary half toggles on/off instead of running a one-shot action: clicking it (or Enter/Space activation) flips IsChecked and raises OnToggle; the dropdown half opens a MenuFlyout. WinUI derives ToggleSplitButton from SplitButton and they share one ControlTemplate; the checked visual-states are driven by InternalIsChecked() (SplitButton.cpp UpdateVisualStates) — so this control is the SplitButton chrome with the accent Checked* token ramps folded in. When checked, both halves fill accent (SplitButtonBackgroundChecked = AccentFillColorDefault), the foreground is TextOnAccent, the outer border is AccentControlElevationBorder, and the inner divider switches to the on-accent divider stroke. Open/close goes through the overlay popup service (Service): re-click toggles, Escape / click-outside light-dismiss, focus captured on open + restored on close (host-wired), FocusTrap roves the menu, and ExpandCollapse Collapsed↔Expanded is raised by the overlay lifecycle. Keyboard: Enter/Space toggle the primary (the engine's focused-clickable activation), Down opens the flyout. IsEnabled gates all interaction via the engine and folds the chrome to the disabled tokens. The checked state is a caller Signal so a page can read/display it; pass OnToggle to observe flips. ToggleSwitch A WinUI ToggleSwitch — geometry/colors/motion from the shipped lifted style (microsoft-ui-xaml controls\dev\CommonStyles\ToggleSwitch_themeresources.xaml, "the template"): a 40×20 pill track (OuterBorder/SwitchKnobBounds, template:507-508) + a knob inside a 20×20 positioning host (SwitchKnob grid, template:509) that translates 0→20 on toggle (KnobTranslateTransform To=20, template:445). The knob is 12×12 at rest (template:510/515 + Normal storyboard :231-242), grows to 14×14 on hover (:268-279), and STRETCHES to 17×14 pinned 3px from the near edge while pressed (size :311-322; the Pressed VisualState.Setters pin SwitchKnobOn right / SwitchKnobOff left with a 3px margin, :284-287). All knob size/anchor changes tween 83ms ControlFasterAnimationDuration with the ControlFastOutSlowInKeySpline (0,0,0,1) (Common_themeresources_any.xaml:602/606 — FluentPopOpen); the disabled return is 250ms ControlNormalAnimationDuration (:357-368); knob travel is WinUI's RepositionThemeAnimation (:418-439), mapped per the repo motion canon to ControlFast = 167ms (Dsl/Motion.cs — the PVL reposition timing is system data, not in the XAML). Behavior follows dxaml\xcp\dxaml\lib\ToggleSwitch_Partial.cpp: tap toggles (TapHandler :874-905); DRAG-TO-TOGGLE — the knob follows the pointer's horizontal delta clamped to the travel (DragDeltaHandler/MoveDelta :823-841/:579-589, SetTranslations clamp :455-457) and on release toggles iff the knob crossed half the travel range (MoveCompleted :591-619); pointer loss mid-drag cleans up without toggling (OnPointerCaptureLost :728-746). Space toggles via the engine's Space-on-keyup activation → OnClick (HandlesKey = Space/GamepadA only, :1002-1007); arrow/Home/End keys toggle directionally (ToggleSwitchKeyProcess.h:52-71 — WinUI acts on KeyUp; the engine routes OnKeyDown, a known timing-only deviation). Controlled — the caller owns isOn. ToggleSwitch.Style ToolTip A WinUI ToolTip (framework ToolTip + ToolTipService): wraps a target element and surfaces a small text bubble anchored above it (or at the pointer in Mouse). 1:1 with the framework's automatic-tooltip behavior: Hover trigger — pointer-over the target opens the bubble after the initial show delay (SPI_GETMOUSEHOVERTIME 400ms × 2 = 800ms; a re-show within BetweenShowDelayMs = 200ms uses the reshow delay — see MouseReshowDelayMs for why that is 400ms, not the spec'd 600ms). Pointer-leave cancels a PENDING open immediately (ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:1435-1442); an OPEN bubble is kept and closed by the safe-zone monitor instead — see the safe-zone item below. Safe zone — an open bubble does NOT close the instant the pointer leaves the target: WinUI keeps it while the pointer is inside owner ∪ tooltip (∪ their convex hull) and a 1s check timer closes it once outside (ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:1433-1453 owner-exit only records the owner; cpp:349-381 OnSafeZoneCheck; cpp:1060-1098 IsToolTipInSafeZone; .h:22 s_safeZoneCheckTimerDuration = 1s). The engine surfaces no global pointer position, so the hull is approximated by the same 1s cadence: leaving owner ∪ bubble arms a SafeZoneCheckMs grace; re-entering either cancels it; the grace elapsing closes the bubble. Keyboard trigger — KEYBOARD focus landing inside the target opens the bubble after 800ms (×2, normal AND reshow: ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:1777-1779), exactly WinUI's OnOwnerGotFocus gate (ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:1648-1664: only FocusState::Keyboard shows the tooltip — pointer focus does not). Focus leaving closes it (cpp:1696-1706 OnOwnerLostFocus → OnOwnerLeaveInternal). Press dismiss — a pointer press over the target closes an open (or pending) bubble, and it stays closed until the pointer LEAVES and re-enters (classic tooltip behavior; WinUI 3's ToolTipService itself registers no PointerPressed handler — it closes via safe-zone exit monitoring, ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:1437-1453 — but cannot re-open a dismissed owner until a real leave + re-enter either, cpp:725-737 CancelAutomaticToolTip). Auto-dismiss — an open bubble closes itself after ShowDurationMs (SPI_GETMESSAGEDURATION default 5s), and the dismissal LATCHES like press dismiss: WinUI's only show trigger is PointerEntered (ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:1395-1418), so in-place hover moves can never re-open a timed-out tooltip — it takes a real leave + re-enter. No click trigger — the wrapper adds NO OnClick (ToolTipService registers none, ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:176-220), so a tooltipped element is never a tab stop by itself. Chrome matches ToolTip_themeresources.xaml: AcrylicInAppFillColorDefault fill, 1px SurfaceStrokeColorFlyout stroke, ControlCornerRadius (4px) corners, the light tooltip elevation (Tooltip), 9,6,9,8 padding, 12px TextFillColorPrimary text, MaxWidth 320, TextWrapping=Wrap. Open/close are the WinUI FadeIn/FadeOutThemeAnimation fades (167ms — Raw in the overlay host). The bubble does NOT trap focus and does NOT light-dismiss on outside click (None) — dismissal is owned by hover-leave/focus-leave/press + the auto-dismiss timer, exactly like ToolTipService. TreeNode One node in a TreeView: a label plus zero or more child nodes. IsEnabled gates interaction (disabled rows dim to 0.3); custom row content comes from the tree's ItemTemplate. TreeView Tween The typed computed-"TemplateSettings" convention — the antidote to WinUI's stringly TemplateSettings bound into storyboards. A control declares a local readonly record struct TemplateSettings(...) of typed values (rotations, heights, clip rects, open/close animation positions), computes it ONCE per state change (a pure factory called in render, or a UseMemo/OnRealized effect that reads the control's own signals), and feeds the fields into the engine's existing channels: static props, TransformBind/OpacityBind/WidthBind/ HeightBind, AnimEngine keyframe seeds, or the clip-rect channel (AnimChannel.ClipL/T/R/B). Never rebuild the record inside a hot bind/effect body — compute it in the memo/factory. ExpanderTemplateSettings is the reference; the same shape serves CommandBarFlyout, NavigationView, Progress*, TeachingTip, TabView, TreeView, … These small helpers are the shared interpolation math those records use. VariableSizedWrapGrid A WinUI VariableSizedWrapGrid: a grid that wraps tiles of varying sizes. This is a self-contained, non-virtualized demo — fixed-base cell tiles where some span multiple cells. Tiles are greedily packed left-to-right into rows that hold columns cells (by ColSpan sum); a tile that would overflow the current row starts a new one. Cells are contiguous (no inter-cell gap) — an N-span tile is exactly N*cell — matching the WinUI base panel, which is an unstyled layout host (no per-tile chrome). A faint fill is kept only for visibility. Viewbox A WinUI Viewbox: scales its single child by a composited transform (no relayout). WinUI computes the scale from a layout measure pass; this engine has no measure-override seam on BoxEl, so the scale is supplied either as an explicit factor (the scale overload) or computed from a known content size and target box via ViewboxStretch/ViewboxStretchDirection logic (the sized overload). The child is wrapped in a centered BoxEl carrying a uniform ScaleX/ScaleY. Virtual Fluent factories for virtualized collections. All recycle over the slab free-list and keep a 0-alloc in-window scroll. Custom(int, IVirtualLayout, Func, Func?, int, bool) takes any IVirtualLayout you implement. The returned VirtualListEl record lives in FluentGpu.Reconciler (the reconciler diffs it directly). WrapTile One tile in a VariableSizedWrapGrid: a label plus how many base cells it spans horizontally (ColSpan) and vertically (RowSpan). Structs ExpanderTemplateSettings Computed template settings for the Expander (the typed-record convention — see Tween): the chevron rotation and whether the content panel participates, derived once from the open state. Mirrors the geometry WinUI's generated ExpanderTemplateSettings binds into its chevron storyboard. InfoBadgeTemplateSettings Computed template settings for the InfoBadge — the typed-record convention modelled on ExpanderTemplateSettings. Mirrors WinUI's generated InfoBadgeTemplateSettings: the display kind (Dot vs Value vs Icon, derived from Value/Icon exactly like OnDisplayKindPropertiesChanged), the resolved badge height/width and the pill corner radius (= ActualHeight/2, per OnSizeChanged), and the per-kind content margin. InfoBar.InfoBarTemplateSettings The TemplateSettings convention (per the audit's P3) - the per-severity computed geometry WinUI binds into its severity VisualState: the standard status glyph, the icon-background fill (SystemFillColorBrush), the inverse glyph foreground, and the tinted content-root background (SystemFillColorBackgroundBrush). Computed once per severity by the pure factory For(InfoBarSeverity) - no per-frame allocation. ItemChromeState Per-item visual state handed to a custom ItemContainerFactory (the L4 skin seam). ItemCollectionTransition WinUI ItemCollectionTransition mapped onto the engine's FLIP/LayoutTransition pipeline (E11-L2): Moves = position FLIP (a reorder/removal slides the survivors), Adds = enter fade-in, Removes = exit fade-out, all over WinUI's ControlFastAnimationDuration 167ms / decelerate spline (0,0,0,1) — the timing the ItemContainer/Repeater storyboards use (ItemContainer.xaml:54-56 KeySpline="0,0,0,1" at ControlFastAnimationDuration). Virtualized caveat (documented, deliberate): enter transitions also play when an item SCROLLS into the realized window as a fresh mount — WinUI's LinedFlow behaves the same way for newly realized elements. MenuFlyoutItem A single row in a MenuFlyout: a label, an optional leading glyph (icon column), an enabled flag, the action to run when chosen, and an optional trailing keyboard-accelerator hint (e.g. "Ctrl+S"). Use Separator for a divider, Toggle(string, bool, Action?, string?, bool) for a checkable item (E73E check column), RadioItem(string, bool, Action?, string?, bool) for a mutually-exclusive choice (E915 bullet), or SubMenu(string, IReadOnlyList, string?, bool) for a cascading sub-menu (E974 chevron column, hover/Right-arrow opens the nested popup). All non-Command extras are optional so the primary (Label, Glyph, Enabled, Invoke) constructor call-sites are unchanged. OverlayPlacementInfo Resolved popup placement in popup-local coordinates, updated by OverlayHost after layout. PartDelta Per-item VARIATION as VALUES (the newly-legal per-item-customization seam — supersedes the banned per-item TemplateParts modifier in recycled scroll paths). Every field is a nullable override: null ⇒ keep the preset default. Applied DURING chrome construction (a plain with-swap into the already-allocated BoxEl/TextEl record), so per-item variation costs ZERO extra allocation and is provably shape-stable (the realizer shape-hash guard + a steady-scroll HotPhaseAllocBytes==0 check enforce it; docs/guide/control-fidelity.md §6). The producing Func must be pure-value: no new/box/LINQ/Animate per call. PersonPicture.PersonPictureTemplateSettings Computed geometry/content WinUI bakes into PersonPictureTemplateSettings + its size-driven font rules. Pure factory (no signals): the resolved initials/glyph string, which face renders it, and the size-derived font/badge metrics — fed straight into the static element props (this is a cold, reconcile-time composition). PopupOptions Optional popup behavior. FocusTrap keeps Tab/Shift-Tab inside the overlay subtree (modal-style). PopupPlacementResult Result of placing an anchored popup: the absolute top-left, which side it opened on (so the open animation can reveal from the correct edge), the width/height that actually fit (after a full collision resize), the EFFECTIVE placement after fallback (WinUI PerformPlacementWithFallback reports the placement that won), and the corner-join against the anchor. ProgressBar.ProgressBarTemplateSettings The computed translate positions WinUI binds from ProgressBarTemplateSettings into the indeterminate storyboards (ProgressBar.cpp UpdateWidthBasedTemplateSettings). Indicator widths follow SetProgressBarIndicatorWidth. ProgressRing.ProgressRingTemplateSettings Computed geometry for one ProgressRing (the audit's P3 TemplateSettings convention — mirrors WinUI's ProgressRingTemplateSettings, recomputed once from the control's size, never inside a per-frame bind). Sweep is the determinate accent-arc sweep in degrees (value·360). For the indeterminate ring it is the fixed visible arc length the rotation loop spins. RatingControlTemplateSettings Computed template settings for the RatingControl (the typed-record convention -- see PersonPicture.PersonPictureTemplateSettings / Expander.ExpanderTemplateSettings): the resolved display value (the rating shown right now -- pointer-over preview, else committed value, else placeholder), the foreground/background brushes for the current visual state, and the partial-star clip width baked for the foreground "Set" layer. Mirrors the geometry WinUI's RatingControl::UpdateRatingItemsAppearance derives each pass and binds into its two clipped stack panels. Pure factory: no signals are read here -- the caller passes the already-resolved inputs, so this struct is cheap to build per render and never allocates in a hot bind. The star foreground recolor is INSTANT, never eased: WinUI drives every star visual state with VisualStateManager::GoToState(useTransitions:false) (see UpdateRatingItemsAppearance), so the brush steps the instant the pointer-over preview resolves. We honor that by computing the resting Foreground directly from the sweep and leaving the glyph's HoverColor at default (A==0 ⇒ the recorder leaves the color untouched and runs no hover ramp) -- a granular re-render swaps the resting color in one step, no cross-fade. RepeatLayout A pluggable layout for ItemsRepeater(int, Func, in RepeatLayout, Func?, int, Action?, Action?, Action?, Action?, ItemCollectionTransition?) — the equivalent of WinUI's Layout object. A readonly struct (no per-call allocation). Stack/Grid/Custom virtualize (only the window is realized); Wrap/Inline are non-virtual (build the whole child list — use for SMALL collections like a nav pane or a toolbar). Measured/LinedFlow/SpanGrid/HorizontalGrid ride the Custom slot (every layout object IS an IVirtualLayout; variable-extent ones are IMeasuredVirtualLayout — E11-L0's one seam). NOTE: the stateful layouts (Measured/LinedFlow/SpanGrid/Grouped) own realize tables — when the OWNING component re-renders, hoist the layout instance (e.g. UseMemo) instead of calling the factory inline each render. RichTextBlock.Options Per-block options (WinUI DP equivalents). Defaults match the WinUI control: selection enabled (RichTextBlock.cpp:1730), theme selection brush, the established 560px reading measure + 10px paragraph gap. StateBrush Reusable, WinUI-accurate discrete-state motion presets for control sub-elements — the checkmarks, dots and glyphs that appear/disappear on a logical state flip (checked, selected, expanded…). These ride the engine's existing enter/exit lifecycle: attach one as Animate on a node the reconciler INSERTS on enter and REMOVES on exit, and it scales+fades in (on insert) / out (orphaned, then reclaimed when the spring settles, on removal). The motion is property-level and signals-native — the state IS the element's presence and the animation falls out of the reconcile diff — so there is no per-control animation code and no VisualStateManager: the opposite of WinUI's stringly-typed GoToState storyboards, and a strict superset in composability. Springs (velocity-carrying, interruptible) are the default; durations map to the Fluent motion tokens. Interfaces IOverlayService Enums AnnotatedScrollBarScrollKind Why an AnnotatedScrollBar scrolled — mirrors WinUI AnnotatedScrollBarScrollingEventKind (AnnotatedScrollBar.idl:6-12). AppBarCommandKind The kind of a AppBarCommand in a CommandBarFlyout/CommandBar: a plain push button, a checkable toggle (PRIMARY toggles paint the accent pill when checked; OVERFLOW toggles show only the E73E check glyph — V2 OverflowChecked, CommandBarFlyout_themeresources.xaml:434-438), or a divider row. BackgroundSizing WinUI BackgroundSizing: whether the control fill stops at the inner border edge or extends under the border to its outer edge. WinUI DefaultButtonStyle = InnerBorderEdge, AccentButtonStyle = OuterBorderEdge (microsoft-ui-xaml controls\dev\CommonStyles\Button_themeresources.xaml:156,238); ToggleButton flips InnerBorderEdge → OuterBorderEdge while checked (ToggleButton_themeresources.xaml:182,6). Carried on the button-family styles as the API/parity contract. HONEST NOTE: the renderer today draws every fill to the OUTER rounded-rect edge with the border stroked on top — i.e. OuterBorderEdge-equivalent for all values; the 1px fill inset of InnerBorderEdge is a named E9 render-primitive gap, not silently dropped. With the WinUI hairline (1px) translucent elevation borders the visual delta is sub-pixel-class. CalendarViewDisplayMode WinUI CalendarViewDisplayMode — the zoom level: Month (day grid) / Year (4-column months) / Decade (4-column years). CalendarViewSelectionMode WinUI CalendarViewSelectionMode: None / Single / Multiple (controls.idl). CheckState The tri-state of a CheckBox / ToggleButton: cleared, set, or mixed (indeterminate). CommandBarDisplayMode WinUI CommandBar.ClosedDisplayMode: how the CLOSED bar renders — Compact (48px icon strip), Minimal (24px sliver with only the … More button), Hidden (collapsed). CommandBarFlyoutShowMode WinUI FlyoutShowMode subset for CommandBarFlyout (CommandBarFlyout.cpp:162-201): Standard opens with the overflow ALREADY expanded — transitions suppressed while opening (m_commandBarFlyoutIsOpening → UpdateUI(false), CommandBarFlyoutCommandBar.cpp:25/:121/:189-197) — and focuses the first command (cpp:29-71). Transient (the default) opens collapsed and never steals focus. AlwaysExpanded forces Standard (CommandBarFlyout.cpp:173-177). ContentDialog.DefaultBtn ContentDialogResult How a ContentDialog was dismissed (WinUI ContentDialogResult): the primary/secondary command, or None (close button / Escape / programmatic / light-of-modal never applies). CornerJoin Which popup corners abut (and so should square against) the anchor edge — WinUI UpdateCornerRadius corner-joining for ComboBox/AutoSuggestBox/DropDownButton, where the dropdown reads as one piece with the field. DismissBehavior Opens anchored, light-dismissable flyouts (menus, ComboBox/DropDownButton/SplitButton dropdowns, ColorPicker). A control captures its own node via OnRealized and passes a thunk for it as the anchor; the host positions the popup relative to the anchor's on-screen rect (flip/nudge into the viewport) and animates it open/closed with the WinUI MenuPopupThemeTransition. Resolve via UseContext(Overlay.Service) inside an OverlayHost. FlyoutPlacement Where a flyout opens relative to its anchor — the full WinUI FlyoutPlacementMode matrix (FlyoutBase_Partial.cpp:84-110 maps each mode to a major side + an edge justification) plus the engine's pre-existing aliases kept for source compatibility: BottomLeft/TopLeft ≡ WinUI BottomEdgeAlignedLeft/TopEdgeAlignedLeft. BottomCenter/TopCenter ≡ WinUI Bottom/Top (center-justified). BottomStretch = the attached ComboBox/AutoSuggest dropdown (flush, width-matched, corner-joined) — not a FlyoutBase mode; WinUI ComboBox places its own popup over/under the field. InfoBadgeSeverity The severity preset that picks an InfoBadge background and (for Icon badges) a default glyph, mirroring WinUI's {Attention,Informational,Success,Caution,Critical}InfoBadgeStyle set (InfoBadge_themeresources.xaml). Default == the plain accent badge. InfoBarCloseReason Why an InfoBar closed (WinUI InfoBarCloseReason): the user clicked the X (CloseButton) or it was closed in code / by the host (Programmatic). InfoBarSeverity The status level of an InfoBar, which selects its standard icon glyph and severity color (WinUI InfoBarSeverity). Default is Informational. ItemContainerTrigger How the user interacted with an ItemContainer — the WinUI ItemContainerInteractionTrigger subset the engine surfaces (ItemContainer.idl:17-26; PointerPressed/ PointerReleased collapse into Tap here — see Build(Element, bool, Action, bool, CornerRadius4?) remarks). ItemsSelectionMode WinUI ItemsViewSelectionMode (ItemsView.idl:6-12): None / Single / Multiple / Extended. ItemsView defaults to Single (ItemsView.h s_defaultSelectionMode). MenuItemKind The kind of a MenuFlyoutItem row: a plain command, a divider, a checkable ToggleMenuFlyoutItem (E73E check), a mutually-exclusive RadioMenuFlyoutItem (E915 bullet), or a cascading MenuFlyoutSubItem (E974 chevron + nested popup). NavPaneDisplayMode WinUI NavigationViewPaneDisplayMode (NavigationView.idl): Auto adapts by width; Left/LeftCompact/ LeftMinimal force a left-pane mode; Top renders the horizontal top bar with overflow. NumberBoxSpinButtonPlacementMode WinUI NumberBoxSpinButtonPlacementMode: where (if anywhere) the up/down spin buttons appear. NumberBoxValidationMode WinUI NumberBoxValidationMode: what happens to invalid / out-of-range input on commit. OverlayCloseCause Where an overlay close request came from; controls map this to their public close reason. PaneMode PasswordRevealMode WinUI PasswordRevealMode (microsoft.ui.xaml.controls.controls.idl:163–167 — Peek, Hidden, Visible): Peek (default) = press-and-hold the reveal "eye" shows the password, release re-masks; Hidden = always masked, no reveal button; Visible = plain text always, no reveal button. PipsPagerButtonVisibility WinUI PipsPagerButtonVisibility (PipsPager.idl:6-11) — how the previous/next navigation buttons show. Both default to Collapsed (PipsPager.idl:56-59). PopupChrome Which presenter chrome + open/close MOTION the overlay host supplies around popup content. Each kind maps 1:1 to the WinUI transition family that owns that surface:
  • — menus (MenuFlyout/DropDownButton/SplitButton/MenuBar/context): MenuPopupThemeTransition (clip-reveal + content translate 250ms cubic-bezier(0,0,0,1); close = 83ms linear fade) — the default.
  • — the WinUI Flyout/FlyoutPresenter (FlyoutBase attaches PopupThemeTransition, FlyoutBase_Partial.cpp:1968–1975): OS-PVL TAS_SHOWPOPUP/TAS_HIDEPOPUP (slide ±50 + delayed fade; close 83ms fade).
  • — the ComboBox dropdown: SplitOpen/SplitCloseThemeAnimation (generic.xaml:9047/9056) — clip reveal 250ms with NO content translate and NO open fade; close = 167ms clip collapse + late 83ms fade. With set, the band is centred on the SEAM (selected row) and grows/shrinks both ways (WinUI OffsetFromCenter); otherwise it reveals from the anchor edge.
  • — ToolTip/Slider value tip: FadeIn/FadeOutThemeAnimation (TAS_FADEIN/TAS_FADEOUT, 167ms linear).
  • — ContentDialog scale/fade; — muxc expand/contract scale.
  • — a bare WinUI Popup with no transitions (the AutoSuggestBox SuggestionsPopup, generic.xaml AutoSuggestBox template — no TransitionCollection and AutoSuggestBox_Partial.cpp adds none): instant.
ProgressBarState The drawing state of a ProgressBar — mirrors WinUI's CommonStates (Determinate / Paused / Error and their Indeterminate counterparts), selected from IsIndeterminate + ShowPaused + ShowError. RepeatKind SelectorVisual The selector-VISUAL presets — the user-pickable item-chrome skins the premiere ItemsView exposes (the WinUI "ListView's accent bar" the user likes, GridView's corner check, ItemContainer's accent ring, …) as a single DATA enum so any layout × any selection mode can wear any selector (no WinUI-style capability cliffs): AccentPill — the WinUI ListViewItem SelectionIndicator (the 3×16 accent bar at the row's left edge over a subtle backplate); the ListView preset's default. Check — the WinUI GridViewItem dual-border + top-right corner check; the GridView preset's default. FullRow — a documented SUPERSET (no exact WinUI analogue): the AccentPill row plate WITHOUT the left pill, so the whole row fills with the accent-subtle selected highlight (full-bleed selected backplate). Border — the WinUI ItemContainer chrome (3px accent ring + 1px inner stroke + checkbox). None — no selection chrome at all (a bare recyclable container) for app-drawn selection. Slider.TickPlacement Where tick marks render — WinUI TickPlacement (visibility mapping: Slider_Partial.cpp:2248-2303). WinUI's default is Inline (marks inside the rail, SliderInlineTickBarFill). SplitViewDisplayMode WinUI SplitViewDisplayMode (XCPTypesAutoGen Modules/Controls/SplitView/SplitView.cs:19-25). Overlay/CompactOverlay float the open pane ABOVE the content (no reflow) and are light-dismissible; Inline/CompactInline reserve layout width for it. Compact modes keep a CompactPaneLength rail visible while closed (the pane is CLIPPED to the rail, never resized — core SplitView.cpp:343-377 template settings). SplitViewPanePlacement WinUI SplitViewPanePlacement (XCPTypesAutoGen SplitView.cs:11-15). SwipeBehaviorOnInvoked WinUI SwipeBehaviorOnInvoked (SwipeControl.idl:11-16). Auto and Close both close the control after the invoke (SwipeItem::InvokeSwipe closes on Close OR Auto — SwipeItem.cpp:40-45); RemainOpen keeps it revealed. SwipeMode WinUI SwipeMode (SwipeControl.idl:20-24): Reveal = resting tappable action buttons; Execute = ONE item stretched to the control width that auto-invokes when the swipe opens past the threshold. TabViewCloseButtonOverlayMode WinUI TabViewCloseButtonOverlayMode (TabView.idl:15-20). Auto/Always show the close button on every closable tab; OnPointerOver shows it only while the tab is selected or hovered (TabViewItem.cpp:251-282). TabViewWidthMode WinUI TabViewWidthMode (TabView.idl:6-11) — how tab headers size. Default Equal: every tab gets clamp(available/count, 100, 240) (TabView.cpp:1180-1228; TabViewItemMinWidth/MaxWidth, TabView_themeresources.xaml:243-244). SizeToContent: intrinsic. Compact: unselected tabs collapse to icon-only (TabViewItem.cpp:284-295; TabView.xaml:484-490). TeachingTip.CloseReason WinUI TeachingTipCloseReason — surfaced on Closing/Closed. TeachingTip.HeroPlacement WinUI TeachingTipHeroContentPlacementMode. TeachingTip.PlacementMode WinUI TeachingTipPlacementMode — all 13 modes are positioned (TeachingTip.xaml PlacementStates). TeachingTip.TailVisibilityMode WinUI TeachingTipTailVisibility. TextChangeReason Why the field's text changed — the WinUI AutoSuggestionBoxTextChangeReason shape, surfaced through LastChangeReason so AutoSuggestBox/ComboBox can split user edits from programmatic writes. ToolTipPlacementMode How the tooltip bubble is positioned — WinUI PlacementMode (the two modes ToolTipService actually drives): Top = centered above the TARGET, flipping on a collision (the WinUI default, ToolTip_Partial.cpp:1119-1122 "Fall back to the default - PlacementMode_Top"), the target rect inflated by the input-mode offset — 20px mouse / 12px keyboard (MousePlacementOffset); Mouse = at the last POINTER position, offset 11px below the cursor (ToolTip_Partial.h:56 m_mousePlacementVerticalOffset = 11; ToolTip_Partial.cpp:976-984 "align ToolTip with the bottom left corner of mouse bounding rectangle"). ViewboxStretch How a Viewbox scales its child to the available box. Mirrors WinUI Stretch (default Uniform). ViewboxStretchDirection Constrains a Viewbox scale direction. Mirrors WinUI StretchDirection (default Both). Delegates ItemContainerFactory Custom item-container factory — the E11-L4 SKIN seam: the List/Grid presets + TreeView supply their WinUI item chrome (ListViewItemPresenter / GridView dual-border / TreeViewItem row) around the engine's ONE selection + keyboard substrate. The returned BoxEl must wire onInteraction (press/Enter/Space → the selector) and onFocusChanged (keyboard-current tracking), and should be Focusable so the engine focus ring lands on items. Null ⇒ the default WinUI ItemContainer chrome. ReorderCrossCommit The ONE cross-list commit callback (plan E5-L3): the app removes the item from from at fromIndex and inserts payload into to at toIndex — one atomic model mutation, both lists re-render from it (signals-first). from is null (and fromIndex −1) when the drag came from a plain BoxEl.Draggable of the shared kind rather than another Reorderable (a deposit-style insert).

api/FluentGpu.Dsl.BoxEl.html.title: Class BoxEl | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Dsl.BoxEl.html.summary: Class BoxEl Namespace FluentGpu.Dsl Assembly FluentGpu.Dsl.dll A box: container/layout node and/or a filled, optionally-clickable surface (VStack/HStack/Button/Box). public sealed record BoxEl : Element, IEquatable<Element>, IEquatable<BoxEl> Inheritance object Element BoxEl Implements IEquatable<Element> IEquatable<BoxEl> Inherited Members Element.Key object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods Modifiers.Acrylic(BoxEl, AcrylicSpec) Modifiers.Alpha(BoxEl, float) Modifiers.Background(BoxEl, ColorF) Modifiers.Border(BoxEl, ColorF, float) Modifiers.BorderBrush(BoxEl, GradientSpec, float) Modifiers.Elevate(BoxEl, ShadowSpec) Modifiers.Gradient(BoxEl, GradientSpec) Modifiers.HoverColor(BoxEl, ColorF) Modifiers.Offset(BoxEl, float, float) Modifiers.Pad(BoxEl, float) Modifiers.Pad(BoxEl, float, float, float, float) Modifiers.PressedColor(BoxEl, ColorF) Modifiers.Rotate(BoxEl, float) Modifiers.Rounded(BoxEl, float) Modifiers.Scale(BoxEl, float) Modifiers.Scale(BoxEl, float, float) Modifiers.Shadow(BoxEl, ShadowSpec) Properties Accelerator Keyboard-accelerator chord (WinUI KeyboardAccelerator): invokes OnClick from anywhere once focused routing leaves the chord unhandled (e.g. Ctrl+W close-tab). public KeyAccelerator? Accelerator { get; init; } Property Value KeyAccelerator? AccessKey Access-key mnemonic (WinUI AccessKey): Alt+letter invokes OnClick. Uppercase 'A'..'Z'/'0'..'9'. public char AccessKey { get; init; } Property Value char Acrylic public AcrylicSpec? Acrylic { get; init; } Property Value AcrylicSpec? ActivateOnEnter WinUI KeyPress::Button bAcceptsReturn: false = Enter does NOT activate this clickable (it falls through to normal key routing) — CheckBox (CheckBox_Partial.cpp:27), RadioButton (RadioButton_Partial.cpp:30) and ToggleSwitch (ToggleSwitch_Partial.cpp:1002-1007) toggle on Space only. Space activation is unaffected. public bool ActivateOnEnter { get; init; } Property Value bool AlignItems public FlexAlign AlignItems { get; init; } Property Value FlexAlign AlignSelf public FlexAlign AlignSelf { get; init; } Property Value FlexAlign AllowFocusOnInteraction WinUI AllowFocusOnInteraction: false = a pointer press never moves focus to this focusable (and never falls past it to an ancestor — focus stays where it was, AppBarButton_themeresources.xaml:136); keyboard Tab still reaches it. True (default) = press focuses the nearest focusable self-or-ancestor. public bool AllowFocusOnInteraction { get; init; } Property Value bool Animate Opt this box into general layout-change animation: the host diffs its presented rect vs its new laid-out rect each commit and drives the residual through the spec's channels/dynamics (no relayout, no per-frame re-render). Null ⇒ snap (the default). See LayoutTransition. public LayoutTransition? Animate { get; init; } Property Value LayoutTransition? Arc public ArcSpec? Arc { get; init; } Property Value ArcSpec? Basis public float Basis { get; init; } Property Value float BorderBrush public GradientSpec? BorderBrush { get; init; } Property Value GradientSpec? BorderColor public ColorF BorderColor { get; init; } Property Value ColorF BorderWidth public float BorderWidth { get; init; } Property Value float BrushTransitionMs Implicit brush transition (WinUI BrushTransition): when a re-render changes Fill/BorderColor on this LIVE node (a logical state flip — checked, selected…), the displayed color cross-fades over this duration instead of snapping. NaN = snap (the default); WinUI control templates use 83ms. public float BrushTransitionMs { get; init; } Property Value float CanDrag Marks this box a drag-reorder source (WinUI CanDragItems/CanReorderItems item container): a left press on it (or any non-draggable descendant) arms the engine's DragController; pointer travel past the 4px drag box (per-axis, ListViewBaseItem_Partial.cpp:1864-1878) promotes the press to a drag — the node follows the pointer at WinUI ListViewItemDragThemeOpacity 0.80 (ListViewItem_themeresources.xaml:7) with a lifted shadow, stops hit-testing, and the eventual release SUPPRESSES the click. public bool CanDrag { get; init; } Property Value bool Children public Element[] Children { get; init; } Property Value Element[] ClipToBounds public bool ClipToBounds { get; init; } Property Value bool Corners public CornerRadius4 Corners { get; init; } Property Value CornerRadius4 CounterScale Opt this child OUT of a ScaleCorrect ancestor's scale: the recorder applies the inverse scale so the child stays undistorted (Framer-Motion projection correction). public bool CounterScale { get; init; } Property Value bool Cursor Pointer cursor shown while hovering this node or any cursor-less descendant (WinUI SetCursor). Null = inherit from the nearest declaring ancestor, else the system arrow — clickability does NOT imply the hand. An explicit value (Arrow included) terminates the lookup, masking an ancestor's I-beam/hand (the TextBox delete button / PasswordBox reveal force Arrow over the field's I-beam — TextBox_Partial.cpp:884). public CursorId? Cursor { get; init; } Property Value CursorId? Direction public byte Direction { get; init; } Property Value byte Draggable E5-L2 typed drag SOURCE (the Flutter Draggable / react-beautiful-dnd model — deliberately NOT WinUI OLE, per the 2026-06-10 user ruling): marks this box draggable (implies CanDrag — the L1 gesture armer) with a string Kind discriminator + a payload factory the engine resolves ONCE when the press promotes past the drag box. The live DragSession then routes to the nearest accepting DropTarget under the pointer on every move. public DragSource? api/FluentGpu.Dsl.Elevation.html.title: Class Elevation | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Dsl.Elevation.html.summary: Class Elevation Namespace FluentGpu.Dsl Assembly FluentGpu.Dsl.dll Fluent elevation presets — the engine's ANALYTIC drop shadows standing in for WinUI's ThemeShadow Z-depth model (we own every pixel; documented visual-equivalent to the DComp shadow). WinUI elevates popups by Z translation — base depth 32 + 8 per nested tier (ElevationHelper.cpp:19-21 s_elevationBaseDepth/s_elevationIterativeDepth) — and the design maps the popup bands to three depth CLASSES: ToolTip (lightest, depth-16 class) < Flyout/Menu (depth-32 class) < Dialog (depth-128 class). The preset blur/offset/alpha values keep the engine's existing visual scale; the CLASS a control uses is the contract: ToolTip → Tooltip; Flyout/MenuFlyout/CommandBarFlyout/TeachingTip → Flyout; ContentDialog → Dialog. Use via .Elevate(Elevation.Card) (Modifiers) or Ui.Card(...) which carries Card by default. public static class Elevation Inheritance object Elevation Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields Card public static readonly ShadowSpec Card Field Value ShadowSpec CardHover public static readonly ShadowSpec CardHover Field Value ShadowSpec Dialog Dialog band (WinUI depth-128 class) — ContentDialog and full-window modal surfaces. public static readonly ShadowSpec Dialog Field Value ShadowSpec Flyout Flyout/menu band (WinUI depth-32 class — ElevationHelper.cpp:19 base elevation depth 32). public static readonly ShadowSpec Flyout Field Value ShadowSpec None public static readonly ShadowSpec None Field Value ShadowSpec Tooltip ToolTip band (WinUI depth-16 class) — lighter than Flyout; tooltips sit on the lowest popup band. public static readonly ShadowSpec Tooltip Field Value ShadowSpec

api/FluentGpu.Dsl.TemplateParts.html.title: Class TemplateParts | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Dsl.TemplateParts.html.summary: Class TemplateParts Namespace FluentGpu.Dsl Assembly FluentGpu.Dsl.dll Lightweight template-part styling — the FluentGpu equivalent of CSS ::part / WinUI lightweight styling, signals-native. A control names its internal template parts (public const string PartHeader = "Header"; …) and routes each built part element through Apply<T>(TemplateParts?, string, T); app code layers ANY element props onto any part with a with expression: Parts = new() { [Expander.PartHeader] = b => b with { StickyTop = 8f, // CSS position:sticky, top: 8px OnPinned = p => stuck.Value = p, // the :stuck observable Fill = stuck.Value ? Tok.FillSolidBase : b.Fill, // restyle ANYTHING off the signal BrushTransitionMs = Motion.ControlFast, // …and the swap cross-fades }, } Contract (apply order): control defaults → part modifier → the control RE-ASSERTS its mechanics-critical props (click handlers, reflow specs, ref captures — chained via Chain<T>(Action<T>?, Action<T>?), never clobbered), so a modifier can restyle everything but break nothing. Modifier rules: a PURE with-copy of its input; reading a signal inside one subscribes the OWNING CONTROL (modifiers run inside its Render — that's the granular :stuck restyle loop); never WRITE a signal in a modifier; no per-frame-hot reads (use FillBind/TransformBind for those); type-preserving (a modifier that changes the record type is ignored — parts style, content SLOTS like Content/HeaderContent restructure); avoid reshaping Children (structural reconcile). List-UNIFORM (content-independent) item-chrome modifiers are cached apply-once via the parts epoch (TryApplyCached(string, Element, out Element)); per-item CONTENT differences use the PartDelta value seam (SelectorVisuals.cs), never a per-item modifier in a recycled scroll path. public sealed class TemplateParts Inheritance object TemplateParts Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods TemplatePartsExtensions.Apply<T>(TemplateParts?, string, T) Properties Epoch Bumped on every modifier-map mutation; the apply-once prototype cache keys on it so an app changing a modifier invalidates the cached list-uniform prototype. public int Epoch { get; } Property Value int this[string] Box parts — the common case, object-initializer friendly: Parts = new() { [Expander.PartHeader] = b => b with { … } }. Setting null removes the modifier. public Func<BoxEl, BoxEl>? this[string part] { set; } Parameters part string Property Value Func<BoxEl, BoxEl> Methods Chain<T>(Action<T>?, Action<T>?) Compose a control-internal handler with a modifier-supplied one (OnRealized/OnPinned): the control's runs first, the user's after. Collapses when the modifier left the control's own handler in place (the with-copy default) or supplied none. public static Action<T>? Chain<T>(Action<T>? control, Action<T>? user) Parameters control Action<T> user Action<T> Returns Action<T> Type Parameters T Set<T>(string, Func<T, T>) Any element type (a TextEl glyph part, an image…). Modifiers must be type-preserving. public void Set<T>(string part, Func<T, T> modify) where T : Element Parameters part string modify Func<T, T> Type Parameters T

api/FluentGpu.Dsl.html.title: Namespace FluentGpu.Dsl | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Dsl.html.summary: Namespace FluentGpu.Dsl Classes BoxEl A box: container/layout node and/or a filled, optionally-clickable surface (VStack/HStack/Button/Box). Element Immutable description of a UI node (the "virtual DOM"). Cheap to build, never touches the scene directly. Elevation Fluent elevation presets — the engine's ANALYTIC drop shadows standing in for WinUI's ThemeShadow Z-depth model (we own every pixel; documented visual-equivalent to the DComp shadow). WinUI elevates popups by Z translation — base depth 32 + 8 per nested tier (ElevationHelper.cpp:19-21 s_elevationBaseDepth/s_elevationIterativeDepth) — and the design maps the popup bands to three depth CLASSES: ToolTip (lightest, depth-16 class) < Flyout/Menu (depth-32 class) < Dialog (depth-128 class). The preset blur/offset/alpha values keep the engine's existing visual scale; the CLASS a control uses is the contract: ToolTip → Tooltip; Flyout/MenuFlyout/CommandBarFlyout/TeachingTip → Flyout; ContentDialog → Dialog. Use via .Elevate(Elevation.Card) (Modifiers) or Ui.Card(...) which carries Card by default. GridEl A CSS-Grid container: column tracks (Pixel/Star/Auto) + gaps; children auto-flow row-major into cells. Rows take RowHeight (or the tallest cell when NaN). True tracks — every cell in a column shares its width (unlike nested-flex faking). Width-aware: resolves inside a ScrollView. (layout.md §7.) IconGlyphs Segoe Fluent glyph codepoints shared across LAYERS (a control and the engine recorder), so every consumer renders literally the same icon. Control-local glyphs stay at their call sites (or Controls.Icons); promote one here only when a layer below Controls needs it — today the scrollbar arrows, drawn both by the ScrollBar/AnnotatedScrollBar templates and by the engine's overlay scrollbar (SceneRecorder.EmitScrollbar). ImageEl An async image (album art): shows Placeholder until the decode lands, then the bitmap. Decode is off-thread + cached + residency-pinned while on screen (see ImageCache). Modifiers Fluent per-element style overrides (React/Compose/WinUI-style): Button("x", onClick).Background(c).Rounded(8). Each returns a record with-copy, so any default style is overrideable inline without forking the control. Motion The real Fluent 2 / WinUI motion durations + entrance metrics (durations only — the easing curves live in FluentGpu.Foundation.Easing, selected by the Hooks-layer motion helpers, since Dsl does not reference Animation). Canonical WinUI names (the motion audit's correction): ControlFaster = 83ms (BrushTransition, hover/press), ControlFast = 167ms (ControlFastAnimationDuration — knob travel, settle), ControlNormal = 250ms (ControlNormalAnimationDuration — reposition / pane motion). PolylineStrokeEl A text run. FontFamily selects the face — a system family name ("Segoe UI", "Segoe Fluent Icons") or a custom file as "path/to.ttf#Family Name" (the WinUI syntax). Null = the theme body font. Combine with an icon-font family + a private-use glyph to render icons (see Ui.Icon). Radii The WinUI Fluent corner-radius ramp (ControlCornerRadius / OverlayCornerRadius + the SelectorBar pill). ScrollEl A clipping, scrolling viewport over a single (possibly oversized) content child. Scroll is layout-free (layout.md §6): layout arranges the content at the content-box origin and publishes ContentSize; the -ScrollOffset translation is the content's LocalTransform, written by Input on wheel/drag. Spacing The WinUI Gallery spacing rhythm (page gutters, card spacing, internal padding, stack gaps). SpanTextEl A rich-text PARAGRAPH of typed inline runs (rtb-01 — the WinUI TextBlock.Inlines/RichTextBlock paragraph model: Run/Bold/Hyperlink): the spans concatenate and shape as ONE flow (one wrap pass — a styled word never re-flows independently of its sentence). Per-span weight/size/family/color/underline/strikethrough overlay the base style here; a span with OnClick is a HYPERLINK — the engine resolves the Hand cursor over its laid rects and fires the action on click (RichTextBlock.cpp:2995 / TextBlock.cpp:3488 SetCursor(MouseCursorHand)); accent foreground + underline are the CALLER's styling (HyperlinkForeground, generic.xaml:1120-1122). No per-span italic: the shaper has no style axis yet (faces resolve by family+weight only). TemplateParts Lightweight template-part styling — the FluentGpu equivalent of CSS ::part / WinUI lightweight styling, signals-native. A control names its internal template parts (public const string PartHeader = "Header"; …) and routes each built part element through Apply<T>(TemplateParts?, string, T); app code layers ANY element props onto any part with a with expression: Parts = new() { [Expander.PartHeader] = b => b with { StickyTop = 8f, // CSS position:sticky, top: 8px OnPinned = p => stuck.Value = p, // the :stuck observable Fill = stuck.Value ? Tok.FillSolidBase : b.Fill, // restyle ANYTHING off the signal BrushTransitionMs = Motion.ControlFast, // …and the swap cross-fades }, } Contract (apply order): control defaults → part modifier → the control RE-ASSERTS its mechanics-critical props (click handlers, reflow specs, ref captures — chained via Chain<T>(Action<T>?, Action<T>?), never clobbered), so a modifier can restyle everything but break nothing. Modifier rules: a PURE with-copy of its input; reading a signal inside one subscribes the OWNING CONTROL (modifiers run inside its Render — that's the granular :stuck restyle loop); never WRITE a signal in a modifier; no per-frame-hot reads (use FillBind/TransformBind for those); type-preserving (a modifier that changes the record type is ignored — parts style, content SLOTS like Content/HeaderContent restructure); avoid reshaping Children (structural reconcile). List-UNIFORM (content-independent) item-chrome modifiers are cached apply-once via the parts epoch (TryApplyCached(string, Element, out Element)); per-item CONTENT differences use the PartDelta value seam (SelectorVisuals.cs), never a per-item modifier in a recycled scroll path. TemplatePartsExtensions TextEl TextTiers Color-tier modifiers for any TextEl: Ui.Body("x").Secondary(). Theme Back-compat facade over the semantic token table (Tok). The flat names here mirror what existing call sites use; each forwards to the active TokenSet, so a theme swap (Use(ThemeKind)) re-themes them too. New code should prefer Tok / Type directly. The host still injects the OS accent + Mica background through the Accent/WindowBackground setters. Tok The active design-token table + the theme switch. Author UI reads tokens via the static getters (e.g. Tok.FillCardDefault); they resolve against T at zero per-read cost. Use(ThemeKind) re-themes the whole app with one pointer write. The OS accent + Mica window background are mutable overrides on top of the set, so they survive a theme swap and let the host inject the live system accent at startup. TokenSet An immutable, baked palette of every semantic Fluent brush for one theme (mirrors WinUI's *_themeresources). Built once per theme; never mutated. Read through Tok, which swaps the active set with a single pointer write. Ui Fluent C# markup. using static FluentGpu.Dsl.Ui; to author trees as expressions. Enums ThemeKind

api/FluentGpu.Foundation.BlurHash.html.title: Class BlurHash | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Foundation.BlurHash.html.summary: Class BlurHash Namespace FluentGpu.Foundation Assembly FluentGpu.Foundation.dll BlurHash decoder — turns a compact ~20–30-char string into a tiny blurred preview (the LQIP placeholder used by Next.js blurDataURL, SwiftUI/Nuke BlurHash). The engine uploads the decoded tile as an image's initial texture so a recognizable blur shows instantly while the full-res art decodes off-thread. Pure math (no deps), AOT-safe. Algorithm: github.com/woltapp/blurhash. public static class BlurHash Inheritance object BlurHash Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods Decode(string?, int, int, Span<byte>, float) Decode hash into bgraPremul (PREMULTIPLIED BGRA8, opaque, stride = width4; at least widthheight*4 bytes). Returns false on a malformed hash. width× height ≈ 32×32 is plenty (BlurHash is low-frequency; the GPU's linear sampler does the rest). public static bool Decode(string? hash, int width, int height, Span<byte> bgraPremul, float punch = 1) Parameters hash string width int height int bgraPremul Span<byte> punch float Returns bool

api/FluentGpu.Foundation.ColorF.html.summary: Struct ColorF Namespace FluentGpu.Foundation Assembly FluentGpu.Foundation.dll Straight-alpha sRGB color (P8: brush color = straight-alpha sRGB float4). public readonly record struct ColorF : IEquatable<ColorF> Implements IEquatable<ColorF> Inherited Members ValueType.Equals(object) ValueType.GetHashCode() ValueType.ToString() object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Constructors ColorF(float, float, float, float) Straight-alpha sRGB color (P8: brush color = straight-alpha sRGB float4). public ColorF(float R, float G, float B, float A) Parameters R float G float B float A float Properties A public float A { get; init; } Property Value float B public float B { get; init; } Property Value float G public float G { get; init; } Property Value float R public float R { get; init; } Property Value float Transparent public static ColorF Transparent { get; } Property Value ColorF Methods FromHsv(float, float, float, float) public static ColorF FromHsv(float h, float s, float v, float a = 1) Parameters h float s float v float a float Returns ColorF FromRgba(byte, byte, byte, byte) public static ColorF FromRgba(byte r, byte g, byte b, byte a = 255) Parameters r byte g byte b byte a byte Returns ColorF Lerp(ColorF, ColorF, float) Linear interpolation between two colors (t in 0..1) in straight-sRGB channels — cheap, for value fades. public static ColorF Lerp(ColorF a, ColorF b, float t) Parameters a ColorF b ColorF t float Returns ColorF LerpLinear(ColorF, ColorF, float) Perceptually-correct interpolation in LINEAR light, ALPHA-WEIGHTED (true premultiplied — the engine's linear-blend/premultiplied color contract) — used for every surface/text cross-fade (hover/press ramps, implicit BrushTransitions, gradient morphs). The weighting matters when the endpoints' alphas differ: a translucent white-tinted card fill (#0DFFFFFF) cross-fading to an opaque dark solid must stay DARK mid-flight — a straight per-channel lerp passes through bright half-transparent grey (the "white flash"). For SAME-alpha endpoints the result is identical to the straight linear lerp. Alloc-free (static local helpers, no captures). public static ColorF LerpLinear(ColorF a, ColorF b, float t) Parameters a ColorF b ColorF t float Returns ColorF ToHex() public string ToHex() Returns string ToHsv() public (float H, float S, float V) ToHsv() Returns (float H, float S, float V) TryParseHex(string, out ColorF) Parse a 6- or 8-hex-digit RGB/RGBA string (with or without a leading '#'). Returns false on malformed input. public static bool TryParseHex(string s, out ColorF color) Parameters s string color ColorF Returns bool

api/FluentGpu.Foundation.Diag.html.title: Class Diag | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Foundation.Diag.html.summary: Class Diag Namespace FluentGpu.Foundation Assembly FluentGpu.Foundation.dll The standardized, reusable engine-wide diagnostics facility. Every subsystem instruments through it the same way: Diag.Count("text.glyph","rasterized"), Diag.Set("d3d12","glyphInstances", n), Diag.Event("rhi","device-lost"), using (Diag.Time("frame","record")) { … }. COST MODEL: the recording methods are [Conditional("DEBUG"), Conditional("FLUENTGPU_DIAG")], so on a Release build (with neither symbol defined) the compiler removes the call site AND the argument evaluation entirely — a Diag.Set("text.atlas","nonZero", ExpensiveScan()) costs literally nothing in production. Define FLUENTGPU_DIAG to keep diagnostics in a Release build; toggle at runtime with the AppContext switch "FluentGpu.Diagnostics" or by setting Enabled. Route output by setting Sink. public static class Diag Inheritance object Diag Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields CompiledIn public const bool CompiledIn = false Field Value bool Enabled Runtime gate (only consulted when compiled in). Defaults off unless FG_DIAG is set; AppContext switch overrides. public static bool Enabled Field Value bool Sink Where Event(string, string)/Dump(string?) output goes (e.g. Console.WriteLine, the devtools panel, a log). public static Action<string>? Sink Field Value Action<string> Methods Count(string, string, long) [Conditional("DEBUG")] [Conditional("FLUENTGPU_DIAG")] public static void Count(string category, string key, long delta = 1) Parameters category string key string delta long Dump(string?) [Conditional("DEBUG")] [Conditional("FLUENTGPU_DIAG")] public static void Dump(string? header = null) Parameters header string EnvFlag(string) public static bool EnvFlag(string name) Parameters name string Returns bool Event(string, string) [Conditional("DEBUG")] [Conditional("FLUENTGPU_DIAG")] public static void Event(string category, string message) Parameters category string message string Reset() [Conditional("DEBUG")] [Conditional("FLUENTGPU_DIAG")] public static void Reset() Set(string, string, object?) [Conditional("DEBUG")] [Conditional("FLUENTGPU_DIAG")] public static void Set(string category, string key, object? value) Parameters category string key string value object Snapshot() Aggregate snapshot of all values + counters (for the devtools panel / a dump). public static string Snapshot() Returns string Time(string, string) Scoped timing: using (Diag.Time("layout","run")) { … }. Internals elide on Release (const-false branch). public static DiagScope Time(string category, string key) Parameters category string key string Returns DiagScope

api/FluentGpu.Foundation.DragEventArgs.h.summary: Class DragEventArgs Namespace FluentGpu.Foundation Assembly FluentGpu.Foundation.dll Drag-reorder lifecycle payload for OnDragStarted/OnDragDelta/OnDragCompleted: the pointer in the dragged node's CURRENT box (Local stays ≈ the grab offset) and in window space, the accumulated gesture translation since the arming press, and the smoothed pointer velocity (px/s, ~50ms EMA) for flick/settle decisions. ONE instance is reused for the whole gesture (0 steady-state alloc per move) — handlers must copy fields they keep, never hold the reference. public sealed class DragEventArgs Inheritance object DragEventArgs Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields Absolute Pointer position in window space. public Point2 Absolute Field Value Point2 Kind public PointerKind Kind Field Value PointerKind Local Pointer position in the dragged node's CURRENT (moving) box. public Point2 Local Field Value Point2 Mods public KeyModifiers Mods Field Value KeyModifiers TotalDx Accumulated translation since the arming press — feed to ReorderList.Update. public float TotalDx Field Value float TotalDy Accumulated translation since the arming press — feed to ReorderList.Update. public float TotalDy Field Value float VelocityX Smoothed pointer velocity (px/s; ~50ms exponential-moving-average horizon). public float VelocityX Field Value float VelocityY Smoothed pointer velocity (px/s; ~50ms exponential-moving-average horizon). public float VelocityY Field Value float

api/FluentGpu.Foundation.DragSession.htm.summary: Class DragSession Namespace FluentGpu.Foundation Assembly FluentGpu.Foundation.dll THE live drag session (E5-L2): ONE mutable instance owned by Input.DragDropContext, opened when an L1 drag promotes on a chain carrying a DragSource (payload resolved once), updated per pointer move, handed to every DropTargetSpec handler, and cleared (incl. the Payload GC edge) when the gesture ends. Handlers copy what they keep — never hold the reference across gestures. public sealed class DragSession Inheritance object DragSession Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields Effect Advisory effect (engine: Move while over an accepting target, None otherwise; targets may refine). public DropEffect Effect Field Value DropEffect Kind The source's kind discriminator (accept tests are string compares, never casts). public string Kind Field Value string Mods public KeyModifiers Mods Field Value KeyModifiers OverTarget The accepting target currently under the pointer (Null when over nothing that accepts). public NodeHandle OverTarget Field Value NodeHandle Payload The typed payload (resolved once at promotion from PayloadFactory). public object? api/FluentGpu.Foundation.DropTargetSpec..title: Class DropTargetSpec | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Foundation.DropTargetSpec..summary: Class DropTargetSpec Namespace FluentGpu.Foundation Assembly FluentGpu.Foundation.dll E5-L2 drop TARGET spec (BoxEl.DropTarget — Flutter DragTarget / SwiftUI dropDestination): receives sessions whose Kind is in AcceptKinds. Discovery is hit-test-chain based — per pointer move the engine picks the NEAREST enabled accepting target under the pointer (a non-accepting target never blocks an accepting ancestor). OnEnter/OnLeave fire on hover transitions, OnOver every move while inside, OnDrop on release over it (BEFORE the L1 completion). The DragSession argument is THE one live reused instance — copy what you keep. public sealed record DropTargetSpec : IEquatable<DropTargetSpec> Inheritance object DropTargetSpec Implements IEquatable<DropTargetSpec> Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors DropTargetSpec(string[], Action<DragSession>?, Action<DragSession>?, Action<DragSession>?, Action<DragSession>?)
