api/FluentGpu.Foundation.GradientSpec.ht.summary: Struct GradientSpec Namespace FluentGpu.Foundation Assembly FluentGpu.Foundation.dll A gradient fill. AngleDeg is the linear axis (0 = left-to-right, 90 = top-to-bottom). Up to 4 stops are carried into the POD draw command; extras are ignored. When set on a node it supersedes the solid fill at record time. public readonly record struct GradientSpec : IEquatable<GradientSpec> Implements IEquatable<GradientSpec> Inherited Members ValueType.Equals(object) ValueType.GetHashCode() ValueType.ToString() object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Constructors GradientSpec(GradientShape, float, GradientStop[]) A gradient fill. AngleDeg is the linear axis (0 = left-to-right, 90 = top-to-bottom). Up to 4 stops are carried into the POD draw command; extras are ignored. When set on a node it supersedes the solid fill at record time. public GradientSpec(GradientShape Shape, float AngleDeg, GradientStop[] Stops) Parameters Shape GradientShape AngleDeg float Stops GradientStop[] Fields MaxStops public const int MaxStops = 4 Field Value int Properties AnchorEnd Anchor the absolute band at the END of the axis instead of the start — the engine encoding of WinUI's RelativeTransform ScaleTransform ScaleY="-1" mirror on elevation brushes (light ControlElevationBorder, Common_themeresources_any.xaml:382-390; AccentControlElevationBorder both themes :198-205/:397-404): the darker secondary edge sits at the BOTTOM. Only meaningful with AxisLengthPx > 0. public bool AnchorEnd { get; init; } Property Value bool AngleDeg public float AngleDeg { get; init; } Property Value float AxisLengthPx WinUI MappingMode="Absolute" (StartPoint 0,0 EndPoint 0,N): the stop ramp occupies this many PHYSICAL px along the axis — outside the band the edge stop's color holds — instead of stretching over the node's full extent. The ControlElevationBorder confines its blend to a 3px band (Common_themeresources_any.xaml:186). 0 (default) = relative-to-bounds. Applied as a record-time stop-offset remap, so the POD command and the gradient shader are unchanged. public float AxisLengthPx { get; init; } Property Value float Shape public GradientShape Shape { get; init; } Property Value GradientShape Stops public GradientStop[] Stops { get; init; } Property Value GradientStop[] Methods Solid(ColorF) A solid "gradient" (one stop) - so a flat border and a gradient border are the SAME knob (BorderBrush). public static GradientSpec Solid(ColorF color) Parameters color ColorF Returns GradientSpec Vertical(ColorF, ColorF) A vertical 2-stop linear gradient (top to bottom). public static GradientSpec Vertical(ColorF top, ColorF bottom) Parameters top ColorF bottom ColorF Returns GradientSpec

api/FluentGpu.Foundation.ImageFit.html.title: Enum ImageFit | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Foundation.ImageFit.html.summary: Enum ImageFit Namespace FluentGpu.Foundation Assembly FluentGpu.Foundation.dll How an image's pixels map into its (possibly differently-shaped) layout box — the CSS object-fit model, cleaner than WinUI's Stretch because box-sizing is a separate concern (see AspectRatio). Default Cover (what album art / thumbnails want): never distorts, never letterboxes. public enum ImageFit : byte Fields Contain = 1 Scale to fit inside the box, preserve aspect, letterbox the remainder (CSS object-fit: contain). Cover = 0 Scale to fill the box, preserve aspect, crop the overflow (CSS object-fit: cover). The default. Fill = 2 Stretch the whole texture to the box, ignoring aspect — may distort (WinUI Fill). The escape hatch. None = 3 Draw at the source's native pixel size (as DIPs), centered, cropped to the box (WinUI None).

api/FluentGpu.Foundation.ImageTransition.title: Struct ImageTransition | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Foundation.ImageTransition.summary: Struct ImageTransition Namespace FluentGpu.Foundation Assembly FluentGpu.Foundation.dll The placeholder→image reveal transition (the cross-fade) — spec'd with the engine's shared motion vocabulary (a duration in ms + an Easing curve) so image reveals animate exactly like every other control. api/FluentGpu.Foundation.SpanRunTable.ht.title: Class SpanRunTable | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Foundation.SpanRunTable.ht.summary: Class SpanRunTable Namespace FluentGpu.Foundation Assembly FluentGpu.Foundation.dll Process-wide registry of span runs — the interner-style side channel that lets the multi-run style overlay ride an int through the POD pipeline (TextStyle.SpanRunId → DrawList DrawGlyphRunCmd.SpanRunId) the way strings ride StringId through StringTable. Seam-safe (threading-render-seam.md §9, the StringTable discipline): chunked backing store — chunks are allocated once and never moved; a release-store of the count publishes each new slot, the reader acquire-loads the count before indexing. The UI thread is the single writer (reconciler, at element-change time — never in frame phases 6–13); the render-side glyph path resolves the previous frame's ids concurrently. Static Shared (deliberate): unlike StringTable (host-instanced and threaded through every constructor), the render-side consumer reaches a span run only through the POD id baked in the draw stream — there is no host seam to hand an instance through. One process = one UI thread = one writer, ids burn monotonically and are never reused, so a process-global table is safe by the same argument that makes the ids safe. Reclamation: owners (the scene's span-text side-table) pair AddRef(int)/Release(int); a run whose count hits 0 has its slot cleared after a creation-distance quarantine (≥ FluentGpu.Foundation.SpanRunTable.QuarantineDistance newer runs registered — far beyond the in-flight frame depth at any realistic churn), so a draw list recorded just before the release still resolves. A cleared id resolves null → consumers fall back to the base style. Ids are never reused. public sealed class SpanRunTable Inheritance object SpanRunTable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields Shared public static readonly SpanRunTable Shared Field Value SpanRunTable Methods AddRef(int) Take an ownership reference (UI thread; the scene's span-text side-table is the owner). public void AddRef(int id) Parameters id int Create(SpanStyle[]) Register a span run (UI thread). The spans array is taken by reference and must not be mutated after. public int Create(SpanStyle[] spans) Parameters spans SpanStyle[] Returns int Release(int) Drop an ownership reference (UI thread). The last release schedules the slot clear behind the creation-distance quarantine. Releasing id 0 / an unknown id is a no-op. public void Release(int id) Parameters id int Resolve(int) Resolve an id to its run, or null (id 0 / reclaimed). Safe from the render thread concurrently with Create(SpanStyle[]) (acquire on count, chunks never move). public SpanRun? Resolve(int id) Parameters id int Returns SpanRun

api/FluentGpu.Foundation.StringTable.htm.title: Class StringTable | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Foundation.StringTable.htm.summary:
Class StringTable Namespace FluentGpu.Foundation Assembly FluentGpu.Foundation.dll String interner with REF-COUNTED reclamation. Lives in Foundation so Render can resolve without depending on Scene.

Seam-safe (threading-render-seam.md §9): the UI thread interns (, the single writer) while the render thread resolves () the previous frame's ids concurrently. The backing store is therefore chunked: chunks are allocated once and never resized or moved, so a concurrent reader can never observe a torn or reallocated array (a plain List would race on its geometric Add-realloc). A release-store of the count publishes each new slot; the reader acquire-loads the count before indexing, so it only ever reads fully-written slots.

Reclamation (virtualization workloads): a 100k-row virtual list streams unique row text through the table — append-only interning is an unbounded leak. Owners (the scene's text columns) pair /; when an id's count hits 0 its map entry is removed (a re-intern of the same content gets a NEW id) and the slot is cleared after a reader quarantine ( per frame, far beyond the in-flight frame depth). Ids are never reused — reclaimed ids stay burned, so a stale id can never alias different content (the shaped-run cache keys on ids and ages out in well under the id space). Fully-dead chunks free their backing array. Strings never AddRef'd are simply permanent.

public sealed class StringTable Inheritance object StringTable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors StringTable() public StringTable() Properties IdHighWater The monotonic next-id counter (one past the highest minted id) — ids never reuse, so this only grows. public int IdHighWater { get; } Property Value int MapCount Live map entries (interned strings currently resolvable by content) — diagnostics/leak checks. public int MapCount { get; } Property Value int PendingReclaim Releases whose slots await the reader quarantine — diagnostics. public int PendingReclaim { get; } Property Value int PendingReclaimCount Releases whose slots await the reader quarantine (census alias of PendingReclaim). public int PendingReclaimCount { get; } Property Value int Methods AddRef(StringId) Take an ownership reference on an id (UI thread). Owners are the scene's text columns; pair with Release(StringId). public void AddRef(StringId id) Parameters id StringId Intern(string?) Intern a string to a stable id. UI-thread only (the single writer). A live id is never moved; reclaimed ids are never reused (re-interning previously-released content yields a fresh id). public StringId Intern(string? s) Parameters s string Returns StringId Release(StringId) Drop an ownership reference (UI thread). The LAST release removes the map entry and schedules the slot clear behind the reader quarantine. Releasing a never-AddRef'd (permanent) id is a no-op. public void Release(StringId id) Parameters id StringId Resolve(StringId) Resolve an id to its string. Safe to call from the render thread concurrently with Intern(string?). public string Resolve(StringId id) Parameters id StringId Returns string Tick() Advance the reclamation clock one frame (host, once per painted frame): clears slots whose quarantine elapsed and frees fully-dead chunks. UI thread. public void Tick()

api/FluentGpu.Foundation.TransitionChann.title: Enum TransitionChannels | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Foundation.TransitionChann.summary: Enum TransitionChannels Namespace FluentGpu.Foundation Assembly FluentGpu.Foundation.dll The general layout-transition spec — the entire authoring surface for "animate this node's layout changes". A plain POD (no closures) so it is hot-path safe, and it lives in Foundation (below the AnimEngine, which reads it and converts the dynamics to its internal spring/tween). It attaches to a node via BoxEl.Animate (or RenderContext.UseLayoutAnimation); the host diffs the node's presented rect vs its new laid-out rect each commit and drives the presented geometry toward target through these channels/dynamics — no relayout, no per-frame re-render, and zero control-type knowledge. [Flags] public enum TransitionChannels : byte Fields Bounds = Position | Size None = 0 Opacity = 4 Position = 1 Size = 2

api/FluentGpu.Foundation.TransitionDynam.title: Struct TransitionDynamics | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Foundation.TransitionDynam.summary: Struct TransitionDynamics Namespace FluentGpu.Foundation Assembly FluentGpu.Foundation.dll The dynamics of a transition. Springs are the default (interruptible, jump-free retarget): Response ≈ the settle time in seconds, DampingRatio 1 = critical (no overshoot), <1 = bouncy. A tween is opt-in: fixed DurationMs + Easing (an EasingSpec, so authored WinUI KeySplines are expressible; a default spec is normalized by the engine to FluentDecelerate). Stored as scalars (not the AnimEngine's SpringParams) so the spec can live in Foundation; the engine converts at seed time. A default value (all zero) is normalized by the engine to the spring defaults. public readonly record struct TransitionDynamics : IEquatable<TransitionDynamics> Implements IEquatable<TransitionDynamics> Inherited Members ValueType.Equals(object) ValueType.GetHashCode() ValueType.ToString() object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Constructors TransitionDynamics(DynamicsKind, float, float, float, EasingSpec) The dynamics of a transition. Springs are the default (interruptible, jump-free retarget): Response ≈ the settle time in seconds, DampingRatio 1 = critical (no overshoot), <1 = bouncy. A tween is opt-in: fixed DurationMs + Easing (an EasingSpec, so authored WinUI KeySplines are expressible; a default spec is normalized by the engine to FluentDecelerate). Stored as scalars (not the AnimEngine's SpringParams) so the spec can live in Foundation; the engine converts at seed time. api/FluentGpu.Foundation.html.title: Namespace FluentGpu.Foundation | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Foundation.html.summary:
Namespace FluentGpu.Foundation Classes ArenaAllocator Per-frame bump allocator. O(1) Reset(). Spans are invalidated by Reset (and by a grow). BlurHash BlurHash decoder — turns a compact ~20–30-char string into a tiny blurred preview (the LQIP placeholder used by Next.js blurDataURL, SwiftUI/Nuke BlurHash). The engine uploads the decoded tile as an image's initial texture so a recognizable blur shows instantly while the full-res art decodes off-thread. Pure math (no deps), AOT-safe. Algorithm: github.com/woltapp/blurhash. CharEventArgs Character (text) input passed to OnCharInput handlers — the layout/IME-resolved Unicode codepoint, distinct from the raw virtual-key of KeyEventArgs (Win32 splits WM_KEYDOWN from WM_CHAR; we mirror that). Diag The standardized, reusable engine-wide diagnostics facility. Every subsystem instruments through it the same way: Diag.Count("text.glyph","rasterized"), Diag.Set("d3d12","glyphInstances", n), Diag.Event("rhi","device-lost"), using (Diag.Time("frame","record")) { … }. COST MODEL: the recording methods are [Conditional("DEBUG"), Conditional("FLUENTGPU_DIAG")], so on a Release build (with neither symbol defined) the compiler removes the call site AND the argument evaluation entirely — a Diag.Set("text.atlas","nonZero", ExpensiveScan()) costs literally nothing in production. Define FLUENTGPU_DIAG to keep diagnostics in a Release build; toggle at runtime with the AppContext switch "FluentGpu.Diagnostics" or by setting Enabled. Route output by setting Sink. DragEventArgs Drag-reorder lifecycle payload for OnDragStarted/OnDragDelta/OnDragCompleted: the pointer in the dragged node's CURRENT box (Local stays ≈ the grab offset) and in window space, the accumulated gesture translation since the arming press, and the smoothed pointer velocity (px/s, ~50ms EMA) for flick/settle decisions. ONE instance is reused for the whole gesture (0 steady-state alloc per move) — handlers must copy fields they keep, never hold the reference. DragSession THE live drag session (E5-L2): ONE mutable instance owned by Input.DragDropContext, opened when an L1 drag promotes on a chain carrying a DragSource (payload resolved once), updated per pointer move, handed to every DropTargetSpec handler, and cleared (incl. the Payload GC edge) when the gesture ends. Handlers copy what they keep — never hold the reference across gestures. DragSource E5-L2 typed drag SOURCE spec (BoxEl.Draggable — the Flutter Draggable / react-beautiful-dnd model; user ruling 2026-06-10: deliberately NOT WinUI's OLE DataPackage/DoDragDrop modal loop): Kind is a string discriminator so target accept-tests are cast-free; PayloadFactory resolves the typed payload ONCE when the L1 press promotes past the drag box (never per move). Trimming-safe: plain delegates. DropTargetSpec E5-L2 drop TARGET spec (BoxEl.DropTarget — Flutter DragTarget / SwiftUI dropDestination): receives sessions whose Kind is in AcceptKinds. Discovery is hit-test-chain based — per pointer move the engine picks the NEAREST enabled accepting target under the pointer (a non-accepting target never blocks an accepting ancestor). OnEnter/OnLeave fire on hover transitions, OnOver every move while inside, OnDrop on release over it (BEFORE the L1 completion). The DragSession argument is THE one live reused instance — copy what you keep. Easings Evaluates an Easing curve at normalized time t (0..1). KeyEventArgs Keyboard event passed to node handlers during tunnel/bubble routing. Handled stops propagation. Carries the modifier chord and the auto-repeat flag (Win32 lParam bit 30) so editors can do Ctrl+arrow word navigation and Shift+arrow selection without re-querying the keyboard. Keys Well-known virtual-key codes (Win32 VK_*) used by the input router. ObjectPool Edge-only recycle pool for managed objects (Component / RenderContext). Cap-bounded. PngWriter Minimal pure-managed PNG encoder for debug frame captures — no WIC / System.Drawing, AOT-safe, zero new deps. Emits an opaque RGB PNG (color type 2) from a tightly-packed top-down BGRA8 buffer (the D3D12 readback format). Used only by the --screenshot tooling path; never on the hot frame path. PointerEventArgs Position-aware pointer-press payload for OnPointerPressed: local coords + the click count (1/2/3 — the dispatcher tracks double/triple-click timing and slop), the modifier chord, the button (0=left 1=right 2=middle) and the device kind. Allocated only on an actual press (cold user-gesture edge). SlabAllocator Stable, generation-versioned slab over T. Index 0 is reserved as the null slot, so default(Handle) is null. Generation is bumped on Free (ABA defense). O(1) alloc/free via an intrusive int free-list. Pointer-bearing T uses a side _next array (we always do, here). SpanRun A registered span run: the immutable per-span shaping overlays plus the seam-published layout artifacts. Lives in Shared; referenced from TextStyle.SpanRunId (the measure-cache / shaped-run-cache key), so a style change ⇒ a fresh run id ⇒ every downstream cache self-invalidates. SpanRunRects An immutable snapshot of a span run's laid-out rect artifacts at one wrap width. Published whole by the text seam on a measure-cache miss (content/width change) and read lock-free by the recorder (decoration bars) and the input dispatcher (hyperlink cursor/click) — steady frames never re-touch the font seam. SpanRunTable Process-wide registry of span runs — the interner-style side channel that lets the multi-run style overlay ride an int through the POD pipeline (TextStyle.SpanRunId → DrawList DrawGlyphRunCmd.SpanRunId) the way strings ride StringId through StringTable. Seam-safe (threading-render-seam.md §9, the StringTable discipline): chunked backing store — chunks are allocated once and never moved; a release-store of the count publishes each new slot, the reader acquire-loads the count before indexing. The UI thread is the single writer (reconciler, at element-change time — never in frame phases 6–13); the render-side glyph path resolves the previous frame's ids concurrently. Static Shared (deliberate): unlike StringTable (host-instanced and threaded through every constructor), the render-side consumer reaches a span run only through the POD id baked in the draw stream — there is no host seam to hand an instance through. One process = one UI thread = one writer, ids burn monotonically and are never reused, so a process-global table is safe by the same argument that makes the ids safe. Reclamation: owners (the scene's span-text side-table) pair AddRef(int)/Release(int); a run whose count hits 0 has its slot cleared after a creation-distance quarantine (≥ FluentGpu.Foundation.SpanRunTable.QuarantineDistance newer runs registered — far beyond the in-flight frame depth at any realistic churn), so a draw list recorded just before the release still resolves. A cleared id resolves null → consumers fall back to the base style. Ids are never reused. StringTable String interner with REF-COUNTED reclamation. Lives in Foundation so Render can resolve without depending on Scene.

Seam-safe (threading-render-seam.md §9): the UI thread interns (, the single writer) while the render thread resolves () the previous frame's ids concurrently. The backing store is therefore chunked: chunks are allocated once and never resized or moved, so a concurrent reader can never observe a torn or reallocated array (a plain List would race on its geometric Add-realloc). A release-store of the count publishes each new slot; the reader acquire-loads the count before indexing, so it only ever reads fully-written slots.

Reclamation (virtualization workloads): a 100k-row virtual list streams unique row text through the table — append-only interning is an unbounded leak. Owners (the scene's text columns) pair /; when an id's count hits 0 its map entry is removed (a re-intern of the same content gets a NEW id) and the slot is cleared after a reader quarantine ( per frame, far beyond the in-flight frame depth). Ids are never reused — reclaimed ids stay burned, so a stale id can never alias different content (the shaped-run cache keys on ids and ages out in well under the id space). Fully-dead chunks free their backing array. Strings never AddRef'd are simply permanent.

WheelEventArgs Element-level wheel payload for OnPointerWheel (WinUI PointerWheelChanged): the platform wheel delta — the same value the viewport scroll path consumes — plus the modifier chord. Setting Handled stops the dispatcher from scrolling the enclosing viewport (NumberBox value-stepping inside a scrollable form, NumberBox.cpp:578-597 OnNumberBoxScroll marks the routed event handled). Unhandled, the wheel keeps walking up to the next handler / the nearest scrollable (routed-event semantics). Allocated per wheel event (cold edge). Structs AcrylicSpec A per-node acrylic (frosted glass): the engine samples the canvas behind the node, resolves transparent backdrop through Fallback, blurs it (BlurSigma), then applies WinUI's luminosity/tint recipe. Realized by the PushLayer/PopLayer backdrop subsystem. Affine2D 2x3 affine (local→parent). 2.5D/perspective out of scope (per spec). ArcSpec A circular-arc stroke (WinUI ProgressRing — a trimmed, round-capped ring). The arc is centred in the node's box with radius (min(W,H)-Thickness)/2, a Thickness-wide stroke, swept from StartDeg for SweepDeg degrees (0° = 12 o'clock, clockwise). The recorder emits a DrawArc; the GPU draws the SDF arc with round caps. A full ring is SweepDeg = 360. BrushHandle Typed wrapper for brushes interned in the BrushTable. ColorF Straight-alpha sRGB color (P8: brush color = straight-alpha sRGB float4). CornerRadius4 CursorId DiagScope Timing scope from Time(string, string). Zero-work on Release (the Diag.CompiledIn const folds out). EasingSpec Edges4 Per-edge thickness (margin/padding/border). [InlineArray]-shaped logically; struct of 4 floats here. EnterExit Presented-space terminal for an inserted/removed node: where it animates FROM on enter / TO on exit (offset + scale + opacity), relative to its laid-out rect. Active=false ⇒ the node simply snaps in/out. GradientSpec A gradient fill. AngleDeg is the linear axis (0 = left-to-right, 90 = top-to-bottom). Up to 4 stops are carried into the POD draw command; extras are ignored. When set on a node it supersedes the solid fill at record time. GradientStop One gradient stop: a normalized position (0..1 along the gradient axis) and its color. Handle Generational handle — 8 bytes {u32 index, u32 gen}. The canonical FluentGpu handle layout. ImageTransition The placeholder→image reveal transition (the cross-fade) — spec'd with the engine's shared motion vocabulary (a duration in ms + an Easing curve) so image reveals animate exactly like every other control. Override per-image via ImageEl.Transition, or change the app-wide Default once at startup. Set DurationMs = 0 (or use None) to DISABLE the fade — the image then appears instantly. KeyAccelerator A keyboard accelerator chord (WinUI KeyboardAccelerator): Key + Mods invoke the owning node's click handler from anywhere (dispatched after focused routing leaves the key unhandled). LayoutTransition The whole authoring surface (interned POD). Channels × dynamics × size-mode × enter/exit compose orthogonally — translate / scale / rotate / opacity / clip-reveal all fall out, with no per-control special cases. NativeHandle POD across the PAL/RHI seam — never a boxed object. The D3D12 leaf switches on Kind. NodeHandle Zero-cost typed wrapper over Handle for nodes in the SceneStore. Point2 PolylineStrokeSpec A retained stroked polyline with normalized trim controls. Points are local DIP coordinates inside the node's box; trim values are path-distance fractions (0..1), so animation can reveal the stroke in draw order. RectF Axis-aligned rectangle in DIPs. Local to its node (P8: Bounds are node-LOCAL). ShadowSpec A drop shadow / elevation descriptor (CSS box-shadow shape). Lives in Foundation so Scene/Render/Reconciler can read it without referencing Dsl. Carried by an elevated node; the recorder emits a DrawShadow beneath the fill. Size2 SpanRect One laid-out rect artifact of a span run (paragraph-node-local DIP): the line-fragment geometry of a decorated/link span. Kind is the single SpanStyle flag bit it serves — Link rects are the FULL line-fragment band (hit-testing, Hand cursor); Underline/Strikethrough rects are the positioned BARS (the recorder fills them verbatim, no font-seam touch at record time). SpanStyle The POD shaping overlay for one span of a span run: the UTF-16 char range [Start, End) of the CONCATENATED paragraph text plus the style deltas the text seam applies over the base TextStyle. Weight 0 / SizeDip 0 / FontFamily empty / Color A==0 = inherit the base. Built by the reconciler from TextSpans; resolved by both text backends and the glyph renderer through Shared, so measure ≡ hit-test ≡ render for span runs too. StringId 4-byte interned string id. Id 0 = empty. No string reaches the paint path. TextSpan One inline run of a rich-text paragraph (the WinUI TextBlock.Inlines Run/Bold/Hyperlink model, rtb-01). A SpanTextEl paragraph is an array of these; the WHOLE paragraph shapes as ONE flow (spans concatenated, wrapped together — exactly how WinUI lays a paragraph's inline collection, RichTextBlock.g.h:63 get_Blocks). Weight 0 / Size null / FontFamily null / Color A==0 all mean "inherit the paragraph base". OnClick non-null makes this a HYPERLINK span: the engine resolves the Hand cursor over the span's laid-out rects and fires the action on click/tap — WinUI's inline Hyperlink (SetCursor(MouseCursorHand) on hyperlink hover, RichTextBlock.cpp:2995 / TextBlock.cpp:3488). Accent foreground + underline styling is the CALLER's job (HyperlinkForeground theming, generic.xaml:1120-1122). No italic axis: the shaper resolves faces by (family, weight) only — DWRITE_FONT_STYLE_NORMAL is fixed (TextLayoutEngine face resolution); FontStyle arrives with the italics workstream (plan §Text display). TrackSize TransitionDynamics The dynamics of a transition. Springs are the default (interruptible, jump-free retarget): Response ≈ the settle time in seconds, DampingRatio 1 = critical (no overshoot), <1 = bouncy. A tween is opt-in: fixed DurationMs + Easing (an EasingSpec, so authored WinUI KeySplines are expressible; a default spec is normalized by the engine to FluentDecelerate). Stored as scalars (not the AnimEngine's SpringParams) so the spec can live in Foundation; the engine converts at seed time. A default value (all zero) is normalized by the engine to the spring defaults. Enums AutomationRole The semantic role of a node, set by the control factories (a button IS a BoxEl, so the role is how it announces itself). Carried on the input/a11y column; surfaced to the future UIA layer (ControlType), devtools, and UI tests. Default None = a plain box/container. DropEffect The semantic outcome of an IN-APP drop (E5-L2 — the Flutter/SwiftUI model, never OLE): advisory — the engine sets Move while over an accepting target, None otherwise; targets may refine it in OnEnter/OnOver. DynamicTextKind Retained text slots whose contents are refreshed by the host without re-rendering or relayout. DynamicsKind Spring (velocity-carrying, interruptible — the default) or an eased fixed-duration tween. Easing Easing curves — a FOUNDATIONAL motion primitive shared by every layer that animates: the animation-track engine, declarative transitions, and the image placeholder→art cross-fade. Living in Foundation lets any subsystem (Dsl, Scene, Render, Animation) spec a transition with the same vocabulary. Includes the real Fluent 2 / WinUI curves. FlexAlign Cross-axis alignment (flexbox align-items / align-self). Auto on a child = inherit the container's align-items. FlexJustify Main-axis distribution of free space (flexbox justify-content). GradientShape ImageFit How an image's pixels map into its (possibly differently-shaped) layout box — the CSS object-fit model, cleaner than WinUI's Stretch because box-sizing is a separate concern (see AspectRatio). Default Cover (what album art / thumbnails want): never distorts, never letterboxes. KeyModifiers Keyboard modifier state, captured per input event at the platform pump (Win32 GetKeyState). LineStacking How an explicit LineHeight interacts with the font-natural line box (mirrors WinUI LineStackingStrategy; BaselineToBaseline is not modeled). MaxHeight — the WinUI BaseTextBlockStyle default (TextBlock_themeresources.xaml:16) — makes the line advance max(font-natural, LineHeight); BlockLineHeight makes the line advance exactly LineHeight (lines may visually overlap when smaller than natural). NativeHandleKind NodeFlags Per-node dirty + state bits (a single 32-bit SceneStore column). Three orthogonal dirty axes. PointerKind The physical pointer device class an event came from (WinUI PointerDeviceType) — controls touch-vs-mouse behavioral splits (RatingControl hover scale, touch paddings). SizeAnchor Which edge of a Reflow node its CONTENT is anchored to while the size animates. Leading = content stays put, the far edge sweeps (a wipe). Trailing = the content's end edge rides the animated edge (the WinUI Expander "slide out from under the header": at reveal 0 the content sits fully behind the leading edge; its trailing rounded corners stay visible mid-motion). Applied by the recorder as a child-group offset — compositor-composed, no per-child knowledge. SizeMode How a SIZE change is animated. Auto picks per node: pure-chrome leaf → ScaleCorrect; a text/wrapping subtree under budget → Relayout; everything else → Reveal. TextLineBounds Which vertical band a text line reports as its box (mirrors WinUI TextLineBounds, minimum set — TrimToCapHeight/TrimToBaseline individually are not modeled). Full — the WinUI BaseTextBlockStyle default (TextBlock_themeresources.xaml:17) — is the font-natural ascent..descent box; Tight trims the box to cap-height..baseline so vertical centering is optical (e.g. PersonPicture initials, PersonPicture.xaml:66). TextTrim How overflowing text is truncated (mirrors WinUI TextTrimming). Applies to the last visible line. TextWrap How a text run breaks across lines (mirrors WinUI TextWrapping). TrackKind A CSS-Grid track sizing function. (Lives in Foundation so both the DSL and the layout/scene see it.) TransitionChannels The general layout-transition spec — the entire authoring surface for "animate this node's layout changes". A plain POD (no closures) so it is hot-path safe, and it lives in Foundation (below the AnimEngine, which reads it and converts the dynamics to its internal spring/tween). It attaches to a node via BoxEl.Animate (or RenderContext.UseLayoutAnimation); the host diffs the node's presented rect vs its new laid-out rect each commit and drives the presented geometry toward target through these channels/dynamics — no relayout, no per-frame re-render, and zero control-type knowledge.

api/FluentGpu.Hooks.ComponentEl.html.summary: Class ComponentEl Namespace FluentGpu.Hooks Assembly FluentGpu.Hooks.dll An element that embeds a child Component (with its own hooks/state) into a parent's render output. The reconciler instantiates the component once (via Factory), reuses it across renders of the same ComponentType, and reconciles its rendered output as this node's content. This is what lets the framework compose many stateful components rather than one root. public sealed record ComponentEl : Element, IEquatable<Element>, IEquatable<ComponentEl> Inheritance object Element ComponentEl Implements IEquatable<Element> IEquatable<ComponentEl> Inherited Members Element.Key object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors ComponentEl(Func<Component>, Type) An element that embeds a child Component (with its own hooks/state) into a parent's render output. api/FluentGpu.Hooks.ReactiveComponent.ht.title: Class ReactiveComponent | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Hooks.ReactiveComponent.ht.summary: Class ReactiveComponent Namespace FluentGpu.Hooks Assembly FluentGpu.Hooks.dll A signals-native component: Setup() runs ONCE at mount (the SolidJS model) — create signals, wire bindings (TransformBind/For/Show) and return the tree. The component never re-renders; fine-grained bindings update exactly the nodes that read a changed signal. Use this for the highest-performance UI; use plain Component when you prefer the re-render model (still granular). public abstract class ReactiveComponent : Component Inheritance object Component ReactiveComponent Inherited Members Component.Context Component.UseState<T>(T) Component.UseSignal<T>(T) Component.UseFloatSignal(float) Component.UseComputed<T>(Func<T>) Component.UseEffect(Action, params object[]) Component.UseLayoutEffect(Action, params object[]) Component.UseReducer<TState, TAction>(Func<TState, TAction, TState>, TState) Component.UseMemo<T>(Func<T>, params object[]) Component.UseRef<T>(T) Component.UseContext<T>(Context<T>) Component.UseAnimatedValue(float, float, Easing) Component.UseSpring(AnimChannel, float, SpringParams, params object[]) Component.UseTransition(AnimChannel, float, float, float, Easing, params object[]) Component.UseImage(string, int, ImagePriority, string) Component.PrefetchImage(string, int) Component.UseKeyframes(AnimChannel, Keyframe[], float, bool, params object[]) Component.UseDrivenAnimation(AnimChannel, Keyframe[], Func<float>, float, float, params object[]) Component.RenderWithHooks() Component.Unmount() object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods MotionHooks.UseEntrance(Component, float, object?) api/FluentGpu.Hooks.RenderContext.html.summary: Class RenderContext Namespace FluentGpu.Hooks Assembly FluentGpu.Hooks.dll Per-component hook storage (ordered; rules-of-hooks = stable call order). In the signals-first model a component is a reactive computation (its render-effect); UseState<T>(T) is a Signal<T> whose only subscriber is that render-effect, so a setState re-renders ONLY this component's subtree (granular), and UseContext<T>(Context<T>) subscribes the render-effect to the provider's signal so a context change propagates to exactly the consumers that read it. High-frequency scalars get UseSignal<T>(T) bound straight to a node channel — no re-render at all. public sealed class RenderContext Inheritance object RenderContext Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields AnchorNode public NodeHandle AnchorNode Field Value NodeHandle Anim public AnimEngine? api/FluentGpu.Hooks.html.title: Namespace FluentGpu.Hooks | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Hooks.html.summary: Namespace FluentGpu.Hooks Classes Component Base for stateful components. Override Render(); use hooks in stable order each render. ComponentEl An element that embeds a child Component (with its own hooks/state) into a parent's render output. The reconciler instantiates the component once (via Factory), reuses it across renders of the same ComponentType, and reconciles its rendered output as this node's content. This is what lets the framework compose many stateful components rather than one root. ContextProviderEl Provides a context value to its subtree (the React Context.Provider). One child. ContextStack Reconcile-time shadow stack of provided context values. The reconciler pushes a provider's value before reconciling its subtree (incl. nested component renders) and pops after; UseContext reads the top. (Push/pop happen on the dirty reconcile path, never the zero-alloc paint half.) Context<T> A context channel with a default. Consumers read the nearest provider's value via UseContext. Ctx Fluent: Ctx.Provide(MyContext, value, child). Embed Fluent helper: Embed.Comp(() => new MyComponent()) embeds a stateful child component. Flow Fluent reactive control-flow: Flow.Show(() => open.Value, panel), Flow.For(() => xs.Value.Count, i => Row(xs.Value[i])). ForEl Reactive keyed list (the Solid <For>). Count / ItemAt read signals; the reconciler runs them inside a boundary effect and re-realizes the children through the existing keyed diff when the list changes — rows are created/moved/removed (and their state preserved by key), with no parent re-render. FrameClock A per-frame tick the host bumps (only while something subscribes, so it's free when idle). A tree-level concern that must poll each frame — e.g. an overlay driving a timed close animation to completion — reads UseContext(FrameClock.Tick) to re-render every frame for as long as it's mounted. InputHooks A host-owned registration surface a tree-level concern can hook into without the host depending on it. The host bridges KeyPreview into the input dispatcher's pre-focus key hook, and runs AfterAnimations after the animation engine ticks but before recording. Read the host instance via UseContext(InputHooks.Current). MotionHooks Reusable Fluent motion patterns built on the component's animation hooks. The entrance mirrors WinUI's implicit show transition (Offset From 0,24 + Opacity 0→1, 400/200ms, the real Fluent decelerate curve). Honors ReducedMotion. ReactiveComponent A signals-native component: Setup() runs ONCE at mount (the SolidJS model) — create signals, wire bindings (TransformBind/For/Show) and return the tree. The component never re-renders; fine-grained bindings update exactly the nodes that read a changed signal. Use this for the highest-performance UI; use plain Component when you prefer the re-render model (still granular). Ref<T> A stable, mutable per-component box that persists across renders (the React useRef container). RenderContext Per-component hook storage (ordered; rules-of-hooks = stable call order). In the signals-first model a component is a reactive computation (its render-effect); UseState<T>(T) is a Signal<T> whose only subscriber is that render-effect, so a setState re-renders ONLY this component's subtree (granular), and UseContext<T>(Context<T>) subscribes the render-effect to the provider's signal so a context change propagates to exactly the consumers that read it. High-frequency scalars get UseSignal<T>(T) bound straight to a node channel — no re-render at all. ShowEl Reactive conditional (the Solid <Show>). When reads signals; the reconciler runs it inside a boundary effect and mounts Then / Else as the condition flips — structural change only, no parent re-render. Viewport The ambient client size (DIP), pushed by the host each frame so components can adapt to available width (responsive layout / NavigationView display modes). Read with UseContext(Viewport.Size).

api/FluentGpu.Input.DragController.html.title: Class DragController | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Input.DragController.html.summary: Class DragController Namespace FluentGpu.Input Assembly FluentGpu.Input.dll The drag-reorder gesture engine (E5). A left press on (or inside) a CanDrag node ARMS a candidate; pointer travel outside the drag box PROMOTES it from a click to a drag (the dispatcher then suppresses the click and routes every move here — capture semantics). The promoted drag draws its visual on the dragged node itself: a parent-space LocalTransform translate + 0.8 opacity + a flyout-class shadow (WinUI ListViewItemDragThemeOpacity = 0.80 — microsoft-ui-xaml controls\dev\CommonStyles\ListViewItem_themeresources.xaml:7), and the node stops hit-testing so drop-target queries see THROUGH the moving visual. Threshold: the Windows drag box is SM_CXDRAG/SM_CYDRAG (4px default), tested per-axis (dx > maxDx || dy > maxDy — microsoft-ui-xaml dxaml\xcp\dxaml\lib\ListViewBaseItem_Partial.cpp:1864-1878). WinUI's list items double it (LISTVIEWBASEITEM_MOUSE_DRAG_THRESHOLD_MULTIPLIER = 2.0, same file :54); this engine uses the 4px base box per plan E5, matching ClickSlopPx. Axis-aware arena-lite (promotion-time arbitration): the item's reorder axis is its PARENT container's main axis (row → horizontal item-drag). A gesture whose dominant axis is PERPENDICULAR to that, with a scrollable ancestor that actually overflows along the gesture axis, yields to the pan (vertical pan over a scrollable beats a horizontal item-drag) — the WinUI manipulation-arena outcome for a tab strip inside a scrolling page. Drop-settle rides the existing FLIP pipeline: Complete restores the resting visuals and reports (draggedRect → restingRect) through OnSettle; the host wires that to AnimEngine.AnimateBounds, so the seeded position spring is retargeted velocity-continuously by the same commit's ApplyProjections when the app's OnDragCompleted handler reorders the collection — displaced siblings and the dropped item all animate through the one layout-transition path (reorder hints come free). Mid-drag live reorder rides the same pipeline: when the consumer re-projects the children ORDER at the dwell-committed target (ReorderList.ProjectOrder), displaced siblings FLIP to their new slots while Move(Point2, KeyModifiers, uint) RE-ANCHORS the pointer-held visual — it strips the translate it last applied to find the node's CURRENT resting origin, then aims at (grab origin + gesture delta), so a slot move under the pointer (or an ancestor scrolling) never jumps the visual (the WinUI outcome: the dragged item tracks the pointer while MoveItemsForLiveReorder shifts the rest — ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:2254). The drag visuals are re-asserted on every move because a mid-drag commit's patch restores the authored opacity/shadow/hit-test (Reconciler ApplyBox writes them unconditionally). GHOST (E5-L2): while a drag is active the lifted node carries DragGhost and is published as DragGhost — the recorder excludes it from the clipped main pass and re-walks its subtree in an UNCLIPPED top band emitted last (mirroring the orphan pass), so the visual escapes every ancestor scissor (a row dragged out of a clipped list keeps drawing) and paints above overlays — the Flutter/rbd ghost layer. Spring-lag follow (the rbd feel): the PRESENTED translate eases toward the gesture target with a critically-damped spring (FollowOmega) instead of pinning rigidly — 0-alloc per move/tick. It engages only when the platform delivers real timestamps (consecutive non-zero TimestampMs); 0-stamp gestures (the headless default) snap presented == target, keeping every position assertion deterministic. SpringFollow false disables it outright. The host advances in-between-move easing (and the pointer-pin while an edge auto-scroll moves the resting origin under a still pointer) via Tick(float). public sealed class DragController Inheritance object DragController Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors DragController(SceneStore, Action) public DragController(SceneStore scene, Action requestRerender) Parameters scene SceneStore requestRerender Action Fields DragOpacity Dragged-visual opacity — WinUI ListViewItemDragThemeOpacity = 0.80 (ListViewItem_themeresources.xaml:7, identical in all ThemeDictionaries incl. _perf2026). public const float DragOpacity = 0.8 Field Value float DragShadow Flyout-class soft shadow under the drag visual (the engine's analytic equivalent of the 32px-depth ThemeShadow WinUI gives lifted drag visuals; values match Dsl.Elevation.Flyout — Input cannot reference Dsl). public static readonly ShadowSpec DragShadow Field Value ShadowSpec DragThresholdPx The drag box half-extent, tested per-axis (Win32 SM_CXDRAG/SM_CYDRAG default = 4; ListViewBaseItem_Partial.cpp:1871-1877. WinUI list items apply a ×2 multiplier — see class remarks). public const float DragThresholdPx = 4 Field Value float FollowOmega Critically-damped spring rate (rad/s) of the ghost's pointer-follow lag — ~150ms visual settle, the react-beautiful-dnd "the ghost breathes behind the pointer" feel. Engine value (the WinUI OLE drag visual is an OS layer with no published spring; the adopted model is Flutter/rbd per the E5 user ruling). public const float FollowOmega = 38 Field Value float OnSettle Set by the host: the drop-settle seam. Fired after Complete(Point2, KeyModifiers, uint, bool)/Cancel() restored the resting visuals, with the dragged presented rect and the resting rect — wire to AnimEngine.AnimateBounds(node, fromAbs, toAbs, spec) so the visual glides from the drop point into its slot (and is velocity-continuously retargeted by the reorder commit's FLIP pass). Null ⇒ the visual snaps home. public Action<NodeHandle, RectF, RectF>? OnSettle Field Value Action<NodeHandle, RectF, RectF> Properties ActiveNode The node whose drag is in flight (Null when idle/armed). The host's FLIP pass must SKIP this node — the pointer owns its presented transform until the drag ends. public NodeHandle ActiveNode { get; } Property Value NodeHandle HasActiveWork True while the presented (spring-lagged) translate is still easing toward the gesture target — the host keeps frames coming and calls Tick(float). Always false for snap-tracking gestures. public bool HasActiveWork { get; } Property Value bool IsActive A drag is in flight (threshold crossed; the pointer is owned until release / Escape / cancel). public bool IsActive { get; } Property Value bool IsArmed A candidate is armed (press seen, threshold not yet crossed). public bool IsArmed { get; } Property Value bool SpringFollow Enable the critically-damped pointer-follow lag (default true). It only engages on gestures whose events carry real platform timestamps — 0-stamp (headless) gestures always track exactly. public bool SpringFollow { get; set; } Property Value bool VelocityX The smoothed pointer velocity (px/s, ~50ms EMA) — fed into the L2 DragDropContext session. public float VelocityX { get; } Property Value float VelocityY public float VelocityY { get; } Property Value float Methods Cancel() Abort the gesture (Escape / pointer-capture loss / window blur): restore the resting visuals, fire OnDragCanceled, and glide the visual home via OnSettle. A no-op when idle; an armed-only candidate silently disarms (WinUI: a canceled drag never raises a click or a drop). public void Cancel() Complete(Point2, KeyModifiers, uint, bool) Release after an active drag: restore the resting visuals, fire OnDragCompleted (the app commits the reorder here), then hand the (dragged → resting) rects to OnSettle for the FLIP glide. Returns true iff a drag was active — the dispatcher suppresses the click. An armed-only candidate just disarms. suppressSettle (E5-L2): an accepted DROP on a non-reorder target skips the glide — the payload was deposited there, so the visual snaps home instead of springing back (SettleOnDrop opts a reorder target back into the glide). public bool Complete(Point2 abs, KeyModifiers mods, uint timestampMs, bool suppressSettle = false) Parameters abs Point2 mods KeyModifiers timestampMs uint suppressSettle bool Returns bool Disarm() Drop an armed candidate that never promoted (release inside the drag box ⇒ a plain click). public void Disarm() Move(Point2, KeyModifiers, uint) Pointer move while armed or active. Armed: crossing the drag box either PROMOTES (fires OnDragStarted then the first OnDragDelta) or YIELDS to a cross-axis pan (arena-lite) and disarms. Active: applies the parent-space translate and fires OnDragDelta. Returns true iff the gesture owns the pointer after this call (the dispatcher then skips hover/scroll/slider routing). public bool Move(Point2 abs, KeyModifiers mods, uint timestampMs) Parameters abs Point2 mods KeyModifiers timestampMs uint Returns bool PruneDead() Called at dispatch start: an armed/active node freed by a reconcile is abandoned WITHOUT touching its (dead) columns — the visual state died with the slot. public void PruneDead() Tick(float) Phase-7 host tick: re-aim at the node's CURRENT resting origin (an edge auto-scroll moves it under a still pointer — the ghost must stay pinned to the grab point) and advance the spring-lag easing between pointer moves. Returns true while the presented translate moved (the host requests the next frame). 0-alloc. public bool Tick(float dtMs) Parameters dtMs float Returns bool TryArm(NodeHandle, Point2, PointerKind, KeyModifiers, uint) Arm a drag candidate from a left press: walk up from pressTarget for the nearest enabled node carrying DragBit (a press on a child of a draggable row arms the row, like WinUI's item container). Returns false when nothing in the chain is draggable. public bool TryArm(NodeHandle pressTarget, Point2 abs, PointerKind kind, KeyModifiers mods, uint timestampMs) Parameters pressTarget NodeHandle abs Point2 kind PointerKind mods KeyModifiers timestampMs uint Returns bool

api/FluentGpu.Input.DragDropContext.html.title: Class DragDropContext | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Input.DragDropContext.html.summary: Class DragDropContext Namespace FluentGpu.Input Assembly FluentGpu.Input.dll L2 of the drag-drop stack (plan E5; user ruling 2026-06-10: deliberately NOT WinUI's OLE DataPackage/DoDragDrop modal loop — the Flutter Draggable/DragTarget + react-beautiful-dnd context + SwiftUI draggable/dropDestination model instead; the frame loop STAYS LIVE for the whole gesture). Layered on the L1 DragController gesture: • A BoxEl.Draggable (DragSource: a string Kind discriminator + a payload factory) marks a node a typed drag source. When the L1 press promotes past the drag box, the dispatcher calls TryBegin(NodeHandle, Point2, KeyModifiers, PointerKind) — the nearest enabled DragSource up the chain opens THE session and its PayloadFactory resolves ONCE (cold gesture edge; never per-move). • A BoxEl.DropTarget (DropTargetSpec) makes ANY surface a candidate receiver. Per pointer move the dispatcher hands Move(NodeHandle, Point2, float, float, KeyModifiers) the hit-test chain under the pointer; the nearest enabled target whose AcceptKinds contains the session's Kind becomes OverTarget (a non-accepting target never blocks an accepting ancestor — the Flutter DragTarget pass-through). The dragged subtree can never self-target: L1 already cleared its HitTestVisible, so hit-testing sees THROUGH the moving visual. • Enter/Leave fire on target transitions, Over on every move while inside, Drop on release over an accepting target (TryDrop(Point2, KeyModifiers, out bool) — fired BEFORE the L1 completion so the target reads the live session). The ONE mutable DragSession instance is reused for the whole gesture (0 steady-state alloc per move); handlers copy what they keep. Edge auto-scroll (the rbd/WinUI "drag near the viewport edge keeps scrolling" behavior) is engine-level here so every drag gets it: while a session is live, the pointer entering the hot zone of the nearest OVERFLOWING scrollable ancestor arms a proportional scroll velocity the host drives via Tick(float). Constants and behavior are WinUI's (microsoft-ui-xaml dxaml\xcp\dxaml\lib\ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp): a 100px edge band (:39 LISTVIEWBASE_EDGE_SCROLL_EDGE_WIDTH_PX), speed varying LINEARLY from 1500 px/s at the edge to 150 px/s at the band's inner rim (:42-47), a 50ms delay-start with instant velocity updates once running (:40, :1749-1753), the start-edge tried before the end-edge (:1660-1707), and scrolling suppressed when already pinned to that boundary (:1686-1690, :1718-1722). public sealed class DragDropContext Inheritance object DragDropContext Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors DragDropContext(SceneStore, Action) public DragDropContext(SceneStore scene, Action requestRerender) Parameters scene SceneStore requestRerender Action Fields EdgeScrollMaxSpeed Speed AT the edge — LISTVIEWBASE_EDGE_SCROLL_MAX_SPEED = 1500 px/s (:47). public const float EdgeScrollMaxSpeed = 1500 Field Value float EdgeScrollMinSpeed Speed at the inner rim of the zone — LISTVIEWBASE_EDGE_SCROLL_MIN_SPEED = 150 px/s (:46). public const float EdgeScrollMinSpeed = 150 Field Value float EdgeScrollStartDelayMs Delay before an armed edge scroll starts moving — LISTVIEWBASE_EDGE_SCROLL_START_DELAY_MSEC = 50 (:40). Velocity CHANGES while running apply instantly (:1749-1753). public const float EdgeScrollStartDelayMs = 50 Field Value float EdgeScrollZonePx Edge hot-zone width — LISTVIEWBASE_EDGE_SCROLL_EDGE_WIDTH_PX = 100 (ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:39). public const float EdgeScrollZonePx = 100 Field Value float Properties HasActiveWork True while an edge auto-scroll is armed or running — the host keeps frames coming and calls Tick(float). public bool HasActiveWork { get; } Property Value bool IsActive A typed session is live (an L1 drag promoted on a chain carrying a DragSource). public bool IsActive { get; } Property Value bool OverTarget The accepting target currently under the pointer (Null when none / idle). public NodeHandle OverTarget { get; } Property Value NodeHandle Session THE live session object (meaningful only while IsActive). One instance, reused. public DragSession Session { get; } Property Value DragSession Methods Cancel() Abort the session (Escape / capture loss / window blur / release over nothing accepting follows TryDrop(Point2, KeyModifiers, out bool) returning false): fires OnLeave on a live current target, then resets. The L1 spring-back (the dispatcher's subsequent Cancel()/Complete) glides the visual home. No-op when idle. public void Cancel() Move(NodeHandle, Point2, float, float, KeyModifiers) Per pointer move while the session is live: update the session coords/velocity, resolve the nearest accepting target on hit's parent chain, fire Enter/Leave on transitions and Over while inside, then re-evaluate the edge auto-scroll. 0-alloc. public void Move(NodeHandle hit, Point2 abs, float velocityX, float velocityY, KeyModifiers mods) Parameters hit NodeHandle abs Point2 velocityX float velocityY float mods KeyModifiers PruneDead() Called at dispatch start: a session whose SOURCE was freed by a reconcile ends (Leave fires on a live target); a freed TARGET/viewport is dropped silently (its columns are dead). public void PruneDead() Tick(float) Phase-7 host tick: drive the armed edge auto-scroll — hold the 50ms delay-start, then scroll the viewport by velocity·dt through FluentGpu.Input.DragDropContext.ScrollBy (clamped writes; hitting the boundary stops it, the WinUI at-the-edge suppression). Returns true while armed/scrolling so the host keeps frames coming. 0-alloc. public bool Tick(float dtMs) Parameters dtMs float Returns bool TryBegin(NodeHandle, Point2, KeyModifiers, PointerKind) Called by the dispatcher at L1 promotion: walk up from the promoted node for the nearest ENABLED DragSource, resolve its payload once, and open the session. Returns false when the chain carries no source — the gesture stays a plain L1 reorder drag and drop targets never see it. public bool TryBegin(NodeHandle promoted, Point2 abs, KeyModifiers mods, PointerKind kind) Parameters promoted NodeHandle abs Point2 mods KeyModifiers kind PointerKind Returns bool TryDrop(Point2, KeyModifiers, out bool) Release: when the pointer is over an accepting target, fire OnDrop(session) and close the session — called by the dispatcher BEFORE the L1 Complete(Point2, KeyModifiers, uint, bool) so the target reads the live session. Returns true on a drop; settleGlide = the target's SettleOnDrop (true ⇒ keep the L1 drop-settle glide — reorder targets; false ⇒ the visual snaps home, the "deposited" feel of a foreign-surface drop). public bool TryDrop(Point2 abs, KeyModifiers mods, out bool settleGlide) Parameters abs Point2 mods KeyModifiers settleGlide bool Returns bool

api/FluentGpu.Input.html.title: Namespace FluentGpu.Input | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Input.html.summary: Namespace FluentGpu.Input Classes DragController The drag-reorder gesture engine (E5). A left press on (or inside) a CanDrag node ARMS a candidate; pointer travel outside the drag box PROMOTES it from a click to a drag (the dispatcher then suppresses the click and routes every move here — capture semantics). The promoted drag draws its visual on the dragged node itself: a parent-space LocalTransform translate + 0.8 opacity + a flyout-class shadow (WinUI ListViewItemDragThemeOpacity = 0.80 — microsoft-ui-xaml controls\dev\CommonStyles\ListViewItem_themeresources.xaml:7), and the node stops hit-testing so drop-target queries see THROUGH the moving visual. Threshold: the Windows drag box is SM_CXDRAG/SM_CYDRAG (4px default), tested per-axis (dx > maxDx || dy > maxDy — microsoft-ui-xaml dxaml\xcp\dxaml\lib\ListViewBaseItem_Partial.cpp:1864-1878). WinUI's list items double it (LISTVIEWBASEITEM_MOUSE_DRAG_THRESHOLD_MULTIPLIER = 2.0, same file :54); this engine uses the 4px base box per plan E5, matching ClickSlopPx. Axis-aware arena-lite (promotion-time arbitration): the item's reorder axis is its PARENT container's main axis (row → horizontal item-drag). A gesture whose dominant axis is PERPENDICULAR to that, with a scrollable ancestor that actually overflows along the gesture axis, yields to the pan (vertical pan over a scrollable beats a horizontal item-drag) — the WinUI manipulation-arena outcome for a tab strip inside a scrolling page. Drop-settle rides the existing FLIP pipeline: Complete restores the resting visuals and reports (draggedRect → restingRect) through OnSettle; the host wires that to AnimEngine.AnimateBounds, so the seeded position spring is retargeted velocity-continuously by the same commit's ApplyProjections when the app's OnDragCompleted handler reorders the collection — displaced siblings and the dropped item all animate through the one layout-transition path (reorder hints come free). Mid-drag live reorder rides the same pipeline: when the consumer re-projects the children ORDER at the dwell-committed target (ReorderList.ProjectOrder), displaced siblings FLIP to their new slots while Move(Point2, KeyModifiers, uint) RE-ANCHORS the pointer-held visual — it strips the translate it last applied to find the node's CURRENT resting origin, then aims at (grab origin + gesture delta), so a slot move under the pointer (or an ancestor scrolling) never jumps the visual (the WinUI outcome: the dragged item tracks the pointer while MoveItemsForLiveReorder shifts the rest — ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:2254). The drag visuals are re-asserted on every move because a mid-drag commit's patch restores the authored opacity/shadow/hit-test (Reconciler ApplyBox writes them unconditionally). GHOST (E5-L2): while a drag is active the lifted node carries DragGhost and is published as DragGhost — the recorder excludes it from the clipped main pass and re-walks its subtree in an UNCLIPPED top band emitted last (mirroring the orphan pass), so the visual escapes every ancestor scissor (a row dragged out of a clipped list keeps drawing) and paints above overlays — the Flutter/rbd ghost layer. Spring-lag follow (the rbd feel): the PRESENTED translate eases toward the gesture target with a critically-damped spring (FollowOmega) instead of pinning rigidly — 0-alloc per move/tick. It engages only when the platform delivers real timestamps (consecutive non-zero TimestampMs); 0-stamp gestures (the headless default) snap presented == target, keeping every position assertion deterministic. SpringFollow false disables it outright. The host advances in-between-move easing (and the pointer-pin while an edge auto-scroll moves the resting origin under a still pointer) via Tick(float). DragDropContext L2 of the drag-drop stack (plan E5; user ruling 2026-06-10: deliberately NOT WinUI's OLE DataPackage/DoDragDrop modal loop — the Flutter Draggable/DragTarget + react-beautiful-dnd context + SwiftUI draggable/dropDestination model instead; the frame loop STAYS LIVE for the whole gesture). Layered on the L1 DragController gesture: • A BoxEl.Draggable (DragSource: a string Kind discriminator + a payload factory) marks a node a typed drag source. When the L1 press promotes past the drag box, the dispatcher calls TryBegin(NodeHandle, Point2, KeyModifiers, PointerKind) — the nearest enabled DragSource up the chain opens THE session and its PayloadFactory resolves ONCE (cold gesture edge; never per-move). • A BoxEl.DropTarget (DropTargetSpec) makes ANY surface a candidate receiver. Per pointer move the dispatcher hands Move(NodeHandle, Point2, float, float, KeyModifiers) the hit-test chain under the pointer; the nearest enabled target whose AcceptKinds contains the session's Kind becomes OverTarget (a non-accepting target never blocks an accepting ancestor — the Flutter DragTarget pass-through). The dragged subtree can never self-target: L1 already cleared its HitTestVisible, so hit-testing sees THROUGH the moving visual. • Enter/Leave fire on target transitions, Over on every move while inside, Drop on release over an accepting target (TryDrop(Point2, KeyModifiers, out bool) — fired BEFORE the L1 completion so the target reads the live session). The ONE mutable DragSession instance is reused for the whole gesture (0 steady-state alloc per move); handlers copy what they keep. Edge auto-scroll (the rbd/WinUI "drag near the viewport edge keeps scrolling" behavior) is engine-level here so every drag gets it: while a session is live, the pointer entering the hot zone of the nearest OVERFLOWING scrollable ancestor arms a proportional scroll velocity the host drives via Tick(float). Constants and behavior are WinUI's (microsoft-ui-xaml dxaml\xcp\dxaml\lib\ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp): a 100px edge band (:39 LISTVIEWBASE_EDGE_SCROLL_EDGE_WIDTH_PX), speed varying LINEARLY from 1500 px/s at the edge to 150 px/s at the band's inner rim (:42-47), a 50ms delay-start with instant velocity updates once running (:40, :1749-1753), the start-edge tried before the end-edge (:1660-1707), and scrolling suppressed when already pinned to that boundary (:1686-1690, :1718-1722). InputDispatcher Phase 2 (input dispatch): hit-tests the committed scene and routes pointer + keyboard. Pointer down→up over the same node fires the click handler and focuses the nearest focusable self-or-ancestor (a WinUI IsTabStop=False part never takes pointer focus; nothing focusable in the chain → focus unchanged); keyboard routes to the focused node and bubbles up ancestors (Handled stops it); Tab moves focus through focusable nodes; Enter/Space activates a focused clickable (the "one declaration, three modalities" contract). The full engine adds tunnel(Preview), gesture arena, XY-focus. Enums FocusDirection Directional focus movement for roving/XY keyboard navigation (arrow keys in lists, grids, menus).

api/FluentGpu.Layout.FlexLayout.html.title: Class FlexLayout | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Layout.FlexLayout.html.summary: Class FlexLayout Namespace FluentGpu.Layout Assembly FluentGpu.Layout.dll Flexbox layout over the SoA scene columns. Two descents: Measure (bottom-up — content/basis base sizes, honoring explicit size + min/max + text content) then Arrange (top-down — distribute free space by flex-grow/shrink, position by justify-content, align the cross axis by align-items/align-self incl. stretch, applying margins). Direction 0 = row (main = X), 1 = column (main = Y). Wrap / grid / absolute positioning are the remaining layout work. public sealed class FlexLayout Inheritance object FlexLayout Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors FlexLayout(SceneStore, IFontSystem) public FlexLayout(SceneStore scene, IFontSystem fonts) Parameters scene SceneStore fonts IFontSystem Methods Run(NodeHandle) public void Run(NodeHandle root) Parameters root NodeHandle Run(NodeHandle, Size2) Lay out the root to FILL the window (the conventional top-level behavior) — an auto-sized root takes the full client area; an explicitly-sized root keeps its size. The slice's direct Run(NodeHandle) keeps content-sizing for golden flexbox checks. public void Run(NodeHandle root, Size2 window) Parameters root NodeHandle window Size2 RunSubtree(NodeHandle) Re-solve ONLY the subtree rooted at node against its current Bounds (or its LayoutInput size if set — a SizeMode.Relayout animation writes the interpolated width there each tick). The parent already placed this node, so this cannot propagate upward — a scoped, per-frame-affordable relayout for live reflow. public void RunSubtree(NodeHandle node) Parameters node NodeHandle

api/FluentGpu.Layout.LayoutInvalidator.h.title: Class LayoutInvalidator | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Layout.LayoutInvalidator.h.summary: Class LayoutInvalidator Namespace FluentGpu.Layout Assembly FluentGpu.Layout.dll Scoped relayout (layout.md §4): consumes the SceneStore's LayoutDirty worklist and, for each dirty node, walks UP to the nearest layout boundary — a fixed-size, non-flexing, clipped container whose own size cannot change due to a descendant — then re-solves just that subtree (RunSubtree(NodeHandle)). The boundary is the firewall: a setState deep inside a fixed-size card relayouts only the card, never the page. Falls back to a full layout from the root when a dirty node has no bounded ancestor. public sealed class LayoutInvalidator Inheritance object LayoutInvalidator Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors LayoutInvalidator(SceneStore, FlexLayout) public LayoutInvalidator(SceneStore scene, FlexLayout layout) Parameters scene SceneStore layout FlexLayout Methods RunDirty(Size2) Re-solve only the subtrees affected by this frame's LayoutDirty marks. O(dirty), firewalled at boundaries. public void RunDirty(Size2 window) Parameters window Size2

api/FluentGpu.Media.Codecs.Wic.WicImageC.title: Class WicImageCodec | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Media.Codecs.Wic.WicImageC.summary: Class WicImageCodec Namespace FluentGpu.Media.Codecs.Wic Assembly FluentGpu.Media.Codecs.Wic.dll Windows IImageCodec leaf (media-pipeline.md §3): Windows Imaging Component constrained decode. Decodes straight to the target bucket size via IWICBitmapScaler (a 3000px cover never materializes full-res in CPU memory) and converts to 32bppPBGRA (premultiplied BGRA — matches the engine's blend posture and the GPU texture format). All WIC COM objects are created and used ENTIRELY on the calling worker thread (per-thread cached factory) and never cross the scheduler seam — only POD pixels do (the FGCOM per-thread-COM rule). public sealed class WicImageCodec : IImageCodec Inheritance object WicImageCodec Implements IImageCodec Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods DecodeConstrained(ReadOnlySpan<byte>, int, int, Span<byte>, out int, out int) Decode encoded into dstBgra8 (premultiplied, row-major, stride = w4) at targetW×targetH. dstBgra8 is at least targetWtargetH*4 bytes. Returns false (no throw) if the bytes are not a decodable image; the scheduler maps that to ImageFailureKind.Decode. Outputs the actual decoded dims (== target for the constrained path). public bool DecodeConstrained(ReadOnlySpan<byte> encoded, int targetW, int targetH, Span<byte> dstBgra8, out int w, out int h) Parameters encoded ReadOnlySpan<byte> targetW int targetH int dstBgra8 Span<byte> w int h int Returns bool

api/FluentGpu.Media.DecodeOptions.html.summary: Class DecodeOptions Namespace FluentGpu.Media Assembly FluentGpu.Media.dll Decode/fetch robustness policy. Defaults tuned for album art over flaky links (slow internet, transient 5xx). public sealed class DecodeOptions Inheritance object DecodeOptions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties BackoffBase Exponential backoff between transient retries: BackoffBase * 2^(attempt-1), capped at BackoffMax. public TimeSpan BackoffBase { get; init; } Property Value TimeSpan BackoffMax public TimeSpan BackoffMax { get; init; } Property Value TimeSpan MaxAttempts Total fetch attempts for a TRANSIENT failure (1 try + N-1 retries). Permanent failures never retry. public int MaxAttempts { get; init; } Property Value int MaxConcurrency Worker (parallel decode) count. 0 ⇒ clamp(ProcessorCount-2, 2..6) per the design's WORKER pool. public int MaxConcurrency { get; init; } Property Value int QueueCapacity Bounded request-channel capacity; a saturated channel drops the newest enqueue (fast-scroll backpressure). public int QueueCapacity { get; init; } Property Value int RequestTimeout Per-attempt deadline — bounds a slow/stalled download so it fails over to a retry instead of hanging. public TimeSpan RequestTimeout { get; init; } Property Value TimeSpan

api/FluentGpu.Media.DecodeScheduler.html.title: Class DecodeScheduler | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Media.DecodeScheduler.html.summary: Class DecodeScheduler Namespace FluentGpu.Media Assembly FluentGpu.Media.dll Off-thread, parallel, non-blocking, PRIORITIZED image decoder (media-pipeline.md §3). Implements the portable IImageDecoder seam the ImageCache drives: Begin(int, string, int, int, ImagePriority) enqueues into one of three priority lanes (Visible > Overscan > Prefetch) — a non-blocking write on the UI thread; a pool of N worker tasks drain the highest non-empty lane and fetch+decode CONCURRENTLY; and Pump(ImageCompleteHandler, ImageReadyHandler) drains finished results on the UI thread and never waits on a decode. Prioritize(int, ImagePriority) promotes a queued prefetch that just scrolled into view; Cancel(int) drops a queued/in-flight decode whose row recycled. Robustness: per-attempt timeout, transient retry with exponential backoff, permanent fail-fast (see ImageFailureKind). Under backpressure the lowest off-screen lane is dropped — never Visible. Diagnostics post to the media counter group. public sealed class DecodeScheduler : IImageDecoder, IDisposable Inheritance object DecodeScheduler Implements IImageDecoder IDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors DecodeScheduler(IImageCodec, IImageFetcher, DecodeOptions?) public DecodeScheduler(IImageCodec codec, IImageFetcher fetcher, DecodeOptions? options = null) Parameters codec IImageCodec fetcher IImageFetcher options DecodeOptions Properties CanceledPending Live entries in the cancellation map — O(1) census. NOTE: this map grows unbounded today (a known issue a later fix stage addresses); this accessor only makes that visible. public int CanceledPending { get; } Property Value int Inflight public int Inflight { get; } Property Value int QueueDepth Requests enqueued in the priority lanes but not yet claimed by a worker — O(1) census. public int QueueDepth { get; } Property Value int RequestCount Pending request descriptors awaiting claim — O(1) census of the _reqs map. public int RequestCount { get; } Property Value int WorkerCount public int WorkerCount { get; } Property Value int Methods Begin(int, string, int, int, ImagePriority) public void Begin(int id, string source, int targetW, int targetH, ImagePriority priority = ImagePriority.Visible) Parameters id int source string targetW int targetH int priority ImagePriority Cancel(int) Cancel an in-flight (or queued) decode — e.g. a virtualized row recycled off-screen. Idempotent. public void Cancel(int id) Parameters id int Dispose() Performs application-defined tasks associated with freeing, releasing, or resetting unmanaged resources. public void Dispose() Prioritize(int, ImagePriority) Raise the priority of a queued decode (e.g. a prefetch that just scrolled into view). Idempotent; no-op once the job has started. public void Prioritize(int id, ImagePriority priority) Parameters id int priority ImagePriority Pump(ImageCompleteHandler, ImageReadyHandler) Drain finished decodes. onPixels gets the bucket pixels (transient span); onComplete gets the final state transition incl. ImageFailureKind + attempt count (after any transient retries the decoder performed internally). public void Pump(ImageCompleteHandler onComplete, ImageReadyHandler onPixels) Parameters onComplete ImageCompleteHandler onPixels ImageReadyHandler

api/FluentGpu.Media.DefaultImageFetcher..title: Class DefaultImageFetcher | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Media.DefaultImageFetcher..summary: Class DefaultImageFetcher Namespace FluentGpu.Media Assembly FluentGpu.Media.dll HTTP(S) + local-file fetcher, configured per the cross-ecosystem consensus (Flutter cache_manager, iOS URLSession, OkHttp/Coil, browsers): ONE pooled SocketsHttpHandler — never new HttpClient() per request (socket exhaustion). PooledConnectionLifetime recycles connections so DNS is re-resolved (stale-DNS / CDN-edge rotation fix) without IHttpClientFactory. HTTP/2 with multiple connections — CDN request multiplexing; bounded MaxConnectionsPerServer. Automatic decompression; per-request deadline via the token (NOT the global HttpClient.Timeout). Disk-first: a persistent DiskImageCache serves instant, offline, restart-surviving hits. Streams the body into an ArrayPool buffer — no per-fetch byte[]. Reuse ONE instance app-wide. Maps transport/HTTP-status to ImageFailureKind for transient-vs-permanent. public sealed class DefaultImageFetcher : IImageFetcher, IDisposable Inheritance object DefaultImageFetcher Implements IImageFetcher IDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors DefaultImageFetcher(HttpClient?, DiskImageCache?, string?) public DefaultImageFetcher(HttpClient? http = null, DiskImageCache? diskCache = null, string? acceptHeader = null) Parameters http HttpClient diskCache DiskImageCache acceptHeader string Methods Dispose() Performs application-defined tasks associated with freeing, releasing, or resetting unmanaged resources. public void Dispose() FetchAsync(string, CancellationToken) public Task<FetchResult> FetchAsync(string source, CancellationToken ct) Parameters source string ct CancellationToken Returns Task<FetchResult>

api/FluentGpu.Media.DiskImageCache.html.title: Class DiskImageCache | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Media.DiskImageCache.html.summary: Class DiskImageCache Namespace FluentGpu.Media Assembly FluentGpu.Media.dll Persistent on-disk cache of ENCODED image bytes (the second tier under the in-memory GPU residency cache) — the memory+disk model every mature loader uses (Flutter flutter_cache_manager, Nuke/Kingfisher DataCache, SDWebImage). Content-addressed by a hash of the source URL, with an LRU byte budget + max-object count + stale period. It makes CDN images instant on the second view, survives app restarts, and serves offline. Stores the compressed source (≈tens of KB), NOT the decoded BGRA (which is the memory tier) — so the budget covers thousands of covers. public sealed class DiskImageCache Inheritance object DiskImageCache Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors DiskImageCache(string?, long, int, TimeSpan?) public DiskImageCache(string? directory = null, long budgetBytes = 268435456, int maxObjects = 4096, TimeSpan? maxAge = null) Parameters directory string budgetBytes long maxObjects int maxAge TimeSpan? api/FluentGpu.Media.IImageCodec.html.summary: Interface IImageCodec Namespace FluentGpu.Media Assembly FluentGpu.Media.dll Portable decode seam (media-pipeline.md §3): turn encoded bytes (JPEG/PNG/WebP/…) into PREMULTIPLIED BGRA8 at a target size, constrained so a huge source never materializes full-res in CPU memory (WIC IWICBitmapScaler on Windows, CGImageSource on macOS). Implemented entirely on a worker thread; the leaf's COM/objc objects never cross the scheduler seam — only POD pixels do. public interface IImageCodec Methods DecodeConstrained(ReadOnlySpan<byte>, int, int, Span<byte>, out int, out int) Decode encoded into dstBgra8 (premultiplied, row-major, stride = w4) at targetW×targetH. dstBgra8 is at least targetWtargetH*4 bytes. Returns false (no throw) if the bytes are not a decodable image; the scheduler maps that to ImageFailureKind.Decode. Outputs the actual decoded dims (== target for the constrained path). bool DecodeConstrained(ReadOnlySpan<byte> encoded, int targetW, int targetH, Span<byte> dstBgra8, out int w, out int h) Parameters encoded ReadOnlySpan<byte> targetW int targetH int dstBgra8 Span<byte> w int h int Returns bool
