Maps transport/HTTP-status errors to ImageFailureKind so the scheduler can distinguish transient (network, timeout, 5xx → retried) from permanent (404, other 4xx → not retried). Honors the cancellation token (per-attempt timeout + shutdown). HTTP(S) + local file by default (DefaultImageFetcher). public interface IImageFetcher Methods FetchAsync(string, CancellationToken) Task<FetchResult> FetchAsync(string source, CancellationToken ct) Parameters source string ct CancellationToken Returns Task<FetchResult>

api/FluentGpu.Media.html.title: Namespace FluentGpu.Media | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Media.html.summary: Namespace FluentGpu.Media Classes DecodeOptions Decode/fetch robustness policy. Defaults tuned for album art over flaky links (slow internet, transient 5xx). DecodeScheduler Off-thread, parallel, non-blocking, PRIORITIZED image decoder (media-pipeline.md §3). Implements the portable IImageDecoder seam the ImageCache drives: Begin(int, string, int, int, ImagePriority) enqueues into one of three priority lanes (Visible > Overscan > Prefetch) — a non-blocking write on the UI thread; a pool of N worker tasks drain the highest non-empty lane and fetch+decode CONCURRENTLY; and Pump(ImageCompleteHandler, ImageReadyHandler) drains finished results on the UI thread and never waits on a decode. Prioritize(int, ImagePriority) promotes a queued prefetch that just scrolled into view; Cancel(int) drops a queued/in-flight decode whose row recycled. Robustness: per-attempt timeout, transient retry with exponential backoff, permanent fail-fast (see ImageFailureKind). Under backpressure the lowest off-screen lane is dropped — never Visible. Diagnostics post to the media counter group. DefaultImageFetcher HTTP(S) + local-file fetcher, configured per the cross-ecosystem consensus (Flutter cache_manager, iOS URLSession, OkHttp/Coil, browsers): ONE pooled SocketsHttpHandler — never new HttpClient() per request (socket exhaustion). PooledConnectionLifetime recycles connections so DNS is re-resolved (stale-DNS / CDN-edge rotation fix) without IHttpClientFactory. HTTP/2 with multiple connections — CDN request multiplexing; bounded MaxConnectionsPerServer. Automatic decompression; per-request deadline via the token (NOT the global HttpClient.Timeout). Disk-first: a persistent DiskImageCache serves instant, offline, restart-surviving hits. Streams the body into an ArrayPool buffer — no per-fetch byte[]. Reuse ONE instance app-wide. Maps transport/HTTP-status to ImageFailureKind for transient-vs-permanent. DiskImageCache Persistent on-disk cache of ENCODED image bytes (the second tier under the in-memory GPU residency cache) — the memory+disk model every mature loader uses (Flutter flutter_cache_manager, Nuke/Kingfisher DataCache, SDWebImage). Content-addressed by a hash of the source URL, with an LRU byte budget + max-object count + stale period. It makes CDN images instant on the second view, survives app restarts, and serves offline. Stores the compressed source (≈tens of KB), NOT the decoded BGRA (which is the memory tier) — so the budget covers thousands of covers. Structs FetchResult The outcome of a fetch attempt. On success it hands back a buffer RENTED FROM Shared plus the valid length — the scheduler returns it to the pool after decode, so a fetch costs no net managed allocation. On failure it carries a classified ImageFailureKind the scheduler uses to pick transient-retry vs permanent-fail. Interfaces IImageCodec Portable decode seam (media-pipeline.md §3): turn encoded bytes (JPEG/PNG/WebP/…) into PREMULTIPLIED BGRA8 at a target size, constrained so a huge source never materializes full-res in CPU memory (WIC IWICBitmapScaler on Windows, CGImageSource on macOS). Implemented entirely on a worker thread; the leaf's COM/objc objects never cross the scheduler seam — only POD pixels do. IImageFetcher Portable fetch seam (media-pipeline.md §3 "Fetch"): turn a source URI into encoded bytes off the UI thread. Maps transport/HTTP-status errors to ImageFailureKind so the scheduler can distinguish transient (network, timeout, 5xx → retried) from permanent (404, other 4xx → not retried). Honors the cancellation token (per-attempt timeout + shutdown). HTTP(S) + local file by default (DefaultImageFetcher).

api/FluentGpu.Pal.Windows.Win32PopupWind.title: Class Win32PopupWindow | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Pal.Windows.Win32PopupWind.summary: Class Win32PopupWindow Namespace FluentGpu.Pal.Windows Assembly FluentGpu.Pal.Windows.dll A borderless, non-activating, owned top-level popup HWND — the Win32 backing of IPlatformPopupWindow and the engine analogue of WinUI's windowed CPopup (microsoft-ui-xaml Popup_Partial.cpp:1019 SetIsWindowed() creates an HWND via PopupSiteBridge so flyouts/menus can render OUTSIDE the XAML window; Popup_Partial.cpp:951-970 IsConstrainedToRootBounds == false for windowed popups). WS_POPUP + owner: borderless, z-ordered above the owner, hidden when the owner minimizes. WS_EX_NOACTIVATE + MA_NOACTIVATE: the popup NEVER takes activation — keyboard focus stays on the owner window exactly like WinUI's popup site (the dispatcher keeps routing keys to the main window). WS_EX_TOOLWINDOW: no taskbar button / Alt-Tab entry. WS_EX_NOREDIRECTIONBITMAP: no GDI redirection surface — the host presents into it with its own composition swapchain (same composited-surface model as the main window). Mouse input over the popup is FORWARDED to the owner Win32Window, translated popup-client → screen → owner-client → owner DIP, so the one InputDispatcher hit-tests the popup subtree at its scene coordinates (the subtree stays in the single SceneStore; out-of-bounds coords hit-test fine). public sealed class Win32PopupWindow : IPlatformPopupWindow, IDisposable Inheritance object Win32PopupWindow Implements IPlatformPopupWindow IDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors Win32PopupWindow(in PopupWindowDesc) public Win32PopupWindow(in PopupWindowDesc desc) Parameters desc PopupWindowDesc Properties BoundsPx Last bounds set via SetBoundsPx(in RectF) (physical virtual-screen px). public RectF BoundsPx { get; } Property Value RectF Handle public NativeHandle Handle { get; } Property Value NativeHandle IsShown Visible (a Show() not yet followed by Hide()/Dispose()). public bool IsShown { get; } Property Value bool Methods Dispose() Performs application-defined tasks associated with freeing, releasing, or resetting unmanaged resources. public void Dispose() Hide() public void Hide() SetBoundsPx(in RectF) Move/size the popup (physical virtual-screen px) WITHOUT activating it. public void SetBoundsPx(in RectF px) Parameters px RectF Show() Show without activating (Win32 SW_SHOWNOACTIVATE) — focus stays on the owner window. public void Show()

A single message can both COMMIT the previous composition (GCS_RESULTSTR) and open the next one (GCS_COMPSTR) — deliver in that order, like Imm32 does. public void OnComposition(long lParam) Parameters lParam long OnEndComposition() public void OnEndComposition() OnStartComposition() public void OnStartComposition() SetCaretRectPx(in RectF) Position the candidate window: the caret rect in PHYSICAL window pixels (CFS_EXCLUDE placement). public void SetCaretRectPx(in RectF rect) Parameters rect RectF SetEditable(bool) Enable/disable the IME for this window (ImmAssociateContext on/off) — flipped when keyboard focus enters/leaves an editable control, so the IME never composes over a button. public void SetEditable(bool editable) Parameters editable bool SetSink(ITextInputSink?) api/FluentGpu.Pal.Windows.Win32Theme.htm.title: Class Win32Theme | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Pal.Windows.Win32Theme.htm.summary: Class Win32Theme Namespace FluentGpu.Pal.Windows Assembly FluentGpu.Pal.Windows.dll Windows system-theme integration: the real accent color + DWM dark titlebar / Mica backdrop. public static class Win32Theme Inheritance object Win32Theme Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods Accent() The Settings > Colors accent (registry AccentColorMenu), falling back to the DWM colorization color. public static (byte R, byte G, byte B)? Accent() Returns (byte R, byte G, byte B)? AccentLight2() The OS-derived SystemAccentColorLight2 shade — what WinUI uses for the dark-theme accent button fill (lighter than the base accent). Read from the AccentPalette blob: 8×RGBA entries, index 1 = Light2 (bytes 4..6). public static (byte R, byte G, byte B)? AccentLight2() Returns (byte R, byte G, byte B)? ApplyWindowMaterial(nint, bool, bool, bool) Dark titlebar + the Mica system backdrop (Windows 11). Mica shows through transparent client pixels. customFrame = the engine draws the titlebar (WindowDesc.CustomFrame): the frame extension is ZERO — the Windows Terminal Mica rule (_useMica ? 0). Any non-zero margin lets DWM paint caption visuals over the client (a -1 sheet-of-glass composites its OWN min/max/close — the "double caption buttons" bug; even a 1px sliver draws a DWM strip and re-anchors the Win11 snap flyout off the extended frame). The Mica backdrop fills the whole window from DWMWA_SYSTEMBACKDROP_TYPE alone — no frame extension needed. public static void ApplyWindowMaterial(nint hwnd, bool dark, bool mica = true, bool customFrame = false) Parameters hwnd nint dark bool mica bool customFrame bool

api/FluentGpu.Pal.html.title: Namespace FluentGpu.Pal | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Pal.html.summary: Namespace FluentGpu.Pal Classes InputEventRing Drained by the host each frame; the window writes POD events into it (move-coalesced). SystemParams OS user-preference parameters the engine honors (WinUI reads them through SystemParametersInfo / the registry). The platform writes them ONCE at startup (Win32: HKCU + SPI reads); headless keeps the defaults for determinism. Read anywhere (controls included) — plain statics, no per-frame cost. Structs ImeClause One clause of the in-flight composition string (UTF-16 char range, local to the composition). InputEvent POD input event drained from the host-owned ring once per frame (no C# events across the seam). ScrollDelta (Wheel only) is in DIP, oriented so positive = scroll toward the content end (offset increases). The platform pump converts WM_MOUSEWHEEL notches → DIP and flips the sign there. Button: 0 = left, 1 = right, 2 = middle. Mods is the modifier chord at the time of the event (pump-captured); IsRepeat = keyboard auto-repeat (lParam bit 30); TimestampMs = the platform message time (drives double/triple-click detection in the dispatcher). PopupWindowDesc Creation parameters for a popup window. Owner = the owning top-level window (the popup stays above it in z-order and never takes activation); BoundsPx = initial bounds in physical virtual-screen px (may be empty — set real bounds via SetBoundsPx(in RectF) before Show). TitleBarRegion One reported titlebar region: a rect in CLIENT DIP (the engine's space) + its non-client classification. Pushed on titlebar relayout only (push-on-change — never per frame). First match wins at hit-test, so callers list interactive islands and buttons BEFORE the catch-all Caption band. WindowDesc Composited = the window is composited with per-pixel alpha (WS_EX_NOREDIRECTIONBITMAP) so a DirectComposition swapchain can show the DWM Mica backdrop through transparent pixels. CustomFrame = the engine draws the ENTIRE titlebar (WinUI ExtendsContentIntoTitleBar): the platform strips the OS caption (WM_NCCALCSIZE) but keeps the resize frame/shadow, answers WM_NCHITTEST from the engine-reported TitleBarRegions, and synthesizes pointer input for the engine-drawn caption buttons. Interfaces IClipboard The system-clipboard seam (plain Unicode text, the TextBox cut/copy/paste contract). UI-thread only. SequenceNumber is the OS clipboard epoch (Win32 GetClipboardSequenceNumber) — context menus gate their Paste item on it changing instead of polling content (the ISystemColors versioned-external-store idiom). IPlatformApp IPlatformLocale Versioned external-store-shaped locale seam (modeled on ISystemColors). L9. IPlatformPopupWindow A borderless, non-activating top-level popup surface (the PAL seam for WinUI windowed popups / E4 out-of-bounds flyouts; later the substrate for E10 tear-out windows). The host owns rendering into it (its own swapchain on Handle); the popup never owns engine state. All bounds are physical virtual-screen px. IPlatformTextInput The per-window IME/text-services seam (Imm32 today; the interface is event-shaped so a TSF/ITextStoreACP implementation can replace it without touching the engine — full in-place TSF is a named hardening item). IPlatformWindow ITextInputSink Receives IME composition events from the platform (the focused editor registers as the sink through the input router). Composition TEXT cannot ride the POD InputEventRing (strings) — the platform invokes the sink directly on the UI thread during the message pump; the typing edge is allowed bounded Gen0. Enums ImeClauseKind How an IME composition clause renders (Imm32 ATTR_* / TSF display attributes): Input = dotted underline (not yet converted), Converted = solid underline, TargetConverted/TargetNotConverted = the clause the candidate window currently targets (thick underline). InputKind TitleBarHit Non-client classification for one engine-reported titlebar rect (engine → WM_NCHITTEST). Client marks an INTERACTIVE ISLAND (search box, back/pane buttons) the engine keeps; Caption is the OS drag-move band; the three buttons get HTMIN/HTMAX/HTCLOSE so Win11 shows the snap-layouts flyout over Max. WindowState Window placement, surfaced for custom-titlebar chrome (State).

api/FluentGpu.Reconciler.TreeReconciler..title: Class TreeReconciler | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Reconciler.TreeReconciler..summary: Class TreeReconciler Namespace FluentGpu.Reconciler Assembly FluentGpu.Reconciler.dll Patches the retained SceneStore from an immutable Element tree. Signals-first: every component is a reactive render-effect (re-renders + reconciles ONLY its own subtree when its state/context changes — granular, never the whole app); fine-grained bindings (TransformBind/OpacityBind/…) and reactive control-flow (ShowEl/ ForEl) are effects too. The keyed positional+type diff is retained as the STRUCTURAL engine (used on re-render and behind For/Show); a reused component on a parent re-render is a no-op (it is autonomous). public sealed class TreeReconciler Inheritance object TreeReconciler Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors TreeReconciler(SceneStore, StringTable, ReactiveRuntime?) public TreeReconciler(SceneStore scene, StringTable strings, ReactiveRuntime? runtime = null) Parameters scene SceneStore strings StringTable runtime ReactiveRuntime Properties Anim Set by the host; injected into each component so animation hooks can seed tracks on their node. public AnimEngine? Anim { get; set; } Property Value AnimEngine ComponentCount Mounted component entries (the _comps anchor map) — O(1) census. public int ComponentCount { get; } Property Value int ImageEpoch Set by the host; bumped on any image status change so UseImage consumers re-render granularly. public IReadSignal<int>? ImageEpoch { get; set; } Property Value IReadSignal<int> Images Set by the host; image nodes request decodes through it and pin/unpin for residency (liveness). public ImageCache? Images { get; set; } Property Value ImageCache LiveComponents Live nested components — the host drains their effects each frame. public List<Component> LiveComponents { get; } Property Value List<Component> NodeBindingCount Nodes carrying reactive bindings / control-flow effects (the _nodeBindings map) — O(1) census. public int NodeBindingCount { get; } Property Value int ProviderCount Context-provider value signals (the _providerSig map) — O(1) census. public int ProviderCount { get; } Property Value int Runtime The reactive scheduler — one per host; signals schedule render-effects/bindings here, the host flushes it. public ReactiveRuntime Runtime { get; } Property Value ReactiveRuntime VirtualBoundaryCount Virtual-list viewport boundaries (the _virtuals map) — O(1) census. public int VirtualBoundaryCount { get; } Property Value int Methods ConsumeReconciled() True (and reset) if any mount/update/remove happened since the last call — the host's "layout needed" gate. public bool ConsumeReconciled() Returns bool ConsumeRenderCount() Number of component render-effects that ran since the last call (proves granular re-render in tests). public int ConsumeRenderCount() Returns int MountRoot(Component) Mount a root COMPONENT as a reactive render-effect (the host path): it renders into Scene.Root and re-renders itself (only) when its own state/context changes. public void MountRoot(Component root) Parameters root Component ReRealizeVirtuals() Re-realize any virtual-list windows flagged VirtualRangeDirty (scroll boundary crossing) — granular, no component re-render. Called by the host each frame. public bool ReRealizeVirtuals() Returns bool ReconcileRoot(Element, Element?) Imperative full reconcile of an explicit element tree (tests / non-host callers). public void ReconcileRoot(Element newRoot, Element? oldRoot) Parameters newRoot Element oldRoot Element SetAmbient(object, Signal<object?>) Publish an ambient context (e.g. Viewport.Size) as a host-owned signal consumers can read. public void SetAmbient(object channel, Signal<object?> sig) Parameters channel object sig Signal<object>

api/FluentGpu.Reconciler.VirtualListEl.h.title: Class VirtualListEl | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Reconciler.VirtualListEl.h.summary: Class VirtualListEl Namespace FluentGpu.Reconciler Assembly FluentGpu.Reconciler.dll A virtualized collection viewport: ONE retained node + a window of keyed children. Each frame the visible range is dirty, the reconciler asks the Layout for [first,last), calls RenderItem for just that window, keys each row, and feeds it to the existing keyed-LIS diff — so recycling IS CreateNode/FreeNode over the slab free-list (no second pool). Scroll is layout-free (the -ScrollOffset is the content's transform). Layout is a pluggable IVirtualLayout (stack / grid / custom — pure, allocation-free). E11-L1: the viewport consumes BOTH seam kinds — an IMeasuredVirtualLayout gets the variable-extent estimate-then-correct arrange (measured rows + scroll anchoring) through the SAME property; when the layout is null, the legacy built-in Fenwick extent-table path runs (kept source/behavior-compatible). The user-facing Virtual.List/Grid/Custom/Measured/VariableList/… factories live in FluentGpu.Controls; this record is the engine primitive the reconciler diffs directly (ElementTypeId 6). See design/subsystems/virtualization.md. public sealed record VirtualListEl : Element, IEquatable<Element>, IEquatable<VirtualListEl> Inheritance object Element VirtualListEl Implements IEquatable<Element> IEquatable<VirtualListEl> Inherited Members Element.Key object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties AlignSelf public FlexAlign AlignSelf { get; init; } Property Value FlexAlign Basis public float Basis { get; init; } Property Value float ElementTypeId Stable per-record-type id for integer type-dispatch in the reconciler (the source-gen'd ElementTypeId). public override ushort ElementTypeId { get; } Property Value ushort EstimatedExtent public float EstimatedExtent { get; init; } Property Value float Fill public ColorF Fill { get; init; } Property Value ColorF Grow public float Grow { get; init; } Property Value float Height public float Height { get; init; } Property Value float Horizontal public bool Horizontal { get; init; } Property Value bool ItemCount public int ItemCount { get; init; } Property Value int KeyOf public Func<int, string>? KeyOf { get; init; } Property Value Func<int, string> Layout public IVirtualLayout? Layout { get; init; } Property Value IVirtualLayout Margin public Edges4 Margin { get; init; } Property Value Edges4 MaxHeight public float MaxHeight { get; init; } Property Value float MaxWidth public float MaxWidth { get; init; } Property Value float MinHeight public float MinHeight { get; init; } Property Value float MinWidth public float MinWidth { get; init; } Property Value float OnItemClearing WinUI ItemsRepeater.ElementClearing — fired with the item index when it leaves the window. public Action<int>? OnItemClearing { get; init; } Property Value Action<int> OnItemIndexChanged WinUI ItemsRepeater.ElementIndexChanged — a persistent bound slot rebound (oldIndex, newIndex). public Action<int, int>? OnItemIndexChanged { get; init; } Property Value Action<int, int> OnItemPrepared WinUI ItemsRepeater.ElementPrepared — fired with the item index when it is realized. public Action<int>? OnItemPrepared { get; init; } Property Value Action<int> OnRealized Called once when this viewport is realized into the scene, with its node handle — the escape hatch a composing control (ItemsView) uses to drive ScrollState (StartBringItemIntoView, sticky pinning). public Action<NodeHandle>? OnRealized { get; init; } Property Value Action<NodeHandle> OnVisibleRange Prefetch hook: the realized window changed → (first, last) exclusive. Use to warm images/data just outside the viewport (the plan's UseVisibleRange surface). Fired only when the range actually moved. public Action<int, int>? OnVisibleRange { get; init; } Property Value Action<int, int> Overscan public int Overscan { get; init; } Property Value int RenderItem public Func<int, Element> RenderItem { get; init; } Property Value Func<int, Element> RowBind Signals-first BOUND row template (the recycler fast path): the template runs ONCE per visible slot with an index SIGNAL; scrolling rebinds a slot by writing its signal, so only the row's reactive binds (TextBind/FillBind/SourceBind/…) re-run — zero element rebuild, zero reconcile, zero keys. When set, RenderItem/KeyOf are ignored. public Func<IReadSignal<int>, Element>? api/FluentGpu.Reconciler.html.title: Namespace FluentGpu.Reconciler | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Reconciler.html.summary: Namespace FluentGpu.Reconciler Classes TreeReconciler Patches the retained SceneStore from an immutable Element tree. Signals-first: every component is a reactive render-effect (re-renders + reconciles ONLY its own subtree when its state/context changes — granular, never the whole app); fine-grained bindings (TransformBind/OpacityBind/…) and reactive control-flow (ShowEl/ ForEl) are effects too. The keyed positional+type diff is retained as the STRUCTURAL engine (used on re-render and behind For/Show); a reused component on a parent re-render is a no-op (it is autonomous). VirtualListEl A virtualized collection viewport: ONE retained node + a window of keyed children. Each frame the visible range is dirty, the reconciler asks the Layout for [first,last), calls RenderItem for just that window, keys each row, and feeds it to the existing keyed-LIS diff — so recycling IS CreateNode/FreeNode over the slab free-list (no second pool). Scroll is layout-free (the -ScrollOffset is the content's transform). Layout is a pluggable IVirtualLayout (stack / grid / custom — pure, allocation-free). E11-L1: the viewport consumes BOTH seam kinds — an IMeasuredVirtualLayout gets the variable-extent estimate-then-correct arrange (measured rows + scroll anchoring) through the SAME property; when the layout is null, the legacy built-in Fenwick extent-table path runs (kept source/behavior-compatible). The user-facing Virtual.List/Grid/Custom/Measured/VariableList/… factories live in FluentGpu.Controls; this record is the engine primitive the reconciler diffs directly (ElementTypeId 6). See design/subsystems/virtualization.md.

api/FluentGpu.Render.AcrylicBackdropMath.title: Class AcrylicBackdropMath | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Render.AcrylicBackdropMath.summary: Class AcrylicBackdropMath Namespace FluentGpu.Render Assembly FluentGpu.Render.dll Portable math for the in-app acrylic effect runner (design/subsystems/backdrop-effects-animation.md §2.3 two-pass PushLayer{Acrylic} schedule + §3 IEffectRunner seam, gpu-renderer.md §7 LayerPool). The GPU leaf (Rhi.D3D12 AcrylicCompositor) consumes these values so the region / size-bucket / kernel logic is headless-verifiable in the VerticalSlice while the COM/HLSL stays render-thread-confined in the leaf. WinUI ground truth: microsoft-ui-xaml AcrylicBrush.h:64 sc_blurRadius = 30.0f, applied as Composition GaussianBlurEffect.BlurAmount (= the gaussian STANDARD DEVIATION, in DIPs) over the backdrop resolved onto the opaque FallbackColor (AcrylicBrush.cpp:500-528). The runner reproduces sigma = BlurSigma·dpiScale physical px with ONE fixed-sigma separable kernel by choosing the snapshot downsample factor instead of the kernel width: down = round(sigmaPhys / KernelSigma), so blurring the 1/down-resolution snapshot with the fixed sigma-7.5 kernel yields the requested full-resolution sigma (30 DIP ⇒ /4 at 100% DPI, /6 at 150%, /8 at 200%). public static class AcrylicBackdropMath Inheritance object AcrylicBackdropMath Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields KernelRadius Discrete kernel radius in downsampled texels (≈3σ support; the tail beyond carries <0.4% weight — the same truncation a D2D GaussianBlur uses for its kernel window). public const int KernelRadius = 22 Field Value int KernelSigma Gaussian standard deviation of the FIXED separable blur kernel, in downsampled texels. public const float KernelSigma = 7.5 Field Value float TapCount Bilinear-optimized taps per pass: the center texel + 11 linear taps, each folding a texel pair of the 45-texel discrete kernel into one bilinear fetch (offset weighted between the pair). public const int TapCount = 12 Field Value int Properties TapOffsets Per-pass bilinear tap offsets in source texels (index 0 = center). One-sided; the shader mirrors. public static ReadOnlySpan<float> TapOffsets { get; } Property Value ReadOnlySpan<float> TapWeights Per-pass tap weights (index 0 = center weight; indices 1.. are applied at ±offset, so the total kernel mass is w[0] + 2·Σ w[1..] == 1). public static ReadOnlySpan<float> TapWeights { get; } Property Value ReadOnlySpan<float> Methods BucketDim(int) LayerPool size bucket (gpu-renderer.md §7.1: "pooled RT textures keyed by quantized power-of-two-ish size buckets"): next power of two ≥ px, floor 64 — few distinct buckets ⇒ high cross-frame/cross-layer reuse, so steady state acquires from the free list and never creates a texture. public static int BucketDim(int px) Parameters px int Returns int DownsampleFactor(float, float) Snapshot downsample divisor for a requested blur sigma (in DIPs — WinUI BlurAmount semantics) at a DPI scale. Effective full-resolution sigma = factor · KernelSigma ≈ blurSigmaDip · scale physical px (exact at 100/125/150/175/200% DPI for the WinUI sigma 30). Clamped to /8 — beyond that the bilinear down/up-sample artifacts outweigh kernel fidelity. public static int DownsampleFactor(float blurSigmaDip, float scale) Parameters blurSigmaDip float scale float Returns int SnapshotRegion(in RectF, float, int, int, int, out int, out int, out int, out int) The backdrop region to snapshot beneath a layer rect, in physical px clamped to the canvas: the rect inflated on every side by the full blur support (KernelRadius·down physical px), so every blurred texel under the rect samples REAL backdrop instead of a clamp-streaked edge (WinUI blurs the whole backdrop; inflating by the kernel support makes the region blur bit-identical inside the rect). public static void SnapshotRegion(in RectF deviceRectDip, float scale, int down, int canvasW, int canvasH, out int x, out int y, out int w, out int h) Parameters deviceRectDip RectF scale float down int canvasW int canvasH int x int y int w int h int

api/FluentGpu.Render.FillKind.html.summary: Enum FillKind Namespace FluentGpu.Render Assembly FluentGpu.Render.dll How a FillRoundRectCmd fills its interior. public enum FillKind Fields Checker = 1 Alpha-transparency checkerboard (the ColorPicker alpha lane / preview swatch): square cells of CellPx alternating Fill (cells where ⌊x/c⌋+⌊y/c⌋ is even) and ColorB (odd) — exactly WinUI's CreateCheckeredBackgroundAsync pattern ((x/CheckerSize + y/CheckerSize) % 2 == 0 ⇒ blank/transparent, else the checker color; CheckerSize = 4 px — microsoft-ui-xaml controls\dev\ColorPicker\ColorHelpers.cpp:9,384-404; the checker color is the SystemListLowColor resource — ColorPicker.xaml:12). Map Fill=transparent, ColorB=checker for WinUI parity. Solid = 0 Flat Fill color (the default; ColorB/CellPx ignored).

api/FluentGpu.Render.LayerKind.html.title: Enum LayerKind | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Render.LayerKind.html.summary: Enum LayerKind Namespace FluentGpu.Render Assembly FluentGpu.Render.dll What a PushLayerCmd…PopLayer pair composites. public enum LayerKind Fields Acrylic = 0 In-app acrylic: snapshot+blur the canvas under DeviceRect, composite the WinUI AcrylicBrush recipe (tint/luminosity/noise — AcrylicBrush.cpp:500-548), then the subtree draws on top. Opacity = 1 Flat opacity group (WinUI Composition LayerVisual semantics): the subtree between Push/Pop renders at FULL alpha into a pooled offscreen RT, then composites ONCE over the canvas at GroupAlpha — overlapping children do not double-blend (unlike the default per-node multiplied opacity, which matches WinUI's plain Visual.Opacity). Tint/blur fields are unused for this kind.

api/FluentGpu.Render.SceneRecorder.html.title: Class SceneRecorder | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Render.SceneRecorder.html.summary: Class SceneRecorder Namespace FluentGpu.Render Assembly FluentGpu.Render.dll Phase 8 (record): walks the retained SceneStore and emits the DrawList. Composites like a browser — each node's geometry is emitted in LOCAL space with a world transform (parent ∘ translate ∘ LocalTransform, scale/rotate about the node's center) and a cumulative opacity, so transform/opacity animate without relayout or re-record of content. Hover/press cross-fade via the eased InteractionAnim row; a focused node gets a dual-stroke focus ring. public static class SceneRecorder Inheritance object SceneRecorder Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods ConfigureScrollbarArrowGlyphs(StringId, StringId, StringId, StringId, StringId) public static void ConfigureScrollbarArrowGlyphs(StringId up, StringId down, StringId left, StringId right, StringId iconFamily) Parameters up StringId down StringId left StringId right StringId iconFamily StringId Record(SceneStore, DrawList, ImageCache?, in FocusVisualStyle, ColorF, ColorF, in TextEditStyle, ReadOnlySpan<NodeHandle>) public static SceneRecordStats Record(SceneStore scene, DrawList dl, ImageCache? images = null, in FocusVisualStyle focus = default, ColorF scrollThumb = default, ColorF scrollTrack = default, in TextEditStyle textEdit = default, ReadOnlySpan<NodeHandle> skipRoots = default) Parameters scene SceneStore dl DrawList images ImageCache focus FocusVisualStyle scrollThumb ColorF scrollTrack ColorF textEdit TextEditStyle skipRoots ReadOnlySpan<NodeHandle> Subtree roots EXCLUDED from this record pass — out-of-bounds popup wrappers that render into their own popup window instead (E4 windowed popups; see RecordSubtree(SceneStore, DrawList, ImageCache?, in FocusVisualStyle, ColorF, ColorF, in TextEditStyle, NodeHandle, Point2)). The subtrees stay in the one SceneStore (layout/hit-test unchanged) — only their pixels move to the popup window's DrawList. Returns SceneRecordStats RecordSubtree(SceneStore, DrawList, ImageCache?, in FocusVisualStyle, ColorF, ColorF, in TextEditStyle, NodeHandle, Point2) Record ONE subtree into its own DrawList, re-origined so the subtree's on-screen top-left lands at the DrawList's (0,0) — the root-override path for E4 out-of-bounds popup windows (the popup subtree stays in the single SceneStore; the host presents this list on the popup window's own swapchain). originDip is the subtree's window-DIP top-left (= the popup's placed position): the walk starts at the subtree root with a parent-world translation of (parentAbs − origin), so every node's own bounds/transform chain composes exactly as in the main pass, shifted into popup-window space. No orphan pass — exit orphans belong to the main window. public static SceneRecordStats RecordSubtree(SceneStore scene, DrawList dl, ImageCache? images, in FocusVisualStyle focus, ColorF scrollThumb, ColorF scrollTrack, in TextEditStyle textEdit, NodeHandle root, Point2 originDip) Parameters scene SceneStore dl DrawList images ImageCache focus FocusVisualStyle scrollThumb ColorF scrollTrack ColorF textEdit TextEditStyle root NodeHandle originDip Point2 Returns SceneRecordStats

api/FluentGpu.Render.html.title: Namespace FluentGpu.Render | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Render.html.summary: Namespace FluentGpu.Render Classes AcrylicBackdropMath Portable math for the in-app acrylic effect runner (design/subsystems/backdrop-effects-animation.md §2.3 two-pass PushLayer{Acrylic} schedule + §3 IEffectRunner seam, gpu-renderer.md §7 LayerPool). The GPU leaf (Rhi.D3D12 AcrylicCompositor) consumes these values so the region / size-bucket / kernel logic is headless-verifiable in the VerticalSlice while the COM/HLSL stays render-thread-confined in the leaf. WinUI ground truth: microsoft-ui-xaml AcrylicBrush.h:64 sc_blurRadius = 30.0f, applied as Composition GaussianBlurEffect.BlurAmount (= the gaussian STANDARD DEVIATION, in DIPs) over the backdrop resolved onto the opaque FallbackColor (AcrylicBrush.cpp:500-528). The runner reproduces sigma = BlurSigma·dpiScale physical px with ONE fixed-sigma separable kernel by choosing the snapshot downsample factor instead of the kernel width: down = round(sigmaPhys / KernelSigma), so blurring the 1/down-resolution snapshot with the fixed sigma-7.5 kernel yields the requested full-resolution sigma (30 DIP ⇒ /4 at 100% DPI, /6 at 150%, /8 at 200%). DrawList Flat POD command stream consumed by the RHI (SubmitDrawList). The slice grows a single contiguous buffer; the full engine uses double-buffered arenas + clean-span memcpy. SortKeys are recorded in parallel. SceneRecorder Phase 8 (record): walks the retained SceneStore and emits the DrawList. Composites like a browser — each node's geometry is emitted in LOCAL space with a world transform (parent ∘ translate ∘ LocalTransform, scale/rotate about the node's center) and a cumulative opacity, so transform/opacity animate without relayout or re-record of content. Hover/press cross-fade via the eased InteractionAnim row; a focused node gets a dual-stroke focus ring. Passed into the recorder by the host (which reads the theme); default = disabled, so the headless/test paths can opt in. PopLayerCmd PushLayerCmd SceneRecordStats TextEditStyle The text-edit decoration brushes (WinUI TextControlSelectionHighlightColor = AccentFillColorSelectedTextBackgroundBrush, TextOnAccentFillColorSelectedTextBrush, and the caret = the text foreground). Passed into the recorder by the host (which reads the theme), like FocusVisualStyle; default = disabled, so existing paths are untouched. api/FluentGpu.Rhi.D3D12.D3D12Device.html.title: Class D3D12Device | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Rhi.D3D12.D3D12Device.html.summary: Class D3D12Device Namespace FluentGpu.Rhi.D3D12 Assembly FluentGpu.Rhi.D3D12.dll The reference Windows RHI backend (design/subsystems/pal-rhi.md, gpu-renderer.md). Real D3D12: hardware device, DIRECT command queue + fence, a DXGI flip-model swapchain on the HWND, an RTV heap, and per-frame record→submit→present. Step 1 clears; the SDF rounded-rect pipeline and the DirectWrite glyph atlas layer on top. public sealed class D3D12Device : IGpuDevice, IDisposable Inheritance object D3D12Device Implements IGpuDevice IDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors D3D12Device(StringTable, bool) public D3D12Device(StringTable strings, bool composited = false) Parameters strings StringTable composited bool Properties BackendName public string BackendName { get; } Property Value string BackendNameSuffix public string BackendNameSuffix { get; } Property Value string DiagGpuDetail One-line GPU residency summary (glyph atlas + image texture store) for the MemCensus GpuDetail hook. Reads the stores' census accessors; null until the device is initialized. Tiny fixed-bucket sums (never per-frame). public string DiagGpuDetail { get; } Property Value string DiagResourceTotals Tracked live D3D12 resource totals (bytes + count) from FluentGpu.Rhi.D3D12.D3D12MemoryDiagnostics — an O(1) read of the running tally for the MemCensus sampler. public (long bytes, int count) DiagResourceTotals { get; } Property Value (long bytes, int count) SizePx public Size2 SizePx { get; } Property Value Size2 Methods CaptureBgra(out int, out int) Debug-only: read the last-rendered back buffer back to CPU as tightly-packed, top-down BGRA8. Used by the --screenshot tooling to produce a PNG for visual fidelity diffing — NOT a hot-path method (it stalls the GPU). With FLIP_DISCARD the just-presented buffer at _frameIndex is still intact until reused. public byte[] CaptureBgra(out int width, out int height) Parameters width int height int Returns byte[] CreateSwapchain(in SwapchainDesc) public ISwapchain CreateSwapchain(in SwapchainDesc desc) Parameters desc SwapchainDesc Returns ISwapchain Dispose() Performs application-defined tasks associated with freeing, releasing, or resetting unmanaged resources. public void Dispose() EvictImage(int) The residency manager evicted imageId — release its GPU texture (deferred behind the frame fence so an in-flight frame can't read freed memory). No-op if not resident. public void EvictImage(int imageId) Parameters imageId int SubmitDrawList(ReadOnlySpan<byte>, ReadOnlySpan<ulong>, in FrameInfo) Record + batch + submit the per-frame DrawList. drawList is the POD command stream. public void SubmitDrawList(ReadOnlySpan<byte> drawList, ReadOnlySpan<ulong> sortKeys, in FrameInfo ctx) Parameters drawList ReadOnlySpan<byte> sortKeys ReadOnlySpan<ulong> ctx FrameInfo UploadImage(int, ReadOnlySpan<byte>, int, int) Hand decoded PREMULTIPLIED BGRA8 pixels for imageId to the backend (the media-pipeline §4.1 texture upload). The backend create-or-replaces a resident texture (or atlas page) keyed by id and samples it from the DrawImage opcode. pbgra8 is valid only for this call — the backend copies it into its texture-staging ring; it is never retained. Rows may not be 256-aligned; the backend pads. Called once per decode completion, before SubmitDrawList(ReadOnlySpan<byte>, ReadOnlySpan<ulong>, in FrameInfo). public void UploadImage(int imageId, ReadOnlySpan<byte> pbgra8, int w, int h) Parameters imageId int pbgra8 ReadOnlySpan<byte> w int h int

D3D12 is the reference backend; Rhi.Headless is the test backend; Metal slots in later behind this seam. ISwapchain

api/FluentGpu.Scene.ExtentTable.html.summary: Class ExtentTable Namespace FluentGpu.Scene Assembly FluentGpu.Scene.dll Per-item extent table for variable-height virtualization: a Fenwick tree / Binary Indexed Tree giving O(log n) OffsetOf(int) (prefix-sum → position a row) and IndexAt(float) (binary-lift → which item is at a scroll offset), with estimate-then-correct extents (an unmeasured item uses the estimate; on realize+measure SetExtent(int, float) corrects it, O(log n)). Folds the Fenwick estimate-then-correct path behind the SAME pluggable seam as the fixed-geometry layouts, so CUSTOM layouts can be variable/sliver-like: unmeasured items report their estimate; at arrange time the layout engine measures each realized row and feeds the real extent back through SetMeasured(int, float, float) (O(log n) correction), then re-pins the scroll anchor via IndexAt(float, float)/OffsetOf(int, float) so corrections above the viewport never jump the visible top (virtualization.md §6.2 — estimate-then-correct + anchoring are seam behavior now, not a special case). api/FluentGpu.Scene.IVirtualLayout.html.summary: Interface IVirtualLayout Namespace FluentGpu.Scene Assembly FluentGpu.Scene.dll A pluggable virtualizing layout: PURE, ALLOCATION-FREE arithmetic mapping (itemCount, viewport, scrollOffset) to the realize window + per-item content-space rects. The engine calls it ONLY on realize/arrange frames (a steady in-window scroll is transform-only and never touches it), and the methods return structs / via out-params — so a custom layout costs zero per-frame managed allocation, honoring the engine's core contract. Implement this to make any virtualized layout (lists, card grids, staggered walls, …). Built-ins: StackVirtualLayout, GridVirtualLayout, HorizontalGridVirtualLayout, LinedFlowLayout, SpanningGridVirtualLayout. Variable-extent (measured) layouts implement the widened seam IMeasuredVirtualLayout — same contract plus estimate-then-correct feedback. public interface IVirtualLayout Methods ContentExtent(int, float) Total scroll-axis extent (the published ContentSize) for itemCount items. float ContentExtent(int itemCount, float crossSize) Parameters itemCount int crossSize float Returns float ItemRect(int, float) Content-space rect of item index (LOCAL to the scroll content origin). api/FluentGpu.Scene.ImageCache.html.title: Class ImageCache | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Scene.ImageCache.html.summary: Class ImageCache Namespace FluentGpu.Scene Assembly FluentGpu.Scene.dll Portable image residency: source→handle dedup, a Pending/Ready/Failed state machine, liveness ref-counting (a pinned/on-screen image is never evicted — the single biggest real-world cache bug, per the research), and an LRU byte budget over decoded images. Decode happens off-thread behind IImageDecoder; Pump() applies completions (+1-frame latency). The GPU texture upload is a needs-pixels leaf concern; this owns the logic. public sealed class ImageCache Inheritance object ImageCache Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors ImageCache(IImageDecoder, long) public ImageCache(IImageDecoder decoder, long budgetBytes = 100663296) Parameters decoder IImageDecoder budgetBytes long Properties Count public int Count { get; } Property Value int HasActiveCrossfades True while any image is still revealing — the host keeps painting so the fade animates to completion. O(1): equivalent to the former per-frame scan (∃ enabled reveal with clockMs < TextureMs+Dur) because that is exactly (_clockMs < max deadline). See FluentGpu.Scene.ImageCache._maxCrossfadeDeadlineMs (wake-04). public bool HasActiveCrossfades { get; } Property Value bool PendingCount Entries still decoding (State==Pending) — O(1) maintained counter (was a per-call scan, wake-04). public int PendingCount { get; } Property Value int ReadyCount public int ReadyCount { get; } Property Value int UsedBytes public long UsedBytes { get; } Property Value long Methods AttemptsOf(ImageHandle) How many fetch attempts the decoder made (≥1 once resolved; >1 means transient retries happened). public int AttemptsOf(ImageHandle h) Parameters h ImageHandle Returns int BucketFor(int) Round a display size up to a decode bucket (64/128/256/512) — the texture-pool / atlas granularity. public static int BucketFor(int px) Parameters px int Returns int Cancel(ImageHandle) Cancel an in-flight decode (row recycled / unmounted) — frees worker + network effort under fast scroll. public void Cancel(ImageHandle h) Parameters h ImageHandle CrossFadeOf(ImageHandle) Placeholder→image reveal progress 0..1, eased by the image's ImageTransition from when its first texture appeared (the BlurHash LQIP, or the full-res decode). 1 once settled, instantly if the transition is disabled, or if there's no texture yet. public float CrossFadeOf(ImageHandle h) Parameters h ImageHandle Returns float FailureOf(ImageHandle) Why an image is Failed (None otherwise) — for app fallbacks / retry UI. public ImageFailureKind FailureOf(ImageHandle h) Parameters h ImageHandle Returns ImageFailureKind Pin(ImageHandle) Pin = "on screen" (a realized node holds it); never evicted while pinned. Unpin on recycle/unmount. public void Pin(ImageHandle h) Parameters h ImageHandle Prefetch(string, int, int) Warm the cache for a source the UI is ABOUT to need (the next scroll page) at Prefetch — decoded ahead, never pinned, so it's instantly Resident when a row scrolls in (Nuke/Coil prefetch). Evictable until a real node pins it. No-op if already requested. public ImageHandle Prefetch(string source, int targetW, int targetH) Parameters source string targetW int targetH int Returns ImageHandle Pump() Apply finished decodes (UI thread, once per frame) then evict to budget. Returns completions this pump. Allocation-free when idle (cached callback; empty-queue Pump does nothing) — safe in the hot phase. public int Pump() Returns int RefsOf(ImageHandle) public int RefsOf(ImageHandle h) Parameters h ImageHandle Returns int Request(string, int, int, ImagePriority, string?, ImageTransition?) Request (or dedup) a decode of source at a target size; returns a stable handle. On a cache MISS, an optional blurHash is decoded ONCE into a tiny LQIP texture (uploaded under this id) so a blurred preview shows instantly until the full-res art lands. A cache HIT returns before any of this — so re-renders of the same image do no work. public ImageHandle Request(string source, int targetW, int targetH, ImagePriority priority = ImagePriority.Visible, string? blurHash = null, ImageTransition? transition = null) Parameters source string targetW int targetH int priority ImagePriority blurHash string transition ImageTransition? Returns ImageHandle SetEvictSink(Action<int>) The host wires this to IGpuDevice.EvictImage; the cache calls it when residency evicts an image so the backend frees the GPU texture. Set once at composition. public void SetEvictSink(Action<int> sink) Parameters sink Action<int> SetPixelSink(ImageReadyHandler) The host wires this to IGpuDevice.UploadImage; the cache forwards each decode's pixels through it during Pump() (transiently — the sink must copy, not store). Set once at composition. public void SetPixelSink(ImageReadyHandler sink) Parameters sink ImageReadyHandler SizeOf(ImageHandle) public (int W, int H) SizeOf(ImageHandle h) Parameters h ImageHandle Returns (int Start, int End) StateOf(ImageHandle) public ImageState StateOf(ImageHandle h) Parameters h ImageHandle Returns ImageState Tick(float) Advance the cross-fade clock by dtMs (call once per painted frame, before record). public void Tick(float dtMs) Parameters dtMs float Unpin(ImageHandle) public void Unpin(ImageHandle h) Parameters h ImageHandle Events ImageStatusChanged Raised (UI thread, during Pump()) when an image reaches a terminal state — Ready or Failed. Apps subscribe for a broken-art fallback, a retry/offline toast, or telemetry. See also FailureOf(ImageHandle). public event ImageStatusHandler? ImageStatusChanged Event Type ImageStatusHandler

api/FluentGpu.Scene.LinedFlowLayout.html.title: Class LinedFlowLayout | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Scene.LinedFlowLayout.html.summary: Class LinedFlowLayout Namespace FluentGpu.Scene Assembly FluentGpu.Scene.dll WinUI LinedFlowLayout — the ItemsView photo-wall: items flow left-to-right into uniform-height LINES, each item's width = its aspect ratio × LineHeight; a line wraps when the next item would overflow the cross size. Uniform line stride keeps windowing O(1); the per-item line table is (re)built lazily ONLY when (itemCount, crossSize) change — realize/arrange calls stay allocation-free per the seam contract. WinUI defaults: LineSpacing 0, MinItemSpacing 0 (LinedFlowLayout.h:25,27 s_defaultLineSpacing/s_defaultMinItemSpacing); LineHeight has no auto here (WinUI s_defaultLineHeight = NaN = auto-from-first-item; the engine takes it explicitly). Items wider than the cross size clamp to it (single-item line). STATEFUL — create once and reuse. public sealed class LinedFlowLayout : IVirtualLayout Inheritance object LinedFlowLayout Implements IVirtualLayout Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors LinedFlowLayout(float, Func<int, float>?, float, float) public LinedFlowLayout(float lineHeight, Func<int, float>? aspectRatio = null, float lineSpacing = 0, float minItemSpacing = 0) Parameters lineHeight float aspectRatio Func<int, float> lineSpacing float minItemSpacing float Fields LineHeight public readonly float LineHeight Field Value float LineSpacing public readonly float LineSpacing Field Value float MinItemSpacing public readonly float MinItemSpacing Field Value float Methods ContentExtent(int, float) Total scroll-axis extent (the published ContentSize) for itemCount items. public float ContentExtent(int n, float cross) Parameters n int cross float Returns float ItemRect(int, float) Content-space rect of item index (LOCAL to the scroll content origin). public RectF ItemRect(int i, float cross) Parameters i int cross float Returns RectF Window(int, float, float, float, int, out int, out int) The [first,last) item range to realize for scrollOffset (incl. overscan). public void Window(int n, float cross, float viewport, float offset, int overscan, out int first, out int last) Parameters n int cross float viewport float offset float overscan int first int last int

api/FluentGpu.Scene.MeasuredStackVirtual.summary: Class MeasuredStackVirtualLayout Namespace FluentGpu.Scene Assembly FluentGpu.Scene.dll The Fenwick variable-extent list behind the IMeasuredVirtualLayout seam (E11-L0): every row starts at Estimate, gets corrected to its measured extent on realize, and the layout engine re-pins the scroll anchor across corrections. Functionally the engine's legacy Layout = null variable path (kept for compatibility), but USER-REACHABLE: compose it, subclass the idea, or build your own measured layout on the same contract. api/FluentGpu.Scene.ScrollState.html.title: Struct ScrollState | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Scene.ScrollState.html.summary: Struct ScrollState Namespace FluentGpu.Scene Assembly FluentGpu.Scene.dll Scroll + virtualization state for a viewport node (marked NodeFlags.Scrollable). There are O(viewports) of these — not one per node — so the store keeps them in a sparse side-table keyed by node index, not a parallel column. Ownership (layout.md §6 / architecture-spec §5.5): Input owns Offset* (clamped to the published content); Layout publishes Content*/Viewport*; the virtualizer owns the Item* / realized-range / anchor fields. Scroll is layout-free: the -ScrollOffset translation is the ContentNode's LocalTransform, never a relayout. public struct ScrollState Inherited Members ValueType.Equals(object) ValueType.GetHashCode() ValueType.ToString() object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Fields AnchorIndex public int AnchorIndex Field Value int AnchorKey public StringId AnchorKey Field Value StringId AnchorViewportDelta public float AnchorViewportDelta Field Value float ContentH public float ContentH Field Value float ContentNode public NodeHandle ContentNode Field Value NodeHandle ContentSized public bool ContentSized Field Value bool ContentW public float ContentW Field Value float ExpandT public float ExpandT Field Value float ExtentTableRef public int ExtentTableRef Field Value int FadeT public float FadeT Field Value float FirstRealized public int FirstRealized Field Value int IdleMs public float IdleMs Field Value float ItemCount public int ItemCount Field Value int LastRealized public int LastRealized Field Value int Layout public IVirtualLayout? api/FluentGpu.Scene.SpanningGridVirtualL.title: Class SpanningGridVirtualLayout | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Scene.SpanningGridVirtualL.summary: Class SpanningGridVirtualLayout Namespace FluentGpu.Scene Assembly FluentGpu.Scene.dll Uniform-row grid with ITEM SPANNING (E11-L0): each item occupies SpanOf(i) columns (clamped 1..Columns) and items pack row-major — a span that doesn't fit the current row wraps to the next (the "hero as the first full-width row" shape: SpanOf(0) == Columns). Placement depends only on the item count (not cross size), so the table rebuilds only on count change; windowing binary-searches the monotonic row column. api/FluentGpu.Scene.TextMeasureCache.htm.title: Struct TextMeasureCache | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Scene.TextMeasureCache.htm.summary: Struct TextMeasureCache Namespace FluentGpu.Scene Assembly FluentGpu.Scene.dll Per-text-node measure cache (layout.md §2.3): a pure-function cache of (text, style, availWidth) → size, so a scoped relayout skips re-shaping a text leaf whose inputs are unchanged. Self-invalidating — any input change makes the stored key not match. Helps the real DirectWrite shaping path; neutral for the headless fake font. Besides the size, the cache retains the face's DECORATION metrics from the same TextMetrics (top-down DIP, the line frame of Baseline — see FluentGpu.Text.TextMetrics): the recorder reads them at record time to place underline/strikethrough bars (NodePaint.TextDecorations) without re-touching the font seam. Filled by the layout engine's measure-miss path; 0 ⇒ the backend reported no face metrics (the recorder falls back to a size-derived approximation). public struct TextMeasureCache Inherited Members ValueType.Equals(object) ValueType.GetHashCode() ValueType.ToString() object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Fields MaxW public float MaxW Field Value float Size public Size2 Size Field Value Size2 StrikeY Strikethrough bar top, measured DOWN from the line top (DWrite strikethroughPosition flipped; headless model: SizeDip × 0.8). public float StrikeY Field Value float Style public TextStyle Style Field Value TextStyle Text public StringId Text Field Value StringId UnderlineThickness Underline bar thickness (DWrite underlineThickness; also reused for the strikethrough bar, the DWrite/WinUI convention). public float UnderlineThickness Field Value float UnderlineY Underline bar top, measured DOWN from the line top (DWrite underlinePosition flipped over the baseline — TextLayoutEngine.cs:141; headless model: baseline + 1). public float UnderlineY Field Value float Valid public bool Valid Field Value bool

api/FluentGpu.Scene.html.title: Namespace FluentGpu.Scene | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Scene.html.summary: Namespace FluentGpu.Scene Classes ExtentTable Per-item extent table for variable-height virtualization: a Fenwick tree / Binary Indexed Tree giving O(log n) OffsetOf(int) (prefix-sum → position a row) and IndexAt(float) (binary-lift → which item is at a scroll offset), with estimate-then-correct extents (an unmeasured item uses the estimate; on realize+measure SetExtent(int, float) corrects it, O(log n)). It persists across frames (only deltas update) and survives a fling because corrections are O(measured-rows). 100k items × 8B ≈ 800KB — within budget. (virtualization.md §6.2 / layout.md §8.4 — the table MATH. It lives here in FluentGpu.Scene because both Layout and the Reconciler depend on Scene; double-precision prefix sums keep it exact at 100k×100px.) FakeImageDecoder Deterministic headless/offline decoder: completes on the NEXT Pump() (the +1-frame latency contract), reporting the requested target size and synthesizing a stable per-id BGRA pattern so the GPU upload + sample path is exercisable without real codecs or network. Proves the residency/state logic. GridVirtualLayout Uniform 2-D grid (album/artist card shelves) — virtualizes by ROW: realizes visible-rows × columns, recycles identically. Columns share the cross size; this is the virtualized UniformGridLayout. GroupedListVirtualLayout Grouped flat list with measured rows (E11-L0 grouping hook): the items are a FLATTENED projection — group headers occupy indices of their own (a header is just a measured item KIND, the app-requirements "Liked Songs" shape). HeaderIndices must be sorted ascending. Headers seed at HeaderEstimate, items at ItemEstimate; both correct to measured extents. StickyHeaderIndexAt(float) is the sticky-header support hook: the header that should pin for a given scroll offset (binary search, O(log groups)) — consumers (ItemsView / a custom list) translate that header's realized node by the pin delta at phase 7 (NodeFlags.StickyPinned). Vertical scroll. STATEFUL — create once and reuse (see MeasuredStackVirtualLayout). HorizontalGridVirtualLayout Uniform 2-D grid scrolling HORIZONTALLY (a shelf of fixed-width columns, Rows cells tall) — virtualizes by COLUMN: realizes visible-columns × rows. The horizontal variant of GridVirtualLayout; pair with VirtualListEl.Horizontal = true. ImageCache Portable image residency: source→handle dedup, a Pending/Ready/Failed state machine, liveness ref-counting (a pinned/on-screen image is never evicted — the single biggest real-world cache bug, per the research), and an LRU byte budget over decoded images. Decode happens off-thread behind IImageDecoder; Pump() applies completions (+1-frame latency). The GPU texture upload is a needs-pixels leaf concern; this owns the logic. LinedFlowLayout WinUI LinedFlowLayout — the ItemsView photo-wall: items flow left-to-right into uniform-height LINES, each item's width = its aspect ratio × LineHeight; a line wraps when the next item would overflow the cross size. Uniform line stride keeps windowing O(1); the per-item line table is (re)built lazily ONLY when (itemCount, crossSize) change — realize/arrange calls stay allocation-free per the seam contract. WinUI defaults: LineSpacing 0, MinItemSpacing 0 (LinedFlowLayout.h:25,27 s_defaultLineSpacing/s_defaultMinItemSpacing); LineHeight has no auto here (WinUI s_defaultLineHeight = NaN = auto-from-first-item; the engine takes it explicitly). Items wider than the cross size clamp to it (single-item line). STATEFUL — create once and reuse. MeasuredStackVirtualLayout The Fenwick variable-extent list behind the IMeasuredVirtualLayout seam (E11-L0): every row starts at Estimate, gets corrected to its measured extent on realize, and the layout engine re-pins the scroll anchor across corrections. Functionally the engine's legacy Layout = null variable path (kept for compatibility), but USER-REACHABLE: compose it, subclass the idea, or build your own measured layout on the same contract. STATEFUL (owns an ExtentTable) — create ONCE and reuse across renders (hoist in a UseMemo); the table self-rebuilds only on item-count change. SceneStore Struct-of-arrays retained RenderNode tree. One spine (gen + free-list) indexes all parallel columns. SpanningGridVirtualLayout Uniform-row grid with ITEM SPANNING (E11-L0): each item occupies SpanOf(i) columns (clamped 1..Columns) and items pack row-major — a span that doesn't fit the current row wraps to the next (the "hero as the first full-width row" shape: SpanOf(0) == Columns). Placement depends only on the item count (not cross size), so the table rebuilds only on count change; windowing binary-searches the monotonic row column. STATEFUL — create once and reuse. StackVirtualLayout Uniform 1-D stack (the WaveeMusic track-list shape) — O(1) windowing. horizontal scrolls X. VirtualWindowing Decides whether a scroll offset needs a virtual-window refresh, using the realized overscan as a guard band. Structs BrushAnim An implicit brush transition (WinUI BrushTransition, 83ms): when a LOGICAL state flip re-renders a node with a different Fill/BorderColor/TextColor and the element opted in (BrushTransitionMs), the reconciler captures the previously-DISPLAYED color here and the recorder cross-fades from it to the new resolved color as T advances (linear-light, like the hover/press cross-fade). Sparse side-table — O(transitioning nodes), 0-alloc steady frames. GridSpec Grid layout spec for a grid container node (sparse side-table, O(grids)). The reconciler writes it from a GridEl; the layout engine resolves column tracks at the final width and auto-flows the cells row-major. ImageBinding What UseImage returns (media-pipeline.md §5): the stable handle + current load state, so a component can render a spinner / a broken-art fallback / read the failure kind, all without prop-drilling. ImageHandle A generational-free image handle into the ImageCache (0 = none). InteractionAnim Eased interaction progress for a node (sparse side-table, O(interacted nodes)). The InteractionAnimator eases HoverT/PressT toward their targets on pointer enter/leave/press; the recorder lerps Fill/Border with them for the WinUI ~83ms brush transition (instead of the instant flag switch). InteractionInfo Hit-test / input column. LayoutInput Layout-input column (flexbox: direction + gap + padding + margin + flex grow/shrink/basis + justify/align + min/max + explicit size + text style). NodePaint Paint column — one cache line of per-node visual state read by the record phase. ScrollState Scroll + virtualization state for a viewport node (marked NodeFlags.Scrollable). There are O(viewports) of these — not one per node — so the store keeps them in a sparse side-table keyed by node index, not a parallel column. Ownership (layout.md §6 / architecture-spec §5.5): Input owns Offset* (clamped to the published content); Layout publishes Content*/Viewport*; the virtualizer owns the Item* / realized-range / anchor fields. Scroll is layout-free: the -ScrollOffset translation is the ContentNode's LocalTransform, never a relayout. TextEditState Sparse text-edit state for an editor's TEXT node (side-table, O(editors)): caret geometry + caret-follow scroll + in-flight IME composition span + focus/blink flags. Written by the editing control (UI thread, edit/drag time) and the CaretBlinker phase-7 ticker; the recorder only READS it (plus the pooled decoration rects on SetTextEditRects(NodeHandle, ReadOnlySpan<RectF>, ReadOnlySpan<RectF>)) to emit selection highlight / selected-text recolor / IME underlines / the caret bar — retained scene state, never composed elements (0 alloc in phases 6–13). TextMeasureCache Per-text-node measure cache (layout.md §2.3): a pure-function cache of (text, style, availWidth) → size, so a scoped relayout skips re-shaping a text leaf whose inputs are unchanged. Self-invalidating — any input change makes the stored key not match. Helps the real DirectWrite shaping path; neutral for the headless fake font. Besides the size, the cache retains the face's DECORATION metrics from the same TextMetrics (top-down DIP, the line frame of Baseline — see FluentGpu.Text.TextMetrics): the recorder reads them at record time to place underline/strikethrough bars (NodePaint.TextDecorations) without re-touching the font seam. Filled by the layout engine's measure-miss path; 0 ⇒ the backend reported no face metrics (the recorder falls back to a size-derived approximation). Interfaces IImageDecoder The decode seam: the portable cache asks a leaf to decode a source to a target size, off the UI thread. The Windows leaf is WIC→GPU (needs-pixels); the headless leaf is deterministic. Pump(ImageCompleteHandler, ImageReadyHandler) drains completions onto the UI thread (the +1-frame latency contract: a request is never ready the same frame), invoking onPixels with the bucket pixels then onComplete with the state transition. IMeasuredVirtualLayout The variable-extent (measured) virtualization seam — E11-L0. Folds the Fenwick estimate-then-correct path behind the SAME pluggable seam as the fixed-geometry layouts, so CUSTOM layouts can be variable/sliver-like: unmeasured items report their estimate; at arrange time the layout engine measures each realized row and feeds the real extent back through SetMeasured(int, float, float) (O(log n) correction), then re-pins the scroll anchor via IndexAt(float, float)/OffsetOf(int, float) so corrections above the viewport never jump the visible top (virtualization.md §6.2 — estimate-then-correct + anchoring are seam behavior now, not a special case). Built-ins: MeasuredStackVirtualLayout (the Fenwick variable list), GroupedListVirtualLayout (group headers = a measured item kind + sticky-header hook). ISceneBackend The reconciler's only window onto the retained tree — handle-in / handle-out, POD-only. (The slice implements this directly on SceneStore.) IVirtualLayout A pluggable virtualizing layout: PURE, ALLOCATION-FREE arithmetic mapping (itemCount, viewport, scrollOffset) to the realize window + per-item content-space rects. The engine calls it ONLY on realize/arrange frames (a steady in-window scroll is transform-only and never touches it), and the methods return structs / via out-params — so a custom layout costs zero per-frame managed allocation, honoring the engine's core contract. Implement this to make any virtualized layout (lists, card grids, staggered walls, …). Built-ins: StackVirtualLayout, GridVirtualLayout, HorizontalGridVirtualLayout, LinedFlowLayout, SpanningGridVirtualLayout. Variable-extent (measured) layouts implement the widened seam IMeasuredVirtualLayout — same contract plus estimate-then-correct feedback. Enums ImageFailureKind Why an image decode ended (for diagnostics + app fallbacks). Transient kinds are retried by the decoder before a request is reported Failed; permanent kinds fail immediately. ImagePriority Decode urgency (media-pipeline.md §3). Workers drain higher priority first; under backpressure the lowest off-screen lane (Prefetch, then Overscan) is dropped — never Visible. ImageState VisualKind Delegates ImageCompleteHandler Decode-completion callback: id, success, decoded dims, the failure kind (if any), and the fetch attempt count. ImageReadyHandler Forwards decoded PREMULTIPLIED BGRA8 pixels to the GPU backend. The span is valid only for the duration of the synchronous call (it is never stored — the backend copies it into its upload heap), so the cache need not own pixel memory: it flows decoder → cache.Pump → host sink → IGpuDevice.UploadImage in one stack. ImageStatusHandler Image lifecycle notification for app observability (e.g. a retry toast, a broken-art fallback, telemetry). Raised on the UI thread during Pump(). attempts is the fetch attempt count (≥1; >1 means transient retries happened).

api/FluentGpu.Signals.Prop.html.summary: Class Prop Namespace FluentGpu.Signals Assembly FluentGpu.Foundation.dll A unified reactive channel property: ONE field that is EITHER a static value (re-asserted by the reconciler on every reconcile of the element, iff not bound) OR a reactive bind — a Func<TResult> thunk or a concrete signal the reconciler wires into a mount-time effect that writes exactly one scene column and marks its dirty axis. Constructed implicitly: Opacity = 0.5f (static), Opacity = () => f(sig.Value) (derived thunk), Opacity = sig (signal-direct — no user closure). default(Prop<T>) is the static default(T) — never use it to mean "unset"; element property initializers (= 1f, = NaN) run the T conversion and survive with clones (a bound clone stays bound). Kind discrimination is a runtime type test on the single payload ref — no tag byte; the static reconcile path reads only IsBound (one null test). Conversions from the IReadSignal<T> INTERFACE are illegal in C# (CS0552) — through an interface-typed variable use the thunk form chan = () => s.Value. public static class Prop Inheritance object Prop Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods Of<T>(Func<T>) public static Prop<T> Of<T>(Func<T> thunk) Parameters thunk Func<T> Returns Prop<T> Type Parameters T

api/FluentGpu.Signals.Reactive.html.title: Class Reactive | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Signals.Reactive.html.summary: Class Reactive Namespace FluentGpu.Signals Assembly FluentGpu.Foundation.dll The reactive core: a Solid/Preact-style fine-grained reactivity graph. Signals are observable cells; computations (effects, memos, component render-effects) auto-subscribe to the signals they READ during a run, and re-run when a read signal changes. This is the single update mechanism the whole engine is built on — a property binding is an effect at node granularity; a component re-render is an effect at subtree granularity. Threading: UI-thread-confined (the engine's single render/reconcile thread), so tracking state is ThreadStaticAttribute. Scheduling is DEFERRED: a signal write marks dependent effects stale and asks the host for a frame; the host drains them once per frame via Flush() (phase 3), which keeps reconcile/layout on their phases and the per-frame paint allocation-free. public static class Reactive Inheritance object Reactive Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods OnCleanup(Action) Register a cleanup to run when the enclosing computation re-runs or is disposed (the Solid onCleanup). public static void OnCleanup(Action cleanup) Parameters cleanup Action Untrack(Action) Read a signal-backed value without subscribing (peek). public static void Untrack(Action fn) Parameters fn Action Untrack<T>(Func<T>) Run fn without subscribing the current computation to any signal it reads. public static T Untrack<T>(Func<T> fn) Parameters fn Func<T> Returns T Type Parameters T

api/FluentGpu.Text.DirectWrite.DWriteIte.summary: Class DWriteItemizer Namespace FluentGpu.Text.DirectWrite Assembly FluentGpu.Text.DirectWrite.dll DirectWrite itemizer (text.md §4.4): segments text into BiDi × script runs and computes UAX #14 line-break opportunities via IDWriteTextAnalyzer. DirectWrite calls back into the hand-authored callee CCWs FluentGpu.Text.DirectWrite.TextAnalysisSourceCcw (supplies the text) and FluentGpu.Text.DirectWrite.TextAnalysisSinkCcw (collects the runs). COM bound via TerraFX raw pointers; the CCW vtables mirror the ComputeSharp PixelShaderEffect pattern. Single-thread-confined: Itemize(ReadOnlySpan<char>, List<ItemRun>, List<BreakOpp>) calls AnalyzeBidi/Script/LineBreakpoints serially on the caller thread. public sealed class DWriteItemizer : ITextItemizer, IDisposable Inheritance object DWriteItemizer Implements ITextItemizer IDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors DWriteItemizer() public DWriteItemizer() Methods Dispose() Performs application-defined tasks associated with freeing, releasing, or resetting unmanaged resources. public void Dispose() Itemize(ReadOnlySpan<char>, List<ItemRun>, List<BreakOpp>) Itemize text into runs (BiDi × script segmentation, logical order) and breaks (one entry per UTF-16 unit). Both lists are cleared and refilled. public void Itemize(ReadOnlySpan<char> text, List<ItemRun> runs, List<BreakOpp> breaks) Parameters text ReadOnlySpan<char> runs List<ItemRun> breaks List<BreakOpp> SelfTest() Smoke test on the real DirectWrite analyzer (Windows only): itemize a mixed LTR/RTL/CJK string and print the runs + break opportunities. api/FluentGpu.Text.DirectWrite.DirectWri.title: Class DirectWriteFontSystem | FluentGpu Developer Docs
api/FluentGpu.Text.DirectWrite.DirectWri.summary: Class DirectWriteFontSystem Namespace FluentGpu.Text.DirectWrite Assembly FluentGpu.Text.DirectWrite.dll The DirectWrite text backend (IFontSystem) — a thin wrapper over TextLayoutEngine. Every member (measurement AND the editor queries) runs the full itemize → shape → wrap/trim → position pipeline (kerning, ligatures, complex script, UAX #14 line breaking, CJK/emoji font fallback), so the measured box, the hit-tested caret and the selection rects all come from the SAME layout the renderer — which uses the same engine — actually draws. measure ≡ hit-test ≡ render: this parity is what keeps shaped glyphs from overlapping (the divergence that read as bold when the renderer was shaped but measurement was per-char). Touched on the UI thread during phase-6 layout and at edit/drag time; the render thread owns a separate engine instance, so queries here can never corrupt render-side buffers. Query results live in the engine's reused grow-only tables — 0 steady-state allocation per keystroke/pointer-move. public sealed class DirectWriteFontSystem : IFontSystem, IDisposable Inheritance object DirectWriteFontSystem Implements IFontSystem IDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors DirectWriteFontSystem(StringTable) public DirectWriteFontSystem(StringTable strings) Parameters strings StringTable Methods Dispose() Performs application-defined tasks associated with freeing, releasing, or resetting unmanaged resources. public void Dispose() GetCaret(ReadOnlySpan<char>, in TextStyle, float, int, out float, out float, out float, out int) Caret geometry for a UTF-16 index: x + the line's top/height (single line: top = 0, height = the line height) + line index. Returns the LEADING position of charIndex; at a soft-wrap boundary the leading position is the START of the continuation line — trailing affinity (pinning the caret to the END of the wrapped line) is the CALLER's job. charIndex == text length → the trailing edge of the last line. An index inside a multi-char cluster snaps to the cluster's edges. Editor-hosting backends must implement this; the default throws. public void GetCaret(ReadOnlySpan<char> text, in TextStyle style, float maxWidth, int charIndex, out float x, out float lineTop, out float lineHeight, out int lineIndex) Parameters text ReadOnlySpan<char> style TextStyle maxWidth float charIndex int x float lineTop float lineHeight float lineIndex int GetRangeRects(ReadOnlySpan<char>, in TextStyle, float, int, int, Span<RectF>) One rect per line fragment covered by [start, end), x-from/x-to via cluster edges. Returns the count written (≤ rects.Length; excess fragments are dropped — callers size generously). Zero-width ranges produce nothing. Editor-hosting backends must implement this; the default throws. public int GetRangeRects(ReadOnlySpan<char> text, in TextStyle style, float maxWidth, int start, int end, Span<RectF> rects) Parameters text ReadOnlySpan<char> style TextStyle maxWidth float start int end int rects Span<RectF> Returns int HitTestText(ReadOnlySpan<char>, in TextStyle, float, Point2, out bool) Point → UTF-16 index. Returns the SNAPPED insertion index — the leading or trailing edge of the hit glyph cluster — and trailing reports which half of the cluster was hit. Points outside the laid-out bounds clamp to the nearest valid index (above → first line, below → last line, left → line start, right → line end), so drag-selection keeps tracking past the box. Editor-hosting backends must implement this; the default throws (the experimental GDI path cannot host editable text). public int HitTestText(ReadOnlySpan<char> text, in TextStyle style, float maxWidth, Point2 point, out bool trailing) Parameters text ReadOnlySpan<char> style TextStyle maxWidth float point Point2 trailing bool Returns int Measure(StringId, in TextStyle, float) Measure a string under a style (intrinsic content size). Feeds the layout engine. When maxWidth is finite and the style wraps, the result is the word-wrapped multi-line box. public TextMetrics Measure(StringId text, in TextStyle style, float maxWidth = Infinity) Parameters text StringId style TextStyle maxWidth float Returns TextMetrics
